The classification layer leverages a combined weighted cross-entropy and focal loss function to address class imbalance, while Grad-CAM and SHAP explainability modules provide transparent and clinically interpretable predictions. Experiments conducted on the ISIC and HAM10000 datasets demonstrate that the proposed system achieves 95% accuracy, 0.94 F1-score, and 0.98 AUC, outperforming widely used baselines such as ResNet50, DenseNet121, and EfficientNet. The results highlight the robustness and clinical relevance of the framework, making it a promising tool for real-world dermatological applications.

title: RAT4D: Rig and Animate Objects without Surface Templates in 4D
authors: Mosam Dabhi, Simon Lucey, László A. Jeni
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: We present a surface-template-free method for reconstructing dense, rigged (re-animatable) 3D models from monocular videos. By combining robust pose optimization with differentiable Gaussian splatting, this work bridges the gap between flexible template-free approaches and the visual quality of template-based methods. Starting from noisy 2D keypoints, we refine 3D poses through kinematic and temporal constraints, then attach Gaussian primitives to the optimized skeleton for differentiable supervision and rendering. We demonstrate dense, rigged (re-animatable) 3D models without surface templates across humans, animals, insects, and everyday articulated objects, and we empirically show that closing the rendering–pose loop improves 3D lifting from noisy landmarks. A key enabler of these template-free reconstructions is our kinematic optimization, which reduces 3D pose error by 20–25% relative to template-free baselines; at the same time, our results approach template-based visual metrics (PSNR/SSIM within 5%). We also demonstrate the method’s practical utility by detecting and correcting geometric inconsistencies in AI-generated videos. While limited to articulated subjects with detectable keypoints, the approach provides a practical pipeline that serves as a drop-in refinement to improve 3D lifting in existing pipelines and enables the creation of rigged 3D assets from casual captures when expensive surface templates (e.g., MoCap-derived) are unavailable.

title: Crowd Behavior Monitoring for Panic or Stampede Prediction using Video Analytics and Multi-Parameter Sensor Fusion
authors: Satvika S S, Tharik Grishan S, Loveline Zeema J
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Stampede incidents at mass gatherings represent a critical public safety challenge, demanding proactive intervention systems. Traditional crowd monitoring employs reactive detection mechanisms that provide insufficient time for effective emergency response. This research presents a comprehensive Multi-Stage Crowd Stampede Prediction and Risk Fusion System that integrates four critical, heterogeneous risk parameters—crowd density, abnormal movements, crowd pressure, and physiological temperature through a weighted fusion model to significantly extend prediction lead time. Our system implements a real-time video analytics pipeline using sliding window analysis (10-frame sequences). Feature extraction is handled by specialized deep learning models: CSRNet for density estimation, a Spatiotemporal Autoencoder for abnormal movement detection, and Random Forest regressors for predicting latent pressure and temperature proxies. The four normalized risk indicators are fused using a weighted linear combination (highest weight: 0.40 for abnormal movements) to generate a unified risk score, enabling a three-tier classification: SAFE (score < 0.30), ALERT (0.30–0.60), and DANGER (score ≥ 0.60). Validation on various datasets shows that the Random Forest models achieve high predictive accuracy (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> > 0.98), and that anomaly detection (F1-score: 88.6%) and accurate transition through risk states are successful. The system offers a stable and open framework while processing video frames at real-time speeds. This framework allows proactive crowd management interventions that may help prevent stampede casualties during major public events by extending prediction horizons and integrating multiple risk modalities.

title: Detection-Driven Object Count Optimization for Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
authors: Oz Zafar, Yuval Cohen, Lior Wolf, Idan Schwartz
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Accurately controlling object count in text-to-image generation remains a key challenge. Supervised methods often fail, as training data rarely covers all count variations. Methods that manipulate the denoising process to add or remove objects can help; however, they still require labeled data, limit robustness and image quality, and rely on a slow, iterative process.Pre-trained differentiable counting models that rely on soft object density summation exist and could steer generation, but employing them presents three main challenges: (i) they are pre-trained on clean images, making them less effective during denoising steps that operate on noisy inputs; (ii) they are not robust to viewpoint changes; and (iii) optimization is computationally expensive, requiring repeated model evaluations per image.We propose a new framework that uses pre-trained object counting techniques and object detectors to guide generation. First, we optimize a counting token using an outer-loop loss computed on fully generated images. Second, we introduce a detection-driven scaling term that corrects errors caused by viewpoint and proportion shifts, etc., without requiring backpropagation through the detection model. Third, we show that the optimized parameters can be reused for new prompts, removing the need for repeated optimization. Our method provides efficiency through token reuse, flexibility via compatibility with various detectors, and accuracy with improved counting across diverse object categories.

title: RemEdit: Efficient Diffusion Editing with Riemannian Geometry
authors: Eashan Adhikarla, Brian D. Davison
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Controllable image generation is fundamental to the success of modern generative AI, yet it faces a critical trade-off between semantic fidelity and inference speed. The RemEdit diffusion-based framework addresses this trade-off with two synergistic innovations. First, for editing fidelity, we navigate the latent space as a Riemannian manifold. A mamba-based module efficiently learns the manifold’s structure, enabling direct and accurate geodesic path computation for smooth semantic edits. This control is further refined by a dual-SLERP blending technique and a goal-aware prompt enrichment pass from a Vision-Language Model. Second, for additional acceleration, we introduce a novel task-specific attention pruning mechanism. A lightweight pruning head learns to retain tokens essential to the edit, enabling effective optimization without the semantic degradation common in content-agnostic approaches. RemEdit surpasses prior state-of-the-art editing frameworks while maintaining real-time performance under 50% pruning. Consequently, RemEdit establishes a new benchmark for practical and powerful image editing. Source code: github.com/eashanadhikarla/RemEdit.

title: ImageChain: Advancing Sequential Image-to-Text Reasoning in Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Danae Sánchez Villegas, Ingo Ziegler, Desmond Elliott
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Reasoning over sequences of images remains a challenge for multimodal large language models (MLLMs). While recent models incorporate multi-image data during pretraining, they still struggle to recognize sequential structures, often treating images independently. This work introduces ImageChain, a framework that enhances MLLMs with sequential reasoning capabilities over image data by modeling visual sequences as a multi-turn conversation. In ImageChain, images are interleaved with corresponding textual descriptions to form a controlled dialogue that explicitly captures temporal dependencies and narrative progression. Our method optimizes for the task of next-scene description, where the model generates a context-aware description of an upcoming scene based on preceding visual and textual cues. We demonstrate that our approach improves performance on the next-scene description task – achieving an average improvement from 3.7% to 19% in SimRate, a metric that quantifies semantic similarity to human-annotated ground truths. Moreover, ImageChain achieves robust zero-shot out-of-domain performance in applications ranging from comics to robotics. Extensive experiments validate that instruction-tuning in a multimodal, multi-turn conversation design is key to bridging the gap between static image understanding and temporally-aware reasoning.<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>

title: Beyond Paired Data: Self-Supervised UAV Geo-Localization from Reference Imagery Alone
authors: Tristan Amadei, Enric Meinhardt-Llopis, Benedicte Bascle, Corentin Abgrall, Gabriele Facciolo
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Image-based localization in GNSS-denied environments is critical for UAV autonomy. Existing state-of-the-art approaches rely on matching UAV images to geo-referenced satellite images; however, they typically require large-scale, paired UAV–satellite datasets for training. Such data are costly to acquire and often unavailable, limiting their applicability. To address this challenge, we adopt a training paradigm that removes the need for UAV imagery during training by learning directly from satellite-view reference images. This is achieved through a dedicated augmentation strategy that simulates the visual domain shift between satellite and real-world UAV views. We introduce CAEVL, an efficient model designed to exploit this paradigm, and validate it on ViLD, a new and challenging dataset of real-world UAV images that we release to the community. Our method achieves competitive performance compared to approaches trained with paired data, demonstrating its effectiveness and strong generalization capabilities.

title: Integrating Pseudo-Supervision and Spatial Constraints for Efficient Clustering of Multimodal Remote Sensing Data
authors: Zhe Cao, Tao Zhang, Zihua Zhao, Haonan Xin, Rong Wang, Feiping Nie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Multimodal remote sensing (RS) clustering synergistically integrates multi-dimensional information, effectively addressing the representational limitations of single-modality data. This integration offers essential technical support for fine-grained recognition and accurate interpretation of ground objects in complex scenarios. However, existing methods still face several challenges, including insufficient utilization of spatial information, limited ability to extract consistent information due to inter-modal heterogeneity, and low efficiency when handling large-scale and complex datasets. To address these issues, we propose an Integrating Pseudo-Supervision and Spatial Constraints for Efficient Clustering (PSSC) of Multimodal Remote Sensing Data model. The proposed method begins by constructing spatial bipartite graphs from multimodal data to fully exploit spatial information while reducing computational complexity. These graphs are then stacked into a third-order tensor, upon which a robust denoised representation is learned to suppress noise and preserve the core structural characteristics of the multimodal inputs. Based on this clean tensor, PSSC captures cross-modal consistency by minimizing the tensor nuclear norm within the low-rank space. To further enhance clustering efficiency and accuracy, a region homogeneity-constrained rapid label generation strategy is proposed, which leverages high-confidence pseudo-supervision information from homogeneous regions to iteratively refine clustering labels, thereby significantly reducing computational overhead. Extensive experiments on real-world multimodal datasets validate the effectiveness and superior performance of the proposed method.

title: Flight Dynamics to Sensing Modalities: Exploiting Drone Ground Effect for Accurate Edge Detection
authors: Chenyu Zhao, Jingao Xu, Ciyu Ruan, Haoyang Wang, Shengbo Wang, Jiaqi Li, Jirong Zha, Weijie Hong, Zheng Yang, Yunhao Liu, Xiao-Ping Zhang, Xinlei Chen
abstract: Drone-based rapid and accurate environmental edge detection is highly advantageous for tasks such as disaster relief and autonomous navigation. Current methods, using radars or cameras, raise deployment costs and burden lightweight drones with high computational demands. In this paper, we propose AirTouch, a system that transforms the ground effect from a stability “foe” in traditional flight control views, into a “friend” for accurate and efficient edge detection. Our key insight is that analyzing drone basic attitude sensor readings and flight commands allows us to detect ground effect changes. Such changes typically indicate the drone flying over a boundary of two materials, making this information valuable for edge detection. We approach this insight through theoretical analysis, algorithm design, and implementation, fully leveraging the ground effect as a new sensing modality without compromising drone flight stability, thereby achieving accurate and efficient scene edge detection. We also compare this new sensing modality with vision-based methods to clarify its exclusive advantages in resource efficiency and detection capability. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that our system achieves a high detection accuracy with mean detection distance errors of 0.051m, outperforming the baseline method performance by 86%. With such detection performance, our system requires only 43 mW power consumption, contributing to this new sensing modality for low-cost and highly efficient edge detection.

title: Analysing Sentiments by Using Machine Learning and Deep Learning Approaches on Dogri Language
authors: Ritika Bandhral, Sandeep Dubey, Shubhnandan Singh Jamwal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: In the digital era, there is very less work on sentiment analysis specially on low resourced Indian languages. Dogri is one such recognized language which still remains less explored by the researchers. It is a morphologically rich language and is spoken by people across J&K and Himachal Pradesh and in some parts of Punjab. Deep learning and neural networks are generally used for NLP applications like detecting emotions, hate speech, text summarization and prediction etc. In this paper, we have used some common machine learning approaches like SVM, Logistic Regression, Random Forest and Naïve Bayes for analysing sentiments. Deep learning approaches which are used in the current research are CNN, RNN and LSTM for analysing the sentiments in Dogri text. After the experimental observation on 1500 sentences, it is observed that the machine learning approaches and deep learning approaches are giving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 2 \%}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 2 \%}$</tex> accuracy respectively.

title: 28 GHz Indoor Continuous-Space Channel Measurements and AI-Enabled 6G Channel Map Construction
authors: Tianrun Qi, Cheng-Xiang Wang, Chen Huang, Junling Li, Xiping Wu, John Thompson
abstract: Conventional wireless channel measurements and modeling typically study channels via discrete spatial sampling. As the sixth-generation (6G) wireless communication places higher demands on the accuracy of channel state information, this discrete approximation becomes insufficient and motivates research on continuous-space channels. In this work, 28 GHz indoor continuous-space channel measurements are conducted, and key channel characteristics are analyzed. Based on the analysis, the necessity of continuous-space channel research is validated, and the role of channel maps is demonstrated. Furthermore, a continuous-space channel map construction method using the Graph SAmple and aggreGatE (GraphSAGE) algorithm is proposed. By the learning on spatial aggregation function rather than performing a passive weighted sum, the proposed GraphSAGE-based map construction method can reduce the performance bias caused by discrete spatial sampling and recover the continuous-space channels accurately. Continuous-space measurements are used as benchmarks to compare the performance of the GraphSAGE-based channel map. Extensive experiments confirm the superiority of the proposed GraphSAGE-based method over existing artificial intelligence (AI) algorithms, providing a robust approach for the 6G continuous-space channel map construction.

title: Machine Learning Assisted Tilt/Azimuth Estimation for Passive Stylus Pens using Position-Based Model Selection
authors: Junseo Jo, Hyungcheol Shin, Youngjoo Lee
abstract: This paper presents an advanced machine learning (ML) model to calculate the tilt and azimuth angles of passive stylus pens on display panels. Based on real-world sensing data, preprocessing steps were developed to automatically capture data windows centered on the peak-value positions, and a custom convolutional neural network (CNN) was applied to estimate the tilt/azimuth information in detail. Considering the missing data samples in edge areas, novel training and inference procedures are newly introduced by adopting the position-based model selection, successfully increasing the estimation quality by assigning the dedicated CNN model per input data window. Compared to the conventional approaches, experimental results reveal that our optimization methods lead to an attractive ML-based tilt/azimuth calculation by significantly reducing the estimation errors when the input position belongs to edge regions.

title: Set-Based Asynchronous State Estimation for Networked Switched Neural Networks
authors: Weizhong Chen, Haoxu Wang, Xiaohua Wang, Ying Zhao, Xudong Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: This study addresses the zonotopic interval estimation for a class of discrete-time switched neural networks (NNs) under an event-triggered mechanism. Unlike the existing studies, it adopts zonotopic set-membership estimation and considers general asynchronism, thereby enhancing the accuracy and applicability of the estimation results. First, by constructing appropriate observer-mode-dependent Lyapunov functions, certain sufficient conditions are established to guarantee the stability and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${\ell }_{\infty }$</tex-math> </inline-formula> performance of the augmented error system. Based on these conditions, we present a co-design approach for the switching signals and event-triggered nonlinear observer. Furthermore, a time-varying state zonotope is established, and the corresponding estimated bounds are derived. Finally, the efficacy of the proposed interval estimation approach is validated through two illustrative examples.

title: A Complementary Positive Feedback-Assisted Current Mirror-Based Level Shifter for Energy-Efficient Level Conversion for Wide Voltage Ranges
authors: Shin Han, Gicheol Shin, Jongmin Lee, Yoonmyung Lee
abstract: A complementary positive feedback-assisted current mirror-based level shifter (CPFLS) is proposed to reliably convert subthreshold signals to higher voltages. By adopting a positive feedback structure, the CPFLS mitigates the delay degradation and short-circuit current issues inherent in a prior art, WCMLS, due to its negative feedback design. Additionally, the CPFLS addresses the instability caused by the dynamic node in the CMLS-LED by incorporating a current-limiting circuit. Moreover, the CPFLS effectively prevents the delay degradation observed in diode-MOSFET insertion-type LSs with split-controlled inverter, when the voltage difference between the input and output is small, leveraging its positive feedback structure to maintain fast and stable operation. The measurement results from a test chip fabricated in 28-nm CMOS technology demonstrate that the CPFLS successfully converts a 0.28-V input signal to a 1.0-V nominal voltage with stable operation, achieving an 89% improvement in the measured delay compared to WCMLS. Furthermore, the CPFLS achieves up to 53%/75% faster measured delay and 63%/89% better energy-delay product (EDP) than the split-level converter (SLC) at V<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\mathrm {DDH}} = 1.0$</tex-math> </inline-formula>/0.7 V, respectively.

title: GPRS-Net: Global Statistical Prior Guided Relation-Aware Symmetric Point Cloud Completion
authors: Zelin Li, Shuanfeng Zhao, Zhengheng Li
abstract: Point cloud completion aims to recover geometrically complete and topologically consistent 3D shapes from partial observations. However, existing methods still struggle with thin structures, sharp features, and strongly anisotropic regions, where local discontinuities, point scattering, and blurred boundaries are likely to occur, especially under weak symmetry and complex topology. This issue largely stems from the lack of reliable global shape constraints during symmetry guided coarse skeleton generation, while isotropic neighborhood aggregation tends to introduce cross part interference and feature confusion in anisotropic areas. To address this problem, we propose a Global Statistical Prior guided Relation Aware Symmetric Network (GPRS Net) for point cloud completion. The proposed method first introduces a directional projection statistics module to extract global statistical priors from the depth distributions of multidirectional projections of the input point cloud. These priors are fused with keypoint features to guide per keypoint transformation and translation regression, producing a structurally consistent symmetric coarse skeleton. A relation aware edge aggregation encoder is then designed to jointly model scale differences and directional deviations on KNN graphs, enabling adaptive edge feature recalibration and aggregation for stronger local geometric representation. Finally, a two stage progressive refinement backbone is adopted to progressively recover fine details while preserving overall structural consistency. Experiments on PCN and MVP demonstrate consistent improvements, as well as better structural stability and detail continuity in challenging cases.

title: GNNLicense: An Active Intellectual Property Protection Technique for Graph Neural Networks based on Autoencoder
authors: Mingfu Xue, Longlong Hu, Linji Zhang, Yang Shi, Liquan Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) are widely utilized in areas such as social network analysis, recommendation systems, and fraud detection. Protecting the intellectual property (IP) of GNN models is crucial to avoid potential economic losses. However, existing researches on GNN IP protection rely on watermarking techniques for passive copyright verification. In this paper, we propose GNNLicense, the first active GNN IP protection and user’s identity management method against GNN piracy. The proposed method employs encoder-decoder models to encode and reconstruct graph features. Since the graph encoded by the encoder can only be decoded by the corresponding decoder, this design achieves active IP protection. In addition, the proposed method leverages fingerprint to manage and distinguish between different authorized users’ identities. The encoder is held by the user, while the decoder is retained by the service provider. The user encodes the input graph data through the encoder, and then submits it to the server for normal predictions. This mechanism makes unauthorized users can only obtain very poor model performance while ensures the normal use of authorized users. Experimental results on five graph datasets and five popular GNN models across node classification and graph classification tasks demonstrate the method’s effectiveness. Moreover, the method maintains its robustness when subjected to fine-tuning and adaptive attacks.

title: DCAChain: A Decentralized Cross-Domain Access Control Architecture with Key Abuse Resistance for the Metaverse
authors: Chien-Ming Chen, Bohao Xiang, Saru Kumari
abstract: The rapid convergence of the Internet of Things and the metaverse has intensified cross-domain data exchange and digital asset sharing, raising new challenges in access control, key abuse prevention, and interoperability across heterogeneous domains. Existing blockchain-based solutions still rely on centralized authorities or isolated single-chain architectures, which limit scalability and flexible cross-domain coordination. To address these issues, this paper proposes DCAChain, a decentralized cross-domain access-control architecture for the metaverse. DCAChain integrates a relay chain–parachain model with hashed timelock contracts to enable verifiable cross-domain coordination, and builds upon accountable CP-ABE/NIZK-based authorization to resist key abuse without trusted intermediaries. The framework further supports dynamic attribute lifecycle management, including expiration, revocation, and key update, and introduces smart-contract-based policy reuse and attribute-set deduplication to reduce repeated encryption redundancy in asynchronous multi-user scenarios. Experimental results on Ethereum and Hyperledger Sawtooth show that DCAChain achieves lower operational cost, reduced latency, and improved scalability compared with representative frameworks. These results indicate that DCAChain is a practical solution for privacy-preserving cross-domain access control in metaverse environments.

title: Ensemble of Convolutional Neural Networks with Transfer Learning for Remote Sensing Image Scene Classification
authors: Richa Kesharwani, Dhirendra Pandey, Pawan Kumar Chaurasia
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Remote sensing Scene classification is a challenging task due to the complex nature of high-resolution data scene data sets. Due to advancement in sensor technology, it has become very easy to collect huge amount of scene data related to the earth surfaces. One major problem in the area of remote sensing is the availability of labeled data. Deep learning techniques are state of art for the scene classification but these methods require huge amount of labeled data. In this work, an ensemble of deep learning method has been proposed which utilize transfer learning to reduce the requirement of labeled data from scene domain. The proposed method achieved 86.40% accuracy which is 2% greater than traditional method. The experimental results show that proposed framework have capability to improve classification accuracy in comparison to standalone deep learning models.

title: Quantum-Inspired Machine Learning for High-Dimensional Data Optimization
authors: Arpana Chaturvedi, Siddharth Shankar Mishra, S. Nagaraju, Amit Verma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: High dimensional data in machine learning (ML) however is not yet fully tapped due curse of dimensionality, sensitivity to local minima and computational burden. In this paper, we will introduce and test a new QIGS algorithm for such problems on classical computer. The efficiency of QIGS is demonstrated to be benefiting from the amplitude–phase hybrid probabilistic encoding and gradient-assisted update for optimizing entangling operations, which can effectively balance global exploration and local exploitation. We test this experiment on synthetic benchmark functions (Rastrigin, Rosenbrock, Ackley up to 5,000 dimensions) and real-world data sets (genomic profiles with 20,531 features and BERT-based sentiment embeddings with 768 features). It is also shown that QIGS achieves a classification accuracy of 94.2% on the genomic data (Adam: 91.5%, SGD: 90.7%, PSO: 89.8%) and reduces the number of iterations for convergence by 35–40% compared to fastest baseline on synthetic benchmarks. In addition, on the BERT representations QIGS got a training loss of 0.083 in 120 iterations (vs Adam's loss of 0.091 in 180 iterations). These results establish that QIGS is not only a faster converging algorithm, but also one which produces solutions of higher quality for many problems, thereby making it an accessible segue between classical optimization and the future of quantum-inspired machine learning.

title: Design and Implementation of an AI-Powered Autonomous Security Robotic Dog with Integrated Face Recognition
authors: Mohammed Mujeerulla, Shakkeera L, Zafar Ali Khan N, Hemanth G, Aditya Sharma, Dashami S, Harsha Vardhana T
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Traditional security systems mostly use fixed surveillance cameras, which often result in persistent blind spots and provide little ability to respond in real time to security threats. To address these limitations, we present the design and implementation of an autonomous robotic security dog, which utilizes recent progress in mobile robotics and artificial intelligence. The robot was built on a specially designed, 3D-printed four-legged frame powered by 12 servo motors for flexible movement, all managed by a Raspberry Pi 5 computing unit. Its intelligence centers on a real-time face recognition system, for which we developed and compared two approaches: a lightweight option using Haarcascades with a Python library and a more advanced method based on the Insight Face deep learning framework paired with Facebook’s AI similarity search tools. In our analysis, the Haarcascade system achieved an average recognition rate of 92.4 % when running on a Raspberry Pi 5, whereas the Insight Face solution reached 95% accuracy on a high-end CPU workstation. This study confirms the potential of a modular robotic platform that strikes a practical balance between computational efficiency and recognition performance in active security environments, while also providing a solid foundation for future upgrades, such as autonomous navigation, more sensors, and responsive real-time behaviours.

title: DynaSplit: Latency-Aware Dynamic Model Partitioning for Large Language Model Inference in the Edge-Cloud Continuum
authors: Tejas Pravinbhai Patel, Sandeep Shivam, Amit Kumar Padhy, Bharadwaj Vulugunda, Chaitanya Kulkarni, Chandrashekhar Medicherla
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) have unlocked new possibilities for interactive intelligence on mobile and edge platforms. However, achieving sub- 300 ms end-to-end latency in real-world mobile environments remains an unsolved systems challenge. Pure cloud inference suffers from unpredictable round-trip delays, while fully local execution on commodity edge hardware is infeasible for multi-billion-parameter models. Existing edge-cloud LLM partitioning systems rely on static policies that ignore dynamic bandwidth fluctuations, interlayer activation variance, and the non-linear interactions of quantization and tensor parallelism. As a result, their latency degrades by up to 47<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>1Static partitioning: 710ms p95 vs. DynaSplit: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$290 \text{ms}=(710-290) / 290 \approx 145 \%$</tex> latency increase or <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1/1.45 \approx 47 \%$</tex> performance degradation. under typical 4G/5G volatility. We present DynaSplit, a latency-aware runtime system that dynamically selects (i) transformer-layer partition points, (ii) per-layer quantization levels, and (iii) cloud-side tensor-parallel degrees at fine-grained 100 ms intervals. DynaSplit incorporates a sub-millisecond performance model, a hybrid greedy-beam search optimizer with a search space reduction of >99.8%, and an asynchronous execution engine that overlaps GPU compute and RDMA transfers with up to 91 % pipeline utilization. Evaluated on an RTX A4000 edge node paired with a cloud A100 GPU and real 4G/5G-Fi traces, DynaSplit achieves 2.1-3.8 reduction in tail latency over DeepSpeed-Inference, PowerInfer, Orca, and vLLM for LLaMA-3-8B, Mixtral-8×7B, and Qwen-14B. To our knowledge, DynaSplit is the first system to consistently meet <300ms p95 latency for 8-14 B models on commodity edge hardware.

title: Predictive People Analytics: Real-Time AI Insights for Engagement and Attrition using Workday Data
authors: Suresh Kurapati, Vinod Veeramachaneni, Mugunth Chandran
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: For companies that wish to maintain a high level of productivity and a stable level of employees, the topics of employee engagement and attrition remain of significant importance. The conventional HR analytics techniques can be said to be reactive in nature, as they rely on lagging indicators as well as batch-based reporting. The current research has proposed a real-time prediction-based people analytics platform that integrates Workday-based information with multimodal AI models that rely on time series signals, organizational graphs, as well as static tabular-based HR data. The methodology has utilized discrete time survival modeling for the prediction of attrition risk, as well as multi-task learning for improving engagement as well as productivity, along with fairness-based intervention optimization. Experimental assessment across several baselines indicates enhanced predictive capability. The suggested model has AUC = 0.88 (4w) and 0.86 (12w) for attrition, which is better than Logistic Regression (0.72) and XGBoost (0.80). With a 24% drop in RMSE and a R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.72, engagement and productivity prediction was better, showing that behavioural modelling was stronger. The calibration study showed that the Expected Calibration Error was smaller (0.032) and the uncertainty coverage was higher (96.1%). The findings of the intervention uplift showed that changing pay (7.2% uplift) and management check-ins (net benefit score = 0.71) were both cost-effective ways to keep employees. Bias audits showed that differences between men and women, people from different places, and those at different career levels were smaller. These results show that the framework can give quick, accurate, and fair information, which lets firms improve engagement and lower the risk of losing employees.

title: A Multilingual AI-Based Conversational Tutor for Language Practice and Grammar Assessment
authors: Sandhiya R, Manasha K, Narmadha G, Genga Lakshmi K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Language learners often face difficulty in achieving fluency due to limited opportunities for real-time conversational practice, inadequate grammar awareness, and lack of pronunciation feedback. Traditional methods largely focus on passive lessons, offering minimal scope for natural interaction or personalized correction, which affects confidence and progress in real-world communication. To address these limitations, an AI-powered language learning platform is proposed and implemented that enables real-time interaction through text and voice across multiple languages. The system integrates speech-to-text and text-to-speech for voice communication and employs AI models to provide instant, context-aware feedback on grammar, sentence structure, and pronunciation. Learners can set proficiency levels, engage in adaptive dialogues on diverse topics, and receive personalized corrections tailored to their performance. Implemented with React.js, Node.js, and Firebase for scalable integration, the platform offers an immersive and effective approach to enhancing language acquisition through interactive practice.

title: Sensing the Neural, Muscular, and Biomechanical Biomarkers to Evaluate Age-Related Neuromuscular Health
authors: Sankhalp NS, A Celestin Jerald
abstract: Age-related neuromuscular changes significantly influence the onset and progression of neuromuscular disorders. This emphasizes the importance of understanding normal age-related neuromuscular variations for improved prevention and intervention strategies. This study aims to analyze neuromuscular dynamics across the aging spectrum using multimodal approaches, including electromyography (EMG), electroencephalography (EEG), and force plate studies. The results obtained in this study portray significant differences in these biomarkers with age. The integration of data from EMG, EEG, and force plate analyses offers a comprehensive framework to identify normal aging, which can be used to identify predictive biomarkers and explore their applications in assessing neuromuscular health. This research holds promise for enhancing diagnostics, advancing targeted therapies, and contributing to the understanding of neuromuscular function in aging populations.

title: Hybrid Quantum-Classical Computing for Privacy-Preserving Machine Learning on Edge Devices
authors: B. Dinesh, M. Thangamani, B. Paulchamy, Lavanya K, D. Anandakumar, Rajasekhara Babu L
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The edge computing environment faces a challenge in maintaining data privacy while performing machine learning tasks. The current solutions for privacy preservation are inefficient or do not provide security guarantees, causing a critical gap in the edge computing environment, which is resource-constrained. The aim of this research work is to design a hybrid quantum-classical computing system that addresses the challenges of privacy preservation and efficiency in machine learning tasks. We design a novel architecture combining variational quantum circuits for secure data encoding with classical neural networks for scalable inference, implemented through federated learning protocols enhanced with quantum differential privacy mechanisms. Experimental evaluation demonstrates a 35% reduction in communication overhead compared to traditional federated learning approaches while maintaining 99.2% classification accuracy across diverse datasets. The quantum-enhanced privacy mechanisms achieve provable security guarantees with information-theoretic bounds, outperforming conventional cryptographic methods by 28% in computational overhead reduction. This hybrid approach establishes a new paradigm for secure and efficient machine learning deployment on edge devices, enabling privacy-preserving artificial intelligence in healthcare, finance, and IoT applications.

title: Zero-Shot Video Deraining with Video Diffusion Models
authors: Tuomas Varanka, Juan Luis Gonzalez, Hyeongwoo Kim, Pablo Garrido, Xu Yao
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Existing video deraining methods are often trained on paired datasets, either synthetic, which limits their ability to generalize to real-world rain, or captured by static cameras, which restricts their effectiveness in dynamic scenes with background and camera motion. Furthermore, recent works in fine-tuning diffusion models have shown promising results, but the fine-tuning tends to weaken the generative prior, limiting generalization to unseen cases. In this paper, we introduce the first zero-shot video deraining method for complex dynamic scenes that does not require synthetic data nor model fine-tuning, by leveraging a pretrained text-to-video diffusion model that demonstrates strong generalization capabilities. By inverting an input video into the latent space of diffusion models, its reconstruction process can be intervened and pushed away from the model’s concept of rain using negative prompting. At the core of our approach is an attention switching mechanism that we found is crucial for maintaining dynamic backgrounds as well as structural consistency between the input and the derained video, mitigating artifacts introduced by naive negative prompting. Our approach is validated through extensive experiments on real-world rain datasets, demonstrating substantial improvements over prior methods and showcasing robust generalization without the need for supervised training.

title: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning with Decentralized AI in Autonomous Drone Swarms
authors: Vibhor Pal, Gaurav Sarraf, Manisha Bhende, Suvarna Patil
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The collaborative aspect of drone swarms endangers smooth functioning of services and security of national facilities. Multi-Agent Deep Learning (DL) Coordinating swarms of drones in dynamic systems are not simple tasks, but learning is proving to be a legitimate solution. The paper introduces a new end-to-end UAV swarm intelligence system that combines DL and multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) to achieve autonomous and coordinated actions of drones. The system uses a new UAV swarm intelligence system that is based on YOLOv8 detection, DeepSORT-like tracking, and multi-agent PPO reinforcement. YOLOv8n model has the following performance: 0.91 precision, 0.83 F1-score, and 6.6 ms processing time per frame. The tracker is running at 151.5 FPS, ensuring the identities of the UAVs are the same throughout the movie. A specialized DroneSwarmEnv trains drones in formation control as well as in collision avoidance and cooperative navigation thus achieving an average reward of 1,886 with minimal collisions. In order to encourage generalization, real and synthetic UAVSwarm datasets are employed, consequently, training diversity and adaptability are multiplied. An in-depth analysis and visualization have indicated that the detection, tracking, and swarm behavior performance is outstanding and proves the policy convergence and policy stability. The system is of low weight, scalable, and is applicable for real-time deployment, thus providing a huge potential for uses such as autonomous surveillance, disaster monitoring, and aerial mission planning.

title: SentrySLA: SLA Breach Predictor for Incident Management Using Machine Learning
authors: Divya Gudibandla, Srithu Gaddolla, Jyothi Borra, Kadari Nandhini
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: In the realm of IT service management, adherence to service level agreements (SLAs) is essential for resolving incidents. However, the challenge is that you can never truly know when your call SLA may be breached. This project will develop a smart SLA breach prediction tool that predicts the likelihood of SLA breaches for ticket incidents using machine learning methods. Each time a ticket is submitted, the predictive tool will characterize the ticket incident using historical incident data while also including information about the ticket incident itself and resolution experience to predict whether a ticket incident is likely to breach SLA timelines. This project will include the processes of data cleaning, feature creation and classification processes. NLP techniques in motivation assessment of ticket descriptions help in improving your prediction accuracy. The solution involves easy-to- view dashboards and visualization that show SLA risk trends along with the likelihood of breaches, thereby helping IT teams take action in time. This predictive capability enhances functionality for service teams through notifications on which particular ticket might breach its SLA. Additionally, it would be able to provide helpful motivations, such as how risky that ticket may be, so the team could act swiftly-for example, routing the ticket to an expert or escalating it. It will shift teams from being reactive to a problem to being able to predict and solve problems before they become close to being big issues. Smoothening of operations and improvement in end-user experience will thus be the result.

title: PALMS+: Modular Image-Based Floor Plan Localization Leveraging Depth Foundation Model
authors: Yunqian Cheng, Benjamin Princen, Roberto Manduchi
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Indoor localization in GPS-denied environments is crucial for applications like emergency response and assistive navigation. Vision-based methods such as PALMS enable infrastructure-free localization using only a floor plan and a stationary scan, but are limited by the short range of smartphone LiDAR and ambiguity in indoor layouts. We propose PALMS+, a modular, image-based system that addresses these challenges by reconstructing scale-aligned 3D point clouds from posed RGB images using a foundation monocular depth estimation model (Depth Pro), followed by geometric layout matching via convolution with the floor plan. PALMS+ outputs a posterior over the location and orientation, usable for direct or sequential localization. Evaluated on the Structured3D and a custom campus dataset consisting of 80 observations across four large campus buildings, PALMS+ outperforms PALMS and F<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>Loc in stationary localization accuracy—without requiring any training. Furthermore, when integrated with a particle filter for sequential localization on 33 real-world trajectories, PALMS+ achieved lower localization errors compared to other methods, demonstrating robustness for camera-free tracking and its potential for infrastructure-free applications. Code and data are available at https://github.com/Head-inthe-Cloud/PALMS-Plane-based-Accessible-Indoor-Localization-Using-Mobile-Smartphones.

title: Text Recognition Using Neural Radiance Fields
authors: Madhav Sharma, Hukam Chand Saini, Samiksha Agarwal, Akash Saxena, Pushpendra kumar Sikarwal, Shalini chaudhary
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: Text reputation in complex scenes is still a difficult problem due to various lighting conditions, occlusions, and mental distortions. In three-dimensional environments, text appears on curved or non-planar surfaces, rendering conventional OCR methods useless. Neural Radiance Fields (NeRFs), originally designed for three-D scene reconstruction, offer a new method for textual content reputation by allowing immoderate-constancy volumetric rendering. Using deep analysis models to extract text records and reconstruct three-dimensional textual content systems from two-dimensional photographs, this paper explores the possible adaptation of NeRFs for text popularity. We evaluate NeRF-based full text recognition on both synthetic and real-world datasets, demonstrating that it performs better than conventional techniques when dealing with intricate text distortions and occlusions.

title: LOD-Net: Locality-Aware 3D Object Detection Using Multi-Scale Transformer Network
authors: Mustaqeem Khan, Aidana Nurakhmetova, Wail Gueaieb, Abdulmotaleb Elsaddik
publish: 2025 Conference on AI, Science, Engineering, and Technology (AIxSET)
abstract: 3D object detection in point cloud data remains a challenging task due to the sparsity and lack of global structure inherent in the input. In this work, we propose a novel MultiScale Attention (MSA) mechanism integrated into the 3DETR architecture to better capture both local geometry and global context. Our method introduces an upsampling operation that generates high-resolution feature maps, enabling the network to better detect smaller and semantically related objects. Experiments conducted on the ScanNetv2 dataset demonstrate that our 3DETR + MSA model improves detection performance, achieving a gain of almost 1% in mAP@25 and 4.78% in mAP@50 over the baseline. While applying MSA to the 3DETR-m variant shows limited improvement, our analysis reveals the importance of adapting the upsampling strategy for lightweight models. These results highlight the effectiveness of combining hierarchical feature extraction with attention mechanisms in enhancing 3D scene understanding.

title: Data-Driven Electromagnetic Wave Propagation Modeling for Urban and Indoor Wireless Channels
authors: Movva Naga Swapna Sri, Bhiksha Gugulothu, A.P. Pushpalatha, Amaleswari Rajulapati, Anusha Peyyala, Vijayakumar Sivasundar
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: This paper examines how EM wave propagates through urban area and also examines the communication happens indoor. Existing methodologies have been based on the physics of the subject area, however, the application of those same methodologies to actualized versions of real world environments have proven to be time-consuming in a computational sense. Therefore, synthetic data from simulated environments was used to create a model of a neural network model utilizing an attention mechanism to find behavior in wireless communications by identifying propagation characteristics. The neural network model generated predictions concerning the amount of signal attenuation experienced by the signals and the variability in the signal strengths experienced within an environment, and was able to do so using only modeled data and without the need for explicit ray tracing methodologies. Further, the neural network model’s outputs showed spatial continuity, consistency in their patterns, and similar levels of error throughout the environment. The model also was found to demonstrate good generalization capabilities for a number of different environments for which it had no prior knowledge. In comparison to reference baselines, this model displayed superior accuracy and improved flexibility to accommodate a variety of environments. Ablation test results suggest that the performance of models can be significantly influenced by both how they are designed (i.e., their use of attention mechanisms) and how the inputs are processed (i.e., feature selection). The output from this model appears to exhibit many of the properties that one would expect of an output generated as part of simulating a realistic environment even though the input data was entirely artificially created. In general, this research provides evidence for the ability of employing data driven methods to guide propagation of modeling toward informing current wireless system planning and future network design.

title: A Decentralized Crypto Launchpad on Binance Smart Chain
authors: Bidyutmala Saha, Priyanka Chakraborty, Sayan Nath, Debtanu Roy, Arna Saha, Anwesa Ghosh, Dewan Habib, Akash Dey
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: This project is a blockchain-based decentralised application that allows cryptocurrency owners to launch new currencies while investors may make first investments in them. The charge of crypto in the foreign websites is high due to currency differences. This is a Make in India initiative and also supports the Digital India campaign by making Indian people more accustomed to the digital market. It emphasizes security, transparency, and community participation to build a trustworthy and sustainable launch pad ecosystem. A block chain consists of a chain of blocks, where each block contains an immutable record of all the transactions or data. The application can easily access transactions by smart contracts. In this paper, our pinpoint is to build a decentralized crypto launch pad based on the Binance Smart Chain (BSC) block chain technology, which is known for its speed, security and low fees. A decentralized wallet add-on has been integrated with the website, so to buy new coins from us, customers need to have a MetaMusk or trust wallet, which they need to connect with our website.

title: Development of Machine Learning Model for Automated Fruit Classification & Ripeness Detection
authors: Anshika Jindal, Monika Nayak, Rekha Manasa, Medha Shukla, K. A. Chinmaya
abstract: This paper delineates the development of two machine learning models: the first for the classification of fruits into distinct categories using the comprehensive Fruits 360 dataset, and the second for the determination of ripeness levels within a specific category, exemplified by bananas. The Fruits 360 dataset, encompassing over 90,000 images of 131 fruit and vegetable types, provides a robust foundation for the initial classification model. In contrast, the banana ripeness dataset, with its focus on various stages of banana maturity, enables the second model to discern between unripe, ripe, and overripe states with remarkable accuracy. The architecture of the models is based on convolutional neural networks (CNNs). It is meticulously trained and validated to achieve high precision in both fruit categorization and ripeness detection. The results of this study not only demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed models but also highlight the transformative potential of machine learning in automating and enhancing agricultural processes.

title: The Impact of Artificial Intelligence on Financial Decision-Making: An Empirical Study in Commercial Bank Operations
authors: Jiawei Yi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is becoming a part of any commercial banking operation and transforming decision-making and organisational performance. This study set out to investigate the effect of central AI-related variables, system accuracy, perceived usefulness, trust, operational integration, and employee competence, on the quality of financial decisions made by employees working in the banking sector. Quantitative research design has been used since a structured survey with a 5-item Likert scale was given to 300 respondents, who were subsequently analysed in terms of reliability, correlation analysis and multiple linear regression. The findings indicate close positive relationships among all AI-related dimensions and the quality of decision (r is between 0.834 and 0.898), and the regression model has a high explanatory power (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.884) with employee AI competence (b = 0.292) and system accuracy (b = 0.287) appearing as the most significant predictors. The research findings conclude that the technological performance and human capability should meet to achieve high-quality financial decision results in an AI-enabled banking setting. One of the most important suggestions is that banks are advised to focus on employee AI training and optimisation of the systems. The practical implication is that improving human-AI synergy can lead to a significant improvement in the performance of organisational decisions. The fact that the self-reported data is cross-sectional and in itself can lack long-term effects on behaviour and actual performance measures is also a great weakness.

title: OpenCV-Based Real-Time Multi-Modal Threat Detection and Alert System for Public Safety Applications
authors: S. P. Chokkalingam, Teja Thatiparthi, LaxmiNarayana Kaya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The introduction of efficient systems which can respond quickly and accurately will result in the safety of the people in crowded or dangerous places. A real-time Threat Detection and Alert System is presented in this paper, which is implemented using OpenCV and utilizes computer vision and deep learning to recognize hazards such as guns, fights, or flames. The intention is to create a system that can ’see’, ’hear’ and ’move’ just like a human being to understand its environment. Live video will be taken using OpenCV, and then every frame will be processed to focus the attention on the important parts of the scene. While the YOLOv8 deep learning model identifies weapons, the 3D Convolutional Neural Networks (3D-CNNs) monitor human’s gestures to classify actions as Fighting or Aggressive. An audio-based CNN will similarly be listening for shocking sounds—screams or gunshots—for instance—to corroborate the camera’s detection. The combination of all these inputs is done through a weighted Decision model which gives a single score indicating the likelihood of a threat’s presence. The system will immediately trigger a loud alarm sound and display a red warning on the screen if this score goes beyond a predetermined limit, thus instantly notifying the closest people to either evacuate or stay safely. The tests carried out in real-life situations showed that the system is very effective, with high accuracy and almost no delay. It is rapid, reliable, and easy to implement, thus making it ideal for locations such as public surveillance systems, train stations, schools, or even smart city infrastructure. By combining visual, movement, and sound analysis, this system offers an intelligent and responsive Approach to public safety beyond previous capabilities.

title: InteracTalker: Prompt-Based Human-Object Interaction with Co-Speech Gesture Generation
authors: Sreehari Rajan, Kunal Bhosikar, Charu Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Generating realistic human motions that naturally respond to both spoken language and physical objects is crucial for interactive digital experiences. Current methods, however, address speech-driven gestures or object interactions independently, limiting real-world applicability due to a lack of integrated, comprehensive datasets. To overcome this, we introduce InteracTalker, a novel framework that seamlessly integrates prompt-based object-aware interactions with co-speech gesture generation. We achieve this by employing a multi-stage training process to learn a unified motion, speech, and prompt embedding space. To support this, we curate a rich human-object interaction dataset, formed by augmenting an existing text-to-motion dataset with detailed object interaction annotations. Our framework utilizes a Generalized Motion Adaptation Module that enables independent training, adapting to the corresponding motion condition, which is then dynamically combined during inference. To address the imbalance between heterogeneous conditioning signals, we propose an adaptive fusion strategy, which dynamically reweights the conditioning signals during diffusion sampling. InteracTalker successfully unifies these previously separate tasks, outperforming prior methods in both co-speech gesture generation and object-interaction synthesis, outperforming gesture-focused diffusion methods, yielding highly realistic, object-aware full-body motions with enhanced realism, flexibility, and control. (https://sreeharirajan.github.io/projects/InteracTalker/)

title: Embedding-Enhanced Deep Neural Network for Hypertension Risk Prediction Using Mixed-Type Clinical Data
authors: Jeena Joseph
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Hypertension is primarily responsible for cardiovascular diseases and death all over the world. The early detection of such individuals is a must for the medical treatment and preventive care. A new hybrid deep learning model for predicting hypertension using structured clinical and behavioral data has been developed and elucidated in this article. Data from Kaggle has been utilized in this research, which consists of 1,985 records that are not only generated but also clinically reasonable to include features like age, body mass index (BMI), salt intake, sleep duration, stress score, blood pressure history, and lifestyle factors like smoking status and physical activity. The architecture of the proposed model is dual-input, which combines embedding layers for categorical variables with batchnormalized fully connected layers for numerical features. The model thus is capable to efficiently identify complex, non-linear interactions that involve such a variety of risks that are categorized as being diverse. Data were preprocessed through label encoding and standard scaling prior to the training conducted in 30 epochs using binary cross-entropy loss and Adam optimization. The model obtained a classification accuracy of 90.1%, ROC AUC of 0.967, and balanced precision, recall, and F1-scores in both classes during evaluation. The results indicate that the model can be considered a very powerful and helpful decision support tool for hypertension risk screening. Furthermore, it highlights the possibility of applying deep learning techniques on health data of mixed types for predictive healthcare purposes.

title: Roadkill Hotspots and Policy Advocacy (RHPA): A Multi-Layered AI and IoT Approach for Real-Time Wildlife Preservation
authors: Lokesh Patra, Indigibilli Harshit, Saumendra Kumar Mohapatra, Apurva Singh, Mihir Narayan Mohanty
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Roadkill episodes are a crucial point where vehicle safety risks and ecological damage converge. The frequency of wildlife-vehicle incidents has increased due to rising urbanization, posing a threat to commuters and causing a major loss of biodiversity. In this study, a novel three-dimensional (3D) approach called “Roadkill Hotspots and Policy Advocacy” (RHPA) is proposed. In contrast to conventional radar-based systems, RHPA creates a preventive and responsive ecosystem by combining Computer Vision (YOLOv5xu), Deep Learning (DenseNet121 and EfficientNet-B3), and the Internet of Things (IoT). Both CNN architectures are trained individually to categorize subjects as “Alive” (High Risk) or “Dead” (Low Risk), achieving an overall accuracy of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\approx} {9 5 {\%}}$</tex>, and YOLOv5xu for real-time detection. Additionally, the system incorporates a policy-level dashboard to detect blackspots and an ESP32-based actuation layer for real-time driver warnings. Pilot testing indicates a potential 36% reduction in roadkill incidents.

title: Design and Simulation of Quantum-Enhanced Hybrid Machine Learning Models for Accelerated Big Data Classification
authors: Nida Afreen Rizvi, Shadab Pasha Khan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: The research article presents a Quantum-Enhanced Hybrid Machine Learning Model (QEHMLM) designed to accelerate data classification in Large-Scale Big Data Environments (LSBDE). The proposed framework integrates Quantum Computing (QC) with Classical Machine Learning Techniques (CMLT) including Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), and Neural Network (Artificial-ANN) to overcome the computational and scalability limitations of classical approaches when processing High-Dimensional Datasets (HDD). The model leverages quantum feature encoding, entanglement-based transformations, and classical optimization to enhance accuracy and convergence speed. Simulations conducted using IBM Qiskit and TensorFlow Quantum on the Mobile Telecom Network Traffic Dataset validate the industrial applicability of the suggested approach in Applied Operational environment. The QEHMLM achieves notable improvements, recording 97% accuracy, 95% F1-score, and nearly 20% reduction in computational time compared to classical counterparts. These outcomes confirm that Hybrid Quantum-Classical Systems (HQCS) provide a scalable, efficient, and high-performance paradigm for next-generation Big Data analytics and intelligent computational ecosystems.

title: Bootstrap-Ensembled Neural Networks for Robust Stellar Temperature and Age Predictions
authors: Marco Tarantino, Loredana Prisinzano, Nicoletta D'Angelo, Francesco Damiani, Giada Adelfio
abstract: This study presents a machine learning approach to predict the effective temperatures and their accuracy of premain sequence stars, which are essential for deriving stellar ages through isochrone fitting and starspot-dependent evolutionary models. We trained a Neural Network on high-quality spectroscopic temperatures from the Gaia-ESO Survey, using Gaia DR3 and 2MASS photometry as input features. To estimate predictive uncertainty, we implemented a Neural Network with bootstrap procedure, where each model was warm-started using the parameters of an initial network trained on the full dataset with K-fold cross-validation. This allowed accurate and robust temperature predictions for large stellar populations lacking spectroscopic data, with strong performance in the lowtemperature regime. The predicted temperatures were used to build Hertzsprung-Russell diagrams and derive stellar ages of young clusters by starspot evolutionary models, achieving good agreement with spectroscopic benchmarks and independent methods, such as lithium equivalent widths.

title: Game Optimization Scheduling Method for Regional Power Grid of 220kV Substation Based on Information Entropy
authors: Meng Ren, Junbao Zhang, Cunliang Wei, Defa Cao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: Multi-stakeholder game scheduling of power grid has high complexity, and the objectives of each stakeholder are very different. These different objectives make it difficult to find a solution that satisfies all parties completely in game scheduling. Therefore, this study takes 220 kV substation as the research object, and designs a game optimization dispatching method for power supply region. The power supply information mining is completed based on information entropy, and the power supply area data scheduling method is designed under the multi-mixed game, and the power supply area scheduling function is solved based on the Bat algorithm. The experimental results show that: Under the application of the proposed substation power supply regional network scheduling method, the power utilization rate is always above 95 %, and the power fluctuation is smaller, and the power generation cost in the power supply region can be significantly reduced, indicating that the research method can effectively optimize the interest game between the power grid multi-target entities.

title: Network Traffic Classification Based on Hadoop Using Gradient Boosting Algorithm
authors: Hmway Hmway Tar, Thiri Shwe Sin Htun
abstract: The proposed system presents a system for network traffic classification that combines Apache Hadoop for processing large data with the Gradient Boosting Classifier (GBC) algorithm. The proposed framework processes web server logs to categorize traffic into High, Medium, and Low priority flows, aiming to improve resource allocation and anomaly detection. The research highlights a gap in existing systems and handles with Gradient Boosting that gets high accuracy for complex pattern recognition in traffic flows. By leveraging the distributed processing power of Hadoop, the system transforms raw, messy network data into structured insights. The integration significantly improves decision-making, optimizes resource allocation, and helps identify critical trends and anomalies in network traffic, offering a robust advancement in big data and machine learning applications for network management.

title: Config-Driven Promptable Semantic Segmentation for Satellite Land Cover Mapping with PyTorch
authors: Dharshan S, Dhanush S, Dhanush Shankar, Dinesh Kumar G, B Uma Devi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Semantic segmentation is a core component of remote sensing, especially in land cover mapping, that can be used to monitor the environment and manage resources. The paper presents a prompt enabled and configuration based segmentation system based on convolutional neural network models, which are configured to classify pixels of satellite images into buildings, vegetation, roads, and bodies of water, among others. This integrated approach ensures consistent performance across varying spatial resolutions and diverse remote sensing conditions. An open AutoML like configuration file format allows the user to directly choose model architectures, optimizers, and learning parameters in the configuration file, thus increasing flexibility and enabling experimental tailoring. The immediate segmentation option also enhances flexibility since visualization of solely chosen land cover classes is available. The modular framework facilitates supervised, semi supervised, and prompt mediated pipelines, making it apt for unique class and multi class scenarios. The experimental outcomes on the dataset of the LandCover.ai show high performance with an accuracy score of ninety two to ninety seven percent. The setup time was reduced by about fifty percent, and timely customization enhanced specific class measures by as much as five percent. Overall, the system provides an effective, configurable, and repeatable land cover mapping system.

title: Object Tracking and Shooting Turret
authors: Kishore A T, Vishnu Murukan, Adarsh P, Akhil V M
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Automation is one of the most important factors in the modern defense and security systems, which in turn guarantee the rapid response and precision. This is a project of a Smart Target Detection and Shootable Turret System that aims to achieve the automatic detection, tracking, and shooting of a target via the use of computer vision and control technologies of an embedded nature. The system uses a YOLO (You Only Look Once) deep learning model integrated with OpenCV for real-time object detection and tracking. The live video is from a laptop camera that is processed to locate the targets and get their coordinates. The coordinates are then changed to angular positions and sent to an Arduino microcontroller.The Arduino drives two MG995 servo motors i.e. one for turret rotation and the other for firing mechanism stabilization thus the target can be fixed accurately by the detected target. When the target is locked, the system can be remotely activated to engage the firing module.The model is a fine example of how deep learning and the use of embedded hardware can be brought together to come up with a cheap, intelligent, and automated defense system of the future. The defense applications are only a stepping stone to the technology, which is being considered for use as an autonomous surveillance system in hard-to-reach areas or places that are dangerous for humans.

title: Electricity Generation from Waste Materials
authors: K Praveen, Jayasri Nerella, Vilasagaram Bhanuteja, Yogeshwar Mahadev, Karuppiah Natarjan, Karuppiah Natarjan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The pressure on utilizing renewable energy is ever increasing and new methods of using waste products to produce energy are becoming relevant. This paper has given a system that can be used to convert waste heat into useful electrical energy using very basic electronic parts like capacitors, resistors, heating panels, temperature sensors, rechargeable batteries and LED lights. The system starts with collection of waste heat in the form of heating panels. Thermal sensors are used to measure thermal energy to optimize the performance of the system. Capacitors and resistors are used in stabilizing the generated voltage and controlling the flow of current. The resulting electricity is stored in rechargeable batteries and applied to run LED bulbs. The system is cheap and environmentally friendly and can be used in the microgrids and the small-scale renewable energy installations. They are the system design, efficiency, and scalability.

title: Lightweight UNet++ Based Multi-Sensor Fusion for Automated Building Footprint Extraction
authors: Nagadi Leela Rao, Deepak Kumar, Shivani Verma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Building Footprints are two-dimensional boundaries that identify buildings on the ground-level surface of the Earth. These are useful for urban planning, infrastructure mapping, disaster management, etc. The extraction process can be auto-mated using deep learning methods, making it faster and more resistant to errors. Although optical images are widely used for this purpose, they are not as reliable in cloud-occluded, shadow-covered areas, and low-contrast regions. Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) data, on the other hand, are immune to these problems, but can show an underwhelming performance when used alone, due to inherent speckle noise. This paper aims to explore the automatic extraction of building footprints by fusing the two modalities using a light-weight, early fusion-based UNet++ model. The SpaceNet 6 Multi-Sensor All-Weather Mapping dataset (MSAW) has been used for this purpose.

title: A Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning Framework for Adaptable Cross-Organizational Collaboration
authors: A Padmavathi, Nimish Kushwaha, Sandesh Varma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The role of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in medical imaging has improved automated disease detection and segmentation to provide faster and more accurate diagnoses. However, developing high-performance deep learning models requires access to large datasets with diverse cases, which is challenging because of privacy rules, ethical considerations, and some organizations’ unwillingness to share patient data in healthcare contexts. Federated Learning (FL) has emerged as a promising paradigm that allows institutions to collaboratively train a shared model without transferring raw data. Although FL preserves privacy by keeping data local, existing FL architectures still face challenges such as non-IID data distribution across institutions, communication overhead, and reduced performance when clients intermittently participate or drop out. This work proposes a collaborative privacy-preserving federated learning framework for medical image segmentation across hospitals. The system has implemented secure aggregation, differential privacy, and communication-efficient optimization to increase robustness in heterogeneous client environments. Evaluating the proposed system on medical imaging datasets shows improved segmentation performance and generalization of the model. Furthermore, the model maintains privacy guaranties while reducing sensitivity to client dropout. The results confirm that privacy-preserving FL can support multi-institutional collaboration for medical imaging, improving diagnostic performance without compromising patient confidentiality, thus providing a scalable direction for real-world healthcare deployment.

title: Architecting Hybrid Data Products Using AI/ML and Agentic AI for Group Insurance and Retirement Solution Platforms with Advanced Data Governance
authors: Ramesh Inala, Goutham Kumar Sheelam, Avinash Reddy Aitha, Aragala Udaya Lakshmi, Kushvanth Chowdary Nagabhyru, Avinash Reddy Segireddy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Hybrid data products combine the content, applicative, and consumption components of data sharing in a singular consumable package that any data consumer can leverage with little or no effort. Architectures facilitating hybrid data products, like data mesh and data fabric, address the increasing complexity of data integration and sharing across silos. At the same time, demand for agentic AI solutions—those that act on behalf of the user—is on the rise. Hybrid data products have particular relevance for group insurance and retirement solution platforms, given the availability of predictive and treatment effect models, semantic simulated events for scenario testing, and personalized decision recommendations. Extra care should be taken to ensure that data products in the financial domain do not perpetuate model or sampling bias and that they adhere to industry regulations, from data privacy—where applicable—to risk provisioning. Although clearly Patterned for Financial Services, these concerns are secondary to the stability, accessibility, and usability of hybrid data products at scale.

title: Reviving Artificial Emotional Intelligence: Deep-Learning Frameworks For Context-Aware Multi-Sentiment Understanding
authors: Bodduna Sriveni, Pramod Kumar P, Satish Muppidi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Multi-sentiment analysis is a more modern and more sophisticated branch of Natural Language Processing that is no longer binary or even ternary in its ability to tell a positive, negative or neutral text. It is more engaged with establishing a number of coexisting conditions of emotions of a shared text that contains the concept of the overlapping, delicate and context- specific nature of human emotions. As the social media and online forums continue to grow exponentially, and more users are producing reviews, huge quantities of information of unknown proportions of complex emotional data have come into view. The processing of this data to derive valuable information creates the need to have reasonable computational means adapted to understand the potential emotional quotient of language. Deep learning (DL) has revolutionized the field of study since the processing of hierarchical and contextual characteristics of raw text can automatically be accomplished. Neural Networks like CNN, RNN and LSTM as well as more modern attention driven transformer networks like BERT, RoBERTa and GPT have proven extremely successful in acquiring deep semantic representations and context dependencies. Nevertheless, even with these developments, there are still some challenges. These the limited availability of completely annotated multi-emotion datasets, challenges in relation-modelling inter-sentiment, expensive computation, interpretability and the ethical issues of bias and privacy of data. The present paper is the detailed introduction of the latest developments in the area of deep learning-based methods of multi sentiment analysis with a focus on hybrid models and multimodal models incorporating text, audio, and visual information.
