title: Metasurfaces-Integrated Wireless Neural Networks for Lightweight Over-The-Air Edge Inference
authors: Kyriakos Stylianopoulos, Mario Edoardo Pandolfo, Paolo Di Lorenzo, George C. Alexandropoulos
abstract: The upcoming sixth Generation (6G) of wireless networks envisions ultra-low latency and energy efficient Edge Inference (EI) for diverse Internet of Things (IoT) applications. However, traditional digital hardware for machine learning is power intensive, motivating the need for alternative computation paradigms. Over-The-Air (OTA) computation is regarded as an emerging transformative approach assigning the wireless channel to actively perform computational tasks. This article introduces the concept of Metasurfaces-Integrated Neural Networks (MINNs), a physical-layer-enabled deep learning framework that leverages programmable multi-layer metasurface structures and Multiple-Input Multiple-Output (MIMO) channels to realize computational layers in the wave propagation domain. The MINN system is conceptualized as three modules: Encoder, Channel (uncontrollable propagation features and metasurfaces), and Decoder. The first and last modules, realized respectively at the multi-antenna transmitter and receiver, consist of conventional digital or purposely designed analog Deep Neural Network (DNN) layers, and the metasurfaces responses of the Channel module are optimized alongside all modules as trainable weights. This architecture enables computation offloading into the end-to-end physical layer, flexibly among its constituent modules, achieving performance comparable to fully digital DNNs while significantly reducing power consumption. The training of the MINN framework, two representative variations, and performance results for indicative applications are presented, highlighting the potential of MINNs as a lightweight and sustainable solution for future EI-enabled wireless systems. The article is concluded with a list of open challenges and promising research directions.

title: Monitoring-Based Prescribed Performance Control for Nonlinear Systems Against Actuator Faults
authors: Teng-Fei Fang, Huimin Wang, Guang-Hong Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: This article investigates the prescribed performance control (PPC) problem for nonlinear systems subject to various degrees of actuator faults. The critical challenges lie in identifying performance deviations and achieving post-fault performance recovery. To address these issues, a performance-based monitoring strategy is first introduced, followed by the construction of a mapping function. Subsequently, by integrating these mechanisms with the actuator redundancy, a unified control scheme is presented, which is capable of handling both the degradation fault and the extreme fault effectively. Compared with the existing solutions, the proposed control scheme exhibits two distinctive features: 1) by incorporating shifting functions, it enables PPC for nonlinear systems independent of initial states; and 2) following the detection of the extreme fault and actuator switching, it facilitates accurate performance recovery within a prescribed time, while reducing dependence on the post-fault information. Finally, comparative simulations are conducted to verify the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Enhancing D2D communication through RIS-assisted NOMA networks:An Underlay Framework with Imperfect SIC
authors: P Baskar, Abhijit Bhowmick, Yogesh Kumar Choukiker
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: This paper investigates a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS)-assisted non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA)-enabled device-to-device (D2D) network operating under cellular spectrum sharing with an interference constraint. In the considered network, direct links are modeled as Rayleigh fading channels to characterize the obstructed propagation environments, whereas RIS-assisted links follow Rician fading. A comprehensive analytical framework is developed to derive closed-form expressions for both the users outage, overall system outage probability, and ergodic sum-rate under both perfect and imperfect successive interference cancellation (SIC). To characterize the fundamental performance limits, asymptotic expressions for outage parameters and throughput are derived in the high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) regime, revealing both achievable diversity order and throughput ceiling behavior. To capture practical deployment scenarios, a mobility-aware channel model is incorporated to evaluate system performance under dynamic user movement. Since system performance strongly depends on RIS placement, a capacity maximization problem is formulated to determine the RIS optimal location within a predefined deployment area, and an efficient optimization algorithm is proposed to obtain the solution. Simulation results shows that increasing the RIS elements significantly decreases the outage probability, while SIC imperfections introduce an outage floor, thereby limiting the achievable diversity order and creating throughput ceilings at high SNR. For example, at 6 dB SNR with a fading parameter (K) of 6 dB, the proposed RIS-NOMA scheme achieves a 23.70% overall outage improvement compared to orthogonal multiple access (OMA). Furthermore, the impact of key system parameters—including the SNR, Rician K-factor, number of RIS elements, and user velocity—on outage and throughput performance is thoroughly investigated.

title: Multi-Regime GRU-Scheduled PID Control for Greenhouse VPD Regulation with Real-Time PLC Deployment
authors: Natthanan Wanna, Kriengkrai Nabudda, Suphasit Sitthaphanit, Natakorn Thasnas
abstract: Vapor pressure deficit is a physiologically meaningful climate variable governing plant transpiration and water transport in greenhouse cultivation. However, accurate regulation in evaporative-cooled greenhouses is challenging due to nonlinear temperature–humidity coupling, time-varying dynamics, and rapidly changing solar disturbances. Conventional fixed-gain proportional–integral–derivative controllers, tuned for a single operating condition, often exhibit performance degradation under regime transitions. This study proposes a multi-regime admissible-region-constrained gain-scheduled control framework in which a gated recurrent unit network serves as a supervisory scheduler to generate smooth, near-optimal controller gains for real-time vapor pressure deficit regulation. Control-oriented first-order-plus-dead-time models are identified under representative operating regimes, and an interval-bounded plant ensemble is constructed to capture dynamic variability. Regime-wise optimal gains are obtained offline using hybrid evolutionary optimization and used as supervisory training labels. To support safe deployment, all scheduled gains are constrained within a pre-validated admissible stability region, such that each deployed gain configuration remains inside an offline-validated frozen-time stabilizing set. The controller is implemented on an industrial programmable logic controller and experimentally validated in a full-scale evaporative greenhouse under real environmental disturbances. Comparative multi-day experiments demonstrate improved tracking accuracy, reduced overshoot, smoother actuator behavior, and enhanced disturbance attenuation relative to fixed-gain strategies, while maintaining deterministic real-time execution. The results establish an industrially deployable learning-based gain-scheduling framework for practical greenhouse climate control.

title: On the Characterization and Limits of 4D Radar for Aided Inertial Navigation
authors: Morten Nissov, Kostas Alexis
abstract: Frequency Modulated Continuous Wave (FMCW) radar is a promising sensor for aided inertial navigation, due to its robustness in environments that challenge traditional alternatives, such as LiDAR and vision. However, its widespread adoption is hindered by complex, noisy measurements, which make reliable estimation difficult. This manuscript addresses these challenges by analyzing the fundamental measurement relations of FMCW radar sensing and developing a reliable estimator. Noise models are derived by applying first principles to the underlying signal processing of a typical radar sensor. These models guide the design of a factor graph-based estimator, utilizing a first-order approximation for the measurement noise propagation. The approach is first examined through simulation, evaluating the significance of different noise sources, the validity of the first-order approximation, and the state-dependent nature of the covariance expressions. Extensive experiments demonstrate the superior robustness and accuracy of the proposed method across diverse field environments and flight profiles, including beyond the radar’s standard operating range. Furthermore, the experiments confirm the insights from the simulation regarding the behavior and performance of different estimator configurations relative to their operating conditions. The evaluation data and estimator implementation are made available at https://github.com/ntnu-arl/rig.

title: Organized Point Cloud Correspondence and Adaptive Weighting for Rotation-Robust LiDAR Odometry
authors: JunHyeong Jo, Chang-bae Moon
abstract: LiDAR odometry is pivotal for autonomous navigation, enabling precise position and orientation estimation through consecutive LiDAR scan analysis. However, existing methods encounter significant challenges, including accumulated drift and performance degradation during rapid yaw rotations. These limitations primarily stem from the reliance of conventional Iterative Closest Point (ICP) methods solely on geometric environmental features, which become unreliable during abrupt directional changes. To address this, we propose Yaw Rotation-Robust ICP (YR-ICP), a novel approach designed to enhance rotational stability. YR-ICP leverages the grid-based arrangement and indexing properties of organized point clouds to refine correspondence accuracy. Furthermore, we introduce an adaptive weighting scheme that dynamically balances geometry-based and organized point cloud-based associations. This hybrid approach ensures high accuracy even when geometric features are sparse due to rapid motion. Experimental evaluations on benchmark datasets demonstrate that YR-ICP achieves a 34% improvement in Absolute Pose Error (APE) compared to KISS-ICP. Moreover, in complex corridor environments requiring frequent rotational maneuvers, the proposed method exhibits superior consistency, showing a threefold reduction in the standard deviation of error compared to conventional methods. These results substantiate the effectiveness of YR-ICP in challenging real-world scenarios.

title: Hybrid-Polarity Logic Design and An Automated Algorithm for Glitch-Optimized Arithmetic Units
authors: Yuxuan Du, Zhangrui Qian, Tuo Li, Changhong Wang, Zhihao Yan, Kaize Zhou, Zhuo Chen, Jun Yin, Yan Lu, Weiwei Shan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems II: Express Briefs
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) processors and Digital Signal Processing (DSP) chips require high-efficiency arithmetic units, while glitch-induced power has become a major obstacle to further efficiency improvement. To address this, a hybrid-polarity logic design method and an automated algorithm are proposed for low-power and glitch-optimized arithmetic units. The output inverters of full/half adders are removed to eliminate redundant switching and alleviate path imbalance. The logical duality of these inverted cells is exploited to enable both conventional logic (positive polarity) and its inverted form (negative polarity) to coexist and hybrid-propagate in arithmetic units. Then, a glitch-analysis-based inverter re-insertion strategy is used to ensure logic correctness with minimal power cost. The automated algorithm merges partial products from complex computations and explores hybrid-polarity circuit netlists with optimized power efficiency. Simulated in a 28 nm CMOS process, the proposed unsigned and signed multipliers achieve up to 54.40% and 55.92% power gains, 13.67% and 11.43% delay reduction, respectively, compared with their corresponding Design Compiler-generated baselines. When applied to dot-product modules in an AI accelerator, our design achieves up to 57.87% power reduction. The results verify the effectiveness of our hybrid-polarity design in mitigating glitches and enhancing efficiency for large-scale digital systems.

title: Interference Sparsity-Aware Turbo Receiver for HF Skywave Massive MIMO
authors: Linfeng Song, Rui Sun, Ding Shi, Yanbo Yu, An-An Lu, Xiqi Gao, Geoffrey Ye Li, Xiang-Gen Xia
abstract: In this paper, we propose a low complexity turbo receiver for high frequency (HF) skywave massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems. We first introduce the beam based channel model (BBCM) with uniform sampling for directional cosine. By leveraging the BBCM, we reveal the interference sparsity of HF skywave massive MIMO systems, which is defined as the asymptotic sparsity of the channel Gram matrix. Exploiting the interference sparsity, we provide a condition of extracting sufficient observation for signal detection. Motivated by this condition, we construct the interference user terminal (UT) set (IUS) and extract the observation vector from the received signal after matched filtering (MF) for each UT. Then, a low-dimensional interference sparsity-aware detector (ISD) is separately designed for each UT by minimizing the mean-squared error (MSE), and the interference sparsity-aware turbo receiver (ISTR) is subsequently formulated using ISDs. Under a relaxed version of the condition for sufficient observation selection, we prove the optimality of the ISTR. Further, we develop an efficient implementation of the ISTR, involving approximate computation of the ISD, the signal reconstructed by ISD and the channel Gram matrix. Moreover, an efficient construction of IUS using the statistical channel state information (CSI) is also proposed. Simulation results confirm that the proposed ISTR achieves excellent performance with relatively low complexity.

title: ASTRAA: AI-Supported Scenario Test-Framework for Real-World ADAS Applications
authors: Benoy Majumder, Rajib Paul, Debolina Pramanik, Madhumita Das Sarkar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Ensuring the safety and reliability of Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) requires rigorous testing across a wide range of real-world driving situations. However, conventional test-case creation relies heavily on manual effort and often fails to capture the diversity and complexity of real-world scenarios. This paper presents a data-driven AI-ML framework for automated ADAS scenario generation using largescale naturalistic driving logs. The proposed approach abbreviated ASTRAA: AI-supported Scenario Test-framework for Real-world ADAS Applications, integrates log-segmentation, event detection, and machine learning-based feature abstraction to automatically derive meaningful test scenarios that reflect actual on-road behaviors. By converting unstructured sensor data into structured logical and concrete scenarios, the framework enables the systematic creation of reproducible test cases that cover both common traffic interactions and safety-critical edge conditions. The generated scenarios can be readily deployed in simulation environments (like CARLA) to support scalable verification and validation workflows. Experimental results on real-world driving data demonstrate the framework's ability to identify diverse traffic situations, reduce manual scenario engineering effort, and enhance scenario coverage for ADAS testing. This work contributes toward building more efficient, data-driven validation pipelines and supports future advancements in automated and intelligent vehicle systems.

title: Automatic Speech Recognition on Mao Language
authors: Pfokreni Adahrii, Malay Kumar Majhi, Sujan Kumar Saha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: The unavailability of annotated speech data renders automatic speech recognition (ASR) of under documented languages a significant research problem as well as language resources. The paper presents the first ASR research on the Mao Naga language that is mainly spoken in the Phek district of Nagaland, India and the Senapati district in the northern part of Manipur, India. Mao is a member of the Tibeto Burman language family and does not yet have a standard writing script, which makes data collection and annotation more difficult. In out experiments, we build a baseline system using Transformer encoder. The Transformer model offers robust sequence modeling but limited local feature extraction because it is solely based on self attention mechanisms. Next we add conformer model for improving the overall recognition rate. Finally we included a large pre-trained model architecture whisper. OpenAI's Whisper, a large scale multilingual and multitask pre trained model, exhibits exceptional zero shot and transfer learning performance. We obtained the results from Transformer-based baseline with the WER of 47.80% and CER of 35.94% and using Conformer model with the WER of 38.95% and the CER of 9.01%. The fine tuned Whisper model has the lowest WER and CER of 3.31% and 2.27% respectively.

title: A Lightweight Real-Time Multilingual Video Communication System using On-Device Speech Translation
authors: Rajat Kumar, Kuldeep Malviya, Avijit Mondal, Priya Pandey, Pravin Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Real-time multilingual interaction during mobile video calls still difficult to achieve due to strict latency, fluc-tuating network conditions, and the limited resources capacity of handheld devices. Although recent speech translation systems show high linguistic accuracy, many studies highly depend on cloud-based pipelines or text-only captions, which disrupt conversational flow and increase deployment cost. We design and implement a lightweight speech-to-speech translation system integrated directly into live video calls. Our system combines ondevice speech recognition and speech synthesis with a lightweight online translation service, coordinated via parallel pipeline that operates independently of the core audio–video stream. To avoid degradation of call quality, translated content is exchanged using a dedicated real-time data channel rather than the audio channel itself. Experiments conducted on physical Android smartphones under realistic network conditions over Wi-Fi and mobile data networks show end-to-end translation latency of approximately 0.5 to 2.0 seconds across multiple language pairs. Subjective user evaluations yield a mean opinion score of 4.2, indicating that the translated speech is sufficiently clear and natural for everyday conversational use.

title: An Enhanced GAN-Based Super-Resolution Framework for Artifact-Resistant Early Diagnosis of Melanoma
authors: Russia S, Jabeen Begum S, Murugan K, Kavya T, Dharanisree A P, Gowtham S
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Melanoma is one of the most life-threatening and aggressive forms of skin cancer. Early detection can greatly improve the outcome of melanoma treatment through dermoscopy — this is a non-invasive imaging modality that enables dermatologists to view lesions on patients' skin. However, low resolution images from dermoscopy often obscure key factors used to diagnose and define melanoma (e.g., irregular borders, slight colour variations and fine microstructures) — therefore, making early diagnosis difficult. To address this issue, a new state-of-the-art super resolution image enhancement network called MELIIGAN (Melanoma Lesion Image Improvement Generative Adversarial Network) is proposed. The MELIIGAN network uses the Real-Enhanced Super Resolution GAN (Real-ESRGAN) framework as a base and provides additional domain specific advances specific to medical imaging, including removing distortion from the images while preserving the important information related to the melanoma lesion. The performance of the MELIIGAN network has been evaluated using the general dermoscopic dataset HAM10000, by comparing its performance against contemporary methods such as Bicubic Interpolation, SRCNN, ESRGAN, and MELIIGAN. The proposed model exceeded all quantitative metrics—achieving a PSNR of 29.85 dB, SSIM of 0.931, and a LPIPS value of 0.084—demonstrating visual fidelity and perceptual quality on the improved visualizations as confirmed by dermatology specialists. The improvements are in the visualization of lesion margins and pigmentation, as well as in microstructural details, that will aid in identifying melanoma. The MELIIGAN++ tool shows great promise for inclusion in computer assisted diagnostic software and teledermatology platforms, and it provides a scalable, efficient, and useful tool improved clinical decision making.

title: Fully Unsupervised Self-debiasing of Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
authors: Korada Sri Vardhana, Shrikrishna Lolla, Soma Biswas
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Text-to-image (T2I) diffusion models have achieved widespread success due to their ability to generate high-resolution, photorealistic images. These models are trained on large-scale datasets, like LAION-5B, often scraped from the internet. However, since this data contains numerous biases, the models inherently learn and reproduce them, resulting in stereotypical outputs. We introduce SelfDebias, a fully unsupervised test-time debiasing method applicable to any diffusion model that uses a UNet as its noise predictor. SelfDebias identifies semantic clusters in an image encoder’s embedding space and uses these clusters to guide the diffusion process during inference, minimizing the KL divergence between the output distribution and the uniform distribution. Unlike supervised approaches, SelfDebias does not require human-annotated datasets or external classifiers trained for each generated concept. Instead, it is designed to automatically identify semantic modes. Extensive experiments show that SelfDebias generalizes across prompts and diffusion model architectures, including both conditional and unconditional models. It not only effectively debiases images along key demographic dimensions while maintaining the visual fidelity of the generated images, but also more abstract concepts for which identifying biases is also challenging.

title: Investigation of Graphene Oxide Coating Methods in Laser-induced Graphene-based Humidity Sensor
authors: Aruloli Kathirvel, Kalpana Settu
abstract: Graphene oxide (GO) is an important sensing material in the fabrication of Laser-induced graphene (LIG) based humidity sensors for health monitoring. The adhesion quality of GO is exceptional, allowing it to adhere to various substrates for an extended period. However, the coating of GO before and after the laser irradiation of the polyimide (PI) film contributes significantly to the performance of the humidity sensor. Herein, two different existing fabrication techniques were used to develop capacitive-based humidity sensors. One humidity sensor was fabricated by coating the GO on PI film before the irradiation of the laser to form an interdigitated capacitor (GO/LIG-IDC), and another by coating the GO on an interdigitated capacitor (LIG-IDC/GO). The fabricated humidity sensor was tested for humidity levels varying from 20% to 90% relative humidity (RH). The GO/LIG-IDC humidity sensor shows better sensitivity of 27.97 pF/%RH compared to the LIG-IDC/GO humidity sensor with the sensitivity of 11.73 pF/%RH.

title: An Investigation into the Low Power Output of a High-Altitude Photovoltaic Power Station
authors: Yan Gao, Quanhai Fan, Song Lv, Haoyi Wang, Cong Duan
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Renewable Energy and Electrical Technology (ICREET)
abstract: An investigation was conducted to examine the power output of a high-altitude photovoltaic (PV) power station that was consistently lower than its design value. A performance model was developed based on the final configuration of the plant. The operational status of equipment was assessed through selective inspections of components such as PV modules and inverters. The impact of dust accumulation on PV string losses was measured onsite. Model parameters were subsequently calibrated, and the computational model was refined using actual power generation data recorded during routine operation. Through comparative analysis of theoretical simulations and actual power generation of the station, the study identifies both external and internal contributors to the low output. The results provide targeted recommendations for enhancing the energy yield of this station and offer valuable references for the design and operation of similar high-altitude PV projects.

title: LUNG-DIET: Lung Diseases Classification via Deep Learning Based DenseNet Using SegNet
authors: Fareesa Amina, Krishnanaik Vankdoth
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The lungs are essential organs that carry out the exchange of oxygen, bringing oxygen into the blood and removing carbon dioxide. Lung diseases can impair this process and may arise from infections, inflammation, environmental factors, and genetic predisposition. Better patient outcomes and efficient treatment depend on early and precise categorization. To get beyond these obstacles, a new deep learning-based LUNG-DIET model has been proposed to detect lung diseases from the CXR images. Initially, the input lung pictures are pre-processed using an adaptive bilateral filter (ABF) to eliminate noise artifacts after the lung images are collected from the databases. The preprocessed images are classified using DenseNet into five different classes including lung cancer (LC), pneumonia, COVID-19, tuberculosis (TB), and normal. Then the COVID-19, pneumonia, TB, and LC classes are segmented using SegNet and segmented lung regions are given as input to the Grad-CAM model to produce heat maps that reliably identify the virus. The classification accuracy of 98.20% for the proposed DenseNet is highly reliable for the available dataset. The suggested LUNG-DIET model attains an overall accuracy of 0.15%, 2.24%, and 1.22% compared to the existing methods such as VGG19+CNN, DCNN, and DNN.

title: Modeling Wallet-Level Behavioral Shifts Post-FTX Collapse: An XAI-Driven GLM Study on Ethereum Transactions
authors: Benjamin Gillen, Rashmi Ranjan Bhuyan, Gourab Mukherjee, Austin Pollok
abstract: The Ethereum blockchain plays a central role in the broader cryptocurrency ecosystem, enabling a wide range of financial activity through the use of smart contracts. This paper investigates how individual Ethereum wallets responded to the collapse of FTX, one of the largest centralized cryptocurrency exchanges. Moving beyond price-based event studies, we adopt a bottom-up approach using granular wallet-level data. We construct a representative sample of Ethereum addresses and analyze their transaction behavior before and after the collapse using an explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) framework. Our proposed framework addresses data scarcity in high-resolution wallet-level daily transactions by employing a calibrated zeroinflated generalized linear fixed effects model. Our analysis quantifies distinct shifts in transaction intensity and stablecoin usage, highlighting a flight to safety within the ecosystem. These findings underscore the value of a bottom-up methodology for quantifying the user-level impact of blockchain-based shocks, offering insights beyond traditional price-level analysis through wallet-level data.

title: Predicting Taxi Ridership Fluctuations Using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Divya Bonigala, Gnana Prakash Dusetty, Nikhitha Guzzarlamudi, Erukala Thirumalesh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The methods utilized for time-series forecasting encompassed data preparation, smoothing techniques, and the implementation of machine learning algorithms, specifically Support Vector Regression (SVR), Random Forest Regressor, and XGBoost. The CRISP-DM framework directed the methodology, encompassing data preparation, algorithm selection, and testing protocols. The dataset was refined b y e liminating outliers and imputing missing data. The focus was on trips to New York City and the integration of areas located within 30 units of each other. We utilized temporal binning alongside the Fourier Transform to discern patterns of recurring demand and identify peak demand periods. The SVR model showed improved performance compared to the other models examined, reaching an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> value of 0.69 and demonstrating the lowest prediction errors.

title: A Real-Time Smart Home Security System for Alert-based Intrusion Detection using YOLO and FaceNet
authors: Dip Bhattacharyya, Tanmoy Chanda, Nabanita Das, Tahamina Yesmin, Soma Chatterjee, Priyanka Das
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: In today's world, home security has become a critical concern, which is now the most prominent challenge due to increasing threats in safety and attempting unauthorized access. In this study, our main concern lies in detecting authorized individuals, identifying intruders, and analyzing maskwearing status through an integrated deep learning surveillance system. From multiple cameras, a large number of detection and recognition decisions are obtained. In real-time video streams, ambiguities arise from illumination variations, partial occlusions, and uncertain face angles. To overcome those challenges, a hybrid framework based on YOLO, FaceNet, MTCNN and MobileNetV2 is designed. The proposed architecture utilizes YOLO for realtime person face detection, MTCNN for face alignment, FaceNet for identity recognition, and a lightweight MobileNetV2-based Mask Classifier to distinguish masked and unmasked faces. For accurate identification under uncertain visual conditions, we incorporate optimized preprocessing techniques such as lightweight alignment, batched embedding extraction etc. An alert will also be triggered instantly when an intruder is detected and an email notification with intruder snapshots will be send to admin's email. Finally, few evaluations are performed to authenticate the efficiency of the proposed method through real-time experiments. Several observations can be drawn regarding the detection accuracy, recognition stability, and processing latency across diverse lighting and pose variations. Interestingly, it is noticed that the proposed hybrid model consistently maintains high recognition confidence for known individuals and accurately flags unknown persons for alert generation. Also, from a comparative study of multiple YOLO versions it can be concluded that YOLO version 12 (nano) offers the most balanced performance in terms of speed, stability, and detection precision which makes it the most suitable model for the proposed smart home surveillance system.

title: Context-Preserving Dermoscopic Editing: Mask-Guided Lesion-Aware Diffusion for Attribute Modification
authors: Tao Sun, Yun Jiang, Yarong Jin, Huanting Guo, Zequn Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Precise manipulation of dermoscopic attributes is essential for augmenting long-tailed datasets and enhancing diagnostic interpretability. However, generic diffusion-based editing methods often induce global distortions or fail to preserve the peri-lesional context critical for diagnosis. To address these limitations, we propose Context-Preserving Dermoscopic Editing (CPDE), a framework tailored for lesion-aware attribute modification. CPDE introduces a dual-branch diffusion pipeline that disentangles lesion editing from background reconstruction. Attribute changes are predicted by a Spatial–channel Transformer at the U-Net bottleneck and are trained with a lesion-aware objective that enforces semantic directionality only inside pathology regions. On the ISIC 2017 and 2018 datasets, CPDE produces spatially localized, clinically coherent edits that preserve lesion extent and surrounding skin. Our method achieves superior image fidelity with an FID of 0.274, while significantly outperforming existing approaches in terms of semantic alignment and background preservation.

title: From Batch to Real-Time: Multi-Agent AI Framework for Autonomous Financial Reconciliation in Wealth Management
authors: Brij Mohan, Shristi Dayal, Anshuman Khandelwal, Chitiz Tayal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Financial reconciliation in wealth management remains predominantly a batch-oriented, end-of-day process plagued by delayed error detection and substantial manual intervention. This paper introduces a novel multi-agent Artificial Intelligence (AI) orchestration framework that transforms reconciliation from reactive batch processing to proactive continuous verification. Our framework deploys specialized autonomous agents for real-time monitoring of positions, transactions, and account balances across fragmented systems while maintaining strict regulatory compliance. We present a hierarchical agent architecture incorporating Scout Agents for data ingestion, Reconciliation Agents for intelligent matching, Anomaly Detective Agents for break identification, Resolution Orchestrator Agents for automated remediation, and Compliance Sentinel Agents for regulatory adherence. The system employs a Bayesian consensus mechanism for ambiguous scenarios and maintains explainable audit trails meeting Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (FINRA), and Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) requirements. Experimental validation on a simulated wealth management environment with 50,000 daily transactions demonstrates 78% reduction in mean time to detection, 82% decrease in false positives, and 71% straight-through processing rate for reconciliation breaks. Our approach represents a paradigm shift from traditional rule-based systems to intelligent, autonomous financial operations with builtin compliance safeguards.

title: AI-Driven Adaptive Turbo Decoding Framework for Dynamic IoT Communication Environments
authors: P.Balakrishna, Pramod Kumar P
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) enables large-scale connectivity across diverse devices, but maintaining reliable communication under dynamic channel conditions remains a significant challenge. Conventional turbo decoders, though effective in 4G and 5G systems, operate with fixed parameters and lack adaptability when faced with fluctuating signal-to-noise ratios (SNR), interference, and fading—typical in IoT networks. This paper presents an AI-driven adaptive turbo decoding framework that enhances decoding efficiency and reliability through intelligent parameter tuning. The proposed model integrates a reinforcement learning (RL) agent with the Log-MAP turbo decoding algorithm to dynamically optimize iteration count and decision thresholds based on real-time channel estimations. Simulations in MATLAB and Python demonstrate that the AI-enhanced decoder achieves up to 25% improvement in bit-error rate (BER) and 30% reduction in decoding latency compared to traditional methods. The framework offers a scalable, software-based solution for energy-efficient, adaptive communication in IoT and future 6G networks, eliminating the need for hardware modification.

title: AI-Based Internship Recommendation Engine for the PM Internship Scheme
authors: D. Rosy Salomi Victoria, L Gokulraj, R Hem Kumar, S. Roselin Mary, S Nithya Roseline
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The proposed research is an artificial intelligence-based hybrid internship recommendation engine to be applied in the context of the Prime Minister Internship Scheme. The abundance of internship opportunities, as well as the varying conditions of eligibility makes it hard to find good internships, especially among the first-generation and rural learners. The proposed system tackles this issue by integrating the eligibility rule filtering, skill-related similarity analysis and a preference-sensitive weighted scoring to provide individual internship recommendations. The aspects that are taken into account during the recommendation include educational qualifications, courses, specialization, academic performance, skills, urban preference, sector, and work mode. Cosine similarity is used to measure skill relevance to allow partial and meaningful skills matching as opposed to strict keyword matching. A weighted scoring system gives priority to the relevance of the skills with also location and sector preferences. The model does internal weight sensitivity analysis, which, however, does not impact user interaction to facilitate robustness. The system delivers few and very relevant internships as well as easy to understand explanations that increase user confidence and faith. The proposed method is suitable to the large-scale implementation in the public sector as experimental assessment can provide better recommendation relevance, transparency, and accessibility than traditional key-word based filtering systems.

title: EcoSort AI: An Intelligent Waste Classification and Disposal Recommendation System
authors: Sherphia Let S, Mohan Bhashya, Prithi Samuel
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Class imbalance has been a notable impediment in the creation of valid deep learning models to classify waste in the real world, because the under-represented classes are often misclassified. This paper introduces EcoSort AI, a multi-class waste-sorting framework based on a balanced system that aims to prevent an unbalanced distribution of classes, using two publicly accessible datasets. The four state-of-the-art CNN models (ResNet, MobileNetV2, EfficientNetB0 and a baseline CNN) were tested with a two-step training protocol, which includes frozen-layer head training and fine-tuning the entire network. Experimental findings reveal that EfficientNetB0 achieves the highest test accuracy i.e $81.1 \%$ and macro-F1 score, indicating better generalization across all waste types. EcoSort AI is a proposed system to combine the Gemini API to convert model predictions into context-sensitive disposal advice, unlike other previous systems, which focus on classification and ignore real-world applications. The paper highlights the role of balancing the datasets, architectural selection, and construction-based design in the creation of scalable AI-based waste management systems.

title: A Hybrid Explainable Deep Learning and Linguistic Feature Framework for Psychological Character Analysis
authors: V Parkavi, Meera Karthikeyan Nair Sathikumari, R. Rajmohan, A. Palaniappan, V. Srija, R. Kanagaselvam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: This study introduces XPsyNarrNet, a hybrid deep learning and linguistic feature framework designed for psychological character analysis in English literary narratives. Traditional literary analytics often lack systematic computational modeling of characters’ psychological traits, especially when dealing with introspective, metaphor-rich narratives. To address this gap, the proposed system integrates a persona-infused transformer for psychological embedding, a graph neural network for narrative role inference, and a novel linguistic-psychometric interaction tensor (LPIT) for multimodal feature fusion. The framework also incorporates a context-aware emotion drift calibration module (CTDF) to manage sentiment inconsistencies across time and a Neuro-Linguistic Attribution Mapping (NLAM) layer for explainable outputs. The system was trained on an annotated corpus of classic and contemporary literature and evaluated using precision, recall, $\mathbf{F 1}$-score, and human interpretability. Results show an overall classification accuracy of 96%, with F1-scores exceeding 0.93 across key psychological traits such as depression, anxiety, and resilience. The explainability layer demonstrated high agreement with human analysts, averaging 0.81 overlap in rationales. This work bridges computational linguistics, psychology, and literary studies, offering a scalable and interpretable solution for narrative understanding. Future extensions may include multilingual corpora, dynamic character arc modeling, and educational deployment in literature and psychology curricula.

title: Decoding Cancer Tissues: A Comparative Deep Learning View of Breast Histopathology
authors: Sanya Malik, Maanasvee Khetan, Reya Oberoi, Hima Deepthi Vankayalapati
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Worldwide, breast cancer continues to be one of the riskiest killers of women, so early and correct diagnoses will be pivotal to treatment. As the field progresses in deep learning, detection of histopathological images has revolutionized the development of automated, reliable and scalable diagnostics.In this study, we present a thorough comparative analysis of six deep learning image classification architectures, including, a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), ResNet50, DenseNet, EfficientNetV2-B0, MobileNetV1, and InceptionV3 in classifying breast cancer histopathological images from the BreaKHis dataset. We report each model performance based upon various key metrics of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score in order to evaluate e Diagnoses Capability and computational efficiency of models. Our study identified significant differences between framework performances, with the transfer models out performing CNN models in accuracy and generalization ability. We provide an insight into the strengths of utilizing deep learning framework architectures for medical imaging and provide new directions for more powerful AI-assisted diagnostic processes.

title: Show Me: Unifying Instructional Image and Video Generation with Diffusion Models
authors: Yujiang Pu, Zhanbo Huang, Vishnu Boddeti, Yu Kong
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Generating visual instructions in a given context is essential for developing interactive world simulators. While prior works address this problem through either text-guided image manipulation or video prediction, these tasks are typically treated in isolation. This separation reveals a fundamental issue: image manipulation methods overlook how actions unfold over time, while video prediction models often ignore the intended outcomes. To this end, we propose ShowMe, a unified framework that enables both tasks by selectively activating the spatial and temporal components of video diffusion models. In addition, we introduce structure and motion consistency rewards to improve structural fidelity and temporal coherence. Notably, this unification brings dual benefits: the spatial knowledge gained through video pretraining enhances contextual consistency and realism in non-rigid image edits, while the instruction-guided manipulation stage equips the model with stronger goal-oriented reasoning for video prediction. Experiments on diverse benchmarks demonstrate that our method outperforms expert models in both instructional image and video generation, highlighting the strength of video diffusion models as a unified action-object state transformer. Our code will be available at https://yujiangpu20.github.io/showme/.

title: Study of Sensitivity and Selectivity of a QCM-Based Biosensor Towards Indian Cinnamon Volatiles
authors: Amrik Basak, Subhojit Malik, Rajiv Pradhan, Subhajit Roy, Prolay Sharma
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: Cinnamon is valued for its distinctive aroma and health-related properties, which arise from various bioactive compounds. Because there are many cinnamon species worldwide, each with unique characteristics, their identification usually requires slow, time-consuming and multi step laboratory analyses. Most existing electronic noses are complex devices that rely on hardwired electrical connections and resource-intensive instrumentation, such as gas chromatography with desorption, chromatography and mass spectrometry, to identify the compounds responsible for cinnamon aroma. The technology presented in this study was not developed as a consumer-oriented product but rather as a low-cost, portable and easily deployable alternative. We developed a rapid analysis method using a Quartz Crystal Microbalance (QCM) sensor coated with acetic acid (CACQCM) to detect one of the dominant volatile compounds in cinnamon. Gas Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry (GCMS) confirmed that Copene (CP) is the most abundant volatile compound in Indian cinnamon compared to other species. Aroma analysis was carried out using six cinnamon samples from different sources at concentrations ranging from 10 to 1000 ppm. The coating material was prepared by solvent mixture with a 2:1 ethanol-to-acetic acid sample ratio and then applied as a drop-coating on the QCM surface. To achieve maximum frequency loading, the coating was applied 11 times. This reduced the initial QCM frequency from 9997756 Hz to 9993908 Hz, resulting in a total frequency shift of 3,848 Hz. We examined key parameters such as sensitivity, selectivity, stability and the influence of temperature and humidity. The sensor demonstrated excellent selectivity, with a response rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.1621 \text{Hz} / \text{mg} \cdot \mathrm{L}^{-1}$</tex> and showed a very strong correlation with cinnamon aroma <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}=0.9432)$</tex>. The sensor highlights the potential for more environmentally friendly, low-cost and userfriendly alternatives to GC-MS. In the coming years, we expect increased use of such affordable methods for screening food quality and environmental contaminants.

title: Generative AI-Driven Framework for Zero-Day Attack Prediction in Next-Gen Cybersecurity Using FusionGuardNet with DualInstinct Optimizer
authors: Nilesh Dnyaneshwar Bhandarwar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The rapid escalation of zero-day attacks has become one of the most critical threats in modern digital ecosystems, exposing vulnerabilities even in advanced cybersecurity infrastructures. These unpredictable and evolving attacks demand adaptive, intelligent, and proactive defense mechanisms capable of identifying zero-day attack. To address this challenge, this paper introduces a hybrid generative AI-powered framework named FusionGuardNet, with the DualInstinct Optimizer (FGN-DIO), designed to enhance the predictability and resilience of next-generation cybersecurity systems. The proposed FusionGuardNet integrates multiple deep learning principles, enabling simultaneous learning of spatial, temporal, and contextual dependencies for robust pattern recognition. To achieve efficient convergence and adaptive parameter optimization, the DualInstinct Optimizer, combining swarm intelligence and cooperative search strategies, ensures stable learning and superior generalization. Experimental evaluation on the CICIDS2017 dataset demonstrates that the proposed model achieves higher accuracy (97%), precision (97%), recall (97%), and F1-score (97%) compared to conventional techniques. Overall, the FGN-DIO provides a scalable and intelligent solution for zero-day attack prediction, establishing a new direction for proactive and resilient next-generation cybersecurity systems.

title: Deep Learning-Based Hybrid Segmentation and Decision-Support Model for Tomato Leaf Disease Classification and Treatment Guidance
authors: R. Usha, Radhika Baskar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: This paper presents TomSDV2A-Net, a unified deep learning framework for tomato leaf disease detection, classification, and treatment recommendation. The model integrates SegFormer for disease-region segmentation, DINOv2 Vision Transformer for feature extraction, and ArcFace for robust classification, coupled with a decision-support layer that maps identified diseases to targeted treatment strategies. A dataset of 7,500 tomato leaf images from PlantVillage, spanning eight categories, was divided into training (5,250), validation (1,500), and testing (750). Experimental results demonstrate that TomSDV2A-Net achieves 99.21% accuracy, 99.3% precision, 98.9% recall, and an F1-score of 98.6%, outperforming baseline models such as Random Forest (90.1%), SVM (92.8%), and simple CNNs (94.1%). ROC curve analysis yielded AUC scores above 0.98 across all classes, confirming strong discriminative ability. Severity analysis revealed lesion coverage of 12.43% in infected leaves, while treatment simulations predicted a 60–75% reduction in recurrence probability when applying recommended fungicides or cultural practices. These results confirm TomSDV2A-Net as a reliable, interpretable, and field-ready solution for precision agriculture and smart crop management.

title: Robogripfusion: a Hybrid Learning Framework for Grasp Prediction in Robotics Using Multisensory Input
authors: Xavier Louis, Prathikshaa C, Bhavana Selvamani, Kabil Chella Pandian, Ajay Vishva Raj, Karthick K
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: Progression of robotic grasping requires accurate, context-sensitive, adaptive models to operate on different object geometries/distances of interaction. In this study we introduce RoboGripFusion, a new hybrid learning framework that uses multisensory data such as RGB, depth, and tactile modalities to categorize optimal robotic grasps. The framework combines visual and tactile information using a Transformer based encoder and uses a dual-branch hybrid network for classification as well as regression-based affordance predictions. In order to improve adaptability, reinforcement learning through Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) improves grip tactic with tactile feedback and, adversarial domain adaptation fills the gap between the simulation and real-world deployment. Compared against the best-in-class models under five major metrics including Grasp Success Rate, Top-1 Prediction Rate, Mean Grasp Score, Pose Accuracies and inference time, RoboGripFusion always outperforms. It gained 93.27% Grasp Success Rate, Top-1 Prediction Accuracy of 91.84 % and Pose Estimation Accuracy of 89.56 % but it came with low Inference Time of 28.70 ms. This hybrid architecture enhances robustness not only but also shows high real time applicability in precision critical manipulation tasks. The proposed system defines a new standard of multi-sensory robotic grasping and provides scalable solution for future industrial, assistive and autonomous robotic systems functioning in dynamic and unstructured environments.

title: Dual-Frequency Ultrasonic Method for Wide-Range, High-Precision Non-Contact Vibration Measurement
authors: Haneesh Sankar T P, Nithya P, Rajesh K R
abstract: This paper presents a novel dual-frequency ultrasonic method for non-contact vibration measurement. Conventional techniques such as laser vibrometry and pulse-echo detection, while widely used for non-contact vibration measurement, are sensitive to alignment or surface reflectivity and often fail in harsh environments. Single-frequency ultrasonic phase-tracking can achieve micrometer resolution, but its measurable amplitude is limited to half the acoustic wavelength, restricting its use for larger vibrations. The proposed method transmits two closely spaced ultrasonic tones and tracks their differential phase, producing a synthetic wavelength much larger than the carrier. This extends the unambiguous amplitude range to the centimeter scale while retaining micrometer-level sensitivity. Because only the phase modulation caused by target motion is tracked, the measurement is independent of absolute distance. The frequency spacing is software-tunable, enabling adaptive trade-offs between range and sensitivity. A theoretical framework, simulations, and validation results confirm low error for amplitudes from 1 µm to 1 cm. Requiring only narrowband transducers and simple processing, the method is robust against temperature, radiation, and electromagnetic noise, making it suitable for structural health monitoring, machinery diagnostics, biomedical sensing, and hazardous environments.

title: Enhanced QR-MCP Framework: A Quantum-Resistant Multipath Cryptographic Protocol with AI-Driven Anomaly Detection
authors: Nishanth Shet, M K Koushik Iyer, Panchadip Bhattacharjee, Somyajeet Arukh, Y.V. Srinivasa Murthy, Dayananda P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Cryptographic protocols that combine quantum resistance, operational efficiency, and autonomous defense are necessary for the shift to post-quantum security. This paper presents the Enhanced QR-MCP Framework, a self-healing crypto-network that uses multipath communication, SPHINCS+ signatures, and Lattice-Based Cryptography (LBC). The framework includes two key innovations: (1) Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) capability, which allows process-without-decrypting functionality for perpetual confidentiality at intermediate nodes; and (2) Adaptive AI-Based Route Reconfiguration using ensemble learning (Isolation Forest, Random Forest, SVM, XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost) for real-time anomaly detection and network self-healing. Enhanced QR-MCP uses algorithmic optimization to achieve better performance: Adaptive Segment Size and ParallelAsynchronous PQC Key Establishment separates lattice operations from data transmission. In contrast to 40.97 ms for Tor, the encryption latency for 4096-byte messages is 61.44 ms, with a decryption latency of 57.34 ms. FHE (204.8 ms) and ZKP verification (122.88 ms) are optional security layers. Simulation testing showed that anomaly detection detected illegal traffic and unauthorized access in real time, reducing network-based attacks by more than 70%. Through multipath fragmentation and SPHINCS+ signature verification, the protocol thwarts side-channel, traffic analysis, Man-in-the-Middle, and collusion attacks. In order to secure 5G/6G and cloud environments, enhanced QR-MCP provides a workable, deployable solution that combines autonomous threat response with quantum resistance through SVP/LWE-based cryptography.

title: Beyond Realism: Learning the Art of Expressive Composition with StickerNet
authors: Haoming Lu, David Kocharian, Humphrey Shi
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: As a widely used operation in image editing workflows, image composition has traditionally been studied with a focus on achieving visual realism and semantic plausibility. However, in practical editing scenarios of the modern content creation landscape, many compositions are not intended to preserve realism. Instead, users of online platforms motivated by gaining community recognition often aim to create content that is more artistic, playful, or socially engaging. Taking inspiration from this observation, we define the expressive composition task, a new formulation of image composition that embraces stylistic diversity and looser placement logic, reflecting how users edit images on real-world creative platforms. To address this underexplored problem, we present StickerNet, a two-stage framework that first determines the composition type, then predicts placement parameters such as opacity, mask, location, and scale accordingly. Unlike prior work that constructs datasets by simulating object placements on real images, we directly build our dataset from 1.8 million editing actions collected on Picsart, an on-line visual creation and editing platform, each reflecting user-community validated placement decisions. This grounding in authentic editing behavior ensures strong alignment between task definition and training supervision. User studies and quantitative evaluations show that StickerNet outperforms common baselines and closely matches human placement behavior, demonstrating the effectiveness of learning from real-world editing patterns despite the inherent ambiguity of the task. This work introduces a new direction in visual understanding that emphasizes expressiveness and user intent over realism.

title: Evaluating Topology Preservation in Dimensionality Reduction Methods for Metagenomic Data: A Comparative Analysis
authors: Chamuditha Jananga, Tharushika Prasadinie, Pasindu Malshan, Vijini Mallawaarachchi, Rajith Vidanaarachchi, Damayanthi Herath
publish: 2026 International Conference on Image Processing and Robotics (ICIPRoB)
abstract: Metagenomics employs high-throughput sequencing and computational methods to analyze microbial communities across diverse environments. This research comparatively evaluates dimensionality reduction (DR) methods for metagenomics by focusing on the inherent computational challenges of high sparsity, compositionality, and dimensionality. We present a comparative study evaluating 8 DR techniques: Principal Component Analysis (PCA), Multidimensional Scaling (MDS), t-distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding (t-SNE), Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection (UMAP), Potential of Heat-diffusion for Affinity-based Transition Embedding (PHATE), Self-Organizing Nebulous Growths (SONG), Pairwise Controlled Manifold Approximation Projection (PaCMAP), and Jaccard Principal Coordinates Analysis (PCoA). The data were processed via a robust preprocessing pipeline integrating non-zero Center Log-Ratio (nzCLR) transformation with iterative matrix completion. We utilize a comprehensive evaluation framework that incorporates both local and global topological preservation metrics: trustworthiness, continuity, normalized stress, k-nearest neighbor preservation. For non-Gaussian and sparse metagenomic data, we incorporated correlation metrics: Bray-Curtis dissimilarity, Aitchison distance, and Jaccard index. Our findings indicate that no single DR method is universally optimal; effectiveness depends on the specific scientific question and the type of biological distance being preserved for metagenomics data.

title:
Analysis and Performance Evaluation of a Compact Hybrid CO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    —VOC Gas Sensor
authors: Jegadeesan Subramani, A Mukhila, S Nidhisaa, K Nisha
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: This paper presents the structural and functional design of a simulation–based hybrid sensor developed for the simultaneous detection of carbon dioxide and volatile organic compounds. Non-dispersive infrared (NDIR) for CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> detection and a Metal-Oxide Semiconductor (MOS) configuration for VOC sensing are two different sensing mechanisms that are combined into a single framework in the suggested model. Compact geometry, effective optical alignment, and material selection are prioritised in the design to guarantee exact gas diffusion and thermal stability. To reduce cross-interference and preserve independent calibration routes between the optical and chemical modules, a layered sensor design is implemented. To improve the even distribution of target gases across both sensor devices and fine-tune chamber size, thermal and flow simulations are employed. Adaptable to embedded system platforms and integrated circuit packaging, the hybrid topology offers low power consumption and modular scalability. Sensitivity, structural simplicity, and manufacturability are all balanced in the overall design approach, which lays the groundwork for nextgeneration hybrid gas sensors.

title: Flying Blind: In-Ground Effect Enabled Haptic Teleoperation of Uncrewed Aerial Vehicles
authors: Nicholas C. Schneider, Jacob M. Anderson, Mathias A. Schoen, Kam K. Leang, Edoardo Battaglia
abstract: Uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs) are increasingly deployed in environments with limited visibility, such as smoke, dust, rain, or fog. In these conditions, conventional visual or range sensors provide little useful information to the operator. To address this, we present a new approach that converts aerodynamic in-ground effect (IGE) interactions into haptic feedback cues. These cues are derived from variations in UAV power without relying on sensors that may fail in low-visibility conditions. We describe the modeling, hardware, and software used to measure and render IGE-based haptic feedback, and evaluate participants' ability to discriminate heights in a user study. Results show users can distinguish different ground distances using only the haptic cues, highlighting the potential of IGEbased feedback in UAV teleoperation. Finally, we present a demonstration experiment where a user is flying the UAV blindly along a path through IGE haptic feedback, showcasing our system's ability to aid navigation under low-visibility conditions.

title: Kullback-Leibler Adversarial Training for Robustness Against Word-Substitution Attacks in Transformer-Based Arabic Text Classifier
authors: Hanin Alshalan, Banafsheh Rekabdar
publish: 2025 Conference on AI, Science, Engineering, and Technology (AIxSET)
abstract: Transformer-based models significantly advanced natural language processing (NLP), yet they remained vulnerable to adversarial attacks. While existing research primarily focused on English, a notable gap existed in evaluating and defending models for morphologically rich languages like Arabic. To address this, we proposed a Kullback-Leibler divergence-based adversarial training method (KL-AT) to enhance the robustness of Arabic Transformer-based models against score-based wordsubstitution attacks. KL-AT regularized the learning process by aligning model predictions between clean and adversarial inputs, enabling the model to resist perturbations to the greatest extent possible. We evaluated our method using the AraBERT model on two large-scale Arabic datasets, HARD and BRAD. Experimental results showed improved classification accuracy and resilience against two strong score-based attacks, TextFooler and PWWS. Specifically, KL-AT achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 \%}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 \%}$</tex> increase in accuracy under both attacks TextFooler and PWWS, respectively. These findings highlighted the effectiveness of KL-AT in defending Arabic NLP classifiers against adversarial attacks at the word level.

title: Post-Quantum Cryptography Linux Distribution
authors: Varnitha Venkatesh, Shreyaa S M Kudremane, Vismitha H, Ritika S Nair, Syed Matheen Pasha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: In this research work, the classic cryptography methods on which modern digital security relies are threatened by an existential risk due to the development of quantum computing. When large-scale quantum computing emerges, algorithms such as RSA, ECC, & Diffie-Hellman, having protected data for several decades, might soon turn vulnerable. This has created a direct need across the globe to migrate to post-quantum cryptographic techniques that can also resist attacks by adversaries, both classical and quantum. In this paper, we authors use the Yocto Project to develop and deploy a PQC-enabled Linux distribution. The system presents a hybrid encryption architecture for quantum-safe file security, combining symmetric encryption using AES-256 with simulated key encapsulation based on CRYSTALS-Kyber. The creation of a special meta-layer known as meta-pqr allows Yocto to be expanded with PQC-specific recipes, system services, and cryptographic tools. A completely automated file encryption daemon transparently encrypts newly created files in real-time while monitoring filesystem events without requiring user input. Platform independence and reproducibility are assured since the entire system is designed, packaged, and tested in a controlled embedded environment built around a QEMU. With thorough functional verification, runtime evaluation, and security assessment, the proposed PQC Linux distribution exhibits reliability, low-latency encryption, stable resource consumption, and seamless integration with embedded Linux workflows. This work provides a validated, modular, and extensible operating system blueprint for PQC-enabled systems that can be used as a foundation for additional research and deployment in quantum-resilient infrastructures.

title: Trajectory Tactics: When Transformers Learn Exploration to Generate Online Signature
authors: Anurag Pandey, Aditya Nigam, Arnav Bhavsar, Ashutosh Sharma, Basu Verma, Divya Acharya, Mohd Amir
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: The increasing need for robust digital signature verification systems has amplified interest in realistic online signature generation to counter digital forgeries. In this work, we propose a novel Decision Transformer based framework that learns Reinforcement Learning output to generate diverse online signatures. Departing from traditional RL approaches that rely on policy gradients or value function estimation, we formulate signature generation as a sequence-modeling problem. Our framework addresses varied free-form signature styles, demonstrating adaptability across linguistic and stylistic variations. Initially, an RL model generates signature trajectories, which are then fed to Decision Transformer, employing an autoregressive sequence modeling approach. To further personalize the generated signatures, we introduce a Q-learning-based module that produces user-specific variations while mitigating noise. By operating in an offline reinforcement learning setting, the proposed method reduces the dependency on extensive online interactions, improving scalability. Experimental results on a publicly available online signature dataset in multiple linguistic script styles show that our approach significantly outperforms traditional generative methods in terms of realism, variability, and mimicry accuracy. These results highlight the potential of Decision Transformers for structured sequence generation tasks beyond their conventional domains. Decision Transforme

title: An Intelligent IoT-Enabled Physiological Surveillance Framework for Early Cardiac Risk Detection and Emergency Intervention
authors: V. Gomathi, K Nandha, M Dhinesh Raj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The Heart Attack Monitoring and Controlling System has been proposed because it offers real-time monitoring of crucial physiological variables, such as heart rate, body temperature, heart beat rate (hbr), and SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, in a network of integrated sensors with a NodeMCU microcontroller. The wireless transmission of the data is through Wi-Fi to an Android application, which allows users to configure the individual settings of every parameter. Above and below those readings, instant alerts are sent to the patient and other emergency contacts, which is important to avert serious consequences of the situation. An Electrodermal Activity (EDA) sensor tracks the variation in the skin conductance with stress or pain, as an extra indication of patient discomfort in conjunction with vital signs. Its portability enables it to be monitored under a variety of settings, which improve patient comfort and autonomy and reduce dependence on hospitals. Taking several sensor sensors and an interactive display, this system allows the early warning against health hazards, especially heart attacks, facilitating proactive healthcare control and quick emergency calls in a dependable and convenient way.

title: Knowledge to Sight: Reasoning over Visual Attributes via Knowledge Decomposition for Abnormality Grounding
authors: Jun Li, Che Liu, Wenjia Bai, Mingxuan Liu, Rossella Arcucci, Cosmin I. Bercea, Julia A. Schnabel
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: In this work, we address the problem of grounding abnormalities in medical images, where the goal is to localize clinical findings based on textual descriptions. While generalist Vision-Language Models (VLMs) excel in natural grounding tasks, they often struggle in the medical domain due to rare, compositional, and domain-specific terms that are poorly aligned with visual patterns. Specialized medical VLMs address this challenge via large-scale domain pretraining, but at the cost of substantial annotation and computational resources. To overcome these limitations, we propose Knowledge to Sight (K2Sight), a framework that introduces structured semantic supervision by decomposing clinical concepts into interpretable visual attributes, such as shape, density, and anatomical location. These attributes are distilled from domain ontologies and encoded into concise instruction-style prompts, which guide region-text alignment during training. Unlike conventional report-level supervision, our approach explicitly bridges domain knowledge and spatial structure, enabling data-efficient training of compact models. We train compact models with 0.23B and 2B parameters using only 1.5% of the data required by state-of-the-art medical VLMs. Despite their small size and limited training data, these models achieve performance on par with or better than 7B+ medical VLMs, with up to 9.82% improvement in mAP<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">50</inf>. Code and models: https://lijunrio.github.io/K2Sight/.

title: Industrial Worker Safety Enhancement Through IOT-Driven Fatigue Detection Using Machine Learning
authors: K. Elavarasi, D. Dhivya, B. Sabeshna
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: The Industrial Worker Safety Enhancement through IoT-Driven Fatigue Detection Using Machine Learning project is aimed at uplifting the level of occupational safety and health of workers in industrial environments by an intelligent, real-time fatigue monitoring system. The proposed system uses several IoT-based wearable sensors, like a heart rate monitor, body temperature sensor, motion sensor, and eye-blink or EEG sensor, to record the physiological and behavioural changes of the workers. The recorded data are sent by the ESP32 microcontroller to the cloud platform, where continuous monitoring is done. A machine learning model, which is already trained, compares the data from the sensors to find out the earliest signs of fatigue, stress, or any abnormal activity patterns that may lead to danger to the worker’s safety. The system sends the worker and supervisor the alerts about the detected condition that is fatigue or irregularity through mobile or webbased dashboards along with a buzzer or vibration signal for the onsite notification. The entire information is stored for subsequent analysis and prediction of the trend. By the integration of IoT-driven data acquisition with machine learning-based prediction, the system provides the means for the prevention of the employee’s interventions; thus, the fatiguerelated accidents are reduced and productivity is improved. This is a project that shows the successful and scalable approach of the management of industrial safety by the use of intelligent technology-driven fatigue detection and monitoring.

title: CHIMERA: An Agent-Based Business Intelligence Framework
authors: Muskan Sethi, Arshit Goel, Santan Ratna
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: Business intelligence (BI) is a critical component of modern decision-making, enabling organizations to transform raw data into actionable insights. However, traditional BI systems often rely heavily on manual processes, specialized technical expertise, and static reporting workflows, which limit accessibility and slow down insight generation. In this paper, we present Chimera, an agent-based business intelligence framework designed to automate the end-to-end BI pipeline from raw tabular data to multi-modal analytical outputs. Chimera employs a coordinated set of specialized agents responsible for data cleaning, schema inference, automated exploratory data analysis, visualization generation, narrative summarization, and conversational querying. By integrating large language models with structured data processing, Chimera enables natural language interaction with datasets while reducing human intervention and analytical overhead. The framework produces interactive dashboards, textual summaries, and conversational insights from a single CSV input. We evaluate Chimera through qualitative analysis, structural ablation studies, and comparative evaluation against traditional BI workflows, demonstrating its potential to improve accessibility, interpretability, and efficiency in business intelligence tasks. This work contributes toward the broader goal of democratizing data analytics by lowering technical barriers and accelerating insight delivery.

title: Customer Behaviour Analysis Using Data Mining Techniques with AI-Driven Recommendation System
authors: Koumudi Prasanna Dasari, Sarika Muda, Sathwika Valija, Thrisha Vijayalakshmi Chenna
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: E-commerce platforms require businesses to understand customer behavior because it enables them to provide better personalized experiences and achieve higher user engagement. The paper describes a customer behavior analysis system which combines data mining methods with an AI-powered hybrid recommendation system to investigate customer behavior and purchasing habits. The system uses customer browsing history and transaction records together with demographic information to create personalized product recommendations based on identified behavioral patterns. The system uses Python together with the Django framework and MySQL database system to handle data efficiently. The data mining process uses association rule mining and content-based filtering techniques to discover important patterns which help increase the accuracy of recommendations. The researchers tested their system using an openaccess e-commerce dataset which includes customer demographic information and their purchasing history. The results show that the hybrid model developed by the researchers achieves 91 percent recommendation prediction accuracy while successfully detecting valuable customer segments. The system enables users to analyze visual data which shows how different demographic groups make purchasing decisions. The proposed approach helps businesses achieve better marketing outcomes through enhanced customer interaction and data-based decision support.

title: SSMT-Net: A Semi-Supervised Multitask Transformer-Based Network for Thyroid Nodule Segmentation in Ultrasound Images
authors: Muhammad Umar Farooq, Abd Ur Rehman, Azka Rehman, Muhammad Usman, Dong-Kyu Chae
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Accurate thyroid nodule segmentation in ultrasound images is essential for effective diagnosis and treatment planning. While multitask learning has shown promise in improving segmentation performance, several challenges remain unresolved: (a) scarcity of labeled data, (b) lack of integration of domain-specific prior knowledge, and (c) limited robustness in real-world clinical scenarios. To address these issues, we propose SSMT-Net, a Semi-Supervised Multitask Transformer-based Network that leverages unlabeled data for an initial unsupervised pre-training phase. In the subsequent supervised phase, our model jointly optimizes thyroid nodule segmentation, thyroid gland segmentation, and nodule size estimation, effectively integrating both local and global contextual cues. This multitask formulation enables the model to generalize better and remain robust across variable clinical conditions. Evaluated on two public datasets, TN3K and DDTI, SSMT-Net sets a new benchmark in thyroid nodule segmentation, achieving up to 3.38% and 1.23% absolute improvements in IoU and DSC, respectively, compared to existing state-of-the-art methods. Our code is available at: https://github.com/Umar-Faroq/SSMT-Net.

title:
CoL
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    A: Convolution-free Local Linear Attention for SpatioTemporal Event Processing
authors: Yusuke Sekikawa, Jun Nagata, Itsumi Araki, Andreu Girbau
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Linear attention is sparse, recurrent, and GPU-parallel; these are essential features for processing sparse data from event-based cameras. We argue that locality is missing to efficiently model event-to-event relationships for continuous spatiotemporal perception. We propose CoL<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>A by introducing locality into linear attention without using a computationally demanding convolution operation. The key idea for the convolution-free formulation is restricting the positional embedding local convolutional kernel into the special class which can be decomposed into two global positional embeddings that can be absorbed into query and key; this replaces convolution with a local sum. To the best of our knowledge, CoL<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>A is the first to equip sparsity, recurrence, GPU parallelism and locality, simultaneously. We demonstrate CoL<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>A’s effectiveness on dense, high-temporal-resolution (> 1000 fps ) prediction task from events, demonstrating real-time capability while maintaining competitive results over the conventional method. https://github.com/DensoITLab/CoLA
