title: Learning spatio-temporal feature representations for video-based gaze estimation
authors: Alexandre Personnic, Mihai Bâce
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Video-based gaze estimation methods aim to capture the inherently temporal dynamics of human eye gaze from multiple image frames. However, since models must capture both spatial and temporal relationships, performance is limited by the feature representations within a frame but also between multiple frames. We propose the Spatio-Temporal Gaze Network (ST-Gaze), a model that combines a CNN backbone with dedicated channel attention and self-attention modules to fuse eye and face features optimally. The fused features are then treated as a spatial sequence, allowing for the capture of an intra-frame context, which is then propagated through time to model inter-frame dynamics. We evaluated our method on the EVE dataset and show that ST-Gaze achieves state-of-the-art performance both with and without person-specific adaptation. Additionally, our ablation study provides further insights into the model performance, showing that preserving and modelling intra-frame spatial context with our spatio-temporal recurrence is fundamentally superior to premature spatial pooling. As such, our results pave the way towards more robust video-based gaze estimation using commonly available cameras.

title: SCORE: Soft Label Compression-Centric Dataset Condensation via Coding Rate Optimization
authors: Bowen Yuan, Yuxia Fu, Zijian Wang, Yadan Luo, Zi Huang
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Dataset Condensation (DC) aims to obtain a condensed dataset that allows models trained on the condensed dataset to achieve performance comparable to those trained on the full dataset. Recent DC approaches increasingly focus on encoding knowledge into realistic images with soft labeling, for their scalability to ImageNet-scale datasets and strong capability of cross-domain generalization. However, this strong performance comes at a substantial storage cost which could significantly exceed the storage cost of the original dataset. We argue that the three key properties to alleviate this performance-storage dilemma are informativeness, discriminativeness, and compressibility of the condensed data. Towards this end, this paper proposes a Soft label compression-centric dataset condensation framework using COding RatE (SCORE). SCORE formulates dataset condensation as a min-max optimization problem, which aims to balance the three key properties from an information-theoretic perspective. In particular, we theoretically demonstrate that our coding rate-inspired objective function is sub-modular, and its optimization naturally enforces low-rank structure in the soft label set corresponding to each condensed data. Extensive experiments on large-scale datasets, including ImageNet-1K and Tiny-ImageNet, demonstrate that SCORE outperforms existing methods in most cases. Even with a 30× compression of soft labels, performance decreases by only 5.5% and 2.7% for ImageNet-1K with IPC 10 and 50, respectively. The code is available at https://github.com/KeViNYuAn0314/SCORE.

title: Enhancing Telegram Scam Detection Using OCR-Based Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Mithun Jayant M, Manikandan K, Gnanasoundharam J
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: As scams on messaging platforms like Telegram continue to rise, the detection of malicious content, particularly from images has become crucial, traditional keyword-based filtering, and Manual moderation is very insufficient since scammers embed harmful text embedded in images to evade detection. This paper proposes an Automated Scam Detection System using Tesseract-OCR with machine learning models for the extraction, preprocessing, and classification of text from image based Telegram messages. TF-IDF-based feature extraction and machine-learning classifiers (SVM/Random Forest) categorize messages into scam or non-scam classes and identify Scam types include crypto scams, gambling scams, and general fraud. Performance measures such as precision, accuracy, F1-score and recall show the effectiveness of the system. This low cost, scalable solution reduces manual workload and enables realtime detection, while being adaptable for future enhancements such as multilingual OCR and deep learning integration.

title: Wavelet-Machine Learning analysis for combined hybrid energy sources with SVC associated microgrid protection under IoT supervision
authors: K.V.Dhanalakshmi, P.Ramakrishna Reddy, K.V.Sowmya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Designing and implementing new security systems to safeguard the power industry integrated with renewable energy sources is essential for the power sector and there is also a need to address many difficulties. With the integration of digital machines and the internet, the Internet of Things is regarded as a revolutionary technology. Wavelet techniques transform essential data into accurate coefficients. Machine learning (ML) techniques are suggested to make the digital reform process simpler. The stability and safety of the electrical system is likely to improve with machine learning techniques. The recommended method generates crucial information with wavelet-machine learning analysis for combined hybrid energy sources against a variety of faults and fault inception angles.

title: Fuzzy Logic-Based Cognitive Model for Adaptive Cybersecurity Threat Prediction
authors: Gnanajeyaraman Rajaram, M. Samanvi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The growing complexity of digital infrastructures has intensified the need for adaptive and intelligent cybersecurity mechanisms. Traditional threat detection systems often rely on rigid rules or static signatures, which struggle to respond to evolving attack patterns and uncertain data. This paper presents a Fuzzy Logic-Based Cognitive Model for Adaptive Cybersecurity Threat Prediction, designed to emulate human-like reasoning in assessing network behavior. The proposed model integrates fuzzy inference with entropy-driven learning to interpret vague and imprecise indicators of threat activity, such as irregular access frequencies, failed login attempts, and abnormal communication flows. By measuring uncertainty through entropy and processing it using fuzzy membership functions, the system continuously refines its understanding of potential risks. This adaptive reasoning framework enables dynamic adjustment of threat levels without requiring explicit retraining, offering a more resilient defense against novel attacks. The approach remains lightweight, interpretable, and suitable for real-time implementation within existing software-based monitoring systems. Evaluation focuses on accuracy, adaptability, and false alarm reduction, demonstrating that the model maintains a balanced trade-off between sensitivity and stability. Beyond its technical contribution, the study highlights how cognitive modeling principles can bridge the gap between human analytical intuition and computational threat analysis, paving the way for transparent, explainable, and self-learning cybersecurity solutions.

title: Real-Time Tracking of Flexible Markers in Low-Contrast Fluoroscopy Using a Deep Neural Network Trained Solely on Synthetic Data
authors: Tomoki Uchiyama, Yukinobu Sakata, Ryusuke Hirai, Hitoshi Ishikawa, Shinichiro Mori
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: In radiation therapy, fiducial markers implanted in a patient’s body are tracked using X-ray fluoroscopy to estimate tumor positions. However, flexible markers, such as Gold Anchor<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">®</sup> (Naslund Medical AB, Sweden), deform within the body, making conventional template matching challenging. While deep learning offers a promising solution, the extensive collection and annotation of clinical data required for training pose a significant barrier to adoption. To address this, we propose a tracking framework that utilizes a lightweight Siamese CNN trained exclusively on synthetic fluoroscopy images. Our method generates synthetic data simulating diverse marker deformations under low-contrast and high-noise conditions, employs dynamic programming for stable initial detection, and performs real-time tracking with a particle filter. In evaluations using clinical data, our method achieves a tracking accuracy of 0.42 ± 0.12 pixels for prostate cancer cases and 0.97 ± 0.53 pixels for pancreatic cancer cases. This significantly outperforms conventional methods, particularly in challenging low-contrast pancreatic cancer cases. With TensorRT optimization, the framework achieves a processing speed of 3.8 ms/frame. This work presents a practical solution for high-accuracy tracking, reducing data collection costs and facilitating the use of deep learning in clinical applications.

title: Analytical Technology Infrastructure and Decision Making Efficiency in the Retail Sector
authors: Arya Kemal, Chato Meyer Alexander, Muhammad Ridho, Robertus Nugroho Perwiro Atmojo
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: This study aims to determine the extent to which the proposed model and data-driven technological aspects influence decision-making effectiveness in the retail industry. The analysis focuses on three independent variables-Big Data, Data Quality, and Data Analysis-evaluated against the dependent variable of decision-making effectiveness. The empirical findings demonstrate that all three variables have a significant impact on decision-making effectiveness, as evidenced by the hypothesis testing results: Data Analysis (T-statistics <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=4.550; \mathrm{P}$</tex>-value <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.000$</tex>), Big Data (T-statistics <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=2.246; P$</tex>-value <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.025$</tex>), and Data Quality (T-statistics <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=3.255; \mathrm{P}$</tex>-value <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.001$</tex>). These results provide strong empirical evidence that data analytics capabilities and data management quality play a crucial role in supporting strategic decision-making processes within the retail sector.

title: An Approach for the Classification of Images of Different Spatial Sizes
authors: Nitish Singla, Tammam Tillo
abstract: Convolutional Neural Networks are widely used for computer vision tasks especially for image classification and recognition. There is always the need to classify images of various spatial sizes without having to train the model again and again. There are various approaches that can be used to classify images of various spatial sizes such as designing the model from scratch for a certain image size or using an image resizing tool. In this paper, an efficient approach has been presented. The main idea is to have one trained network without the need for a resizing tool. The framework has been developed to restructure the input unit of a convolutional neural networks and train the restructured network on images of small sizes thus to try to reduce the amount of training. Various experiments are done on this proposed framework. To test the proposed framework, experiments have been done using various batch sizes and learning rates for different epochs.

title: An Evolutionary Optimized Explainable AI Framework for Accurate and Reliable Diabetic Retinopathy Severity Classification
authors: Vivekanand Aelgani, Akansha Singh, V. A. Narayana
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is the primary cause of irreversible blindness among individuals with diabetes. Early identification of DR severity can significantly help to maintain people's eye health and prevent irreparable damage to the eye. Retinal images are generally blurry and dull, which makes it more challenging to make predictions in advance. It is hard to find DR severity level because there aren't enough skilled retina doctors. To address the issues in diabetic retinopathy (DR) diagnosis, we present XDRnet. This accurate and reliable deep learning model utilises a residual inception network as its backbone, enabling efficient detection of both minor and major structural abnormalities. The proposed model is fine-tuned using the Crested Porcupine Chaotic Lévy Optimisation (CPCLO) algorithm. The LIME explainer is used to explain the XDRnet model predictions. When tested on the EyePACS dataset, XDRnet achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$99.3 \pm 0.2 \%$</tex> accuracy and an AUC of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$99.63 \pm 0.01$</tex>, outperforming several commonly used benchmarks. More crucially, it made these improvements in an efficient manner, which means it can be easily integrated into real-world screening procedures, primarily when a reliable automated system assists doctors in their work without compromising the quality of diagnosis or putting patients at risk.

title: A Federated Learning Framework for Quality Inspection and Anomaly Detection in AI-Enabled Manufacturing Systems for Sustainable production
authors: K. Thiruppathi, Gururama Senthilvel P, M. Arivukarasi, P. Kalaivani, G. Vennila
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: This study introduces a blockchain-enabled federated learning system for intelligent, privacy-preserving quality assessment in dispersed industrial contexts. To handle non-IID and heterogeneous datasets, the suggested system uses multimodal feature extraction from visual, vibration, and temperature data with adaptive federated optimisation. Smart contracts and a blockchain trust layer avoid data tampering and model poisoning by verifying model modifications transparently. Experimental assessments employing casting, electronics, and assembly datasets show 32% accuracy, 14.2% precision, and 16.8% recall advantages over centralised models, coupled with 40% lower communication costs. Results confirm system scalability, energy efficiency, and resilience, making it suitable for real-time industrial implementation. The proposed system provides safe, interpretable, and decentralised Industry 4.0 quality assurance and predictive maintenance.

title: Hybrid NLP Framework for Detecting Anxiety and Depression on Social Platforms
authors: Prinoor Kaur Buttar, Priyal Dargan, Ritu Rani, Arun Sharma
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The fast-paced changes in society have left a drastic impact on the physical as well as the mental state of human beings. Within the previous decade, people have experienced an overwhelming turn of events such as the COVID-19 pandemic which generated a feeling of secludedness leading to a sudden rise in the number of cases of depression and anxiety. Thereby, this motivated us to conduct a comprehensive study by analyzing mental health-related text collected from raw data from multiple online sources such as Reddit, Twitter and from chatbot conversations. The initial stage involved cleaning and compiling collected data after which TF-IDF features were applied to classical models like Logistic Regression and Random Forest, sequence embeddings for LSTM, and contextual embeddings from DistilBERT. To further enhance the performance, model explainability was incorporated using LIME to interpret predictions. The results obtained suggested that DistilBERT with Logistic Regression provided an optimal solution with an accuracy of 96.66 %, hence providing a special emphasis on the semantic analysis of the text.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Skin Cancer Detection Using Convolutional Neural Networks and Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Manicka Senthamarai N, Nivetha I, Kavitha V, Sureshkumar T, Durga P, Gokula Priya S
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: In the world, skin cancer is a major and most prevalent types of cancer and can pose serious health risks if not diagnosed quickly or accurately. Recently, the diagnostic methods currently used can require extensive work and the accuracy can greatly depend on the clinician performing the diagnosis which can sometimes lead to misdiagnoses. The current work introduces a Hybrid Deep Learning Framework (HDLF) incorporating Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) in various types of Machine Learning (ML) classifiers for improving accuracy in disease detection. While using CNNs to obtain discriminative things in dermoscopic lesion, and extracted deepfeature images will be sent through an RF classifier and a SVM classifier for differentiation. Evaluation of the suggested framework's performance against both a CNN-based framework and an ML classification on hand-crafted features. The performances of both classification methods were provided through an experimental design. Analyses show that the proposed HDLF achieved an accuracy across classes of 93.28%, exceeding the performance of the whole CNN (85.12%), that of ML on hand-crafted (78.45%) feature classification methods, with an average AUC of 0.978 and Melanoma recall rate of 99.00%. The efficiency of the proposed system can provide an automated, scalable, efficient, and clinically feasible method to aid in the early detection in dermoscopic lesion.

title: Knowledge Distillation for Cataract Classification Using a ViT Densenet Framework
authors: Shobana T S, Rashmi K B, Megh Mayur M R, Mohd. Viquaruddin Anas, Vedant Khandelwal, Karthik N H
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Cataracts are one of the major causes of preventable blindness globally and the elderly are particularly at risk for the disease, especially in low and middle income countries. The World Health Organization(WHO) reports that cataracts cause almost half of all blindness worldwide, and it is predicted that this will rise because of aging populations and inadequate access to early healthcare services. Conventional diagnosis through slit lamp examination requires trained professionals and specialised equipment that makes large scale screening difficult in resource limited settings. Fundus imaging is a workable alternative for large-scale screening as it is inexpensive, widely available, and easy to implement. In this work, a strong binary cataract classification framework with deep learning is proposed in a Knowledge Distillation paradigm. A Vision Transformer ViT B 16 is applied as the teacher network to train a compact DenseNet 121 student model. To overcome the extreme class imbalance that exists in the ODIR 5K dataset, a balanced training corpus was developed that includes 7000 images of the fundus, 3000 of which are of a cataract case or 4000 of a non cataract case, based on clinically valid data augmentations. Model evaluation was carried out on a totally isolated test set of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2, 5 4 7}$</tex> images to ensure unbiased and reliable performance evaluation. Through the knowledge distillation process, the student model exhibited improved feature robustness and achieved a test accuracy of 98.19%. This marks a significant increase compared to the baseline DenseNet 121 model and its CLAHE enhanced version. The proposed system attained a precision of 98.96% and a sensitivity of 97.41%, both of which are particularly important for automated screening systems where reducing missed diagnoses is important. Comprehensive benchmarking against traditional machine learning techniques like PCA based Random Forest and XGBoost in addition to modern convolution neural networks architectures shows that the proposed method outperforms existing techniques. These results confirm that the knowledge acquired by transformer based models can be successfully transferred to light models, which can achieve accurate results in cataract detection in the hardware with lesser resources.

title: An Efficient Approach for Intrusion Detection in Networks using VQC
authors: Naveenkumar S, Anwar Basha H, Mariselvam A K, Madhan Veeramani, S. Sasikumar, V M Senthilkumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: This article presents a viable strategy for intrusion detection in contemporary networks that applies to a Variational Quantum Classifier (VQC), with the potential of improving a more substantive response to the continual evolution and realities of speed in targeted harassing techniques. Given the increasing dynamic nature of both network traffic and adversary evasion techniques in recent networks, expert-based classical machine learning models often cannot provide high confidence detection accuracy without extreme computation costs. To address this gap, the proposed VQC-based machine learning framework adopts quantum feature mapping and parameterized quantum circuits to capture the subtle, high-dimensional behavior of network traffic out of reach of conventional or the default strategy of most classical classifiers. The model was trained and evaluated on a benchmark intrusion detection dataset, achieving competitive performance across all evaluation metrics. The VQC achieved an accuracy of 90.15%, with a precision of 88.9%, recall of 91.45% and F1 score of 90.16%, indicating the effectiveness of the model in detecting both normal traffic and attacks. In addition to high accuracy, a major outcome of this study was computational efficiency. The model only took 42.31 seconds of training time which is dramatically less than traditional deep-learning methods require for training, and the 3.89 second inference time shows the VQC's capability to perform in near-real-time for live networks. According to the findings, VQC provides a potential avenue for high-performing, lightweight, and scalable intrusion detection. This research positions quantum-enhanced learning as a tangible and meaningful avenue to strengthen network security in the post-classical era.

title: An Attention-Based and YOLO Hybrid Vision Framework for Real-Time Road Obstacle Detection and Driver Distraction
authors: S Revathi, N Satish, P Balu, S Mohammed Navas, M Velusamy, N Rajeshkannan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: A real-time, context-aware system that can concurrently monitor driver behavior and identify dynamic road barriers is necessary to ensure road safety. This research presents a novel hybrid framework that combines EfficientNet-B2 improved with Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) attention for distraction classification with YOLOv7-tiny for obstacle detection. By combining cross-domain data from both driver and road perspectives, the system is further enhanced by Hierarchical Attention Distillation (HAD) and Gradient-Sensitive Spatial Encoding (GSSE). The Hybrid Firefly - Whale Optimization Algorithm (HFWOA) is used for optimization in order to balance low-latency performance and detection accuracy on embedded devices such as Jetson Xavier NX. In real-time at $\boldsymbol{\geqslant} \mathbf{3 0}$ frames per second, evaluation results demonstrate an accuracy of 94.6% for distraction classification, mean average precision (mAP) of 0.99, and IoU up to 0.97 in road obstacle recognition. Adaptive fusion across views based on scene criticality is ensured by the Contextual Voting Attention (CVA) module. A high-fidelity perception model appropriate for incorporation into autonomous or ADAS systems is offered by this dual-stream ensemble framework. The suggested approach lowers the likelihood of accidents brought on by distraction or missing driving indications by concurrently analyzing driver states and external threats.

title: FedSBS: A Privacy-Preserving Approach for Prediction in Smart Bus Systems Using Federated Learning
authors: Shivani Nayak, Charchit Anubhav Mohanty, Rajalaxmi Parida, Yashashree Mishra, Nevan Kumar, Ria Bhatt, Niranjan K. Ray
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Public Transport systems produce significant volumes of operational data, yet most prediction models demand dissemination of sensitive passenger and vehicle data between depots. We suggested a federated learning model to prevent that, in which bus depots can mutually train the models without sharing raw data. The system has two major activities that are integrated in a single pipeline, namely passenger load prediction on the next ride and Remaining Useful Life (RUL) estimation of buses to facilitate maintenance planning. It involves automated information cleaning, feature processing, normalization of passenger-load targets and mileage-grade degradation modelling. Load forecasting is done by using multi-layer perceptrons whereas the LSTM and GRU networks predict vehicle life. Variants like FedAvg, FedProx and server-side momentum are federated, which deal with non-IID and unbalanced client data. When applied to real bus service data, it is observed that the system is as accurate as centralized training, while also providing precise kilometre-level RUL estimates that can be used to realize scalable, privacy-preserving demand forecasting and predictive maintenance.

title: PathoSynth-XR: Biomedical Text-Conditioned Diffusion with Cross-Attention for Rare Cancer Histopathology
authors: Nilanjana Bose, Saurabh Bilgaiyan, Chiranjib Parida
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Instigating the deep integration of BioGPT and diffusion model for zero-shot and few-shot classification of breast cancer subtypes which is rare. It is highly significant for human society because it addresses several critical challenges. The diagnosis of rare breast cancer subtypes is hampered by the scarcity of data in histopathology. We propose the first framework that integrates BioGPT deeply with diffusion models via crossattention, embedding biomedical knowledge directly into the generative process. This produces high-fidelity synthetic images across 16 subtypes, that mainly includes invasive micro papillary carcinoma. Synthetic data is proven to be highly effective. It improved the classification accuracy to 96.8% with an additional boost of 3.25% when augmentation is done in real slides. Medically aware generative AI can help bridge the data scarcity gap, providing new tools to support more accurate and equitable cancer diagnosis, particularly in rare and hard-to-detect cases.

title: FARMARK-AI Powered Seamless Recommendation System For Farm-to-Consumers Market
authors: Nagaraj R, Sandhya M, Divyadharshini B, Janani M
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Farmers often face challenges in selling their produce at fair prices, relying heavily on middlemen, while consumers struggle to access fresh and reliable products. Farmark is a digital platform developed to bride this gap by connecting farmers directly with consumers. The platform uses smart features like AI-assisted freshness scoring and geo-mapping to ensure that produce reaches buyers in the best conditions at fair prices. Farmers can list their products, get insights on pricing, and build credibility through ratings, while consumers can easily find nearby farms, check freshness, and make informed purchases. By simplified the farm-to-table process, Farmark not only empowers farmer but also enhances trust and convenience for consumers, offering a practical solution to the challenges in today’s agricultural supply chain.

title: NeuroSpeechNet: Hierarchical Attention-Enhanced EEG-to-Speech Synthesis via Deep Neural Mapping
authors: Hanumanthappa H, Vidyadevi G Biradar, Megha Arakeri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: This study introduces NeuroSpeechNet, a novel end-to-end neural framework for transforming non-invasive EEG signals into intelligible speech. The approach integrates a hierarchical attention-based feature extraction mechanism, enabling the selective amplification of speech-relevant neural patterns at both local and global levels within the EEG signal. These enriched representations are processed by a deep sequence-to-sequence model employing self-attention to capture complex temporal dependencies, thus facilitating a robust mapping from neural activity to acoustic space. The output acoustic representations are synthesized into continuous speech waveforms, fully automating the EEG-to-speech process without manual feature engineering or intermediate labels. Experimental evaluations on benchmark EEG-speech datasets demonstrate that NeuroSpeechNet achieves superior performance in terms of correlation accuracy, reconstruction error, and speech intelligibility compared to classical and recent deep learning baselines. Objective metrics show notable improvements in both feature prediction and audio quality, while subjective listening tests confirm the enhanced naturalness and clarity of the synthesized speech. These findings substantiate the efficacy of attention-driven neural modeling for advancing brain-computer interface-based speech prostheses.

title: A DeBERTa-Based Claim Verification Framework with Integrated Retrieval and NLI Reasoning
authors: Manthri Parthiv, Kethavath Sai Naik, Ms. Prachi Dusa
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: In today’s world, internet usage has gone up drastically, and we have a rapid spread of misinformation online, which is difficult for users to identify as factual content or fabricated claims, leading to large-scale public confusion and reducing trust in digital news sources. To address this issue, this paper presents a DeBERTa-based claim verification framework that integrates multi-source evidence retrieval with Natural Language Inference (NLI), which enables real-time fact-checking. The system retrieves evidence from trusted news platforms like GNews, Wikipedia, and research corpora through a hybrid pipeline that combines keyword-based and semantic search methods.The retrieved documents are then ranked and passed to a fine-tuned DeBERTa model trained on the FEVER dataset, which helps classify claims as SUPPORTS, REFUTES, or NOT ENOUGH INFO. To enhance explainability, the framework provides brief evidence snippets and an LLM-generated explanation of the model’s reasoning, thus making this system a reliable approach for fact verification in dynamic news environments.

title: Remote Healthcare Monitoring for the Aging Population: A Systematic Review of Datasets, Techniques, and Challenges
authors: Abha Tewari, Dhananjay Kalbande, Sanchi Khopkar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The rapid growth of the aging population has increased the demand for innovative healthcare solutions, particularly in fall detection, vital sign monitoring, and activities of daily living. Older adults are more vulnerable to health concerns, and timely intervention through remote monitoring systems Can help mitigate the risks that come with sudden illnesses. and falls. The paper describes a systematic literature survey. (SLS) of remote monitoring technologies for elderly healthcare. focusing on the datasets, techniques, and methodologies published. During the period 2019-2025, the survey highlights the integration of deep learning models and telehealth technologies to provide continuous and real-time care to the elderly, supporting global health objectives. We analyze 15 publicly available datasets, examining their characteristics, sensor modalities and preprocessing techniques, and applicability to machine learning and deep learning. (DL) models. Main challenges include diversity of datasets, real-world Applicability, and relevant privacy concerns are discussed along with potential solutions such as multi-sensor fusion, hybrid models. and edge computing. The findings provide guidelines for researchers and practitioners, while providing a roadmap for future advances in Remote healthcare monitoring of the aging population.

title: Leveraging Artificial Intelligence for Efficiency in Software Engineering and Banking
authors: Nikhil Teja Gurram
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: AI is changing the landscape of software development and banking, allowing us to work more efficiently, accurately, and quickly by taking advantage of real-time datadriven decision-making. In this paper, I surveyed the AI methods and tools in Software Development methodologies, including Banking. Software Development in line with the accelerated development velocity, AI along the software cycle assists in generating code automatically, predicting anomalies on already implemented code, “predictively” (not preventively) maintaining applications, testing more smartly and to optimize a project management, lowering overall time and rolling costs of development - but still providing more quality. Banking does it in the field of fraud detection and risk analysis, as well as complex data analysis / IP management products, personalized customer service, workflow automation, and quicker implementation speed backed by enhanced security to ensure a better client experience. This paper also explained real-life application areas and effects grounded in the two fields with leading AI technologies, including machine learning (ML), natural language processing (NLP), and robotic process automation (RPA). This paper addresses the struggle related to privacy, ethical concerns, regulation, and provisioned system. AI adoption is employed strategically to achieve not only operational benefits but also innovation and competitive advantage in software engineering and in banking, thereby supporting firms' capability to address continuous technological and business change.

title: Humanizing the Machine: A Study of Chatbot Continuance Intention in the Indonesian Banking Sector
authors: Keith Kanto, Nathanael Joshua Heriyando, Valdo Reyes Kurniadi, Santy
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: While banking chatbots are widely deployed, the drivers of long-term continuance in emerging markets remain under-researched. This study addresses this gap by integrating the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) and the Expectation-Confirmation Model (ECM) to examine chatbot's continuance usage in Indonesia. Analyzing survey data via PLS-SEM, the results show that Information Quality and Expectation Confirmation are critical to Customer Satisfaction, while Perceived Usefulness, Trust, and satisfaction most strongly predict continuance intention. The findings reveal that service quality does not significantly impact trust or satisfaction, suggesting a fundamental shift toward functional and cognitive evaluations in AI driven environments. This research demonstrates that conventional service quality metrics have limited explanatory power for autonomous agents. Practically, banks should prioritize information accuracy and expectation management over traditional service dimensions to ensure sustainable digital strategies.

title: Enhancing Cloud Reliability Through Multi Agents Based Self-Healing Architecture
authors: Rajat Pundir, Yuvraj Kabadwal, Sujal Thapa, Chandradeep Bhatt, Anupam Singh, Rohan Verma
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: India is one of the major economies across the world but intensively hit by a peculiar challenge which is traffic congestion - high pedestrian density, diverse mix of vehicles, and unpredictable choke points. This leads to increased waiting times, CO2 emissions, and fuel use. This system leverages real time data camera feeds to perceive the current traffic state and uses Computer Vision (CV) which acts as an eye of this system, converts this raw data into actionable data on vehicle count and speed, then model this structure into interconnected graph, to recognize these intersection a graph Neural Network(GNN) with an attention mechanism (GAT) is used; this identifies which intersection is the most significant at any given time, Structured data is transferred to the main Muti Agent (DRL) framework. Through this framework each traffic signal acts as an autonomous agent and trained with a Multi Agent Proximal Policy Optimization (MAPPO) algorithm, which helps to decentralize the control, and to learn cooperative control policies that helps to effectively work as coordinated team to minimize the congestion and delays. The proposed framework is validated through SUMO simulations that replicate the diverse traffic conditions characteristic of Indian metropolitan cities, including varied vehicle categories, seasonal demand patterns, and pedestrian flow. In a comparative analysis model resolves the contemporary RL based controller and fixed time, achieving a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 25\%$</tex> in queue length, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 15\%$</tex> reduction in estimated emission, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 20 \%$</tex> reduction in average travel time. The proposed system is modular, scalar, and infrastructure-agnostic, providing pragmatic and future-proof architecture for intelligent traffic.

title: Smart Vehicle Black Box for Enhanced Road Safety and Crash Investigation
authors: M Dinesh Kumar, R Srikumar, S Sahaya Sam, P Sharon Raj, M Indumathi, S Revathy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Growing severity of road accidents and the lack of reliable post-crash evidence require smart vehicular surveillance systems that can provide real-time analytics and forensic data storage. A smart vehicle black box design has been introduced, which combines the concepts of multi-sensor signal imprinting, physics-guided impact modeling, and embedded crash intelligence to achieve road safety and accuracy in investigation. Its system utilizes a tri-axial inertial sensor, forceenergy severity estimation, collision discrimination, and probabilistic collision, as well as on-device classification to confirm the crash events and activate the emergency communication. The accuracy of collision detection was more than 98%, and false event suppression was more than 95%, as experimentally tested. The latency of impact confirmation was less than 12 ms, whereas embedded inference took not more than 4.1 ms. The response time to dispatch emergency alerts with end-to-end was 214 ms, and the forensic data integrity was 99.97% under the high-impact conditions. The proposed framework is highly effective in enhancing crash recognition reliability, response timeliness, and evidentiary strength. It can be deployed in intelligent transportation systems that need reliable safety analytics and legally sound crash reporting.

title: Q-AuthNet: A Hybrid Quantum-Classical Neural Framework for Deepfake Authentication with Explainability
authors: Uroosa Fatima Zaidi, Rakesh Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: This paper presents a Quantum-Enhanced Hybrid Machine Learning Model (QEHMLM) designed to accelerate data classification in Large-Scale Big Data Environments (LSBDE). The proposed framework integrates Quantum Computing (QC) with Classical Machine Learning Techniques (CMLT) including Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), and Neural Network (Artificial-ANN) to overcome the computational and scalability limitations of classical approaches when processing High-Dimensional Datasets (HDD). The model leverages quantum feature encoding, entanglement-based transformations, and classical optimization to enhance accuracy and convergence speed. Simulations conducted using IBM Qiskit and TensorFlow Quantum on the Mobile Telecom Network Traffic Dataset validate the industrial applicability of the suggested approach in Applied Operational environment. The QEHMLM achieves notable improvements, recording 97% accuracy, 95% F1-score, and nearly 20% reduction in computational time compared to classical counterparts. These outcomes confirm that Hybrid Quantum-Classical Systems (HQCS) provide a scalable, efficient, and high-performance paradigm for next-generation Big Data analytics and intelligent computational ecosystems.

title: Multimodal Deep Learning for Dissociation Detection: Window-Based, Sequential, and Attention-Augmented Architectures
authors: Nischal V Pattedar, Punav Anirudh Potluri, Raunak Choudhury, Sravan Kumar Reddy, Deepa Gupta
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Accurate assessment of dissociative states remains difficult due to dependence on subjective self-reporting and clinician interpretation. This work introduces a multimodal deep learning framework that integrates visual behavioral features, Mel-spectrogram representations of audio, and linguistic cues to enable objective dissociation detection. Seven architectures were examined, including CNN-based models, sequential models, and a lightweight Deep Neural Network (DNN) that fuses a 64-dimensional latent vector extracted from a convolutional autoencoder with 16 normalized tabular features. Among all models, an EfficientNet-B0 backbone equipped with a Squeeze-and- Excitation attention mechanism achieved the highest window- level performance with 99% accuracy. A rigorous preprocessing pipeline grounded in feature-wise knee-point analysis established a 1.5σ Z-score threshold for outlier detection, ensuring robust multimodal inputs. Overall, the proposed system highlights the potential of deep learning to support more reliable dissociation assessment.

title: Soil Moisture Prediction for Indoor Potted Plants Using an ANN-Based Drying Model
authors: Tzu-Hsuan Tsai, Ping-Tse Lee, Yu-Chun Wang, Chan-Yun Yang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Image Processing and Robotics (ICIPRoB)
abstract: Improper watering is a common cause of indoor potted-plant failure. This paper presents a low-cost sensing and data pipeline in which an ESP32, equipped with a soil moisture sensor and a DHT11 sensor, uploads time-stamped measurements to a local server via HTTP every 30 minutes and stores them in a MySQL database to support long-term logging and offline analysis. Because the self-collected indoor potted-plant dataset is currently limited, model validation is primarily conducted using no-rain drying segments from a publicly available meteorological station soil-moisture dataset, with the 10-cm soil water content selected as the prediction target. An artificial neural network (ANN) is trained to predict the moisture change ΔSM = SM (t + 1) − SM (t) and is evaluated for short-term forecasting at a 30-minute interval. Results show that the model can stably track soil-moisture variations under natural drying conditions, providing a foundation for future irrigation decision support and indoor system deployment.

title: Clustering Disaggregated Household Consumption Expenditure for Policy Targeting: Evidence from Malaysia's HES 2022
authors: En Lee, Thian Song Ong, Yvonne Lee
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: This paper investigates whether disaggregated household consumption can be used to identify households facing budget constraints by applying machine learning clustering on household-head observations from Household Expenditure Survey (HES) in Malaysia. Unsupervised clustering is performed using K-means and Gaussian Mixture Models to segment households based on their consumption profiles. We transform the original <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3}$</tex> disaggregated consumption expenditure features into per-capita scale using OECD equivalence scale. To address the inherent highly right-skewed and sparsity data, we augment the dataset with two more binary features and ten engineered summary features. The performance of K-means and Gaussian Mixture Models are then evaluated across four alternate preprocessing pipelines (Robust scaling, Winsorization + Robust scaling, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{K}$</tex>-distance trimming, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{K}$</tex>-distance trimming <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{+}$</tex> Winsorization). Following preprocessing, dimensionality reduction is performed by using Principal Component Analysis (PCA), and clustering quality is then assessed across different preprocessing pipelines using silhouette score. Results suggest the presence of four stable and interpretable clusters for policy recommendations. Among them, two clusters are identified as budget-constrained groups with low total spending and disproportionately high allocations of Food and Beverage spendings. The best-performing pipeline is K-Means model on the robust pipeline with silhouette score of 0.7031. These findings demonstrates that disaggregated consumption data, combined with simple engineered features allow the K-Means model to effectively segment households into well separated clusters. This study contributes to evidence-based policymaking by supporting target intervention such as cash transfer for budget-constrained groups. Moreover, this study also presents a reproducible preprocessing and clustering pipeline to handle sparse and highly skewed data.

title: Elephant Poaching Prevention: An AI-Driven Approach to Advancing SDG 15 – Life on Land
authors: Harsh Kanakia, Jay Kanani, Dviti Mehta, Pragati Khare, Manisha Tiwari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Poaching of elephants is still a key driver of loss of biodiversity, nourished by international criminal organizations and increasing ivory demand. Conventional conservation approaches typically disappoint in combating such threats, especially in low illumination or hard to reach field situations. This article highlights an AI augmented, drone mounted surveillance framework exploiting thermal imagery and recent single stage object detectors to detect three operationally relevant classes humans, elephants, and vehicles in drone video feeds. A combined YOLO-format dataset was built through aggregating various thermal feeds, reinforced with custom annotated elephants. Three generations of YOLOs (YOLOv8s, YOLOv9s, and YOLOv11s) were trained in identical regimes and evaluated using accuracy, recall, F1 score, and COCO mAP, showcasing accuracy/latency trade offs for edge applications. The detecting pipeline slots into a compact Flask dashboard displaying outputs, executing short duration object tracking to dampen false positives, and invoking adjustable ranger alerts via WhatsApp. Experimental outcome validates efficient human detection in night time scenarios, with elephants and cars being included as augmentative cues. By providing a reproducible end to end pipeline, this work augments operational capacity for anti poaching actions and directly contributes to Sustainable Development Goal 15 (Life on Land).

title: Enhancing SME Financial Accuracy through Prompt Engineering Services to Address Manual Recording Challenges (Phenomenological Study)
authors: Khalid Muhammad Alfarisi, Riza Rizqiyah
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: The majority of Indonesian Small to Medium Enterprises (SMEs) still remain on using manual financial recording despite digital tools being widespread and available to access, resulting in hindered efficiency and data inaccuracies. AI technology are currently on the rise, creating new jobs on tuning AI to fit individual needs. Earlier studies predominantly focuses on AI adoption in large enterprises, overlooking prompt engineering potentials as a bridge on smaller enterprise digitalization. This research gaps are addressed through this research by studying the lived experiences and operational workflows of seven Small to Medium Enterprise (SME) owners and accountant to identify resistance factors and adoption perquisites through a phenomenological approach. High AI enthusiasm and low technical readiness are revealed in this research, currently 70% of the participants uses manual/hybrid recording. The research also reveal that tiered prompt engineering service monetization to be crucial, it helps improving financial accuracy by 27.3% and predictability by 34.5%. This research shows scalable monetization processes that match highlevel computational intelligence with technical and resource-based limitations of emerging economies, therefore supporting SDG 8 and SDG 9.

title: Ephemeral Bastion: A Lightweight Zero-Trust Framework utilizing Blockchain-Backed Auditing and JIT Certificate-Based Authentication
authors: Ramu Para, S.M. Udhaya Sankar, Aswanth Babu, N Kishan Jai Soorya, G Harikumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Cloud-natives infrastructure and perennially remote administration has broadened the attack surface inside regular perimeter based security types. Static SSH keys and longlived passwords remain as a major form of credential, despite the security risk they carry for theft, brute force and lateral movement attacks. Compounding this problem, centrally managed SQL audit logs can be easily modified by privileged insiders and destroy forensic non-repudiation. In response, we present Ephemeral Bastion, a lightweight zero trusting lightweight just in time (JIT) certificate based SSH authentication and SHA-256 hash chained audit ledger. A custom Python Certificate Authority is used to issue short lived session certificates ($\Delta t=300 \mathrm{~s}$), and thus reduces the credential exposure window length by 98.4% compared with static-key deployments. Kernel level EXECVE events received by Linux auditd are sent over an append-only and single-node ledger over TLS secured sockets with 100% tamper detection. When compared with static SHH keys, SQL-based logging, and Ethereum-based private blockchain (EPB), the framework consumes 68.2% fewer resources than EPB and increases the service load balancing index by 15.57%. By having data on authenticated identities experimentally visually tied to unchangeable command data, Ephemeral Bastion paints over that blind spot to identity audit with out actually incurring the consensus costs that ground within the construct of fully decentralised blockchains.

title: An Adaptive Quantum Key Distribution Framework Using Fuzzy Reinforcement Learning for Secure Communication
authors: D Nagarajan, J C Kavitha, Subitha D
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) has the main advantage of providing unconditional security, which is a consequence of the fundamental principles of quantum mechanics. For decades, key quantum distribution system has been the technological heart of security systems. However, the existing real QKD approaches have performance limitations. The channel experiences interference, photon loss, and fluctuations in eavesdropping conditions that reduce the efficiency and reliability of key generation. To address these issues, a fuzzy reinforcement learning-enhanced quantum key distribution (FRL-QKD) system is proposed to obtain secure and adaptable quantum communications. The integrated network combines fuzzy logic with reinforcement learning (RL) to expedite the localization of the photon polarization basis selection, control of key rates, and identification of error correction thresholds for optimized key generation. Fuzzy inference mechanisms are utilized within the FRL-QKD model to interpret an ambiguous environment. This first-stage implementation of the symbiosis of quantum cryptography, fuzzy intelligence and adaptive learning is a potent resource for future quantum-secure communications.

title: BHARTI Brain Hyperspectral Attention ResNet U-Net with Transparent Interpretation Using Grad-CAM and Guided Grad-CAM
authors: Hariharan R, Anna Sian, Parv Gupta, Harsh Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Accurate brain tumor segmentation from Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) is vital for diagnosis, surgical planning, and patient monitoring. Manual annotation is laborious and subject to observer variability, highlighting the need for automated methods. While deep learning models such as U-Net have achieved strong results, their limited interpretability reduces clinical trust. This work presents an interpretable segmentation framework that combines an Attention Residual U-Net with explainable AI techniques. Residual connections ensure stable training, while attention gates enhance tumor localization. A lightweight preprocessing pipeline employing CLAHE, Otsu’s thresholding, and morphological operations is introduced for skull removal and image enhancement. Data scarcity is addressed through synthetic augmentation using random rotations and Gaussian noise. Model transparency is achieved via Grad-CAM and Guided Grad-CAM, which generate multi scale heatmaps to visualize decision critical regions. Experimental results demonstrate that the framework achieves nearly 98.915% training accuracy with a reduced loss of 0.045. The Dice coefficient (0.919) and IoU score (0.850) demonstrate strong segmentation performance, enabling accurate isolation of brain structures and precise tumor boundary delineation. By combining spectral spatial learning with explainability, the method remains robust to noise and intensity variations while producing interpretable results consistent with radiological assessment.

title: Green AI in Education: Leveraging AI for Sustainable Learning and Eco Skills Monitoring
authors: Farangiz Nurmamatova, Zukhra Aripova, Doniyor Saidov, Alisher Vasiev, Khurshida Khudayarova, Zokhida Adilova

title: Causal Attribution in Digital Marketing: Integrating Media Mix Modeling with Experimentation and Path Analytics
authors: Gulsara Ostonakulova, Sitora Odilova, Sayyora Ismailova

title: Valley-Preserving Spread-Spectrum Jitter for Quasi-Resonant Flyback Converters to Reduce Conducted EMI
authors: Ming-Chang Tsou, Qing-Mu Chen
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Power Electronics
abstract: This paper presents a spread-spectrum control method for a quasi-resonant (QR) flyback converter that reduces conducted electromagnetic interference (EMI) while preserving soft-switching in discontinuous-conduction mode (DCM). The proposed controller injects an adjustable and bounded jitter signal into the peak-current threshold, thereby modulating the switch on-time, varying the effective switching frequency, and dispersing the switching spectral energy. A direction-aware modulation algorithm is developed to limit cycle-to-cycle energy variation, mitigate interaction with the voltage-regulation feedback loop, and maintain output regulation. Both SIMPLIS simulations and experiments on a 65-W (20 V/3.25 A) QR flyback prototype validate the approach. Compared with a conventional frequency-spreading method, the proposed controller reduces the quasi-peak (QP) conducted EMI by up to 5 dBμV in the 400–700 kHz band, corresponding to an average broadband reduction of approximately 3 dBμV, while maintaining tight line and load regulation.

title: Electrical Consumption Data Aggregation Impact on REC Analysis: A Real Case Study Including Industrial and Commercial Users
authors: Valentina Consolo, Roberto Rugani, Elena Mura, Marco Raugi
abstract: Renewable energy communities (RECs) are emerging as key contributors to grid decarbonization and energy poverty alleviation. However, the accuracy of REC performance assessments strongly depends on the resolution of input consumption data. Since shared energy is defined hour by hour, an accurate analysis of a REC should be based on an hourly simulation of the community’s energy flows. While detailed hourly data are ideal, they are often unavailable in practical applications. This study addresses the gap by evaluating how different levels of consumption data aggregation affect both technical and financial outcomes in REC feasibility studies. A real-world REC case study in central Italy, including commercial and industrial users, was simulated using a MATLAB-based model across four scenarios: hourly measured data and three synthetic profiles based on monthly time bands, monthly totals, and annual consumption. The results show that higher aggregation leads to significant losses in hourly accuracy, particularly in the presence of large prosumers with seasonal load variations. Still, monthly aggregated models—especially those disaggregated by time bands—yield robust approximations of long-term financial metrics. As expected, the estimation of hourly shared energy from aggregated data introduces information loss and unrealistic dynamics. Nonetheless, monthly banded profiles provide a reliable compromise. Future work should improve disaggregation methods—e.g., through typical load curves by building use—and extend validation across diverse REC configurations to refine control strategies and planning.

title: Differential RF-MEMS Enabled Power Divider Based Phase Shifters
authors: Zehan Guo, Padraig Fitzgerald, Dimitra Psychogiou
abstract: New types of co-designed differential RF-MEMS enabled power divider-based phase shifters (PDPSs) are reported. They are based on T-junction power dividers co-integrated with single-pole N-throw (SPNT) RF-MEMS switches that simultaneously control the power division and differential phase shift (Δφ) between adjacent antenna elements, allowing to steer the beam of an antenna array to N distinct directions. Scalability to alternative levels of Δφ and number of antenna elements are demonstrated through the implementation of four- and eight-way PDPSs. Practical realization aspects are discussed and validated through the design, manufacturing, and testing of two PDPS prototypes at X-band using commercially-available RF-MEMS switches. They exhibit the following RF performance characteristics: i) four-way PDPS: tunable Δφs: –39.2°, –106.2°, +105.1°, +38.8° and maximum phase shift range: 314.2° at 8 GHz with an insertion loss (IL): 3.09 ± 2.04 dB; ii) eight-way PDPS: tunable Δφs: –38.1°, –106°, +106.3°, +38.2° and maximum phase shift range: 741° at 8 GHz with an IL: 4.57 ± 2.08 dB.

title: Digital Twin-assisted Handover Scheme for Mobile Networks using Generative AI
authors: Lal Verda Cakir, Mehmet Ali Erturk, Mehmet Ozdem, Berk Canberk
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Handover management in mobile networks is challenged by high latency and reduced reliability in dense deployments and under user mobility. Here, existing schemes improve handover initiation by optimising the candidate handover at the decision time. However, these are applied after a non-negligible delay due to the control-plane signalling. Then, when applied, it may become invalid or degrade performance. To address this, we propose a Digital Twin (DT)-assisted handover scheme that performs predictive execution-time validation prior to the preparation of the Next Generation (NG)-based handover. To this end, the DT-What-If Generator (DT-WIG) is used to emulate short-horizon future network states under uncertainty. Here, the DT-WIG is a spatiotemporal graph generative model that uses variational latent sampling to generate counterfactual post-handover trajectories for the candidate handover decision. Then, the AMF estimates the failure and QoS risks associated with the candidate handover and approves/rejects it via standard-compliant signalling. With this, we form a policy-agnostic mechanism that runs on the underlying handover policy. Consequently, we evaluate performance using ns-3/5G-LENA trace generation and replay-based policy analysis, with OpenAirInterface-based signalling evaluation. The results show that the proposed method reduces the handover failure rate and handover interruption time while improving latency, jitter, throughput, and packet loss.

title: A Frame-Theoretic Approach to Robust Filter Pruning in Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Pradyumna Pradhan, Pradip Sasmal, Ramunaidu Randhi
abstract: Filter pruning method is an effective approach to eliminate redundant filters in convolutional neural networks. Most of the existing techniques rely on threshold parameters for filter selection and employ multi-shot pruning, resulting in increased computational overhead. In addition, many methods primarily optimize test accuracy while overlooking robustness to noise, which is critical in practical and safety-critical applications. Since Incoherent Unit Norm Tight Frames (IUNTFs) are inherently robust to noise, we propose a filter pruning technique that removes redundant filters such that the retained set approximates an IUNTF structure. This structure enhances noise robustness and promotes the extraction of diverse and complementary features. Unlike conventional approaches, the proposed framework requires no threshold tuning and adopts a one-shot pruning strategy. Experimental results on the CIFAR-10 dataset using VGG-16, ResNet-56, and GoogLeNet demonstrate that the proposed method achieves robustness under noise and improved accuracy compared to existing approaches while pruning 85.12%, 57%, and 59% of the parameters and 69.66%, 57%, and 58% of the FLOPs, respectively.

title: Numerical Analisys of Quasi-Periodic Concentric Hexagonal Ring Antenna Array Based on the Thue-Morse Sequence
authors: F. P. Chietera, W. U. Usmani, L. Mescia
publish: IEEE Journal of Radio Frequency Identification
abstract: This work investigates performance enhancement in phased antenna arrays arranged using a quasi-periodic concentric hexagonal ring array structure. The proposed PAA employs an unconventional geometric layout in which antenna elements are placed on concentric hexagonal rings, with inter-ring spacing determined by the Thue–Morse sequence. The impact of this quasi-periodic variation on array performance is systematically analyzed in terms of half-power beamwidth, sidelobe level, and scan capability, focused on the grating lobes behavior. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed concentric hexagonal rings array configuration yields measurable improvements if compared with standard ones, especially in terms of grating lobes mitigation, highlighting its potential for advanced phased array applications.

title: Adversarial Machine Learning: A 20-Year Survey of Attacks, Defenses, and Standards
authors: Benhur Tekeste, Khalil Al-Hussaeni, Benjamin C. M. Fung, Ibtesam Alawadhi, Claude Fachkha
abstract: Adversarial Machine Learning (AML) presents a significant barrier to the large-scale deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in safety-critical environments. While early research focused on algorithmic robustness, the field has evolved into a complex intersection of security, assurance, and policy. This paper presents a comprehensive, multidisciplinary survey of the AML landscape, covering over 250 peer-reviewed contributions. We implement a lifecycle-oriented taxonomy that maps attack vectors and defense mechanisms to specific stages of the AI pipeline from data collection to deployment, expanding the traditional Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability (CIA) triad to include Governance and Regulation.We identify critical research gaps, including certified robustness for Natural Language Processing (NLP), and emerging threats in Generative AI. To ground these theoretical insights in practice, we analyze five domain-specific case studies: Autonomous Vehicles, Medical AI, Financial Systems, Natural Language Processing (NLP), and the Internet of Things (IoT). Uniquely, this survey bridges the gap between academic literature and industrial practice by mapping technical AML findings to emerging standards, including the NIST AI Risk Management Framework (RMF), MITRE ATLAS, and ISO/IEC 42001. We conclude by providing a roadmap for researchers, practitioners, and regulators to build verifiable, trustworthy, and compliant AI systems.

title: Morphology-Driven and Symmetry-Guided Geometric Segmentation of White Blood Cells with Self-Consistency Reliability Assessment
authors: P Umarani, P Viswanathan
abstract: Accurate segmentation of blood cells in microscopic images is a fundamental step in automated hematological analysis, yet it remains challenging because of staining variability, illumination inconsistencies, imaging noise, and severe cell overlap. This paper presents a unified, fully automated morphological and symmetry-based framework for white blood cell region segmentation in blood smear images. The proposed method integrates Laplacian-of-Gaussian-based contrast enhancement, hybrid color-space analysis in RGB, CIELab∗, and HSV domains for candidate region extraction, hierarchical segmentation refinement, and marker-controlled watershed for instance-level separation of touching and overlapping cells. To support annotation-free internal assessment, a novel Self-Consistency Reliability Index (SCRI) is introduced by combining stage-wise structural stability, boundary-alignment fidelity, and geometric shape reliability. Experimental evaluation on 50 high-resolution microscopic blood smear images yielded 1,422 detected regions, corresponding to an average of 28.44 cells per image. The mean Dice coefficients were 0.8777 for Level 2 versus Level 3 and 0.9997 for Level 3 versus final segmentation, while the corresponding mean IoU for the final refinement stage was 0.9993. Boundary-alignment evaluation produced a precision proxy of 0.7890, recall proxy of 0.0571, and F1 proxy of 0.1045. In addition, consensus analysis between refinement stages achieved precision, recall, and F1-score values of 1.0000, 0.9993, and 0.9997, respectively. The mean shape reliability was 0.6884, and the overall SCRI was 0.5975±0.0204. These findings demonstrate strong internal consistency and stable geometric performance across the dataset. The framework provides an interpretable and data-efficient alternative for microscopy image segmentation, although external validation against annotated ground-truth datasets remains necessary for definitive accuracy assessment.

title: Enhanced Ensemble Temporal Convolutional Network With Genetic Algorithm Optimization for Anomaly Detection in CAVs
authors: Elvin Ugonna Eziama, Omar Abou Kasm, Mahreen Nasir
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Malicious attacks on Connected and Automated Vehicle (CAV) systems can severely impact transportation safety and efficiency. They can manipulate sensor readings, disrupt service, or falsify environmental perceptions, leading to incorrect decisions and potential catastrophes. Detecting such anomalies is complex due to challenges such as false positives and negatives, sensor interference, resource constraints, and diverse types of anomalies. Existing traditional methods, including statistical approaches, signature-based detection, behavioral analysis, and network intrusion detection systems, often suffer from high false positive rates, sensitivity to noise, reliance on thresholds, and inability to detect evolving or subtle patterns. Recent machine learning and hybrid methods improve on traditional techniques but struggle with frequency bias and time scale selection, limiting real-time applications in CAVs networks. To address these challenges, we propose ET-DRF-GA, a novel method that combines an ensemble-based exploration of Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCN), Differential Receptive Fields (DRF), and Genetic Algorithms (GA). This approach offers dynamic parameter adjustment via genetic algorithms, enhanced robustness and stability through ensemble-based model selection, and optimized sensitivity to different anomaly types using DRF. Experiments on benchmark datasets, Safety Pilot Model Deployment (SPMD) and Vehicular Reference Misbehavior (VeReMi), demonstrate the method’s robustness and effectiveness in anomaly detection for CAV systems, paving the way for future research in this critical domain.

title: Shared and Individual Resting-State MEG Network Signatures of Tinnitus Revealed by Holistic Graph Learning
authors: Payam S. Shabestari, Harry H. Behjat, Dimitri Van De Ville, Christopher R. Cederroth, Niklas K. Edvall, Adrian Naas, Tobias Kleinjung, Patrick Neff
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Engineering in Medicine and Biology
abstract: Tinnitus, the perception of sound without an external source, affects many individuals, yet its impact on the brain' functional connectome remains underexplored. Traditional functional connectivity (FC) methods, such as Pearson correlation, phase lag index, and coherence, rely on pairwise comparisons between activity of macro-scale brain regions, limiting holistic characterization. We used an approach that estimates the entire connectivity structure by analyzing all time-courses simultaneously, robust even for short recordings and suitable for real-time applications. Using resting-state MEG from tinnitus patients and controls, learned connectomes outperformed correlation-based ones in fingerprinting individuals across test/retest. Group analyses revealed altered FC across multiple frequency bands, impacting default mode, auditory, visual, and salience networks, indicating large-scale reorganization. Tinnitus exhibited highly individualized whole-brain FC profiles, highlighting the importance of individual variability and paving the way for personalized models to optimize patient-specific interventions.

title: Towards a 3D Thermal-Electrodynamic Simulation of Non-Insulated ReBCO Coils: The H-FoSTER Model
authors: Rinaldoni Davide, Bordini Bernardo
abstract: Accurately modeling the transient behavior of non insulated (NI) ReBCO superconducting coils is crucial for fully assessing their potential for high-field magnet applications. 3D Finite Element (FE) models are among the most promising approaches for capturing the thermal-electrodynamics of these coils. However, most popular mathematical formulations of Maxwell's equations for superconductors, such as the well-known H-φ formulation, are currently too computationally expensive to simulate large-scale systems like accelerator magnets. To address this challenge, we present a novel mathematical formulation that couples a 3D FE magnetic model with a 1D FE + 2D Finite Difference (FD) electric model, implemented in COMSOL Multiphysics. The formulation has been used to develop a model that simulates the electrodynamics of large ReBCO NI pancake coils. Although still under development, the model has been validated against other models across various test cases, and preliminary results demonstrate its ability to efficiently capture critical phenomena such as persistent current effects while significantly reducing the computational time. In this paper, we describe the electrodynamic model and present selected results benchmarked against more established approaches (e.g., the H φ formulation). Work is currently ongoing to integrate thermal behavior into the model.

title: Adversarial Decision-Making in Partially Observable Multi-Agent Systems: A Sequential Hypothesis Testing Approach
authors: Haosheng Zhou, Daniel Ralston, Xu Yang, Ruimeng Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: Adversarial decision-making in partially observable multi-agent systems requires sophisticated strategies for both deception and counter-deception. This paper presents a sequential hypothesis testing (SHT)-driven framework that captures the interplay between strategic misdirection and inference in adversarial environments. We formulate this interaction as a partially observable Stackelberg game, where a follower agent (blue team) seeks to fulfill its primary task while actively misleading an adversarial leader (red team). In opposition, the red team, leveraging leaked information, instills carefully designed patterns to manipulate the blue team's behavior, mitigating the misdirection effect. Unlike conventional approaches that focus on robust control under adversarial uncertainty, our framework explicitly models deception as a dynamic optimization problem, where both agents strategically adapt their policies in response to inference and counter-inference. We derive a semi-explicit optimal control solution for the blue team within a linear-quadratic setting and develop iterative and machine learning-based methods to characterize the red team's optimal response. Numerical experiments demonstrate how deception-driven strategies influence adversarial interactions and reveal the impact of leaked information in shaping equilibrium behaviors. These results provide new insights into strategic deception in multi-agent systems, with potential applications in cybersecurity, autonomous decision-making, and financial markets.

title: Switching Rollout-Enhanced Value Iteration With Stabilization Guidance for Active Vehicle Suspension Systems: Theory and Experiment
authors: Liangju Zhang, Xiang-Peng Xie, Ju H. Park
abstract: Active vehicle suspension systems (AVSSs) are critical to ride comfort and road-holding performance. However, conventional learning-based methods for solving optimal control problems often suffer from slow convergence and overly restrictive stability requirements, thereby limiting their practical applicability. To address these issues, a three-stage learning scheme, termed switching rollout-enhanced value iteration (SRVI), is proposed to obtain the optimal control policy for AVSSs. Unlike conventional value iteration methods that bound successive value function (VF) differences by the instantaneous utility, the proposed approach adopts a less conservative finite-window stability criterion, enabling earlier identification of stabilizing policies. Building on this, a rollout-based incomplete policy evaluation mechanism is developed to reduce redundant VF exploration by rapidly steering the VF estimate toward a near-optimal region. Subsequently, a monotonic value update scheme is employed to ensure stable convergence to the optimal solution. The effectiveness of the proposed SRVI method is validated through hardware-in-the-loop experiments on a nonlinear active suspension system. Moreover, the proposed method reduces training time by 38.8% compared with existing approaches, while the learned control policy improves ride comfort and road-holding performance, reducing vertical suspension cost by up to 9.9% and control energy consumption by up to 9.0%.

title: Generative AI Meets 6G and Beyond: Diffusion Models for Semantic Communications
authors: Hai-Long Qin, Jincheng Dai, Guo Lu, Shuo Shao, Sixian Wang, Tongda Xu, Wenjun Zhang, Ping Zhang, Khaled B. Letaief
abstract: Semantic communications mark a paradigm shift from bit-accurate transmission toward meaning-centric communication, essential as wireless systems approach theoretical capacity limits. The emergence of generative AI has catalyzed generative semantic communications, where receivers reconstruct content from minimal semantic cues by leveraging learned priors. Among generative approaches, diffusion models stand out for their superior generation quality, stable training dynamics, and rigorous theoretical foundations. However, the field currently lacks systematic guidance connecting diffusion techniques to communication system design, forcing researchers to navigate disparate literatures. This article provides the first comprehensive tutorial on diffusion models for generative semantic communications. We present score-based diffusion foundations and systematically review three technical pillars: conditional diffusion for controllable generation, efficient diffusion for accelerated inference, and generalized diffusion for cross-domain adaptation. In addition, we introduce an inverse problem perspective that reformulates semantic decoding as posterior inference, bridging semantic communications with computational imaging. Through analysis of human-centric, machine-centric, and agent-centric scenarios, we illustrate how diffusion models enable extreme compression while maintaining semantic fidelity and robustness. By bridging generative AI innovations with communication system design, this article aims to establish diffusion models as foundational components of next-generation wireless networks and beyond.

title: Hyde: Inferring Cardiovascular Hemodynamics Using BCG for Cross-user Hypertension Detection
authors: Yandao Huang, Cong Li, Chenggao Li, Lin Chen, Junyao Peng, Weibin Cheng, Qian Zhang, Kaishun Wu
abstract: Hypertension, a leading contributor to global mortality, affects 1.28 billion adults worldwide, and its early detection is essential to prevent adverse outcomes. However, relying solely on blood pressure for hypertension detection proves insufficiently accurate. Recognizing this limitation, we have identified a range of multi-dimensional hemodynamics as novel biomarkers that can offer more comprehensive cardiovascular insights for detecting hypertension. Specifically, we propose Hyde, a cross-user hypertension detection system that leverages ballistocardiogram (BCG) signals to infer multi-dimensional hemodynamics and naturally detect hypertension without disrupting users' daily routines. Hyde features a multi-task, multi-branch, unsupervised domain adaptation learning framework, enabling simultaneous prediction of five hemodynamic biomarkers in the auxiliary regression task. This design addresses several challenges, including unlabeled target domain, single-source bias, and complete negative transfer. We implement a flexible optic fiber sensor mat to collect high-quality BCG signals and deploy it in a four-month clinical trial involving 85 subjects with different conditions.
