A passage decision-aiding warning is produced if a boundary is breached. The proposed system increases the safety of the driver or control system by providing advanced situational awareness and can be integrated into the Safe Autonomous Vehicle Driving Safety Framework while retaining its functionality.

title: PSDiffusion: Harmonized Multi-Layer Image Generation via Layout and Appearance Alignment
authors: Dingbang Huang, Wenbo Li, Yifei Zhao, Xinyu Pan, Yanhong Zeng, Bo Dai
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Transparent image layer generation plays a significant role in digital art and design workflows. Existing methods typically decompose transparent layers from a single RGB image using a set of tools or generate multiple transparent layers sequentially. Despite some promising results, these methods often limit their ability to model global layout, physically plausible interactions, and visual effects such as shadows and reflections with high alpha quality due to limited shared global context among layers. To address this issue, we propose PSDiffusion, a unified diffusion framework that leverages image composition priors from pre-trained image diffusion model for simultaneous multi-layer text-to-image generation. Specifically, our method introduces a global layer interaction mechanism to generate layered images collaboratively, ensuring both individual layer quality and coherent spatial and visual relationships across layers. We include extensive experiments on benchmark datasets to demonstrate that PSDiffusion is able to outperform existing methods in generating multi-layer images with plausible structure and enhanced visual fidelity.

title: Countering Multi-modal Representation Collapse through Rank-targeted Fusion
authors: Seulgi Kim, Kiran Kokilepersaud, Mohit Prabhushankar, Ghassan AlRegib
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Multi-modal fusion methods often suffer from two types of representation collapse: feature collapse where individual dimensions lose their discriminative power (as measured by eigenspectra), and modality collapse where one dominant modality overwhelms the other. Applications like human action anticipation that require fusing multifarious sensor data are hindered by both feature and modality collapse. However, existing methods attempt to counter feature collapse and modality collapse separately. This is because there is no unifying framework that efficiently addresses feature and modality collapse in conjunction. In this paper, we posit the utility of effective rank as an informative measure that can be utilized to quantify and counter both the representation collapses. We propose Rank-enhancing Token Fuser, a theoretically grounded fusion framework that selectively blends less informative features from one modality with complementary features from another modality. We show that our method increases the effective rank of the fused representation. To address modality collapse, we evaluate modality combinations that mutually increase each others’ effective rank. We show that depth maintains representational balance when fused with RGB, avoiding modality collapse. We validate our method on action anticipation, where we present R3D, a depth-informed fusion framework. Extensive experiments on NTURGBD, UTKinect, and DARai demonstrate that our approach significantly outperforms prior state-of-the-art methods by up to 3.74%. Our code is available at: https://github.com/olivesgatech/R3D.

title: Study of Conductivity of Metal Thin Films Composed of Biogenic Silver Nanoparticles Prepared Using Bermuda Grass (Cynodon Dactylon) Extract
authors: Kaushik Roy, Surajit Basak, Avali Banerjee, Moumita Pal, Ranjana Ray
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: This work presents an easy, eco-friendly and sustainable approach to synthesize silver nanoparticles using aqueous extract of Bermuda grass (Cynodon dactylon) and silver nitrate. The formation of nanoparticles was confirmed through ultraviolet-visible (UV-Vis) spectroscopy. Their crystalline nature and morphological features were examined using X-ray diffraction (XRD) and high resolution transmission electron microscopy (HRTEM), while Fourier-transform infrared (FTIR) spectroscopy identified surface functional groups responsible for nanoparticle stabilization. Thin films fabricated from these green-synthesized nanoparticles were subjected to controlled DC bias to evaluate their electrical behavior. The results demonstrated that the films exhibited semiconducting-like characteristics at room temperature, indicating potential for applications in electronics and sensing devices.

title: A Vision-Based Assistive Navigation System for Visually Impaired Individuals Using CNN–SSD
authors: Jayakumar M, Anu G Kumar, Deepshiga M, Manu Neethi Chozhan A M
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Physiological or neurological conditions leading to visual impairments significantly hamper independent mobility and safe navigation. This work introduces a low-cost, user-friendly smart navigation system for indoor applications to support people with visual impairments. The system concept involves a wearable camera on smart glasses with a Raspberry Pi 4B processor, allowing real-time obstacle detection and audio-based guidance. The camera constantly picks up the visual data from the environment and processes it through the deep learning framework made upon CNN and SSD. Through real-time processing of those detected objects and obstacles, some relevant audio cues will be generated to keep the user informed through earphones. It is a way of providing hands-free mobility to the users themselves while detecting stationary and moving obstacles. The proposed solution is affordable, portable, and can be used easily, thus being relevant for daily indoor navigation assistance. This work emphasizes practical system design and real-time validation on embedded hardware rather than proposing a novel object detection algorithm. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the system to detect common indoor objects with timely auditory feedback, thereby enhancing environmental awareness and user safety.

title: Federated Multi-Modal Learning System for Privacy-Preserving Medical Diagnostics in Next-Generation Healthcare
authors: M. Dharma Teja, Hayel Khafajeh, Ankur Bahl, Preethi S, Akila Venkatraman, Priyadharisini
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The high-growth rate of multi-modal clinical data has necessitated the need to develop diagnostic models that are both patient confidential and provide high predictive accuracy. The proposed work presents a Federated Multi-Modal Learning System that incorporates imaging, clinical text, and structured EHR capabilities through decentralized optimization to avoid sharing raw data. The system uses secure aggregation, multi-branch encoders, and an attention-based fusion unit, which is trained using privacy-sensitive gradient updates. Experimental analysis shows significant improvements in the performance of 97.4% diagnostic accuracy, 96.1% F1-score, 0.982 AUC, and 28% of latency reduction over local uni-modal baselines. The model achieves a 35% enhancement in multi-modal features, a 42% reduction in communication cost, and 0% risk of data exposure, making it feasible in a heterogeneous clinical node. The findings verify that federated multi-modal integration increases diagnostic reliability and ensures a high level of confidentiality. The findings indicate that the proposed system will be a scalable backbone to the next-generation privacy-preserving medical diagnostics.

title: Tactile Feature Changes are Detected Faster than Tactile Location Changes
authors: Lauren E. Horde, J. Michael Bertsch, Sara L. Riggs
abstract: Vibrotactile stimuli are increasingly being deployed in applied settings to convey information. Because novel tactile displays often depend on a user's ability to accurately detect when a change occurs, understanding what drives change detection performance is critical. This work compares the change detection performance of tactile stimuli defined by featural properties—i.e., decreases in intensity, pulse number, or pulse width/interpulse interval-to stimuli that change in spatial location, using a two-alternative forced choice paradigm. Results show that, across the board, changes in featural properties are detected more rapidly than changes in location. However, sensitivity and response bias values remain largely comparable for both featural tactile changes and location changes. This pattern may indicate a positive priming effect resulting from repeated tactile stimulation of the fingertips, or alternatively, the effects of temporal summation for featural conditions or a distributed attentional demand elicited by spatial stimuli. Overall, these findings reinforce prior work and demonstrate a significant reaction time advantage for featural tactile changes, offering meaningful insights for the design of future tactile displays that need to prioritize faster responses.

title: Legalese Explainer: Leveraging Artificial Intelligence to Simplify Legal Language and Improve Public Understanding
authors: Faseeha Nazli K R, Reena Roy R, Chandratapa Dewan, Anika Singhal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Legal contracts sometimes contain complex clauses that are hard to understand specially for non-legal users. They may hide many risks. These hidden risks can lead to confusion and potential legal consequences. This research presents a "Legalese Explainer", an intelligent system developed to identify and analyze risky clauses in legal documents and explains the clauses in a simplified manner. The system uses transformer-based models and natural language processing (NLP). The LEDGAR dataset, which is a large-scale legal corpus dataset of contract clauses, has been used to fine-tune pre-trained language models Legal-BERT and RoBERTa. This allows accurate classification and contextual understanding of complex legal text. Attention-based mechanisms are then used to extract important phrases showing contractual risk. These extracted phrases are then processed by using a generative model that rewrites the clauses in plain-language and assigns risk scores based on the possible legal and financial implications. The results are finally combined into a structured JSON report containing the clause-level annotations, phrase-level explanations, and the aggregate risk scores. Overall, the framework proposed represents a notable step towards developing smarter, clearer, and reliable AI-driven tools for document analysis.

title: The Effect of e-WOM, Price Perception, and Trust on Shopee Users' Purchase Decisions in Bekasi
authors: Evans, Rafael Newantona Sinurat, Danang Prihandoko, Leo Andri Yulius Caesar
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: This study investigates the impact of digital marketing factors and consumer perceptions on purchasing decisions of Indonesian e-commerce users in Bekasi. The research explores the determinants of purchasing decisions on the Shopee platform, examining the direct relationships between Electronic Word of Mouth (e-WOM), Price Perception, and Trust as key drivers in the online shopping environment. This quantitative study used 405 survey responses from active Indonesian e-commerce consumers in Bekasi, aged 17–59, who participated in online shopping between November and December 2025. The data were analyzed using SmartPLS 4.0 with Structural Equation Modeling Partial Least Squares (SEM-PLS). The results show that trust had the strongest direct effect on purchasing decisions compared to price perception and e-WOM, although all three variables demonstrated a positive and significant influence. This study provides practical insights for researchers and industry practitioners regarding the importance of consumer trust, pricing strategies, and digital reviews in sustaining competitiveness in the e-commerce sector.

title: Analyzing the Impact of Early Adoption of Post Quantum Cryptography in Apache Kafka
authors: Saahil Pakhare, Shreya Hegde, Animesh Giri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Today, data is the forefront of modern decision making, it forms the foundational piece for a majority of business decisions. With the rising importance of data, real-time streaming platforms like Apache Kafka play a crucial role as they enable data movement across systems, ensuring its timely availability, and with its value increasing it is of utmost importance ensure it is secured. While data can be considered secure at the source of its generation, it is most vulnerable when in transit. While traditional encryption techniques like Advanced Encryption Standard are currently considered secure, the pace at which quantum computers are evolving presents a growing threat. This paper presents an experimental study examining the real-world impact of securing data transmission pipelines against post quantum adversaries through the use of Post Quantum Cryptography. To ensure a fair comparison, identical payloads were transmitted through three distinct pipelines, one transmitting plaintext, another employing AES-256 encryption, and a third utilizing a hybrid quantum-safe model which combines Kyber512 based key encapsulation with AES-256 based data encryption.

title: Improving Head Detection and Counting Accuracy via Patch-Wise YOLOv8 Inference
authors: Saw Mya Nandar, Soe Mya Thu, Ah Nge Htwe
abstract: Accurate head detection and counting in crowded scenes remains a significant challenge in environments such as classrooms, lecture halls, and public gatherings. Object recognition models that are used today often have problems with absence, scale variation, and finding small objects, which makes counting less accurate. So, the system proposed a patch-wise YOLOv8 (You Only Look Once version 8) inference approach to improve head detection and counting accuracy. The YOLOv8 detector is applied to each patch, and patch-level predictions are merged using non-maximum suppression (NMS) and coordinate re-mapping to obtain the final detection results. The proposed system is evaluated on the SCUT-HEAD dataset and CCTV dataset. The experimental results demonstrate that the patchwise YOLOv8 inference system outperforms higher than the original YOLOv8 model and other methods achieving Average Precision (mAP) 91.4 %, recall 89.6 %, and counting accuracy 88.2%.

title: Novel Approach for Design of Autonomous Multi-Robot Networks with Efficient Deployment and Obstacle Avoidance in Unknown Environments
authors: R Premalatha, A. Vanathi, Arun Kumar Selvam, Venkatesh Vakucherla, S.A Shifani, R Kamalakannan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: An Autonomous Multi-Robot (AMR) system is a network of robots that are connected through ad hoc networks and thus collaborate for different tasks such as exploration and search/rescue. A model named the Autonomous Multi-Robot Energy-efficient Deployment and Docking Optimisation (AMREDO) has been developed which combines several modules such as multi-robot network deployment, LiDAR pre-processing, hybrid docking control, and SNN-based navigation. Through a weighted utility function, the components of the model command navigation, network formation, and docking in complex environments, thereby increasing the efficiency of operations. They minimize the noise in LiDAR scans. The hybrid docking control method integrates visual perception and LiDAR alignment for staging docking phases based on a Finite State Machine (FSM) architecture. The visual PID controller helps the drone in its movements through detection and alignment, whereas the SNN-based Navigation module changes the sensor inputs into spiking neural signals for enhanced decision-making. The evaluation of the method is a precision & recall graph of 0.98, a precision of 1.00, a recall of $0.03, \mathrm{~F} 1$-score graph of 0.88.

title: Finite State Machines in the Context of Knowledge Based Transformations
authors: Johannes Schick, Marc Wagner, Wolfram-Manfred Lippe
abstract: In this paper we apply a novel approach for transforming models between textual and graphical representations using knowledge graphs within the sphere of meta-modeling and model transformations. We will use an example involving a finite state machine (FSM). FSMs are basic components in many modeling languages. Unlike traditional transformation methods, our technique uniquely addresses the limitations in transforming complex models between modalities, specifically textual and graphical representations. This approach has significant implications for the development of advanced AI-driven multi-modal transformation tools and knowledge graphs, providing a robust framework for future research and practical applications in various domains.

title: A Data-Driven Approach to Customer Churn Prediction in Banking: A Comparative Study of Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Sathyadevi G, Heltin Genitha C
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Customer churn remains a major challenge for banks, Since customer retention typically incurs lower costs than customer acquisition, banking sectors prioritize retaining existing clients. Accurate prediction of churn enables financial institutions to design focused retention programs and reduce revenue leakage. Predicting potential churners allows banking sectors to take proactive measures and enhance customer loyalty. This study develops a comprehensive churn prediction framework using datasets [2] derived from the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) repositories. A unified preprocessing and evaluation pipeline was employed here to implement and compare the different machine learning models: Logistic Regression classifiers, Random Forest classifiers, XGBoost and CatBoost. The dataset undergoes systematic preprocessing that includes imputation of missing values, normalization of numeric variables and encoding of categorical attributes. A time-based train validation test division is adopted to ensure temporal consistency and prevent data leakage. Each model is fine-tuned using optimal hyperparameters and evaluated through comprehensive performance metrics. Experimental findings indicate that the Random Forest model achieved enhanced performance with respect to ROC curve and test accuracy, demonstrating strong generalization and robustness. CatBoost and XGBoost also achieve high predictive efficiency, while Logistic Regression remains valuable for its transparency and interpretability. Feature-importance and SHAP-based analyses further identify the most influential factors driving customer churn.

title: A PCA-Enhanced Pruned CNN-BiLSTM Framework for Automated Prostate Cancer Detection and Gleason Grading Using mp-MRI and Histopathology
authors: Mamata Beura, Partha Sarathi Pattnayak, Pradeep Kumar Mallick, Debahuti Mishra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Prostate cancer is one of the most common cancers that is afflicting men in the world today and therefore requires proper and prompt diagnostic methods. The paper introduces a new hybrid model that combines synergistically and using a principal component analysis (PCA) and state-of-the-art deep learning structures to automatically detect prostate cancer and grade it using multi-parametric magnetic resonance imaging (mpMRI) and histopathological data. The suggested architecture utilizes a two-way pathway of feature extraction that incorporates both PCA-based dimensionality reduction and convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with the help of transfer learning using the pre-trained models, such as ResNet-50 and EfficientNets. A feature fusion module combines complementary data though both pathways and then classifies it. For all benchmark datasets, ProstateX (n=1,400), TCGA-PRAD (n=499) and a multi-center clinical dataset (n=2,000), comprehensive experiments were done. The proposed hybrid framework is significantly better in performance with 96.8% accuracy, 95.2% sensitivity, 97.1% specificity, and area under the ROC curve (AUC) of 0.98. The work sets the current standard of the diagnosis of prostate cancer with AI assistance.

title: MMHOI: Modeling Complex 3D Multi-Human Multi-Object Interactions
authors: Kaen Kogashi, Anoop Cherian, Meng-Yu Jennifer Kuo
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Real-world scenes often feature multiple humans interacting with multiple objects in ways that are causal, goal-oriented, or cooperative. Yet existing 3D human-object interaction (HOI) benchmarks consider only a fraction of these complex interactions. To close this gap, we present MMHOI – a large-scale, Multi-human Multi-object Interaction dataset consisting of images from 12 everyday scenarios. MMHOI offers complete 3D shape and pose annotations for every person and object, along with labels for 78 action categories and 14 interaction-specific body parts, providing a comprehensive testbed for next-generation HOI research.Building on MMHOI, we present MMHOI-Net, an end-to-end transformer-based neural network for jointly estimating human–object 3D geometries, their interactions, and associated actions. A key innovation in our framework is a structured dual-patch representation for modeling objects and their interactions, combined with action recognition to enhance the interaction prediction. Experiments on MMHOI and the recently proposed CORE4D datasets demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance in multi-HOI modeling, excelling in both accuracy and reconstruction quality. The MMHOI dataset is available at https://zenodo.org/records/17711786.

title: Privacy-Preserving Facial Emotion Recognition Using Federated Few-Shot Learning
authors: G. Jyothi, P. Nirmala
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Facial Emotion Recognition (FER) is an important sub-field of affective computing, and is used in medicine, education, human-computer interaction, and security. Although variability in expressions, limited labeled data, and privacy concerns were crucial to the existing FER systems, even though considerable advances have been made, the systems remain limited. To overcome these issues, this study designs a Federated Few-Shot Ensemble Learning (FFSL) system that combines three complementary methods namely (i) Ensemble Learning, which ensures high robustness and accuracy through a combination of different base classifiers including (ii) Support Vector Machines (SVM) and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and (iii) Federated Learning (FL) which provides privacy-preserving and scalable learning through aggregation of model updates by distributed clients without explicit information sharing. Experimental studies on CK+ dataset show that the suggested framework works with better recognition accuracy where the ensemble+FSL module provides the highest confidence (90 percent) of target classes than individual models. The FFSL model therefore provides a privacy preserving, data saving and correct FER solution which can be generalized to a wide range of settings. This contribution supports the creation of ethically oriented, real time and scalable emotion recognition systems towards real world implementation.

title: DuPLUS: Dual-Prompt Vision-Language Framework for Universal Medical Image Segmentation and Prognosis
authors: Numan Saeed, Tausifa Jan Saleem, Fadillah Maani, Muhammad Ridzuan, Hu Wang, Mohammad Yaqub
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Deep learning for medical imaging is hampered by task-specific models that lack generalizability and prognostic capabilities, while existing ’universal’ approaches suffer from simplistic conditioning and poor medical semantic understanding. To address these limitations, we introduce DuPLUS, a deep learning framework for efficient multimodal medical image analysis. DuPLUS introduces a novel vision-language framework that leverages hierarchical semantic prompts for fine-grained control over the analysis task, a capability absent in prior universal models. To enable extensibility to other medical tasks, it includes a hierarchical, text-controlled architecture driven by a unique dual-prompt mechanism. For segmentation, DuPLUS is able to generalize across three imaging modalities, ten different anatomically various medical datasets, encompassing more than 30 organs and tumor types. It outperforms the state-of-the-art task-specific and universal models on 8 out of 10 datasets. We demonstrate extensibility of its text-controlled architecture by seamless integration of electronic health record (EHR) data for prognosis prediction, and on a head and neck cancer dataset, DuPLUS achieved a Concordance Index (CI) of 0.69. Parameter-efficient fine-tuning enables rapid adaptation to new tasks and modalities from varying centers, establishing DuPLUS as a versatile and clinically relevant solution for medical image analysis. The code for this work is made available at: Code

title: Deep Latent Representation Learning for Pan-Cancer Classification with Explainable Boosting from High-Dimensional Gene Expression Data
authors: Diptadip Maiti, Madhuchhanda Basak
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: High-dimensional gene expression data pose substantial challenges for predictive modeling due to noise, redundancy, and the curse of dimensionality. This work introduces a hybrid machine learning framework that integrates a Variational Autoencoder (VAE) for nonlinear dimensionality reduction with an Explainable Boosting Machine (EBM) for transparent multiclass cancer classification. The VAE compresses over 20,000 transcriptomic features into a 64-dimensional latent space while preserving biologically meaningful structure, yielding improved separability across tumor types. These latent representations are then classified using an EBM, enabling inherently interpretable predictions supported by global feature importance and SHAPbased local explanations. Experiments on the TCGA Pan-Cancer dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed pipeline, achieving 0.98 accuracy, 0.99 macro-precision, 0.97 macro-recall, and 0.98 macro-F1, with per-class F1-scores ranging from 0.95 to 1.00. The results highlight that the VAE-EBM framework provides both high predictive performance and clinical interpretability, offering a promising direction for precision oncology.

title: Energy-Aware RISC-V SoC with Integrated AI Accelerator for Edge Computing Applications
authors: Balamurugan. M, Mahmoud Odeh, Atul Kumar Singla, Preethi S, Ramya M, D. Siri
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Edge intelligence requires processing that is very efficient on-device, with the capability to run neural loads with very tight requirements in terms of energy and latency. A 16x16 quantized tensor accelerator and multi-bank on-chip memory subsystem is made energy-aware with an energy-efficient RISC-V System-on-Chip to meet these requirements. Some of the architecture features used are fused micro-operations, dataflow-based scheduling, workload-based DVFS, and quantized systolic computation. Based on experimental assessment, it has achieved significant improvements: 9.8 TOPS/W energy efficiency, 62% drop in off-chip memory access, 17.4% fall in micro-op energy, 94.7% PE utilization, 31% idle-power, and 11.3 ms edge workloads inference latency. These findings prove significant changes in the computational density, dataflow performance, and the responsiveness of the system. The proposed SoC provides a scalable platform to support advanced edge-AI applications with the need to execute high-performance, low-power, and real-time inferences.

title: Efficient Task Execution Model with System Safety Using Edge Convolutional Neural Networks in Computer Vision Cloud Service
authors: K Ganapathi, R Manasa, Ashraf Shaqadan, Srilakshmi Aluri, Maranan Ramya, Vijaya Vardan Reddy S. P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The efficiently determines the task’s location by examining various factors such as the deadline time, resource availability, and communication delay. It makes a choice between local edge servers or the cloud. This paper introduces the ETSECC model, which stands for Efficient Task Execution and Secure Collaboration in Cloud. It is a model framework that enables efficient collaboration between local and cloud computing for faster task execution, system monitoring in real-time, and data security management for resource-constrained environments. The two-architecture system processes sensor, image, and system data through either cloud or edge pipelines depending on network conditions. Cloud processing refers to the scenario when data from Autonomous Underwater Vehicle (AUVs) is sent to a remote location, which results in longer response times, whereas edge processing diminishes delays and improves stability by processing locally. A Task Execution Model (TEM) smartly outlines how to allocate tasks between the cloud data centers and the edge servers by taking into account the factors of execution times and resource availability, in real-time, to meet the deadlines effectively. The simulation analysis comprises prediction accuracy, delay, remaining battery, task processing time, and average processing cost calculations.

title: Next-Generation Sensor Networks for Material Moisture Measurement: Wired and Cordless Solutions
authors: Palvan Kalandarov, Rashid Nasimov

title:
Study on a TM
                    <sub>220</sub>
                    ModeOutput Cavity for X-Band Multibeam Klystron and Its ModeStability
authors: Yuan Liang, Honghong Gu, Bin Shen, Dengfeng Lu, Xin Guo, Haibing Ding
abstract: This article focuses on the design and stability study of the TM<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{{220}}~2\pi $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-mode of the double-gap output cavity of the multiple-beam klystron, which consists of the four fundamental mode subcavities with seven beam-tunnels inside each of them. First, based on the 3-D electromagnetic simulation, the basic designs of the output cavity, mode converter, and output system were completed, and the specific cold test method for the gap impedance was provided. To address the complex mode oscillation problem introduced by the high-order mode scheme, combined with particle-in-cell (PIC) simulation calculations, an absorber combination scheme was proposed to suppress or attenuate the oscillation modes. The klystron integrated with this output circuit was tested. During the hot testing, the stable power output and on-site spectral data demonstrated the effectiveness of the output cavity’s mode stability design.

title: SMS-EMOA-Driven Multiobjective Design-Technology Co-Optimization for 5-nm Gate-All-Around Transistors
authors: Hongwei Yao, Xinyu Zou, Xingyu Shang, Jianing Zhang, Xiaojin Li, Yang Shen, Ziyu Liu, Bingyi Ye, Yuhang Zhang, Yanling Shi, Yabin Sun
abstract: Due to the complexity of physical effects and second-order effects, optimizing the electrical properties of sub-5-nm devices is exceptionally challenging. This article presents an S-Metric Selection Evolutionary Multiobjective Optimization Algorithm (SMS-EMOA)-driven Design-Technology Co-Optimization (DTCO) framework. By uniquely integrating a lightweight performance prediction surrogate model, it efficiently identifies Pareto fronts under strict manufacturing constraints. This achieves a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$62\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> speedup and reduces peak memory usage from 12GB to ~200 MB compared to direct technology computer-aided design (TCAD). Furthermore, artificial neural network (ANN)-based compact models can be automatically generated from <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$I$</tex-math> </inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$C$</tex-math> </inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V$</tex-math> </inline-formula> data for fast circuit-level verification. Considering back-end interconnect parasitics, DTCO is performed on a layout-aware 97-stage NAND2 ring oscillator. Compared with random baselines, the optimized device achieves up to 34% lower power and 65% higher performance, while delivering a > 25% improvement in SRAM Static Noise Margin (SNM) for robust stability. This framework establishes a tight, scalable connection across process, device, layout, and circuit co-optimization.

title: Learn to Solve Constrained Routing Problems in V2V Social Vehicle Networks
authors: Wei He, Junliang Ye, Lijun Wang, Xiaohu Ge, Tao Han
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: In vehicle social networks (VSN), multi-hop vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) routing must minimize end-to-end delay while satisfying a social trust budget. This trust-constrained routing can be formulated as an NP-hard resource-constrained shortest path problem (RCSPP), while the per-link delay is analytically intractable because the global co-channel interference is unknown. To this end, this paper proposes a proactive infeasibility pruning (PIP) mechanism within a constrained Markov decision process (CMDP) framework. PIP constructs a budget-consistent action mask from a shortest-path-to-go prior, providing a conditional guarantee that any policy respecting the mask produces trust-feasible paths. The empirical conditioning rate of this guarantee matches the feasibility ceiling of an exact label-setting (LS) oracle to within 0.8 percentage points across the full trust-budget range. A graph neural network delay surrogate learns the interference-inclusive delay from local observations. System-level evaluation on the Wuhan Optics Valley, China, traffic demonstrates that the proposed method approaches the routing feasibility upper bound and delivers 5.3× more packets than the deep reinforcement learning baselines using only local one-hop information.

title: Interactive Residual Domain Adaptation Networks for Partial Transfer Industrial Fault Diagnosis
authors: Gecheng Chen, Zheng Luo, Zeyu Yang, Kai Wang, Xinkai Chen, Jianqiang Li, Chengwen Luo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: The partial domain adaptation (PDA) challenge is a prevalent issue in industrial fault diagnosis. Current PDA approaches primarily rely on adversarial learning for domain adaptation and use reweighting strategies to exclude source samples deemed outliers. However, the transferability of features diminishes from general feature extraction layers to higher task-specific layers in adversarial learning-based adaptation modules, leading to significant negative transfer in PDA settings. We term this issue the adaptation-discrimination paradox (ADP). Furthermore, reweighting strategies often suffer from unreliable pseudo-labels, compromising their effectiveness. In this work, we propose a novel PDA framework called Interactive Residual Domain Adaptation Networks (IRDAN), which introduces domain-wise models for each domain to provide a new perspective for the PDA challenge. Each domain-wise model is equipped with a residual domain adaptation (RDA) block to preserve the discriminative structure of each domain and mitigate the ADP. Additionally, we introduce a confident information flow via an interactive learning strategy, training the modules of IRDAN sequentially to avoid cross-interference. We also establish a reliable stopping criterion for selecting the best-performing model, ensuring practical usability in real-world applications. Experiments have demonstrated the superior performance of the proposed IRDAN.

title: Stacking Multi-Variate Time-Frequency Information: A Deep Learning Approach for IMU-MMG based Hand Gesture Recognition
authors: Sadman Rahman, Md. Mehedi Hasan Bhuiyan, Nasiruddin Mahmud Himel, Rishi Raj Sharma, Kazi Newaj Faisal
abstract: Natural and reliable hand gesture recognition is becoming a key enabler for intuitive human-computer interaction in internet of things (IoT) environments, especially for wearable and assistive systems where camera-based sensing is unsuitable. This paper presents an inertial measurement unit (IMU) based framework that converts raw tri-axial accelerometer and gyroscope signals into structured time-frequency images and classifies them using a lightweight convolutional network. First, we propose a stacking-based multivariate time-frequency representation method termed TFR-StackGrid. In this method, the smoothed pseudo Wigner-Ville distribution (SPWVD) is computed for each of the six IMU-MMG channels. The resulting time-frequency maps are then systematically arranged into a fixed 2×3 kinematic grid. This structured configuration preserves the spatial relationship between translational acceleration and rotational motion, thereby integrating multivariate information into a single stacked time-frequency representation, referred to as S-TFR. A compact Gesture Net architecture is then designed to operate directly on these images, combining stacked 2D convolution blocks with a shallow classification head for 11 dynamic hand gesture classes. We conduct a comprehensive evaluation on the large-scale hand gesture accelerometer and gyroscope Dataset (HGAG-DATA), containing 11 gestures, 23,650 samples from 43 participants, and compare seven different time-frequency schemes, including wavelet and other quadratic distributions. The proposed SPWVD-based TFR-StackGrid with Gesture Net achieves 96.21% micro accuracy and 96.28% macro accuracy, with balanced per-class precision, recall, and F1-scores, while relying only on a single wrist-worn IMU based mechanomyography signals. These results demonstrate that structured time-frequency imaging combined with a lightweight convolutional backbone can effectively mitigate the limitations of template matching, handcrafted feature pipelines, and heavier deep sequence models for practical IMU-MMG-based hand gesture recognition.

title: Rethinking the Role of Vector Quantization for Blind Image Restoration_supp1-3688406.pdf
authors: Jun Xu
abstract: Vector-Quantization (VQ) generative models like VQGAN have been widely employed in blind image restoration (BIR) methods to learn powerful high-quality (HQ) priors. However, the inconsistency between the generation-oriented VQ and the reconstruction-oriented BIR tasks would lead to suboptimal restoration performance, which has rarely been discussed. In this paper, we first investigate the role of VQ in VQ-based BIR methods and demonstrate that the HQ feature extracted from the HQ image achieves much better reconstruction results than the correspondingly VQ-quantized HQ feature. This inspires us to use the HQ feature as a better learning target than the VQ-quantized feature, which potentially improve the restoration of low-quality (LQ) images. For flexible approximation of the HQ feature, we propose a Vector Regression (VR) module to learn regression weights over the HQ codebook, providing more accurate feature representation than the VQ operation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our VR module consistently improves four VQ-based BIR methods on different BIR tasks. The code and pretrained models will be publicly released.

title: A Broadband Hybrid Rogowski Coil Sensor Based on Comparative Study of Operating Modes for Switching Transient Measurement
authors: Lisong Wang, Fujie Li, Yongliang Han, Yuhang Ma, Zhongqiang Hu, Zhiguang Wang, Ming Liu
abstract: Rogowski coil sensors, characterized by high bandwidth, non-saturating behavior, and non-invasive measurement, have become essential mainstream devices for the development, testing, and application of wide-bandgap semiconductor power devices. The simultaneous optimization of their low-frequency and high-frequency cut-off characteristics requires a systematic engineering approach that regards the coil and integrator as an integrated measurement chain. This paper presents a comparative analysis of three typical operating modes of Rogowski coils—differential mode, self-integrating mode, and transmission line mode—regarding bandwidth, phase response, output voltage, and other key performance indicators. A hybrid Rogowski coil sensor is then proposed, combining the high-output advantage of the differential mode with the high-bandwidth merit of the self-integrating mode, achieving an extended operating bandwidth from 6.8 kHz to 485.5 MHz. Finally, the performance of the sensor prototype is verified through double-pulse tests, which validate its ability to accurately capture the switching current waveforms of SiC MOSFETs.

title: Early Thermal Runaway Warning in Solid-State Batteries: A Dual-Sensing Fusion Framework
authors: Haoyi Yu, Jun Zhao, Ziliang Zhao, Bin Guo, Peiyu Zhang
abstract: As the safety demands of electrified transportation escalate, solid-state batteries (SSBs) are emerging to replace traditional lithium-ion batteries (LIBs), yet their thermal runaway (TR) mechanisms remain poorly understood. Current research on TR warning focuses on LIBs and relies on contact-based electrical methods, most notably electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS). Theoretically, the fixed perturbation signal’s repeated calculation within EIS causes information redundancy since only the response varies. Practically, the methods fail at high temperatures due to interfacial impedance heterogeneity in SSBs. To overcome these bottlenecks, this paper proposes a novel dual-stage TR warning framework fusing contact-based electrical and non-contact magnetic sensing. Stage 1 implements online hardware assessing SSBs’ response to a 1 kHz perturbation, extracting novel time-frequency indicators to eliminate computational redundancy, achieving a 9-minute earlier TR warning than conventional assessment. Stage 2 deploys magnetic monitoring for explosion precursors, providing 20-second advance warning.

title: Advanced 3-ω Method for Evaluating Anisotropic Thermal Conductivity in Carbon Nanotubes
authors: T.-W. Chen, M.-H. Liao, C.-W. Yao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: As the limitations of Moore’s law become increasingly apparent, three-dimensional integrated circuits (3-DICs) have emerged as a viable solution to sustain the scaling and performance of semiconductor devices. However, the vertical integration of components in 3-DICs introduces significant thermal management challenges due to increased power density and restricted heat dissipation pathways. Carbon nanotubes (CNTs) have been proposed as a promising alternative to copper for filling through silicon vias (TSVs), owing to their superior thermal conductivity, electrical performance, and mechanical stability. Notably, CNTs exhibit anisotropic thermal transport properties, which can be exploited to enhance heat dissipation efficiency in both the cross-plane and in-plane directions within 3-DIC architectures. Despite their potential, limited research has focused on characterizing the directional thermal behavior of CNT films. In this study, we present a refined measurement technique based on the conventional third harmonic (3-ω) method, specifically designed to quantify the thermal conductivity of CNT films along both principal directions. The methodology employs two metal lines, one functioning as a heater and the other as a temperature probe, to detect thermal fluctuations and facilitate accurate κ-value extraction. This work offers a comprehensive framework encompassing the measurement principle, fabrication process flow, and experimental design, thereby contributing to a deeper understanding of CNTs’ anisotropic thermal characteristics and their applicability in advanced microelectronic systems.

title: AaSP: Aliasing-aware Self-Supervised Pre-Training for Audio Spectrogram Transformers
authors: Kohei Yamamoto, Kosuke Okusa
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: Transformer-based audio self-supervised learning (SSL) models often operate on spectrogram-based time-frequency representations and apply vision-style Transformer architectures (e.g., ViT-style) together with masked modeling objectives (e.g., MAE/MIM-style). Typically, they use convolutional patchification with temporal downsampling. This lowers the effective Nyquist frequency and introduces aliasing, while naïve low-pass filtering removes task-relevant high-frequency cues. In this study, we present AaSP, an aliasing-aware self-supervised pre-training framework for audio spectrogram transformers. AaSP combines an aliasing-aware patch representation, teacher-student masked modeling, a cross-attention predictor, and multi-mask contrastive regularization to learn representations that can better integrate features derived from alias-prone modulation bands while remaining more stable across masked views. Its patch-embedding component, Aliasing-aware Patch Embedding (AaPE), augments standard patch tokens with features derived from alias-prone modulation bands using a band-limited complex sinusoidal kernel with a two-sided exponential window. The kernel's frequency and decay parameters are estimated from the input, enabling adaptive subband analysis whose outputs are fused with the standard patch tokens. These components encourage consistency across masked views, which appears important for realizing the empirical benefit of the aliasing-aware patch representation. We pre-train on AudioSet and evaluate the learned representations via fine-tuning and linear evaluation on downstream benchmarks covering general acoustic/environmental, speech-related, and music-related audio recognition tasks. Under fine-tuning, the full AaSP framework achieves state-of-the-art results on AS-20K, ESC-50, and NSynth among the compared self-supervised baselines, while remaining competitive on the other benchmarks. Linear evaluation shows a broadly consistent trend, with clear improvements on several benchmarks (e.g., US8K and NSynth). Overall, these results suggest that the full AaSP framework can learn representations that are more stable under aliasing-sensitive temporal perturbations and competitive for downstream transfer.

title: Distilling Large Language Models for Network Active Queue Management_supp1-3690076.pdf
authors: Shiva Raj Pokhrel
abstract: We propose AQM-LLM, a framework that distills Large Language Models for Active Queue Management in modern networks. Unlike conventional learning-based AQMs that require extensive feature engineering and struggle with dynamic conditions, our approach leverages the contextual reasoning capability of LLMs to enhance the Low Latency, Low Loss, and Scalable Throughput (L4S) architecture with minimal manual intervention. The L4S-LLM design introduces three key components: (i) a state encoder that transforms heterogeneous network telemetry into token embeddings, (ii) a specialized L4S-LLM head that produces congestion actions in a single inference step, and (iii) a data-driven Low-Rank Adaptation scheme that drastically reduces trainable parameters while preserving accuracy. Our open-source FreeBSD-14 implementation demonstrates improved queue delay stability and higher bandwidth utilization for both DCTCP and UDP Prague traffic. We emphasize that this work demonstrates architectural feasibility through controlled experiments; deployment on router-class hardware will require additional model optimization (e.g., compression, pruning, or quantization) and is left for future work.

title: A Multiport DC Circuit Breaker With N-1 Security Ability
authors: Yanxun Guo, Tongxin Zhao, Xiaomei Yao, Haifeng Li, Wei Han
abstract: In DC grids, multiple DC circuit breakers (DCCBs) on a DC bus can be replaced by a multiport DCCB (MDCCB) to reduce the investment cost. However, compared with a single DCCB, the MDCCB contains more branches and has a greater probability of branch unavailability; furthermore, its interruption failure caused by branch unavailability leads to more severe consequences. A few studies avoid the interruption failure caused by ultrafast disconnector (UFD) failure, but the interruption failure caused by the semiconductor branch unavailability is rarely considered. This article proposes a novel MDCCB with N-1 security ability, which is applicable for different numbers of ports and can interrupt faults even when any internal branch is unavailable. The working principles under normal condition and branch unavailability conditions are presented. The fault currents of DC grids are calculated to design the parameters of MDCCB. The feasibility of the proposed topology is validated by a small-scale prototype, and the working performance is further validated by simulations in the DC grid. Compared with existing MDCCBs, the proposed MDCCB combines the characteristics of low investment cost and high reliability, which can ensure the safety and economy of DC grids.

title: Geometry-Aware Scene Configurations for Novel View Synthesis
authors: Minkwan Kim, Changwoon Choi, Young Min Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: We propose scene-adaptive strategies to efficiently allocate representation capacity for generating immersive experiences of indoor environments from incomplete observations. Indoor scenes with multiple rooms often exhibit irregular layouts with varying complexity, containing clutter, occlusion, and flat walls. We maximize the utilization of limited resources with guidance from geometric priors, which are often readily available after pre-processing stages. We record observation statistics on the estimated geometric scaffold and guide the optimal placement of bases, which greatly improves upon the uniform basis arrangements adopted by previous scalable scene representations. We also suggest scene-adaptive virtual viewpoints to compensate for geometric deficiencies inherent in view configurations in the input trajectory and impose the necessary regularization. We present a comprehensive analysis and discussion regarding rendering quality and memory requirements in several large-scale indoor scenes, demonstrating significant enhancements compared to baselines that employ regular placements.

title: Gradient Boosting for Automatic Epileptic Seizure Detection in EEG Signals: A Comparative Study with Classical Models
authors: Ignacio D. Lezcano, Edgar H. Ayala-Britez, Diego P. Pinto-Roa
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The automatic detection of epileptic seizures from EEG signals is a clinical and technical necessity for improving monitoring and medical decision-making. The literature has reported on classical machine learning and deep learning approaches, the former being efficient and the latter requiring significant computational resources. In this regard, it is necessary to develop solutions that achieve a good balance between performance and low computational training costs. The objective of this study is to evaluate an ensemble classifier based on Gradient Boosting, comparing it with classic machine learning methods (SVM, KNN, GNB, DT, LDA, and LR) for binary seizure/non-seizure detection on EEG segments in accordance with the technical recommendations of the ILAE. From 10-second segments of raw signals from the CHB-MIT Scalp EEG Database, feature extraction obtained thirty linear temporal features per channel to capture statistical, shape, and dynamic information in the time domain. The models were trained using stratified crossvalidation with optimal hyperparameters. Gradient Boosting achieved the best overall performance, outperforming classical classifiers in all evaluated metrics, with the following results: Accuracy = 0.9793, Precision = 0.9868, Recall = 0.9720, F1-Score = 0.9792, and Specificity (TNR)=0.9866. The results demonstrate that the Gradient Boosting classifier, when tuned and evaluated using stratified procedures, is a promising alternative for the automatic detection of epileptic seizures in EEG signals. These findings support the use of ensemble approaches as a complementary tool in monitoring systems and clinical decision support.

title: Exploiting Label-Independent Regularization from Spatial Patterns for Whole Slide Image Analysis
authors: Weiyi Wu, Xinwen Xu, Chongyang Gao, Xingjian Diao, Siting Li, Jiang Gui
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Whole slide images, with their gigapixel-scale panoramas of tissue samples, are pivotal for precise disease diagnosis. However, their analysis is hindered by immense data size and scarce annotations. Existing MIL methods face challenges due to the fundamental imbalance where a single bag-level label must guide the learning of numerous patch-level features. This sparse supervision makes it difficult to reliably identify discriminative patches during training, leading to unstable optimization and suboptimal solutions. We propose a spatially regularized MIL framework that leverages inherent spatial relationships among patch features as label-independent regularization signals. Our approach learns a shared representation space by jointly optimizing feature-induced spatial reconstruction and label-guided classification objectives, enforcing consistency between intrinsic structural patterns and supervisory signals. Experimental results on multiple public datasets demonstrate significant improvements over state-of-the-art methods, offering a promising direction. The code is available at https://github.com/wwyi1828/SRMIL.

title: Distilling What and Why: Enhancing Driver Intention Prediction with MLLMs
authors: Sainithin Artham, Avijit Dasgupta, Shankar Gangisetty, C. V. Jawahar
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Predicting a drivers’ intent (e.g., turns, lane changes) is a critical capability for modern Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS). While recent Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) show promise in general vision-language tasks, we find that zero-shot MLLMs still lag behind domain-specific approaches for Driver Intention Prediction (DIP). To address this, we introduce DriveXplain, a zero-shot framework based on MLLMs that leverages rich visual cues such as optical flow and road semantics to automatically generate both intention maneuver (what) and rich natural language explanations (why). These maneuver–explanation pairs are then distilled into a compact MLLM, which jointly learns to predict intentions and corresponding explanations. We show that incorporating explanations during training leads to substantial gains over models trained solely on labels, as distilling explanations instills reasoning capabilities by enabling the model to understand not only what decisions to make but also why those decisions are made. Comprehensive experiments across structured (Brain4Cars, AIDE) and unstructured (DAAD) datasets demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art results in DIP task, outperforming zero-shot and domain-specific baselines. We also present ablation studies to evaluate key design choices in our framework. This work sets a direction for more explainable and generalizable intention prediction in autonomous driving systems. Project webpage: https://avijit9.github.io/DriveXplain/

title: Context Meets Modality: Deep Learning Innovations for Next-Generation Recommendation Systems
authors: D.Venkata Subramanian, Bhuvan Unhelkar, S Ramacharan, Prasun Chakrabarti
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The complexity and preferences for users have increased exponentially due to the growth and multi-modality of digital content. Users in the modern world typically deal with multiple data types, including video, audio, text, and images, which are influenced by time, place, and society. The existing techniques, namely collaborative filtering and other methods, were not effective due to the sparsity of data and also the partial awareness of the context. To avoid these drawbacks, it is essential to consider multimodal deep learning-based methods to gain awareness of the given context. New architecture models using Multi-modal approaches aim to handle any type of data sources and also based on personalized preferences of users. The developments in attention mechanisms, large language models, transformers, and graph neural networks provide opportunities for dealing with any domain and enable proper synthesis of semi-structured, structured, and unstructured data. This article aims to explore all the research works and innovations carried out in fusion models, attention, graphs, agentic models, and other related developments. In addition to the developments in the frameworks, this paper also gathers information about the explainability, trustworthiness, and fairness of the works carried out by the researchers. The third part of this work is to highlight the summary of the methods and future directions for the frameworks that can support real-time reasoning with fairness so that multimodal data can be handled along with plain text.

title: A Study on Maternal and Child Health Monitoring system using IoT and Machine Learning
authors: Sunitha Tappari, N. Sreshta, Medha Sree Aare, Rakshitha Bheemanadhuni, A. Hinduja Reddy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Pregnancy is a critical period, and the timely detection of physiological changes helps in the early prevention of complications. Wearable sensors measure essential parameters like heart rate, temperature, and SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, transmitting the data to the ThingSpeak cloud via Wi-Fi. It is analyzed for the differentiation of normal and abnormal conditions via a Random Forest algorithm, which generates alerts in case of deviations. This allows for early intervention, reducing risks, and enhancing maternal safety. It is low cost, compact, and user-friendly, thus allowing home-based monitoring without repeated visits to the hospital. It supports real-time visualization, record storage, and remote sharing of data with healthcare providers through cloud integration. Prototype testing confirms correct sensor readings and reliable ML predictions.

title: Rethinking Real Image Editing: Unleashing Diverse Editing Operators via Multi-Objective Optimization
authors: Siyuan Wang, Xi Yang, Zihao Zhou, Huiru Shao, Rui Zhang, Qiufeng Wang, Guangliang Cheng, Kaizhu Huang
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Text-conditioned diffusion models have revolutionized the field of controllable real image editing, enabling high-fidelity and precise image manipulation. Recent methods target specific editing tasks, using internal representations from reconstruction to ensure consistency. Although effective for single tasks, they fail to balance precision and consistency across diverse image editing tasks. In this work, we propose a novel inference-time real-image editing framework that enables executing multiple editing tasks by tuning editing operators. Our key insight is to treat real image editing as a multi-objective optimization problem, optimizing editing operators for a Pareto optimal solution that balances editing accuracy and consistency at each denoising iteration. Additionally, we design a benchmark for operator-guided real-image editing that covers various local and global editing tasks. Extensive experimental evaluations demonstrate the method’s effectiveness in executing precise edits while preserving image fidelity across all tasks, thereby establishing it as the new state-of-the-art.

title: Parameter Optimization of a Magnetic Fault Current Limiter Using Biogeography-Based Optimization
authors: Tirtha Sankar Daphadar, Tapan Santra, Amalendu Bikash Choudhury
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: This paper presents an optimization study of a permanent-magnet biased fault current limiter (PMFCL) using the Biogeography-Based Optimization (BBO) algorithm. With the increasing expense of magnetic cores and rare-earth permanent magnets, reducing material usage while maintaining reliable fault suppression has become essential. In this work, the PMFCL design parameters are optimized through BBO to minimize device volume and improve performance. The optimization framework addresses three key objectives: (i) minimizing the physical size,(ii) reducing the first fault-current peak, and (iii) limiting voltage drop during normal operation. A prototype was constructed based on the optimized parameters, and its performance was validated experimentally. Both simulation and test results confirm that the BBO-optimized PMFCL provides effective fault current limitation while ensuring acceptable voltage regulation in steady state.

title: A Market Monitoring Framework for European Broadband Using PPP-Adjusted Segmentation and Transition Tracking
authors: Ivan Jajić
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: This paper proposes a data-driven framework for segmenting European fixed broadband markets using purchasing power parity (PPP)-adjusted prices. The analysis focuses on the least expensive fixed broadband offers in 27 European countries for 2019 and 2023, based on Eurostat data. The aim is to identify stable and changing affordability segments, rather than to analyse any single country in detail. Methodologically, the paper combines descriptive statistics, k-means clustering with internal validation indices, an analysis of cluster transitions and repeatedmeasures inference, complemented by a country fixed-effects model. The results indicate a substantial decline in PPP-adjusted prices and a persistent, yet partially mobile, segmentation of the market into cheaper and more expensive groups of countries. In both clusters, the 2023 centroids are lower than in 2019, while the internal validation indices suggest clearer separation between clusters in the later year. A transition matrix shows that around two-thirds of countries remain in the same cluster, whereas the remaining third move between segments. The paper demonstrates how relatively simple clustering and panel techniques can be effectively integrated into a business intelligence framework for monitoring price-based market segments over time.

title: See, Record, Do: Automated Generation of UI Workflows from Tutorial Videos
authors: Adam Beauchaine, Craig Shue
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: System designers and developers need data-driven approaches for user-interface (UI) development and testing. They need trace-based workflows to support UI navigation agents and inputs for UI code generation. Given the high production costs of manually constructing such workflows, the UI agent research community has explored automated, fully-synthetic UI workflow generation [16]. However, there is an open need to characterize the fidelity and effectiveness of these synthetic approaches with respect to the current manual approaches.In this work, we aim to provide larger-scale synthetic workflows based on past human usage. We particularly focused on the desktop application modality since prior synthetic generation has mostly targeted mobile or web applications. Using video tutorials with permissive licenses, we derive associated UI behaviors to construct synthetic dataset of UI workflows in desktop applications. We provide data from these videos to a large language model (LLM) to generate a set of "task list" instructions that replicate the videos’ actions. We execute the task list instructions in a UI agent within an instrumented desktop operating system. Using the sensor data from that environment, we can enable simpler actuation scripts for replay of the tasks. Along with detailing our approach, we provide a dataset with over 5,000 workflows across a range of desktop applications with costs that are significantly lower than manual construction.

title: SOAF: Scene Occlusion-aware Neural Acoustic Field
authors: Huiyu Gao, Jiahao Ma, David Ahmedt-Aristizabal, Chuong Nguyen, Miaomiao Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: This paper tackles the problem of novel view acoustic synthesis along an arbitrary trajectory in an indoor scene, given the audio-video recordings from other known trajectories of the scene. Existing methods often overlook the effect of room geometry, particularly wall occlusions on sound propagation, making them less accurate in multi-room environments. In this work, we propose a new approach called Scene Occlusion-aware Acoustic Field (SOAF) for accurate sound generation. Our approach derives a global prior for the sound field learning through distance-aware parametric sound propagation modeling and then transforms it based on the scene structure learned from the input video. We extract features from the local acoustic field centered at the receiver using a Fibonacci Sphere to generate binaural audio for novel views with a direction-aware attention mechanism. Extensive experiments on the real dataset RWAVS and the synthetic dataset SoundSpaces demonstrate that our method achieves superior performance in spatial audio generation.

title: Training-Free Few-Shot Segmentation via Vision-Language Guided Prompting
authors: Euihyun Yoon, Taejin Park, Jaekoo Lee
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Object segmentation relies heavily on costly pixel-level annotations and struggles to generalize to unseen domains. The recent introduction of the Segment Anything Model (SAM), a foundation model for segmentation, offers a prompt-driven, zero-shot capability that has been applied in various domains (e.g., autonomous driving, satellite imagery, medical imaging) and extended to Few-Shot Segmentation (FSS) tasks. However, existing SAM-based FSS methods typically generate prompts by using a vision encoder to measure support–query image similarity, which often biases towards the support images and fails when there are significant support–query context shifts. To address this limitation, we propose a training-free FSS approach that combines visual and textual cues to generate effective prompts for the target class. By leveraging both vision and language information, our approach bridges the support–query gap and guides SAM to segment novel objects more reliably. Without any additional training, our method outperforms previous state-of-the-art FSS methods on established benchmarks (COCO-20<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</sup>, Pascal-5<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</sup>), demonstrating its effectiveness and robust generalization. Our code is publicly available on GitHub.

title: AEON: Adaptive Embedding Optimized Noise for Robust Watermarking in Diffusion Models
authors: Muhammad Shahid Muneer, Simon S. Woo
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: The widespread use of synthetic image generation models and the challenges associated with authenticity preservation have fueled the demand for robust watermarking methods to safeguard authenticity and protect the copyright of synthetic images. Existing watermarking methods embed. Invisible signatures in synthetic images often compromise image quality and remain susceptible to multiple watermark removal attacks, including reconstruction and forgery methods. To overcome this issue, we propose a novel watermarking approach, AEON, which seamlessly integrates the watermark into the latent diffusion process and ensures the watermark aligns with scene semantics in the final image. Unlike existing invisible in-diffusion watermarking and traditional hash-based methods, our approach adapts the neural synthesized hash-based watermark to the semantics of the generated image during the intermediate diffusion process instead of embedding traditional hashes with the initial noise. Our proposed approach a) modulates the noise sampling in each diffusion denoising iteration through a learnable watermark embedding, b) optimizes consistency, reconstruction, and similarity loss, enforcing local and global alignment between the watermark structure and the underlying image content, and c) generates a strong watermark by allowing late embedding of the watermark in the diffusion process. Empirical results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach in retaining quality and its robustness against cumulative adversarial attacks.

title: GeneVA: A Dataset of Human Annotations for Generative Text to Video Artifacts
authors: Jenna Kang, Maria Beatriz Silva, Patsorn Sangkloy, Kenneth Chen, Niall L. Williams, Qi Sun
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Video generated by the current state-of-the-art generative models contain undesirable artifacts. We introduce GeneVA, the first large-scale dataset of human-annotated artifact bounding boxes in AI-generated videos. The dataset consists of 16,356 AI-generated videos, each labeled by a human annotator with per-frame artifact bounding boxes, their labels and descriptions, and video quality ratings. A custom data collection pipeline was developed in Prolific, and a novel taxonomy for spatio-temporal artifacts present in AI-generated videos was defined. The videos were from the VidProM [41] dataset, with text prompts from this dataset then used to generate an additional subset of videos using Sora. We trained an artifact detector and caption generator using a pre-trained image-based model, and a custom temporal fusion module. The dataset can be found at https://www.immersivecomputinglab.org/publication/geneva. We hope that datasets like GeneVA will encourage improvements in artifact detection in AI-generated video towards applications such as deepfake detection.

title: A Hybrid Transformer Framework for Text-Visual Thyroid Nodule Segmentation in Ultrasound Diagnostics
authors: Hussainbi Shaik, Hussain Syed
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Correct segmentation of thyroid nodules in US images is crucial for accurate diagnosis and treatment planning. However, the performance of most current methods is suboptimal in terms of both high accuracy, and clear interpretability and generalization. In this work, we present Text-Vision Aligned Transformer (TVAT), a novel multimodal segmentation framework that combines state-of-the-art visual– language model and a neural architecture for addressing these challenges. The proposed methodology splits visual information into Broad-Scale and Detail-Scale sets of features. Text cues are combinational optimized with large-scale information to help reinforce semantic recognition. The broad-Type Extractor harvests abundant context features from a fixed vision– language backbone, and the detail- Type Refiner raises spatial accuracy by improving with residual U-Net fashion blocks. Experiments on our newly collected PKTNS dataset and other public datasets demonstrate that TVAT achieves competitive performance. Additional ablation studies reinforce the importance of text guidance in performance, especially when it is adaptive instead of fixed or absent prompts.

title: Deep Learning Approaches for Predicting Attack Vulnerabilities in Quantum-Secure Financial Networks
authors: Saurabh Kakkar, Sathiyaviradhan Janarthanan, Bhulakshmi Makkena, Armaan Kakkar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: In the age of quantum communication and 6G, the safety of financial networks has become a more important concern as cyber threats become more sophisticated. This study examines how deep learning models can be used to improve vulnerability detection and prediction in quantum-secure financial settings. Based on the Quantum-Secure 6G Slicing Dataset, which combines the latest encryption methods, including ECC, AES, and QKD, the study employs preprocessing steps— data cleaning, normalizatio, label encoding, and SMOTE-based balancing to ensure optimal model performance. The suggested ANN was compared with other deep learning and machine learning methods, including Decision Trees (DTs), Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), and Logistic Regression (LR). The results of the experiments were found to be the highest with ANN, the accuracy, 92.25, precision, 89.58, recall, 93.99, and F1-score, 91.73, which indicates that the ANN outperforms the existing models. These findings indicate that the ANN will be a powerful and robust method for detecting fraud and forecasting attack vulnerability in quantum-secure financial networks, as it implies that the ANN is an effective way to capture complex nonlinear dependencies and achieve better generalization and predictive stability.

title: SV3.3B: A Sports Video Understanding Model for Action Recognition
authors: Sai Varun Kodathala, Yashwanth Reddy Vutukoori, Rakesh Vunnam
publish: 2025 Conference on AI, Science, Engineering, and Technology (AIxSET)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of automated sports video analysis, which has traditionally been limited by computationally intensive models requiring server-side processing and lacking fine-grained understanding of athletic movements. Current approaches struggle to capture the nuanced biomechanical transitions essential for meaningful sports analysis, often missing critical phases like preparation, execution, and followthrough that occur within seconds. To address these limitations, we introduce SV3.3B, a lightweight <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3. 3 B}$</tex> parameter video understanding model that combines temporal motion difference sampling with self-supervised learning for efficient on device deployment. Our approach employs a DWT-VGG16-LDA based keyframe extraction mechanism that intelligently identifies the 16 most representative frames from sports sequences, followed by a V-DWT-JEPA2 encoder pretrained through mask-denoising objectives and an LLM decoder fine-tuned for sports action description generation. Evaluated on a subset of the NSVA basketball dataset, SV3.3B achieves superior performance across both traditional text generation metrics and sports-specific evaluation criteria, outperforming larger closed-source models including GPT-4o variants while maintaining significantly lower computational requirements. Our model demonstrates exceptional capability in generating technically detailed and analytically rich sports descriptions, achieving improvement over GPT-4o in ground truth validation metrics, with substantial improvements in information density, action complexity, and measurement precision metrics essential for comprehensive athletic analysis.

title: ObjectMeshDeform : Towards recovering precise 3D geometry of real objects via image-guided mesh deformation of 3D generative priors
authors: Siddharth Katageri, Sanjana Sinha, Sourav Ghosh, Soumyadip Maity, Brojeshwar Bhowmick
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: 3D Generative Models that synthesize high fidelity 3D assets from single view or multi-view images cannot recover precise 3D geometry and dimensions of real-world objects, which is often required by practical applications. On the other hand, multi-view 3D reconstruction methods based on structure from motion, implicit surfaces, gaussian splatting, fail to recover high fidelity object meshes with dense geometry, shape regularity, smooth surfaces present in real world objects. In this paper we propose a novel approach that leverages a 3D mesh prior synthesized by generative models pre-trained on large scale 3D synthetic datasets. Our method automatically refines the 3D geometry of the generated meshes for improved geometric precision using only sparse multi-view input images, while maintaining the geometric fidelity of the generated prior meshes. Our method can automatically reconstruct meshes from images of real world objects, without requiring any additional large-scale training data or manual inputs for shape deformation.

title: Anatomy-VLM: A Fine-grained Vision-Language Model for Medical Interpretation
authors: Difei Gu, Yunhe Gao, Mu Zhou, Dimitris Metaxas
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Accurate disease interpretation from radiology remains challenging due to imaging heterogeneity. Achieving expert-level diagnostic decisions requires integration of subtle image features with clinical knowledge. Yet major vision-language models (VLMs) treat images as holistic entities and overlook fine-grained image details that are vital for disease diagnosis. Clinicians analyze images by utilizing their prior medical knowledge and identify anatomical structures as important region of interests (ROIs). Inspired from this human-centric workflow, we introduce Anatomy-VLM, a fine-grained, vision-language model that incorporates multi-scale information. First, we design a model encoder to localize key anatomical features from entire medical images. Second, these regions are enriched with structured knowledge for contextually-aware interpretation. Finally, the model encoder aligns multi-scale medical information to generate clinically-interpretable disease prediction. Anatomy-VLM achieves outstanding performance on both in- and out-of-distribution datasets. We also validate the performance of Anatomy-VLM on downstream image segmentation tasks, suggesting that its fine-grained alignment captures anatomical and pathology-related knowledge.
