abstract: Selection of features is a process involving the enhancement of the quality and effectiveness of machine learning systems especially in the case of high dimensional data. The article is a description of a hybrid feature selection methodology that combines Genetic Algorithms (GA) and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) to locate the most optimal combination of features in solving classification problems. The suggested method utilizes the global exploration power of GA and high-speed convergence ability of PSO. Studies were carried on various benchmark datasets in order to find out its effectiveness and compare it with singly applied GA, PSO, and traditional feature selection algorithms. The findings indicate that the hybrid approach of using GA-PSO is highly beneficial in increasing the accuracy of classification and minimizing the selected characteristics. Moreover, it is also repeatable over a number of classifiers, which shows that it can be used in medical diagnosis, finance, and text classification.

title: DocMind: Conversational PDF QA with RAG
authors: B J Rakshitha, Renil Dharuni, Likhitha M, S Lakshmi, V Ravi Kumar
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: Traditional keyword searches offer no understa–nding of the semantics of the documents being searched, and Large Language Models (LLMs) are prone to hallucinations, making the extraction of relevant information from large PDF documents problematic. In this paper, we present DocMind, which is a framework for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for conversational question answering on PDF documents. The system combines semantic document parsing with token-based chunking, sentence-transformer embeddings, and HNSW-indexed vector retrieval. To find the relevant context for a document chunk, a user query is embedded in the same vector space, and the document chunk is provided to an LLM for generating a cohesive answer. DocMind, unlike standard RAG systems, has multi-turn conversational memory and a transparency feature that indicates when an answer is not justified by the document the user uploaded. Our experiments show DocMind outperforms BM25 and TF-IDF by a large margin in the BERT F1 score, ROUGE-L, and the faithfulness metric. DocMind, by semantic retrieval, combines controlled generation to enhance information accessibility and reduce hallucinations while providing reliable, transparent, and domain-agnostic question answering.

title: DreamCatcher: Efficient Multi-Concept Customization via Representation Finetuning
authors: Jungwon Lee, Changhun Lee, Eunhyeok Park
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Recent advances in customizing Text-to-Image models allow users to generate personalized images with just a few samples. As demand for multi-concept generation grows, methods using weight fusion and test-time optimization have emerged, integrating multiple concepts within a single image. However, these approaches inject concept knowledge into the parametric space, leading to high overhead in multi-concept generation. We introduce DreamCatcher, an efficient framework based on representation finetuning. Our key innovation embeds conceptual information into the feature space, achieving up to 5× faster multi-concept generation while reducing learnable storage per concept by 88%, all without quality loss. Besides, our method is highly versatile, enabling personalized inpainting without additional training.

title: WaveletMixer: When Wavelet Transform Meets Self-Attention in Single Image Super-Resolution
authors: Qibin Zhang, Licheng Liu, Tingyun Liu, Lin Ma, Yaonan Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Transformer-based methods have shown strong potential for single image super-resolution (SISR) due to their ability to model long-range dependencies. However, most existing approaches primarily focus on global representation learning while underexploring high-frequency priors that are crucial for recovering fine textures and sharp edges. In this work, we present a wavelet-transform-based analysis showing that transformers are effective at modeling low-frequency structures but comparatively less effective than convolutional neural networks (CNNs) in recovering high-frequency details. Motivated by this observation, we propose WaveletMixer, a novel SISR framework that integrates wavelet decomposition into feature learning to exploit the complementary strengths of transformers and CNNs in the frequency domain. WaveletMixer adopts a dual-branch architecture for low- and high-frequency wavelet feature extraction, introduces Wavelet Channel Attention (WCA) to adaptively emphasize informative wavelet subbands, and employs Deformable Cross Attention (DCA) for cross-wavelet subbands interaction and fusion. In addition, an Omni Channel Attention (Omni-CA) module is designed to enhance spatial-channel feature aggregation through multi-axis interaction. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate that WaveletMixer consistently outperforms several state-of-the-art methods and achieves a favorable trade-off between reconstruction performance and model complexity.

title: A Class of Single-Sensor Balancing Techniques for Flying-Capacitor Odd-Level Converters
authors: Siddharth Iyer, Sayan Paul, Luca Corradini
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Flying-capacitor odd-level (FCOL) converters are prone to unbalanced operation in the presence of non-idealities such as timing mismatches, requiring active closed-loop balancing of the flying-capacitor (FC) voltages. In this work, a class of low-complexity balancing techniques for FCOL converters presented, in which balancing is achieved by only sensing the innermost FC voltage regardless of the number of levels. Balancing is achieved using phase shift modulation via a single balancing loop based on a standard proportional-integral (PI) controller, making the approach especially attractive for low-cost applications based on custom-designed analog or digital ICs. A novel design approach, based on deliberate mismatching of the flying capacitors, is also introduced and shown to improve the closed-loop dynamics of the balancing loop and of the unregulated FC voltages. Simulation and experimental results are carried out on 5, 7, and 9-Level converter case studies to validate the approach.

title: Ambiguity-Free Ranging Beyond Coherence Length with Quasi-CW Multi-Tone LiDAR
authors: Ataberk Atalar, Ozan Berk Boyraz, Xun Li, Abdelrahman Abdelhameed, Jim Phillips, Arash Fereidouni, Ozdal Boyraz
abstract: Phase-based multi-tone continuous-wave (PB-MTCW) coherent LiDAR enables precision ranging beyond the coherence length of the laser by using inter-tone phase differences that reject common phase noise, while simultaneously providing velocimetry through Doppler shifts. However, the measured distance is inherently periodic, leading to ambiguity for targets located beyond the repeat length set by the RF tone spacing. In this paper, we propose and demonstrate a quasi-continuous-wave (QCW) PB-MTCW architecture that resolves this ambiguity by pulsing the CW multi-tone waveform to obtain a coarse time-of-flight estimate while preserving the fine precision of phase-based ranging in kilometer-scale distances. Experimentally, it is shown that stationary targets at an optical path length of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim \text{1.8}~\text{km}$</tex-math></inline-formula> are correctly disambiguated, both multi-meter and centimeter-scale displacements are resolved with <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim \text{3}~\text{mm}$</tex-math></inline-formula> average precision. Moreover, dynamic target measurements provide simultaneous absolute range and velocity vector with on average <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim \text{2.5}~\text{mm}$</tex-math></inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim 3.82~\mathrm{mm/s}$</tex-math></inline-formula> error.

title: Decentralized Learning-Driven AoI Optimization in UAV-Assisted Wireless Powered Edge Networks_supp1-3688661.pdf
authors: Zhaolong Ning
abstract: Studies on the Age of Information (AoI) in Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV)-assisted wireless powered edge networks have attracted increasing attention. To minimize the AoI, it is necessary to jointly schedule UAVs and sensors. However, the high-dimensional state space arising from the joint scheduling of multi-dimensional resources poses challenges to the effectiveness and convergence of conventional Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) methods in edge networks. Therefore, we propose a decentralized learning algorithm to jointly optimize UAV trajectories, charging time allocation, association and transmission scheduling of sensors in UAV-assisted wireless powered edge networks. First, by incorporating both UAV time constraints and sensor energy constraints, we formulate the AoI minimization problem. Second, we decompose it into two interdependent subproblems, each modeled as a decentralized partially observable Markov decision process. To solve these subproblems, we design an improved decentralized multi-agent DRL algorithm based on a dual-network sequential processing architecture and value function factorization, which fits the relationship between local and global action values and facilitates cooperation among agents. Finally, we derive the theoretical lower bound of average AoI, and extensive simulation results show that the proposed algorithm outperforms the other five representative algorithms.

title: An Overview of Fluid Antenna Systems: Fundamentals, Performance Analysis and Challenges
authors: Tianwei Hou, Jiangsheng Huangfu, Zhengyu Song, Anna Li, Xin Sun, Arumugam Nallanathan
abstract: The upcoming sixth-generation (6G) is envisioned to transform wireless communications through groundbreaking performance targets that far exceed previous generations. Among the emerging technologies, the fluid antenna system (FAS) has gained significant attention as a flexible and reconfigurable antenna architecture. FAS can dynamically alter the position and shape of the radiating elements to exploit additional spatial degrees of freedom. In this paper, a comprehensive overview of FAS is first provided, including its fundamental principles and implementation methods. Then, the impact of key system parameters— such as the number of ports, the physical size of FAS, and the channel environment—on the performance of FAS is thoroughly investigated. Finally, open research challenges and future directions are highlighted for integrating FAS with other advanced 6G technologies. Ultimately, this paper aims to serve as a foundational reference for researchers seeking to unlock the full potential of fluid antennas in 6G wireless systems.

title: 2025 Conference on AI, Science, Engineering, and Technology (AIxSET)
authors: N/A

title: Energy-efficient Data Offloading for Ultra-dense Heterogeneous Vehicular Networks: A Multi-Population Mean-Field Reinforcement Learning Approach
authors: Fei Song, Zhe Wang, Jun Li, Long Shi, Yiyang Ni, Shi Jin
abstract: For ultra-dense vehicular networks, dynamic resource optimization among a large number of heterogeneous agents is rather challenging. This paper proposes a learning-based resource allocation scheme in a vehicle-assisted mobile edge computing (MEC) network, where the uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs) and uncrewed ground vehicles (UGVs) are equipped with the MEC servers to provide the computational offloading services to the ground users with time-varying computing demands. Each self-interested vehicle jointly optimizes its trajectory planning and data offloading policies to maximize the expectation of its locally cumulative energy efficiency under the collision and energy constraints. We model the non-cooperative interactions among the massive co-channel vehicles as a multi-population mean-field game (MPMFG), where each vehicle constructs two types of mean-field terms to model the UAVs’ and UGVs’ population distributions, respectively. We propose a multi-population mean-field parameterized deep Q network (MPMF-PDQN) algorithm to solve the equilibrium among the vehicular servers in a discrete-continuous hybrid action space. The simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm significantly enhances the average energy efficiency of the vehicles compared with the baseline algorithms.

title: Spatial-Semantic Alignment and Change-aware Network for Remote Sensing Image Change Captioning
authors: Zhigang Yang, Huiguang Yao, Junzhen Wu, Linmao Tian, Weiping Ni, Qiang Li, Qi Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Remote sensing image change captioning aims to generate natural language descriptions of dynamic changes between bitemporal images. This task faces challenges of aligning high-level semantics with sparse change regions and modeling long-range dependencies in diverse change patterns. To alleviate these limitations, we propose the Spatial-Semantic Alignment and Change-aware Network, dubbed as SACNet. It introduces a semantic-spatial collaborative encoding module synergizing a CNN-based spatial encoder with a CLIP-based semantic encoder, using precise spatial cues to refine global semantic features. A change-driven adaptive feature calibration module emphasizes discriminative change signals via inter-temporal feature dissimilarity modeling, suppressing background noise and highlighting key changes. Additionally, a multi-path SSM-guided spatial representation module is designed to capture diverse complex spatial change topologies. Extensive experiments on the LEVIR-CC and WHU-CDC datasets demonstrate that SACNet achieves state-of-the-art performance, generating both high-precision textual descriptions and approximate change masks. Code will be available at https://github.com/CVer-Yang/SACNet.

title: Optimal Keyframe Selection for Hair Strand Animations with Root-Tip Structural Weighting
authors: Jong-Hyun Kim
abstract: This paper proposes an optimal keyframe selection framework for efficiently and perceptually plausibly summarizing hair animation data composed of a large number of strands. Hair strand animations represent each strand as a continuous curve formed by multiple connected particles, and exhibit high-dimensional deformations over time, which makes it difficult to directly apply conventional particle-based or joint-hierarchy-based keyframe selection methods. To address this challenge, we explicitly exploit the connectivity of strands by defining a root-to-tip relative coordinate system and introducing a Root–Tip structural weighting scheme that assigns higher perceptual importance to regions closer to the strand tip. All strands are normalized into a fixed-dimensional representation via arc-length-based resampling, and global translation components are removed using the mean position of strand roots. This enables a stable high-dimensional vector representation for each frame. Based on the resulting frame representation, we propose a dynamic programming-based globally optimal keyframe selection algorithm that uses segment approximation error as its cost function. The cost combines reconstruction error induced by linear interpolation between keyframes and, optionally, a curvature-based regularization term, so that short yet perceptually important deformations are not missed. Experimental results on diverse hair strand animation sequences demonstrate that the proposed method effectively preserves global shape changes and the dynamic behavior of tip regions even under high frame reduction ratios. In particular, keyframe distribution analysis and position error visualizations confirm that, compared to uniform sampling, our method concentrates keyframes in perceptually important temporal intervals, thereby achieving an effective balance between visual fidelity and data reduction. The proposed framework can be used as a practical keyframe-based summarization approach for storage, transmission, preview, and reuse of hair animations.

title: Multidimensional Theory of Charge Partitioning in Semiconductor Devices
authors: Kejun Xia
abstract: We present a multidimensional theory (2-D or 3-D) for charge partitioning in semiconductor devices with an arbitrary number of terminals, extending the well-known 1-D Ward–Dutton (WD) theory and its derivatives. The charge partitioning factors are governed by differential equations subject to appropriate boundary conditions. When the conductivity depends explicitly only on voltage, we show that the partitioning factors can be interpreted in terms of either voltage distribution or current splitting in a companion device with homogeneous conductivity, which is simpler than the original device. As illustrative applications, closed-form solutions are derived for 2-D circular MOSFETs, 3-D cylindrical through-silicon vias (TSVs), and 3-D spherical BJTs. The analysis is further extended to account for field-dependent mobility and position-dependent conductivity.

title: 2025 4th International Conference on Renewable Energy and Electrical Technology (ICREET)
authors: N/A

title: Towards Robust Detection of Malicious Encrypted Traffic Using Only Low-Quality Training Data
authors: Yuqi Qing, Qilei Yin, Xinhao Deng, Yihao Chen, Zhuotao Liu, Kun Sun, Ke Xu, Jia Zhang, Qi Li
abstract: Machine learning (ML) is promising in accurately detecting malicious flows in encrypted network traffic; however, it is challenging to collect a training dataset that contains a sufficient amount of encrypted malicious data with correct labels. When ML models are trained with low-quality training data, they suffer degraded performance. In this paper, we aim to address a real-world low-quality training dataset problem, namely, detecting encrypted malicious traffic generated by continuously evolving malware. We develop RAPIER+ that fully utilizes different distributions of normal and malicious traffic data in the feature space, where normal data is tightly distributed in a certain area, and the malicious data is scattered over the entire feature space to augment training data for model training. RAPIER+ includes two pre-processing modules to convert traffic into feature vectors and correct label noises. We evaluate our system on two public datasets and one combined dataset. With 1000 samples and 45% noise from each dataset, our system achieves the F1 scores of 0.78, 0.84, and 0.87, respectively, achieving average improvements of 358.5%, 314.0%, and 221.1% over the existing methods, respectively. Furthermore, we evaluate RAPIER+ with a real-world dataset obtained from a security enterprise. RAPIER+ effectively achieves encrypted malicious traffic detection with the best F1 score of 0.81 and improves the F1 score of existing methods by an average of 288.7%.

title: A Compact Solid-State Linear Transformer Driver Based on Blumlein Pulse Generation Modules
authors: Hao Zhou, Yuxin Hao, Song Qiu, Qingxiang Liu
abstract: Solid-state linear transformer drivers (SSLTDs) have emerged as a promising solution for generating high-repetition-rate, long-lifetime, highly reliable, and compact pulsed power outputs for high-power microwave (HPM). However, their development is often limited by the high-voltage isolation requirements and structural complexity of conventional bricks. To address these challenges, this article proposes a novel bipolar-charged Blumlein pulse generation module (BC-BPGM) as the brick for SSLTD systems. The proposed BC-BPGM employs a common-ground bipolar-charged stacked-Blumlein configuration, which eliminates the need for high-voltage charging-port isolation and effectively equalizes the insulation stress among semiconductor switch drivers, thereby simplifying the overall insulation design and improving module compactness. A prototype BC-BPGM capable of producing 15-kV nanosecond pulses was developed, and based on this module, a 45-stage compact solid-state linear transformer driver (CSSLTD) was constructed. The magnetic core design and internal insulation configuration were optimized to ensure reliable operation under high-voltage and high-repetition-rate conditions. The experimental results demonstrate that at a charging voltage of 7.5 kV, the CSSLTD delivers a peak output voltage of 553 kV, a peak current of 6.13 kA, and a peak power of 3.4 GW, with a current rise time of 17.6 ns and a full-width at half-maximum (FWHM) of 71.6 ns. The system operates stably at 50 Hz with an overall energy efficiency of 83.2%, achieving one of the highest power densities reported for SSLTDs.

title: Metasurface-FSS-Integrated Stealth Radome Based on Photonic Spin Hall Effect
authors: Qi Zheng, Honghui Jiang, Yike Wang, Xue-Xia Yang, Xiaoyan Pang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A planar stealth radome that has a wide in-band transmission window with a wide out-of-band radar cross section (RCS) reduction is proposed. The proposed radome is simply cascaded by a Pancharatnam-Berry (PB) metasurface and a third-order bandpass frequency selective surface (FSS). The outof- band RCS reduction is based on the photonic spin Hall effect (PSHE) realized by the PB metasurface, achieving the abnormal reflected beams and polarization conversion simultaneously. A radome with an underneath microstrip patch antenna is employed as a proof of concept. For the working band of the antenna, the radiation performance of the antenna is nearly unperturbed. For the scattering, over 10 dB RCS reduction with respect to the equal-sized metallic plate is obtained from 15.0 to 25.0 GHz (50%) under normal impinged linearly polarized electromagnetic wave, and it remains stable at a maximum 45° incident angle. The experimental results measured in the far-field are in good agreement with the simulations, which show its potential for planar stealth antenna radomes requiring X-band transmission and Ku-/Ka-band RCS reduction.

title: A Novel Misalignment Tolerant DSSq Receiver Pad With High Power Density for Wireless EV Chargers
authors: Rajanikant, Vivek Agarwal
abstract: Multi-Coil-Multi-Winding (MCMW) pad structures, such as DDQ and bipolar, enhance performance in a Wireless Electric Vehicle Charger (WEVC) for both aligned and misaligned cases. However, an increased copper usage and bulkier design make them less attractive as a Receiver (Rx) for WEVC. This paper proposes a novel Double Set Square (DSSq) MCMW Rx pad, with the objective of reducing copper consumption and achieving a compact design to augment power density. The DSSq Rx pad consists of four right-angled triangular coils arranged to form a rectangular structure where alternate coils are connected in series to form two independent windings. The DSSq Rx coil dimensions are optimized for maximum surface area utilization, and such winding orientations of the coils that improve the coupling coefficient, Power Transfer Efficiency (PTE), reduce cross-coupling, and ensure a stable mutual inductance profile across varying spatial and angular misalignments. An experimental resonant WEVC prototype was tested at a rated power of 1.45 kW, achieving a DC-DC PTE of 92.67% when aligned and at least 83% during misaligned, thereby surpassing the SAE-J2954 standards. Compared to DDQ, copper usage was reduced by 23.54% and required 50.31% less space, and 48.15% less space than the bipolar pad, thereby achieving a high power density.

title: Decentralized Power Allocation Strategy for Fuel Cell/Battery/Ultracapacitor Hybrid Power Systems in Electric Propulsion Aircraft
authors: Fei Deng, Xiangke Li, Yang Qi, Wenli Yao, Tao Lei, Weilin Li, Xiaobin Zhang
abstract: Aviation electrification is a promising solution to tackle the global energy crisis, which fosters electric aircraft. Besides, hybrid power systems involving the fuel cell, battery, and ultracapacitor are promising to provide propulsion power. Serving as the key device, fuel cells suffer from undesirable operation conditions, e.g., start–stop transition, heavy load, light load, and power variation, which degrade their operation efficiency and lifespan. To address this issue, a decentralized power allocation strategy is proposed in this article. In this method, the ultracapacitor is regulated based on a virtual capacitance to handle dynamic power demand. Besides, the battery is exploited to undertake partial steady-state load power when in heavy- and light-load conditions, allowing the fuel cell to operate in the high-efficiency range to the greatest extent possible. Consequently, both dynamic and steady-state power allocations are realized, ensuring high-efficiency and reliable operation of the hybrid power systems. Meanwhile, note that the control algorithms in this method for all three units are implemented based on their local information, respectively, no dedicated communication network is required. Finally, the validation of this strategy is proven by hardware-in-loop (HIL) test results in various scenarios.

title: From Point Clouds to Digital Narratives: A Generative AI Framework for Immersive Media Reconstruction and Cultural Storytelling of Classical Garden Heritage
authors: Shawei Zhang, Xuefei Li, Ran Guo, Ting Yang
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 2nd International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Digital Media
abstract: None

title: Clash of the Titans: A User Experience Comparison of Amazon, Apple, and Google Smart Home Technology
authors: David Wright, Sarah E. Hercula, Daniel B. Shank, Adam Rentz, Julianna Farr
abstract: None

title: Optical emission spectroscopy (OES) for leak detection and impurity analysis for lab grown diamond (LGD) application
authors: Vikas Sharma, Priyadarshini Ghosh
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: Diamond Industry based on the MPCVD (Microwave Plasma Chemical Vapor Deposition), HORIBA introduce an important tool EV 2.0 for Plasma & Chamber health monitoring and Advance process control. EV 2.0 spectroscopy provides the Recipe optimization in diamond growth process. This platform allows satisfying all the needs of in-situ plasma chamber health monitoring, fault analysis diamond growth process in the presence of different working gases in the chamber.

title: Orientation-Invariant Step Length Estimation for Arbitrary Indoor Trajectories
authors: Saptadipa Mazumder, Pampa Sadhukhan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: In this study we have implemented a new smartphone-based Orientation-Invariant Step Length Estimation (OI-SLE) method. OI-SLE is achieved through the development of three features: —orientation compensation using quaternion, slope-adaptive stride length estimation, and turn aware compensation. By converting sensor readings into the Earth Coordinate System and automatically adjusting for changes in direction and surface slope, the method performs well on straight paths, curves, stairs, and inclined surfaces. Tests in different walking conditions show that OI-SLE improves the accuracy of step length estimation by about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10-15 \%$</tex> compared to basic linear models, proving it works effectively for all kinds of indoor routes.

title: DRTA: Dynamic Reward Scaling for Reinforcement Learning in Time Series Anomaly Detection
authors: Bahareh Golchin, Banafsheh Rekabdar, Kunpeng Liu
publish: 2025 Conference on AI, Science, Engineering, and Technology (AIxSET)
abstract: Anomaly detection in time series data is important for applications in finance, healthcare, sensor networks, and industrial monitoring. Traditional methods usually struggle with limited labeled data, high false-positive rates, and difficulty generalizing to novel anomaly types. To overcome these challenges, we propose a reinforcement learning-based framework that integrates dynamic reward shaping, Variational Autoencoder (VAE), and active learning, called DRTA. Our method uses an adaptive reward mechanism that balances exploration and exploitation by dynamically scaling the effect of VAE-based reconstruction error and classification rewards. This approach enables the agent to detect anomalies effectively in low-label systems while maintaining high precision and recall. Our experimental results on the Yahoo A1 and Yahoo A2 benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms state-of-theart unsupervised and semi-supervised approaches. These findings show that our framework is a scalable and efficient solution for real-world anomaly detection tasks.<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>Code is available at GitHub Repository.

title: Ego-EXTRA: video-language Egocentric Dataset for EXpert-TRAinee assistance
authors: Francesco Ragusa, Michele Mazzamuto, Rosario Forte, Irene D’Ambra, James Fort, Jakob Engel, Antonino Furnari, Giovanni Maria Farinella
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: We present Ego-EXTRA, a video-language Egocentric Dataset for EXpert-TRAinee assistance. Ego-EXTRA features 50 hours of unscripted egocentric videos of subjects performing procedural activities (the trainees) while guided by real-world experts who provide guidance and answer specific questions using natural language. Following a "Wizard of OZ" data collection paradigm, the expert enacts a wearable intelligent assistant, looking at the activities performed by the trainee exclusively from their egocentric point of view, answering questions when asked by the trainee, or proactively interacting with suggestions during the procedures. This unique data collection protocol enables Ego-EXTRA to capture a high-quality dialogue in which expert-level feedback is provided to the trainee. Two-way dialogues between experts and trainees are recorded, transcribed, and used to create a novel benchmark comprising more than 45k high-quality Visual Question Answer sets, which we use to evaluate Multimodal Large Language Models. The results show that Ego-EXTRA is challenging and highlight the limitations of current models when used to provide expert-level assistance to the user. The Ego-EXTRA dataset is publicly available to support the benchmark of egocentric video-language assistants: https://fpv-iplab.github.io/Ego-EXTRA/.

title: FARF-Net: Frequency-guided Adaptive Receptive Field Network for Edge-enhanced Polyp Segmentation
authors: Xue Li, Aiwen Jiang, Hongqian Yu, Yang Xiao
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of colorectal polyps plays a vital role in the early diagnosis and prevention of colorectal cancer. Despite notable progress, existing methods struggle with limited region adaptability due to fixed receptive fields, lack explicit boundary modeling, and are prone to interference from background noise, leading to suboptimal segmentation results. To address these issues, we propose FARF-Net, a novel edge-aware segmentation framework that leverages frequency-domain adaptive receptive fields. Built upon the Pyramid Vision Transformer v2 backbone, FARF-Net introduces three tailored components. Specifically, EdgeKAN module applies Kolmogorov–Arnold Networks for channel-wise nonlinear modeling, enhancing local edge semantics and boundary detail representation. Adaptive Receptive Field module adjusts spatial receptive fields based on localized frequency energy, boosting sensitivity to high-frequency boundaries. Frequency-Guided Dual-Supervision Decoder integrates high-frequency structural features and boundary priors to refine edge predictions and suppress irrelevant high-frequency background noise. Extensive experiments on five public polyp segmentation benchmarks demonstrate that FARF-Net consistently surpasses state-of-the-art methods. Notably, it achieves superior boundary reconstruction and robustness in challenging cases such as blurred contours and small polyps.

title: HDR Reconstruction Boosting with Training-Free and Exposure-Consistent Diffusion
authors: Yo-Tin Lin, Su-Kai Chen, Hou-Ning Hu, Yen-Yu Lin, Yu-Lun Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Single LDR to HDR reconstruction remains challenging for over-exposed regions where traditional methods often fail due to complete information loss. We present a training-free approach that enhances existing indirect and direct HDR reconstruction methods through diffusion-based inpainting. Our method combines text-guided diffusion models with SDEdit refinement to generate plausible content in overexposed areas while maintaining consistency across multi-exposure LDR images. Unlike previous approaches requiring extensive training, our method seamlessly integrates with existing HDR reconstruction techniques through an iterative compensation mechanism that ensures luminance coherence across multiple exposures. We demonstrate significant improvements in both perceptual quality and quantitative metrics on standard HDR datasets and in-the-wild captures. Results show that our method effectively recovers natural details in challenging scenarios while preserving the advantages of existing HDR reconstruction pipelines. Project page: here.

title: UAV Based Precise Multi Payload Dropping System Using Vision Guidance and RTK Positioning
authors: Bodnayakuni Tarun Kumar, Sanju Kumar NT, Syam Narayanan S, Raju Santhani, Pavan Sai Kumar Reddy T, P. Rajalakshmi
abstract: The precision dropping of payloads using unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) has gained critical importance in both military and civilian contexts. Accurate target positioning can determine mission success in defence operations, medicine, and disaster-affected areas. However, conventional UAV-based payload release systems face significant challenges in limited accuracy for targeting position, imprecise release mechanisms, and poor adaptability to diverse mission requirements. To overcome the challenge, this paper presents the development of a precise multi-payload drop system integrated with a drone, capable of carrying and deploying up to three payloads with a total capacity of 10–15 kg. The system first navigates to the target waypoint, then activates a vision-based precision drop routine. A downward-facing camera identifies the ground marker, and the drone adjusts its position until alignment is achieved. Once within the accuracy threshold, the payload is released, while target coordinates and telemetry are streamed to the ground station in real time. The system combines vision guidance with real-time kinematic (RTK) positioning to achieve sub-meter level accuracy in payload deployment. Comparative analysis is conducted in three operational model manual releases with camera-based target localisation, GPS-assisted release, and vision-RTK fusion. The system employs a servo-driven release mechanism connected to the flight controller, enabling manual operation via radio transmitter and autonomous triggering. Designed with a modular plug-and-play architecture. Experimental results demonstrate that the hybrid approach significantly improves precision and reduces error rates compared to conventional methods. The proposed VTOL-based multi-payload system demonstrates strong potential for tactical and civilian applications, offering accuracy, flexibility, and reliability in complex operational environments.

title: Evaluating Best Practices of Financial Management in Healthcare Service Sector for Integration with AI
authors: Rashmikanta Tripathy, Susanta Kumar Mishra, Sisir Ranjan Dash, Purwono Purwono, Amit Kumar, Ajitav Acharya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: The present research is the conclusive part of a project which started with the overall objective to evaluate best practices of financial management for Indian healthcare service providers. The initial exploratory phase has been carried out as qualitative research in which we have discovered that financial management in Indian healthcare service sector is getting done with the use of artificial intelligence and other advanced technologies. Using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A I$</tex> the finance managers are now able to analyze huge amount of health data and then taking decision that consequently improving patient care, operational efficiency and outcomes. But we have also identified a research problem that the advanced technologies including AI which are getting used for financial management needs to be integrated with the best practices in the industry. So, this study is an attempt to map the best practices of financial management in the healthcare sector and recommend the research community about its findings. The findings of the present study will be unquestionably relevant for researchers, students, faculty members, technology experts and entrepreneurs in specific.

title: Guardian Sense-AI Powered Home Security
authors: K.Madhavi, M Divyasri, G Akshara, K.Poojitha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Security of homes and properties needs smart means that can detect threat in the right way. The design proposal presents the introduction of a smart human detection alarm system by applying AI camera surveillance and object recognition. It constantly scans the environment and raises an alarm only in case it sees unauthorized or suspicious persons. The system is able to distinguish between the human beings, animals and pets and avoid false alarms due to innocent motions. Also, it incorporates the facial recognition with a database of known members of the family, so that only unknown and unauthorized people are indicated. This is the least false positive, and it is more reliable, and identifies threats accurately. Smart homes, remote locations and sensitive premises are an ideal environment where the proposed solution can be used to provide efficient, accurate and intelligent monitoring.

title: Quantum-Enhanced Convolutional Neural Network for Multiclass Potato Leaf Disease Classification
authors: Arpan Murmu, Pritam Mitra, Amlan Chakrabarti
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Potato leaf diseases pose a serious challenge to agricultural sustainability and food security in regions such as West Bengal, India, where potato cultivation plays a vital socio-economic role. Although classical deep learning models demonstrate strong performance, they often suffer from overfitting, limited generalization, and heavy reliance on large labeled datasets, while manual inspection remains time-consuming and error-prone. This work proposes a hybrid Quantum Convolutional Neural Network (QCNN) integrated with 4-qubit parameterized quantum circuits (PQCs) for accurate classification of five major potato leaf disease categories, including nutrient deficiency, fungal, and viral infections. The model incorporates quantum activation layers, quantum softmax, and a focused loss function to enhance non-linear feature representation and multi-class separability. Grad-CAM is employed to improve the interpretability of predictions. Experiments conducted on a diverse, field-collected dataset from Bardhaman, Bankura, and Purulia districts achieve a classification accuracy of 99.94%, demonstrating superior generalization, robustness, and explainability compared to classical approaches. This study highlights the practical potential of quantum-enhanced AI for precision agriculture.

title: Referring Change Detection in Remote Sensing Imagery
authors: Yilmaz Korkmaz, Jay N. Paranjape, Celso M. de Melo, Vishal M. Patel
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Change detection in remote sensing imagery is essential for applications such as urban planning, environmental monitoring, and disaster management. Traditional change detection methods typically identify all changes between two temporal images without distinguishing the types of transitions, which can lead to results that may not align with specific user needs. Although semantic change detection methods have attempted to address this by categorizing changes into predefined classes, these methods rely on rigid class definitions and fixed model architectures, making it difficult to mix datasets with different label sets or reuse models across tasks, as the output channels are tightly coupled with the number and type of semantic classes. To overcome these limitations, we introduce Referring Change Detection (RCD), which leverages natural language prompts to detect specific classes of changes in remote sensing images. By integrating language understanding with visual analysis, our approach allows users to specify the exact type of change they are interested in. However, training models for RCD is challenging due to the limited availability of annotated data and severe class imbalance in existing datasets. To address this, we propose a two-stage framework consisting of (I) RCDNet, a cross-modal fusion network designed for referring change detection, and (II) RCDGen, a diffusion-based synthetic data generation pipeline that produces realistic post-change images and change maps for a specified category using only pre-change image, without relying on semantic segmentation masks and thereby significantly lowering the barrier to scalable data creation. Experiments across multiple datasets show that our framework enables scalable and targeted change detection. Code will be made publicly available on Github.

title: Ai-Integrated Cyber Security Model for Intelligent Intrusion and Anomaly Detection in Next-Generation Communication Systems
authors: Kammari Srujana, Nemalipalli Madhu Sudhan, Sandrapati Vinodh Kumar, Yaganti Ravi Teja
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The next generation communication systems are becoming very susceptible to advanced cyber threats because of high-speed, heterogeneous, large scale. The conventional intrusion detection systems which are rule based are inadequate in relation to the zero-day attacks and evolving attacks. This paper suggests an AI-based Cyber Security Model that is a combination of deep traffic embedding, semantic log analysis, graph-based structural learning, supervised intrusion classification, and unsupervised anomaly detection in an ensemble decision fusion structure. The discussed hybrid architecture relies on the multimodal feature representation to provide greater detection robustness and flexibility. The experimental analysis based on CICIDS2017, UNSW-NB15, and simulated next-generation traffic proves to be better than single stand-alone SVM, DNN, and clustering-based approaches. The model had an accuracy of 98.5%, precision of 98.2% and recall of 98.7% and a false positive of about 0.1. The analysis of confusion matrix showed that there were only 15 false positives and 12 false negatives, which shows that classification is rather stable. The ROC and Precision-Recall tests prove that the discriminative capacity is strong at the imbalanced and bursty traffic scenarios. The findings affirm that a combination of supervised, unsupervised and semantic intelligence is much better in enhancing detection accuracy, strength and scalability of next-generation communication infrastructures.

title: NoHumansRequired: Autonomous High-Quality Image Editing Triplet Mining
authors: Maksim Kuprashevich, Grigorii Alekseenko, Irina Tolstykh, Georgii Fedorov, Bulat Suleimanov, Vladimir Dokholyan, Aleksandr Gordeev
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Recent advances in generative modeling enable image editing assistants that follow natural language instructions without additional user input. Their supervised training requires millions of triplets ⟨original image, instruction, edited image⟩, yet mining pixel-accurate examples is hard. Each edit must affect only prompt-specified regions, preserve stylistic coherence, respect physical plausibility, and retain visual appeal. The lack of robust automated edit-quality metrics hinders reliable automation at scale. We present an automated, modular pipeline that mines high-fidelity triplets across domains, resolutions, instruction complexities, and styles. Built on public generative models and running without human intervention, our system uses a task-tuned Gemini validator to score instruction adherence and aesthetics directly, removing any need for segmentation or grounding models. Inversion and compositional bootstrapping enlarge the mined set by ≈ 2.6×, enabling large-scale high-fidelity training data. By automating the most repetitive annotation steps, the approach allows a new scale of training without human labeling effort. To democratize research in this resource-intensive area, we release NHR-Edit, an open dataset of 720k high-quality triplets, curated at industrial scale via millions of guided generations and validator passes, and we analyze the pipeline’s stage-wise survival rates, providing a framework for estimating computational effort across different model stacks. In the largest cross-dataset evaluation, it surpasses all public alternatives. We also release Bagel-NHR-Edit, a fine-tuned Bagel model with state-of-the-art metrics.Project page, dataset, and model: https://riko0.github.io/No-Humans-Required/

title: StreetView-Waste: A Multi-Task Dataset for Urban Waste Management
authors: Diogo J. Paulo, João Martins, Hugo Proença, João C. Neves
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Urban waste management remains a critical challenge for the development of smart cities. Despite the growing number of litter detection datasets, the problem of monitoring overflowing waste containers — particularly from images captured by garbage trucks — has received little attention. While existing datasets are valuable, they often lack annotations for specific container tracking or are captured in static, decontextualized environments, limiting their utility for real-world logistics. To address this gap, we present StreetView-Waste, a comprehensive dataset of urban scenes featuring litter and waste containers. The dataset supports three key evaluation tasks: (1) waste container detection, (2) waste container tracking, and (3) waste overflow segmentation. Alongside the dataset, we provide baselines for each task by benchmarking state-of-the-art models in object detection, tracking, and segmentation. Additionally, we enhance baseline performance by proposing two complementary strategies: a heuristic-based method for improved waste container tracking and a model-agnostic framework that leverages geometric priors to refine litter segmentation. Our experimental results show that while fine-tuned object detectors achieve reasonable performance in detecting waste containers, baseline tracking methods struggle to accurately estimate their number; however, our proposed heuristics reduce the mean absolute counting error by 79.6%. Similarly, while segmenting amorphous litter is challenging, our geometry-aware strategy improves segmentation mAP@0.5 by 27% on lightweight models, demonstrating the value of multimodal inputs for this task. Ultimately, StreetView-Waste provides a challenging benchmark to encourage research into real-world perception systems for urban waste management.

title: Understanding Human-Like Biases in VLMs via Subjective Face Analytics
authors: Chaitanya Roygaga, Aparna Bharati
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) effectively integrate visual and textual information, often relying on shared embedding spaces to align modalities. However, the extent to which these spaces capture complex, subjective human judgments, such as perceived facial trustworthiness, and whether they replicate associated human social biases, remains underexplored. This paper investigates the representation of subjective face attributes within VLM embedding spaces, examining whether these representations encode human-like biases and assessing their interpretability. Using probing techniques on face datasets annotated with human judgments, we analyze the structure of VLM embeddings <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>. Our findings demonstrate that similarity scores between face image and textual description in the VLM embedding space align with human ratings of subjective attributes, and crucially, these representations exhibit correlations and demographic disparities mirroring known biases in human social perception. We also show that the use of variable context via face and attribute-specific captions can provide increased diagnostic value for revealing and mitigating bias, by increasing the alignment of the VLM embedding space with human impressions. Interpreting the embedded social biases highlights the need for critical evaluation and bias-aware development of VLMs to mitigate the risk of perpetuating harmful stereotypes in downstream applications that involve Human-AI interaction.

title: Evaluation of an AI-Based Semantic Chatbot for Quran and Hadith Retrieval Using Expert Validation and NLP Performance Metrics
authors: Dian Pratiwi Wahyuddin, Amil Ahmad Ilham, Yuspiani
abstract: This study presents the design and evaluation of an artificial intelligence (AI)-based semantic chatbot developed to retrieve Quranic verses and Hadith references in response to thematic user queries. The system integrates a natural language processing (NLP) pipeline to interpret user intent and match it with a curated corpus of authenticated Islamic sources using a hybrid semantic retrieval approach based on TF-IDF and BERT embeddings. Evaluation was conducted through two complementary approaches: expert validation and technical performance measurement. Expert validation involved two domain specialists in Al-Qur'an and Hadith sciences who rated 30 thematic queries using a 4-point Likert scale across three criteria: authenticity, thematic relevance, and contextual accuracy. The technical evaluation used standard NLP metrics, including Precision, Recall, and F1-Score. The dataset consisted of 6,236 Quranic verses and 14,838 Hadiths from Sahih Bukhari and Sahih Muslim in Bahasa Indonesia. Results demonstrated a mean Precision of 0.78, Recall of 0.98, and F1-Score of 0.84, indicating the chatbot's high accuracy and retrieval comprehensiveness. Expert assessments further confirmed strong reliability, with an average validation score exceeding 85% across all criteria. These findings highlight the potential of semantic AI systems in supporting digital Islamic learning environments and automated religious information services. Future work will extend semantic understanding to multilingual queries and incorporate user-centered evaluation and broader tafsir integration to enhance contextual reasoning.

title: Blockchain and Graph Neural Network-Based Peer-to-Peer Energy Trading Mechanism for Smart Grids Integrating Renewable Energy Resources
authors: N. Selvam M. E., Nalin Kumar S P, Sanjaai U N, Sharmitha S P
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The growing adoption of renewable energy sources, including solar and wind in smart grids, creates a chance and also challenges to the field of decentralized energy management. In this project, a new framework of blockchain-based and Graph Neural Networks-based (GNNs) Peer-to-peer (P2P) energy trading is proposed that allows a secure, transparent, and intelligent energy exchange among prosumers. Within this model, producers and consumers of energy deal directly with each other without having to middlemen when exchanging excess energy. Smart contracts are trading processes that are represented by blockchain and can be automated to allow dynamic pricing, buyer-seller matching, and payment settlements that are not subject to modification, auditability, and lower transaction costs. In the meantime, GNNs learn complicated spatial and relational relationships in the energy network, and they are capable of predicting the patterns of renewable energy generation and consumption to guide optimal trade-off strategies. According to simulation findings, the proposed system is able to enhance the efficiency of the grid, minimize the energy waste, and facilitate the adoption of the sustainable energy, which proves that the proposed system could become the scalable solution to the intelligent and secure management of the energy in the smart grids.

title:
DM
                    <sup>3</sup>
                    Net: Dual-Camera Super-Resolution via Domain Modulation and Multi-scale Matching
authors: Cong Guan, Jiacheng Ying, Yuya Ieiri, Osamu Yoshie
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Dual-camera super-resolution is highly practical for smartphone photography that primarily super-resolve the wide-angle images using the telephoto image as a reference. In this paper, we propose DM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>Net, a novel dual-camera super-resolution network based on Domain Modulation and Multi-scale Matching. To bridge the domain gap between the high-resolution domain and the degraded domain, we learn two compressed global representations from image pairs corresponding to the two domains. To enable reliable transfer of high-frequency structural details from the reference image, we design a multi-scale matching module that conducts patch-level feature matching and retrieval across multiple receptive fields to improve matching accuracy and robustness. Moreover, we also introduce Key Pruning to achieve a significant reduction in memory usage and inference time with little model performance sacrificed. Experimental results on three real-world datasets demonstrate that our DM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>Net outperforms the state-of-the-art approaches.

title: The Cognitive Impact of AI Chatbots Dependency Among Students
authors: William Chandra, Andy Effendi
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: The usage of artificial intelligence (AI) chatbots in education has changed the way students access information and complete their academic tasks. While AI Chatbots offer efficiency and convenience in helping students perform various task, students may have not realized that they are slowly getting dependent on AI Chatbots and that dependency can affect their cognitive abilities. This research aims to examine the effects of AI Chatbots dependency on student's cognitive engagement and critical thinking. The significance of this study lies in its contribution to understanding how reliance on AI assistance may influence student's cognitive abilities. This research uses a quantitative approach by using a survey method. Research data were collected from 150 students across various educational levels who are currently enrolled in various learning institutions. The questionnaire were distributed online and designed to measure students levels of AI Chatbots dependency, cognitive engagement and critical thinking ability. The collected research data were analyzed using statistical techniques to identify the general patterns and the relationships between the variables. This research's results indicate that AI Chatbots dependency has a visible influence on student's cognitive engagement and their critical thinking abilities. Overall, higher levels of AI dependency is associated with reduced cognitive abilities, which suggests the need for more guided use of AI Chatbots in various learning institutions.

title: Sign Language Gesture Recognition via Temporal Attention Networks
authors: S Poonguzhali, N. Sarika, D. Meenakshi, R. Muthulakshmi, G. Elakkiyaselvam, S. Sowmyayani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: A Temporal Attention Network (TANet) is proposed for robust sign language gesture recognition in both isolated and continuous video settings. TANet extracts frame-level spatio-temporal features using a two-stream backbone (appearance + pose), fuses them and applies a learnable attention module that emphasizes frames most discriminative for a given gloss/word. The model is trained end-to-end and evaluated on standard sign language benchmarks (WLASL, RWTH-PHOENIX, ASLLVD). Experimental results show TANet improves recognition accuracy over baselines (ResNet3D+LSTM, TCN, standard Transformer) while requiring comparable compute. The studies demonstrate the contribution of temporal attention, pose stream fusion and multi-scale temporal context. The TANet achieves Top 1 accuracies of 91. 4 %, 85. 2 % and 9 2. 6 % on the respective datasets, outperforming existing CNN-RNN, GCN and Transformer-based methods by 3-4 %. Furthermore, the proposed model operates at nearly real-time speed (~48 fps), making it suitable for assistive communication systems and human-computer interaction.

title: Multimodal Deep Learning for Early Cardiovascular Risk Prediction: A Systematic Review and the OC-GRU-RAE Framework
authors: J. Jerin Jose, T. Prem Jacob
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease (CVD) is a significant cause of death in the globe and its early and correct diagnosis is extremely significant in the appropriate clinical treatment of the disorder. Despite the success of deep learning models with a single clinical data modality such as tabular electronic health records, physiological time-series signals, and cardiac imaging, the existing ones generally. Besides that, another set of issues, such as sub-optimal representation of high-cardinality categorical data, low temporal modeling capacities, and low interpretability remain undermining clinical acceptance. The paper will summarize 40 peer-reviewed publications about early CVD detection with the application of deep learning models on unimodal and multimodal data that have been published since 2018. Based on the gaps of the conceptual framework, one can consent to the suggestion of the proposal of a conceptual framework called the Optimized CatBoost-Gated Recurrent Unit Recursive Autoencoder (OC-GRU-RAE) as the direction of the forthcoming research. The proposed framework combines sequential categorical representation, category-sensitive temporal coding and recursive multimodal representation learning to offer powerful, explainable and clinical consistent early cardiovascular risk prediction.

title: SphereEdit: Spherical Semantic Editing in Diffusion Models
authors: Salamata Konate, Hassan Hamidi, Elham Dolatabadi, Frank Rudzicz, Laleh Seyyed-Kalantri
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Despite significant advances in diffusion models, achieving precise and composable image editing without task-specific training remains a challenge. Existing approaches often rely on iterative optimization or linear latent operations, which are slow, brittle, and prone to attribute entanglement (e.g., editing "lipstick" inadvertently alters skin tone). We introduce SphereEdit, a training-free framework that leverages the spherical geometry of diffusion embeddings and token aware cross-attention to enable interpretable, fine-grained control. We represent semantic attributes as unit vector directions in the denoiser’s prediction space and show that antipodal symmetry (‘old’ is approximately the negation of ‘young’) naturally supports bidirectional edits, while approximate orthogonality enables clean composition through spherical coefficient. At inference, these directions modulate cross-attention activations, producing spatially localized edits without optimization or fine-tuning. SphereEdit achieves sharper, more disentangled edits than prior baselines, while remaining plug-and-play and applicable across diverse image editing tasks. The code is available at https://github.com/sala-kon/SphereEdit

title: Enhancing Privacy-Preserving Healthcare Analytics via Quantum-Inspired Federated Optimization
authors: Mithaguru, Rakshita R, Joshuva Arockia Dhanaraj, Vegi Fernando A, Mude Nagarjuna Naik, Lakshmanan M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Federated learning (FL) allows a joint model training between decentralized data sources and does not involve the exchange of sensitive records, which is appealing in the context of health care. Nevertheless, the widely applied Federated Averaging (FedAvg) algorithm cannot perform well in the non-independent and identically distributed (non-IID) data, resulting in slow convergence and poor performance. In order to overcome this limitation, this paper examines a quantum-inspired aggregation strategy in which Quantum-Behaved Particle Swarm Optimization (QPSO) is considered. On an artificial PubMed-style text classification dataset split into four clients with Dirichlet non-IID splits, we compare FedAvg and QPSO after three communication rounds. The experimental findings indicate that QPSO has much better validation accuracy (0.978 vs. 0.618) and final test accuracy (0.956 vs. 0.624) and faster convergence. These findings show that the QPSO-based aggregation can add value to global model in federated healthcare analytics with heterogeneous data distributions.

title: Rice Leaf Disease Early Detection Image Classification Approach Based on Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Inderjeet Singh, Yadwinder Singh, Manik Pathria, Udit Kathuria, Rajendra Dhaka, Sourav Thakur
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Finding diseases in rice leaves on time is very important for getting better crop production. Normally, farmers check the leaves by looking at them, but this takes a lot of time and may not always be correct. It is also difficult to check large fields in this way. With the help of deep learning, we can now create systems that can detect diseases automatically and save time. In this work, we used a CNN model to identify rice leaf diseases using 5,932 images. The images belong to four disease types: Bacterial Blight, Blast, Brown Spot, and Tungro. We created a simple CNN model and trained it from the beginning. We made all the pictures <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$128 \times 128$</tex> so that the model could easily and quickly work with them. The model was trained using standard methods and then tested on data that was not used for training. It got about 96.4% of accuracy, which is a good score. The model's simplicity and lightweight in design. This study shows that a basic Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) can help identify plant diseases, and it has potential for future improvements.

title: Optimized AI Models for Multi-Class Malware Detection: Balancing Datasets with O-SMOTE and Detecting Ransomware with 1D-CNN
authors: Harshitha B K, Priya Nandihal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Because of the enormous harm it does to people's finances, reputations, and operations, ransomware has arisen as a major cybersecurity concern in recent years. Criminals online will always find a way to circumvent security measures put in place, and they will use these new platforms to steal vital information from unsuspecting consumers. This might lead to disastrous cyberattacks such as monetary losses, reputational damage, ransomware, or the transmission of contagious software. Ransomware is among the most destructive forms of malware since it encrypts user data and demands money to decrypt its contents. Loss of critical data, interruption of company operations, and substantial financial harm are all possible outcomes of ransomware attacks. The detection of Android ransomware attacks has been greatly improved by AI-based techniques, including machine learning (ML). Nevertheless, there has been a lack of thorough investigation into ensemble models and DL models. To efficiently detect multi-class threats such as adware, ransomware, rootkit, SMS malware, and scareware, this work proposes a multi-stage learning model that uses a 1 dimensional convolutional neural network (1D-CNN) and decision tree-based classification. Using the stadium spectator's optimizer (SSO) algorithm to optimally select the sample rate, the optimized synthetic minority oversampling procedure (O-SMOTE) is used to balance the dataset. In order to build cyber systems that are resistant to cyber threats, our symmetrical research in AI and cybersecurity analytics will improve our capacity to identify, analyze, and mitigate malware. Using a dataset that includes recentlyreleased dangerous software, the study proved that our technique is effective and efficient in meeting real-world needs, thus validating the model. Combining behavior-based deep learning with heuristic-based approaches beats static deep learning tactics for malware discovery and classification, according to the experimental data.

title: Food Image Generation on Multi-Noun Categories
authors: Xinyue Pan, Yuhao Chen, Jiangpeng He, Fengqing Zhu
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Generating realistic food images for categories with multiple nouns is surprisingly challenging. For instance, the prompt "egg noodle" may result in images that incorrectly contain both eggs and noodles as separate entities. Multi-noun food categories are common in real-world datasets and account for a large portion of entries in benchmarks such as UEC-256. These compound names often cause generative models to misinterpret the semantics, producing unintended ingredients or objects. This is due to insufficient multi-noun category related knowledge in the text encoder and misinterpretation of multi-noun relationships, leading to incorrect spatial layouts. To overcome these challenges, we propose FoCULR (Food Category Understanding and Layout Refinement) which incorporates food domain knowledge and introduces core concepts early in the generation process. Experimental results demonstrate that the integration of these techniques improves image generation performance in the food domain.

title: Towards Intelligent Traffic Signal Control in Internet of Vehicles Using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Satish Kumar Negi, Suraj Sharma, Pushpendra Kumar Chandra, Niranjan Kumar Ray
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Traffic jams in cities and more cars connecting to the internet mean that traffic signal control systems need to be smart and adaptable. This paper provides an extensive examination of artificial intelligence-based methodologies for intelligent traffic signal management in Internet of Vehicles contexts. There are four main types of existing methods: rule-based, optimization-based, learning-based, and hybrid. A comparison of average delay, queue length, throughput, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> emissions shows that reinforcement learning reduces delays by about 30% to 40%, deep reinforcement learning boosts performance by up to 45% to 50%, and IoV-enabled hybrid approaches achieve the biggest gains of over 55%. We talk about open questions and future research directions for intelligent traffic signal control systems that are safe, scalable, and easy to use.

title: Armos: A Proxy Based Security Architecture for Mitigating Unauthorized LLM Access to Web Applications
authors: Gundu Bhavana, Dhanyasree Thallapalli, S. Saravanan
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Large Language Models as Web scrapers cause a massive threat to the news and the media houses by even permitting the unauthorized extraction, reinterpretation, and even use of proprietary materials. Unlike traditional bots, these are sophisticated scrapers which use semantic reasoning, browser computerization, and simulation of human navigation behaviour, in this way beating conventional anti-bot defenses and augmenting the reputational risk because of hallucinated or falsely attributed content. This is a proxy based multi-layered proxy called Armos. middleware model which integrates active HTTP header. fingerprinting, behavioral monitor-TLS/JA3 anomaly detection. ing, honeypot-based deception, and machine learning-based deception. driven classification. Working as a mediating inspection. layer, Armos will allow detecting in real time and counter-adaptively. termeasures on malicious traffic by blocking, throttling, or premeditated fraudulent reaction. We perform an experimental assessment of its efficacy: alone, the use of static fingerprinting produced Accuracy on known bot and LLM client signatures is 97.8 percent and the behavioral and honeypot systems detected 91 percent of the. activities of adaptive LLM scrapers out of their inelastic and low-variance. timing patterns, such as 0.9 sec mean request interviewe. compared to 7.8 sec by humans. The final XGBoost incumbent. generated 96.5 % validation accuracy whose characteristics in Header. Assumptions of entropy, JA3 Mismatch and Behavioral Anomaly Score. greater than 70 percent of predictive value. These findings verify that Armos should provide a stalwart defense and at the same time retain fair authentication of authorized access, and its intrinsic feedback of learning loop will also ensure flexibility to varying scraping techniques and improve the safety of the digital journalism content within the times of generative AI.

title: Exploring UAV-Based Multispectral Crop Yield Analysis: Opportunities and Challenges
authors: Sreenivasa Chakravarthi Sangapu, M Sai Deekshith, P Chandrasena Reddy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Combining UAVs with multispectral imaging technology has enabled high-resolution, real-time monitoring of crops and prediction of crop yields, revolutionizing precision farming. This research focuses on UAV yield estimation, along with machine and deep learning models with Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) for multispectral imaging. The research has deployed UAVs to gather other relevant data with multispectral imaging and generated orthomosaics along with vegetation indices, which are NDVI, EVI, and GNDVI, as well as different agronomic and environmental data. Afterward XGBoost, LASSO regression predictive models, and dimensionality reduction were compared to Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). The spatial and spectral Ubisoft models were also incorporated, where CNNs provided the most predictive power with a validation R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.8486. The XAI tools proposed, SHAP and Grad-CAM, determined which vegetation indices and spatial patterns apportioned at the field level influenced yield forecasting. The research has shown predictive AI for actionable insights in precision farming. The research aims to develop hybrid deep learning models for rapid, scalable, real-time use on a multi-crop dataset. The research proposes focusing on its real-time applications.

title: An Optimal Evaluation of Entanglement Depth in Variational Quantum Classifiers for Efficient Deployment of Machine Learning Applications
authors: Aryan Sahni, Krishnesh Mathur, Yashvi Diya Singh, Kowsigan M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Variational Quantum Classifiers (VQCs) are a common class of hybrid quantum-classical models developed for nearterm quantum machine learning applications. Despite growing focus, it is still unclear how circuit design choices, particularly entanglement depth, affect learning performance and optimisation stability when compared to reliable classical baselines [1]. In this research, we provide a controlled empirical analysis of entanglement depth in VQCs for medical image classification utilizing pre-extracted deep feature representations. Under a predetermined experimental design, we assessed VQCs with no entanglement, single-layer entanglement, and two-layer interleaved entanglement with different training set sizes. Our findings demonstrate that in low-data regimes, increasing entanglement could negatively impact accuracy due to optimization instability [2] and may not always enhance performance. Since classical machine learning baselines consistently match or surpass quantum models, there is no performance advantage of variational quantum classifiers was observed under the evaluated experimental conditions and preprocessing constraints. These results emphasize the significance of thorough, comparative benchmarking for real-world use and draw attention to the expressivity optimization trade-offs inherent in variational quantum learning.

title: Benchmarking Multi-Agent Collaboration Against Single LLM Systems in E-Commerce
authors: Saikiran Gogineni, Chinnam Yuvaraju, G Suryanarayana, Kanaka Durga Returi, Vaka Murali Mohan, M Nagabhushana Rao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have emerged as effective methods for logic-based reasoning, retrieval, and synthesis tasks across complex domains. However, LLM-based systems have limitations. They struggle under high task loads. LLMs often hallucinate, and lack mechanisms to verify their own output in multi-stage workflows. This study provides an empirical evaluation of a single LLM design versus a multi-agent LLM architecture in the context of e-commerce. In the single-agent approach, a single model performed retrieval, filtering, verification, and structured data generation. The multi-agent architecture divides the workflow into stages, and assign among agents. In the multi-agent architecture, we employed a specially designed Filtering agent, Generative agent, and an Analytical agent. We evaluated performance by analysing precision, factual consistency, and inter-agent coordination. Though communication and synchronization challenges were introduced, the multi-agent architecture can improve factual accuracy and structured output reliability. Our research provides a practical architecture and a framework to evaluate multi-agent AI systems in real-world applications that require accurate information retrieval.

title: Evaluation and Future Exploration of Lightweight Cryptography on FPGA: Challenges and Opportunities for AES, TEA, and PRESENT
authors: Sandhya C P, Manjith B C
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: In this digital era, the security and privacy of transmissive data are critically important. AES, TEA, and PRESENT are three block-based lightweight ciphers that can be used for encrypting sensitive data. To understand the desired goal of security and privacy, this paper focuses on modeling of major lightweight ciphers, namely Advanced Encryption Standard (AES), Tiny Encryption Algorithm (TEA), and PRESENT algorithms, using the Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) hardware platform. This paper presents the implementation and pseudocode for the AES, TEA, and PRESENT ciphers in Xilinx Vivado 2016. The evaluation performance is based on the metrics namely resource utilization, area, throughput, maximum frequency, latency, and energy consumption. The results demonstrate that the PRESENT cipher performs better in terms of resource efficiency compared to AES and TEA ciphers by 32% and 60%, respectively. Regarding maximum frequency, latency, and throughput, the TEA cipher performs better. However, when compared to energy efficiency, the PRESENT cipher consumes less than AES and TEA ciphers. AES and TEA ciphers consume 68.82% and 96.6% of the energy, respectively. In conclusion, considering resource utilization and energy consumption, the PRESENT cipher is recommended, and the TEA cipher is recommended for performance in IoT domain applications.

title: Guided Model Merging for Hybrid Data Learning: Leveraging Centralized Data to Refine Decentralized Models
authors: Junyi Zhu, Ruicong Yao, Taha Ceritli, Savas Ozkan, Matthew B. Blaschko, Eunchung Noh, Jeongwon Min, Cho Jung Min, Mete Ozay
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Current network training paradigms primarily focus on either centralized or decentralized data regimes. However, in practice, data availability often exhibits a hybrid nature, where both regimes coexist. This hybrid setting presents new opportunities for model training, as the two regimes offer complementary trade-offs: decentralized data is abundant but subject to heterogeneity and communication constraints, while centralized data—though limited in volume and potentially unrepresentative—enables better curation and high-throughput access. Despite its potential, effectively combining these paradigms remains challenging, and few frameworks are tailored to hybrid data regimes. To address this, we propose a novel framework that constructs a model atlas from decentralized models and leverages centralized data to refine a global model within this structured space. The refined model is then used to reinitialize the decentralized models. Our method synergizes federated learning (to exploit decentralized data) and model merging (to utilize centralized data), enabling effective training under hybrid data availability. Theoretically, we show that our approach achieves faster convergence than methods relying solely on decentralized data, due to variance reduction in the merging process. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our framework consistently outperforms purely centralized, purely decentralized, and existing hybrid-adaptable methods. Notably, our method remains robust even when the centralized and decentralized data domains differ or when decentralized data contains noise, significantly broadening its applicability.

title: DNA: Dual-branch Network with Adaptation for Open-Set Online Handwriting Generation
