authors: Tsai-Ling Huang, Nhat-Tuong Do-Tran, Ngoc-Hoang-Lam Le, Hong-Han Shuai, Ching-Chun Huang
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Online handwriting generation (OHG) enhances handwriting recognition models by synthesizing diverse, human-like samples. However, existing OHG methods struggle to generate unseen characters, particularly in glyph-based languages like Chinese, limiting their real-world applicability. In this paper, we introduce our method for OHG, where the writer’s style and the characters generated during testing are unseen during training. To tackle this challenge, we propose a Dual-branch Network with Adaptation (DNA), which comprises an adaptive style branch and an adaptive content branch. The style branch learns stroke attributes such as writing direction, spacing, placement, and flow to generate realistic handwriting. Meanwhile, the content branch is designed to generalize effectively to unseen characters by decomposing character content into structural information and texture details, extracted via local and global encoders, respectively. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our DNA model is well-suited for the unseen OHG setting, achieving state-of-the-art performance.

title: Narrating For You: Prompt-guided Audio-visual Narrating Face Generation Employing Multi-entangled Latent Space
authors: Aashish Chandra K, Aashutosh A V, Abhijit Das
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: We present a novel approach for generating realistic speaking and talking faces by synthesizing a person’s voice and facial movements from a static image, a voice profile, and a target text. The model encodes the prompt/driving text, the driving image, and the voice profile of an individual and then combines them to pass them to the multi-entangled latent space to foster key-value pairs and queries for the audio and video modality generation pipeline. The multi-entangled latent space is responsible for establishing the spatiotemporal person-specific features between the modalities. Further, entangled features are passed to the respective decoder of each modality for output audio and video generation. Our experiments and analysis through standard metrics demonstrate the effectiveness of our model. All model checkpoints, code, and the proposed dataset can be found at https://github.com/narratingForYou/NarratingForYou.

title: FLoMo-Net: A Novel Task-Adaptive Mixture of Experts Routing Framework with Frequency and Uncertainty Correction for Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Md Rayhan Ahmed, Patricia Lasserre
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Medical image segmentation (MIS) is challenged by anatomical variability, ambiguous boundaries, and subtle textures, demanding an efficient balance between fine local details and global context. Existing architectures often suffer from suboptimal fusion of spatial and frequency-domain features, limiting their ability to capture richer structural and textural representations. To specifically address these challenges, we introduce FLoMo-Net, a modular MIS architecture organized around a principled route → select → refine → correct pipeline: (1) The Local–Global Mixture of Experts encoder adaptively routes features across specialized convolutional branches to capture scale-appropriate context; (2) The Dual-Attention Selective Aggregator then jointly selects informative channels and spatial regions using frequency-guided modulation; (3) At the bridge, the Frequency-Aware Multi-Scale Refinement module refines edges and textures through explicit low/high-frequency decomposition; (4) Finally, the False Positive/Negative Corrective Attention Module leverages uncertainty, derived from entropy and cosine dissimilarity, to produce a residual corrective mask that suppresses semantic drift and improves boundary delineation in the decoder stages. Across four MIS benchmarks, FLoMo-Net achieves superior boundary-aware performance and faster inference with fewer parameters than prior state-of-the-art 2D MIS models. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/rayhan-ahmed91/FLoMo-Net.

title: Learning Group Actions In Disentangled Latent Image Representations
authors: Farhana Hossain Swarnali, Miaomiao Zhang, Tonmoy Hossain
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Modeling group actions on latent representations enables controllable transformations of high-dimensional image data. Prior works applying group-theoretic priors or modeling transformations typically operate in the high-dimensional data space, where group actions apply uniformly across the entire input, making it difficult to disentangle the subspace that varies under transformations. While latent-space methods offer greater flexibility, they still require manual partitioning of latent variables into equivariant and invariant subspaces, limiting the ability to robustly learn and operate group actions within the representation space. To address this, we introduce a novel end-to-end framework that for the first time learns group actions on latent image manifolds, automatically discovering transformation-relevant structures without manual intervention. Our method uses learnable binary masks with straight-through estimation to dynamically partition latent representations into transformation-sensitive and invariant components. We formulate this within a unified optimization framework that jointly learns latent disentanglement and group transformation mappings. The framework can be seamlessly integrated with any standard encoder-decoder architecture. We validate our approach on five 2D/3D image datasets, demonstrating its ability to automatically learn disentangled latent factors for group actions in diverse data, while downstream classification tasks confirm the effectiveness of the learned representations. Our code is publicly available at GitHub.

title: Honeypots to Spot Personal Account Credential Stuffing Attacks
authors: Trilochan Patra, Swarup Kumar Mitra, Ujjal Bhattacharya, Shampurna Rakshit, Alakesh Hazra
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: The rate of credential-stuffing attacks is rising exponentially, as cybercriminals look for ways to break into accounts on different sites using stolen login credentials and passwords. Despite much research into safeguarding large enterprises from such attacks, there are few techniques focused on protecting individual accounts. This paper describes the development and use of honeypots for detecting credential stuffing attacks against personal web accounts (email, social media). This “The Spawn” Honeypots are used to make phoney accounts that act like real users to lure attackers into using stolen credentials. This research analyzes how well these honeypots identify and derive credential-stuffing techniques and focuses on the ease at which non-technical users can perform these attacks. Providing users with an accessible and affordable way to proactively detect and combat the threat of credential-based attacks. This paper aims to bring user personal cyber security closer to realization. These findings demonstrate the feasibility of the use of honeypots as an operational countermeasure against growing cyber-threats directed towards individual online identities. The paper addresses a gap in personal cyber security by presenting a widely available and cost effective way for an individual to detect and mitigate credential-based risks.

title: ST-Think: How Multimodal Large Language Models Reason About 4D Worlds from Ego-Centric Videos
authors: Peiran Wu, Yunze Liu, Miao Liu, Junxiao Shen
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Humans excel at spatial-temporal reasoning, effortlessly interpreting dynamic visual events from an egocentric viewpoint. However, whether multimodal large language models (MLLMs) can similarly understand the 4D world remains uncertain. This paper explores multimodal spatial-temporal reasoning from an egocentric perspective, aiming to equip MLLMs with human-like reasoning capabilities. To support this objective, we introduce Ego-ST Bench, a novel benchmark containing over 5,000 question-answer pairs across four categories, systematically evaluating spatial, temporal, and integrated spatial-temporal reasoning. Additionally, we propose ST-R1 training paradigm, a video-based reasoning model that incorporates reverse thinking into its reinforcement learning process, significantly enhancing performance. We combine long-chain-of-thought (long-CoT) supervised fine-tuning with Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) reinforcement learning, achieving notable improvements with limited high-quality data. Ego-ST Bench and ST-R1 provide valuable insights and resources for advancing video-based spatial-temporal reasoning research.

title: Preliminary Evaluation of Polymer-Based Mechanical Intracranial Pressure Sensor With Ultrasound Readout
authors: Colleen Chemerka, Lukas Selzer, Florian Solzbacher
abstract: Intracranial hypertension requires continuous monitoring to improve patient outcomes. However, existing implantable sensors rely on electronic or semiconductor components that require active power, limiting long-term stability and compatibility with necessary diagnostic tools. This study introduces a novel working principle of a fully mechanical, passive, polymer-based pressure sensor that utilizes ultrasound as a readout method. The sensor translates exerted pressure into a geometrical change within the micromachined structure, thereby altering ultrasound transmission or reflection properties. In a water tank, experiments showed that ultrasound transmission was significantly modulated between different sensor configurations or states. The pressure sensors representing no applied pressure showed, on average, more than twice the transmission loss compared to the simulated pressure. Experimental data are in good agreement with a one-dimensional mathematical model. Compared to current alternatives that rely on resonance-based behavior, the proposed transmission approach is less sensitive to manufacturing variation and material aging. These findings suggest a promising alternative to current pressure sensors with the prospect of allowing long-term monitoring and implantation.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Optimization of Turbo Decoder Iterations for Low-Power IoT Devices
authors: P.Balakrishna, Pramod Kumar P
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The exponential growth of Internet of Things (IoT) networks demands low-power and highly reliable data transmission despite constrained energy budgets and dynamic wireless channels. Traditional turbo decoders, although proven in 4G and 5G systems, rely on fixed iteration counts that cause unnecessary energy consumption in favorable channel conditions and inadequate correction in adverse ones. This paper presents a Reinforcement Learning-Based Optimization Framework that adaptively controls the number of decoding iterations in real time based on channel observations. Using a Q-Learning model integrated with a Log-MAP turbo decoder, the system learns an optimal iteration policy that minimizes power consumption without degrading bit-error-rate (BER) performance. The framework is implemented entirely in MATLAB and Python, and results under AWGN and Rayleigh fading channels show up to 34 % energy savings and 28 % latency reduction compared to conventional decoders. This approach provides a scalable software solution for energy-aware, intelligent IoT communication and forms a foundational step toward autonomous physical-layer adaptation in future 6G systems.

title: Key Frame Optimization of Videos Using Image Captioning Model
authors: Mohammed Inayathulla, A Lakshman, V Sujay, SMP Qubeb, KR Thukaram, G Raju
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: One of the most reliable information mediums is video, and in recent years, both online and offline video consumption has risen to previously unheard-of heights. In contrast to images, where context can be understood from a single frame, videos require the viewer to watch the entire clip in order to be understood. This presents a fundamental problem for information extraction from videos. To reduce the length of the video an optimized method to extract key frames of the video is proposed. Transformers based image captioning model is implemented as the first step in our work. After extracting the key frames using an unsupervised clustering technique, we have used the captioning model and a scoring function to determine how similar a frame is to a manually compiled corpus of videos belonging to a particular category. Next, we choose only those frames from the entire video that have similarity scores that are higher than the threshold. We conducted experiments on different categories of videos from TVSUM50 dataset and the findings were satisfactory.

title: RSTMG: A Computationally Efficient Framework for Region Specific Tissue Segmentation of Whole Slide Images
authors: Pillai Praveen Thulasidharan, Leena Mary, Keshab Nath, Jayakrushna Sahoo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Region or patch based tissue segmentation is an important pre-processing step during the computerized analysis of giga-pixel histopathology images like the Whole Slide Image (WSI). The existing techniques iterate the segmentation logic on every patch individually requiring to store each patch separately, thereby resulting in more memory usage. Here, we propose a framework that first uses the low-resolution version of the original high-resolution WSI to generate context information about the overall tissue boundary, which is then deployed to generate the region-wise tissue boundary of the original image. The context is computed only once taking into account the scaling data available from the WSI's pyramidal structure and without explicitly processing the high-resolution WSI. This considerably reduces the quantity of image entities that are processed thereby achieving less memory usage compared to the existing techniques. Furthermore, experimental results on standard datasets show that the method achieves more than 90 % segmentation accuracy with 0.80 Jaccard's Index.

title: Reinforcement Learning Driven Augmentation in Self-Supervised Human Activity Recognition
authors: Dhvani Vachhani, Khetwat Saritha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) has attracted significant research attention due to its wide applicability in domains such as healthcare, smart homes, fitness monitoring and context awareness. Recent advances in self-supervised learning have shown strong potential for HAR by enabling representation learning from unlabeled sensor data. However, the effectiveness of self-supervised learning critically depends on the choice of data augmentations used to construct positive pairs, which determines the invariances enforced during representation learning. Most existing approaches rely on fixed or manually tuned augmentation strategies, which may fail to adapt significant variability across data and often lead to suboptimal representations. This limitation motivates treating augmentation selection as a dynamic decision process rather than a static design choice. To address this limitation, we propose an adaptive augmentation framework for self-supervised HAR built on the SimSiam model. Augmentation selection is formulated as a sequential decision-making problem and optimized using the REINFORCE reinforcement learning algorithm, enabling dynamic selection among jittering, scaling, and a composite resampling operation consisting of time shifting, magnitude warping, and time masking based on training feedback. The learned augmentation policy is integrated into a two-stage pipeline consisting of self-supervised pretraining of an encoder followed by supervised fine-tuning of classifier. The effectiveness of the proposed framework is validated using three widely adopted benchmark datasets for HAR, namely MHEALTH, HAPT, and UCI HAR—under varying levels of label availability. Extensive experiments with 10%, 20%, and 30% labeled data demonstrate that the proposed method consistently achieves superior performance even in label scarce conditions compared to fixed augmentation strategies and competitive supervised, semi-supervised and self-supervised state-of-art. These results highlight the effectiveness of reinforcement-driven adaptive augmentation in improving representation robustness and label efficiency for sensor-based HAR.

title: Image-Based Obfuscated Malware Detection Using Lightweight Binarized Neural Network Architecture
authors: Khushboo Das, Dhruba Kumar Bhattacharyya
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: With the rapid proliferation of increasingly sophisticated malware, there is a critical need for robust and efficient detection methods capable of identifying both obfuscated and non-obfuscated threats. Image-based malware classification using deep learning offers significant advantages by enabling the automatic learning of complex patterns, thereby eliminating the need for manual feature engineering. In this work, two deep learning frameworks were designed to accurately classify obfuscated and non-obfuscated samples of both benign and malicious Android applications. The publicly available AndroDex dataset is utilized for experimental validation. The proposed approaches are evaluated and compared against state-of-the-art transfer learning models, including EfficientNetB7 and MobileNetV3Large, in terms of classification performance and model complexity. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed binarized neural network achieves competitive performance while significantly reducing parameter size. Specifically, the model attains average 10-fold cross-validation scores of 94.27% accuracy, 95% precision, 94.23% recall, and 94.27% F1-score. These findings highlight the potential of the proposed approach for effective and lightweight malware detection, particularly in resource-constrained hardware such as microcontrollers, FPGAs, etc., because the network has very low memory footprints. Moreover, the deployment of the proposed framework on hardware devices enabling near real-time detection may be considered an intriguing possibility for future research.

title: Automated Feature Selection and Hyperparameter Optimized XGBoost for Multi-Domain Disease Prediction
authors: G.V. Vidya Lakshmi, Sanjay Mandru, Deepak Reddy Avuthu, Anitha Rani Palakayala, Kommineni Madhavi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The proposed method aims to enhance patient out-comes and optimization of healthcare resources by early and accurate prediction of chronic diseases, based on patient real-world data, through an integrative machine learning framework of multi-disease prediction, specifically heart disease, breast cancer and diabetes. The data processing commenced with the preprocessing of the data, including imputation. For missing values, encoding of categories, feature scaling, class balancing with the use of SMOTE, and correlation analysis. Five standard machine learning models-Support Vector Machine, Random Forest, K-Nearest Neighbors, XGBoost, and Decision Tree-were trained and tested. The improvements to model interpretability included the following visual tools like feature heatmaps, ROC curves, performance comparison charts and confusion matrices. Comparative analysis between diseases revealed good and interpretable results, and the optimized feature selection was a success. This framework is easily generalizable to the modular designed to an increasing number of disease domains, which is a possible solution to comprehensive multi-disease prediction in real-world healthcare settings.

title: Zero-Day Detection of AI-Generated Images Using Perturbation-Response Analysis and Zero-Shot CLIP Embeddings
authors: Pooja S, Ezhil Arasi V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: The advancement of AI-generated image synthesis models, such as GANs and Diffusion Models, has quickly progressed and has continued to not only blur but eventually remove the lines between real and synthetic images, particularly in cases where the image generator is not known. Existing detection techniques often require retraining, thus lacking generalizability to all attacks and thus are only applicable to non-zero-day attacks. The paper introduces a retrain-free, generator-agnostic detection system that combines perturbation-response analysis with zero-shot CLIP semantic embeddings. The method adds controlled pixel-level and semantic-level perturbations to input images and checks the stability of their CLIP embeddings to discriminate between real and synthetic images. The system utilizes CLIP's strong cross-domain generalization to identify effectively AI-generated images without having been trained on the generator. Large-scale experiments carried out on benchmark datasets such as FFHQ, StyleGAN2, DIRE, PromptBench, and Spectral exhibit higher accuracy, robustness, and flexibility. The results verify that integrating perturbation-response analysis with semantic embedding evaluation offers a scalable, retrain-free, and highly reliable framework for zero-day detection of synthetic images, with implications for visual forensics, media authenticity verification, and AI-generated content monitoring.

title: HiGlassRM: Learning to Remove High-prescription Glasses via Synthetic Dataset Generation
authors: Sebin Lee, Heewon Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Existing eyeglass removal methods can handle frames and shadows but fail to correct lens-induced geometric distortions, as public datasets lack the necessary supervision. To address this, we introduce the HiGlass Dataset, the first large-scale synthetic dataset providing explicit flow-based supervision for refractive warping. We also propose Hi-GlassRM, a novel pipeline whose core is a network that explicitly estimates a displacement flowmap to de-warp distorted facial geometry. Experiments on both synthetic and real images show that this flowmap-centric approach, trained on our data, significantly improves identity preservation and perceptual quality over existing methods. Our work demonstrates that explicitly modeling and correcting geometric distortion via flowmap estimation, enabled by targeted supervision, is key to faithful eyeglass removal. Project page: https://higlassrm.github.io/

title: Hybrid Machine Learning approach and Statistical Ensemble Model for Spatiotemporal Crop Yield Prediction
authors: N Satish, K Akash, A Kishor, M Kumaravel, Gokulraj, K Sabari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Prediction of crop yield is necessary to ensure food security, timely and accurate strategic agricultural planning and management of resources. The proposed research presents a new hybrid modeling system, which is Statistical Ensemble Stacked-Hybrid Machine Learning System (SES-HMS) and the aim of this paper is to predict crop yield using spatiotemporal environmental, meteorological, and geospatial data. It combines satellite-derived NDVI, soil parameters, rainfall, temperature, and past yield statistics via uncommon processing methods including Spatiotemporal Kriging, Empirical Mode Decomposition (EMD), and Symbolic Aggregate approximator (SAX) to preprocess and upscale the data respectively. The ensemble structure is consisting of Bayesian Hierarchical Yield Modeling with sophisticated machine learning architectures such as Gradient Boosting, LSTM and Gaussian Process Regression. Quantile Regression Averaging is used to refine and conformal prediction is used to calibrate yield prediction to assure uncertainty estimation. Ranging experiments carried out in five different agro-ecological zones also indicate that SES-HMS is significantly more effective at prediction with a mean accuracy of 94.6 and lowering of RMSE by more than 18 % relative to conventional methods. The suggested approach guarantees the spatiotemporal compatibility of the predictions and demonstrates a high level of generalizability to various climatic scenarios. These findings confirm the possibility of using SES-HMS as a powerful, scalable and interpretable tool in data-scarce settings to determine precision agriculture and agricultural policy-making.

title: AllerSafe: Real-Time Allergen Detection Using Knowledge Graph-Enhanced Transformer Classification
authors: Samanvitha L, Varun Aditya, Tanisha R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Problem: Globally allergies caused by food has affected 8% of children and 11% of adults, and due to barcode-only lookup only 40-70% coverage gaps exist for regional markets. Method: This paper AllerSafe, combines OCR-based extraction of text, a domain-specific knowledge graph (AllerGraph: 847 nodes, 2,156 edges), and transformer-based DistilBERT classification for detection of allergens in real-time. AllerGraph addresses semantic blindness by encoding ingredient-allergen derivation paths. Results: Evaluation demonstrates 94.3% OCR accuracy, 96.7% classification F1-score (95% CI: [96.2%, 97.1%]) across nine FDA allergen categories, and 4.7s average response time. The full system improves F1 by 18.4% over keyword matching, with AllerGraph contributing +7.3% over DistilBERT alone. Field deployment with 2,847 users achieved 76% repeat usage and 98.2% product coverage. Conclusion: The proposed system demonstrates practical utility for real-world allergen detection.

title: Quantum-Enhanced Cross-Attention for Multi-Modal Image-Text Retrieval
authors: Gnanajeyaraman Rajaram, M Surya Narayanan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Multi-modal systems for vision-language tasks of- ten struggle with semantic ambiguity. Overlapping or context- dependent features in cross-modal representations lead to poor alignment and performance. To tackle this, we introduce Quantum-Enhanced Fusion (QE-Fusion), a new cross-attention framework inspired by quantum interference principles. QE- Fusion works in a complex-valued domain. This allows it to capture both magnitude and phase information in cross-modal attention scores.By representing queries and keys as complex-valued tensors, QE-Fusion simulates both constructive and destructive interference through the magnitude of their complex dot product. This mechanism enables the model to dynamically boost relevant cross-modal correlations while reducing irrelevant ones. As a result, it achieves better feature disambiguation. Unlike standard attention mechanisms, which use real-valued scores, QE-Fusion’s phase-aware computation offers more flexibility for modeling complex feature interactions.The framework is implemented as a modular PyTorch layer, making it easy to integrate into existing multi-modal Transformer architectures. We validated our approach using the Flickr8k image-text retrieval benchmark. While initial training showed some instability, we improved this by adjusting the learning rate. After tuning, the stabilized QE-Fusion model showed clear and consistent performance gains compared to the standard attention baseline. These results position QE-Fusion as a strong and efficient method for strengthening the reasoning abilities of multi-modal systems. They also highlight the potential of quantum-inspired principles in traditional deep learning models.

title: Unlocking Prospects of Digital Twin in Healthcare
authors: Nayanjyoti Deka, Anjana Raut, Swati Samantaray, Purnendu Bhushan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: This article discusses the application of artificial intelligence and digital twin technology with a focus on its use in healthcare and individualized treatment planning. Deep AI methods like convolutional neural networks and hybrid mathematical models facilitate dynamic, predictive virtual patient modeling, allowing anatomical segmentation, landmark detection, and treatment simulation with improved accuracy and efficiency. Substantial advancements are observed in various domains of medical science with multimodal imaging and real-time data integration. The work also assesses new ideas like blockchain-secured data management and the metaverse, with implications for data security and operational efficiency. Technical, clinical, and regulatory challenges are critically discussed, with recommendations for standardized protocols, AI applications, and effective privacy measures. The paper intends to explore AI-augmented digital twin platforms synergizing individualized, predictive, and preventive patient management, while shaping their responsible and feasible use in the clinical environment.

title: Quantum Machine Learning–Driven AI Predictive Model for Cluster-Level Optimization of Manufacturing Line
authors: Rajan Sharma, Nasir Khan, Pushyamitra Mishra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: This paper presents a Quantum Machine Learning– Driven AI Predictive Model (QML–AI PM) for cluster-level optimization of manufacturing lines. The proposed framework integrates Quantum Features Encoding (QFE), Variation Quantum Circuits (V.Q.C.), and the Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) with advanced AI models to address uncertainty, nonlinear behavior, and high-dimensional interactions in industrial environments. A combined dataset of multi-cluster manufacturing operations was used to evaluate the system, and performance was compared with classical models such as SVM, RF, LSTM, and XGBoost. Results show that the proposed model achieves 94.6% prediction accuracy, 93.4% optimization efficiency, and lower computation time, outperforming all baseline techniques. The hybrid architecture significantly improves scheduling, resource allocation, and predictive maintenance at the cluster scale. Overall, the QML–AI PM demonstrates substantial potential for next-generation smart manufacturing and establishes a foundation for future quantum-enabled industrial optimization.

title: Boosting Brand Recognition Within Large Language Models and AI-Driven Search Systems
authors: Uma Uppin, Vibha Uppin
abstract: This paper provides a comprehensive understanding of how brand visibility occurs in AI Large Language Models (LLMs) and Augmented Engine Optimization (AEO). The review paper examines the fundamental differences between static pretrained LLMs and search-augmented systems, establishing strategic frameworks for monitoring, feedback implementation, and optimization strategies to enhance brand visibility in LLMs and search-augmented systems. Through systematic analysis of platform architectures, and optimization methodologies, this study reveals that brand awareness correlates with AI mentions [4]. The findings demonstrate that approximately 9 0% of ChatGPT's citations originate from search results ranking beyond position 21, indicating AI systems access broader content pools than traditional search engines. [1] This research contributes novel insights into the evolution of digital marketing from traditional Search Engine Optimization (SEO) to Generative Engine Optimization (GEO), providing actionable strategies for businesses seeking enhanced visibility in AI-powered search environments.

title: OpenCowID: Zero-Shot Visual Identification of Dairy Cows
authors: Omkar Prabhune, Younghyun Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Accurate identification of individual cows is essential to precision dairy farming. While computer vision offers a non-invasive alternative to ear tags and RFID systems, its practical deployment remains limited by the need for zero-shot identification in dynamic herds where test identities are unseen during training. In this work, we propose OpenCowID, a unified framework that addresses this challenge. First, we introduce a stochastic cow coat synthesis pipeline that efficiently generates large-scale, diverse images. Second, using the generated large-scale high-quality data, we present a centroid-guided feature learning strategy that forms a well-structured embedding space using virtual class centroids, enabling generalization to unseen identities. OpenCowID achieves state-of-the-art zero-shot and open-set identification on real-world cow benchmarks, without requiring any real labeled training data. This work contributes to the advancement of automated livestock monitoring, enabling robust, non-invasive identification. Code is available at https://github.com/neis-lab/OpenCowID.

title: Network Packet Sniffer and Traffic Analyzer for Real-Time Data Monitoring
authors: C R Rathish, Sooraj Naik, Rahul Patil, Srushti Navale, Ravikumar A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: The exponential growth in digital communication has made modern networks increasingly complex and vulnerable to performance degradation and security threats. This paper presents the design and development of a real-time network packet sniffer and traffic analyzer aimed at enhancing data flow visibility, improving operational efficiency, and identifying potential cybersecurity risks. Leveraging robust packet-capturing libraries and advanced traffic analysis algorithms, our system captures, decodes, and visualizes live network traffic to extract critical parameters such as protocol type, IP addresses, packet size, and payload content. The tool incorporates real-time anomaly detection and customizable filters to flag irregularities, offering actionable insights to network administrators. Designed with scalability and usability in mind, it provides a graphical interface for monitoring bandwidth utilization, protocol distribution, and security alerts. By enabling proactive management and deep analysis of network activity, this system addresses the increasing demand for secure, reliable, and transparent digital infrastructure across enterprises and institutions.

title: RhythmSense-DSTNet: A Dual-Stream Spatiotemporal Learning Framework with Selective Attention for Robust ECG Arrhythmia Detection
authors: Md. Shamshad Begum, Nimmagadda Padmaja
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: In this paper, RhythmSense-DSTNet, a multi-class ECG signal arrhythmia detection hybrid deep spatiotemporal neural network, is proposed. The model combines the dual-stream time refinement with specific emphasis on features, making it possible to highly discriminate in a wide range of the cardiac rhythms despite noise and morphological distortion. RhythmSense-DSTNet reaches 98.92% accuracy, 98.61% sensitivity, 99.15% specificity, and F1-score of 98.74, which is 24000% worse than the state-of-the-art baselines. The high-resolution ROC curves also demonstrate that the AUC is close to 0.99 in all the major rhythm classes, which is a good indicator of a robust generalization ability. The findings reveal that RhythmSense-DSTNet can be used to screen arrhythmia and help in clinical decision-making in a real-time manner.

title: ZonUI-3B: Competitive GUI Grounding with a 3B VLM Trained on a Single Consumer GPU
authors: ZongHan Hsieh, ShengJing Yang, Tzer-Jen Wei
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: In this paper, we present ZonUI-3B, a lightweight Vision-Language Model (VLM) that can be fully trained on a single consumer-grade GPU (RTX 4090) while delivering performance comparable to significantly larger models on GUI grounding tasks. The model incorporates several key innovations: (i) integration of multiple open-source datasets into a cross-platform, multi-resolution corpus of 24K examples covering mobile, desktop, and web GUIs, effectively addressing data scarcity in high-resolution desktop environments; (ii) a two-stage fine-tuning strategy, where initial cross-platform training builds robust GUI understanding, followed by specialized fine-tuning on high-resolution data to enhance adaptability; and (iii) data curation with redundancy reduction, where our results reveal that training on a smaller randomly sampled subset can maintain accuracy while significantly reducing training time, highlighting that data diversity is more critical than sheer dataset scale. Empirical evaluation on standard GUI grounding benchmarks—including ScreenSpot, ScreenSpot-v2, GroundUI-1K, and the challenging ScreenSpot-Pro—demonstrates ZonUI-3B’s strong performance, achieving 84.9% on ScreenSpot, 86.4% on ScreenSpot-v2, and 82.4% on GroundUI-1K, surpassing all prior models under 4B parameters. Ablation studies further confirm the effectiveness of two-stage fine-tuning in improving robustness across diverse GUI environments, particularly in high-resolution desktop scenarios.

title: Learnify-Massively Online Open Courseware
authors: Archana Das, Neetu, Aditya U, Deekshith, K Sinchana, Kavya Vasant Saraf
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: This study presents Learnify, a cutting-edge MOOC platform that uses AI-driven customization and engagement to revolutionize online learning. Using collaborative filtering to recommend courses and reinforcement learning algorithms to adjust quiz difficulty, the system tailors each user's learning experience according to their behavior and performance. Sentiment analysis enhances the mood-based user interface by increasing its emotional responsiveness. The platform is more responsive and scalable thanks to its modular design, which integrates React.js, Node.js, and MongoDB. Compared to standard MOOCs, the review revealed a 32% increase in learner engagement and a 27% increase in course completion. This approach would transform the way personalized e-learning operates by fusing AI intelligence with game-like and community-driven element

title: An Efficient Retinal Vessel Segmentation Through Modified NOA Algorithm based Feature Selection and CrossViT for Feature Extraction
authors: M Soujanya, A Swathi, Imad Shalout, Preeti Khurana, G. Ramkumar, Chitukula Navya Sree
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Early identification of retinal vessel abnormalities is critical to diagnosing vision-related diseases. However, the methods currently in use are encountering difficulties differentiating small vessels and complex vascular patterns, especially when the data is unbalanced. This work, therefore, proposes the Enhanced Retinal Vessel Segmentation and Neuro-Classification (ERVSNC) model, which is based on multi-scale feature extraction, refined feature selection, and transformer-assisted convolutional learning. The main data undergo pre-processing and training through three benchmark datasets, DRIVE, CHASEDB1, and HRF, to guarantee the model’s robustness under varying imaging scenarios. A CrossViT architecture to capture detailed retinal features through a dual-branch attention mechanism. A CrossViT architecture to capture detailed retinal features through a dual-branch attention mechanism and a changed Northern Owl Optimisation (NOA) to get rid of the most irrelevant features, thus making the classifier more efficient, is employed. The simulation analysis is ERVSNC loss of 3.0, F1-Score of 0.98 at 4096 points, fitness value of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> to 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> at 300 iterations, ERVSNC average of 98%, 91%, 88%, and 84%, and accuracy calculations of 99%.

title: TihanFly: A Multi-Drone Ground Control Station for Scalable UAV Operations
authors: Pavan Sai Kumar Thummala, Mohan Kumar J, Syam Narayanan S, B. Tarun Kumar, Raju Santhani, P. Rajalakshmi
abstract: Advanced Air Mobility (AAM) and multi UAV operations demand ground control systems that scale beyond the single-drone paradigm. Conventional ground control stations (GCS) face limitations in scalability, real time responsiveness, and integration with emerging use cases such as Beyond Visual Line of Sight (BVLOS) and heavy payload flights. We introduce TihanFly, an indigenous GCS designed for multi-drone connectivity, real-time monitoring, and mission planning. The architecture employs a producer consumer threading model for parallel telemetry handling and a link manager that assigns each UAV a unique identifier for efficient retrieval, control, and scalable multi-UAV management. TihanFly has been validated in BVLOS and heavy payload trials, demonstrating robust performance and scalability. Beyond core command and control, it integrates external services including weather and traffic data enhancing situational awareness and flight safety. These features position TihanFly as an AAM-ready GCS capable of enabling swarm coordination, proactive airspace monitoring, and mission critical applications in high density aerial environments.

title:
A Robust Supervisory Control Framework with H
                    <sub>∞</sub>
                    Strategy for Hybrid Renewable Energy Systems
authors: Pranati Rani Purohit, Arnab Ghosh, Pravat Kumar Ray
abstract: The rapid growth of renewable energy sources (RES) such as photovoltaic (PV) and wind power has made low-voltage DC micro grids (LVDC-MGs) a key solution for efficient and reliable power delivery. However, their intermittent nature, coupled with realistic delays and CPL, introduces voltage instability, power imbalance, and increased stress on energy storage systems. This paper proposes an Improved Supervisory Power Management Strategy (ISPMS) integrated with delay aware robust discrete-time H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">∞</sub> controller for a PV-wind-battery-super capacitor (SC) based DC micro grid, addressing these challenges. The ISPMS introduces feed-forward compensation from renewable generation and DC bus voltage error, along with an error-based scaling gain in the SC current loop, enabling superior disturbance rejection, reduced oscillations, and minimized battery stress. An H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">∞</sub> controller is employed in the voltage regulation loop, while the current loop combines a PI and H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">∞</sub> controller to achieve both steady-state accuracy and robustness against uncertainties for a real-world scenario. A comparative analysis demonstrates that the proposed strategy ensures an improved dynamic response, simplified tuning, and effective power sharing. It establishes a reliable DC bus voltage control framework in renewable-integrated LVDC micro grids.

title: Hybrid Direct-Optimization Distributed Small-Signal Modeling of GaN HEMTs Toward Reliable mm-Wave Circuit Design
authors: Aisha M. Mansour, Husna Hamza, Julie Roslita Rusli, Anwar Jarndal
abstract: Operating Gallium Nitride (GaN) transistors at high frequencies, especially in the millimeter-wave (mm-Wave) range, introduces additional parasitic effects that significantly impact the RF characteristics of device. In such cases, a distributed-element model becomes essential. However, direct extraction of these distributed elements is challenging, particularly for large, multi-finger devices. As a result, optimization-based methods present one of the most effective alternatives. This research focuses on modeling a 22-element small-signal distributed equivalent circuit of GaN high electron mobility transistors (HEMTs). A parameter extraction procedure based on a hybrid approach, combining direct extraction and global optimization, was developed and applied to GaN HEMT on Diamond substrate. Further improvement is obtained by fine tuning the model parameters using Levenberg-Marquardt (LM) local optimization method. The proposed modeling approach was implemented in MATLAB software and applied to GaN HEMTs of different sizes. The model was validated by comparing simulated and measured S-parameters up to 80 GHz under various bias conditions to verify its applicability for designing mm-Wave application circuits. The results show excellent agreement, confirming the accuracy and effectiveness of the proposed techniques for reliable design of RF circuits of next-generation communication systems. Moreover, the whole model has been implemented in advanced design system (ADS) software and demonstrated for designing microwave and mm-Wave linear amplifiers for different applications.

title: Interactive News Analytics for Everyday Investors
authors: Andrew P. Berg, Qian Zhang, Mia Y. Wang
abstract: The financial analytics industry, propelled by Generative Artificial Intelligence (GAI), holds immense potential to transform investment decision-making; however, high-cost solutions, often priced in the five- to six-figure range remain inaccessible to everyday investors. We propose a framework to harness GAI for affordable, high-quality news sentiment analysis and stock research; empowering both consumer and professional investors. By leveraging live data feeds from major news outlets, the interface will deliver actionable insights into market trends and sentiment-driven stock performance on a minute by minute basis. Our methodology will explore the integration of GAIdriven natural language processing with financial analytics to create a user-friendly, cost-effective platform. Potential methodologies include sentiment modeling, emerging narratives, and visualization tools, drawing on interdisciplinary insights from data science, finance, and human-computer interaction. The framework addresses barriers to entry in financial analytics by prioritizing scalability and accessibility without compromising analytical rigor or reliable audit logs.

title: Diverse Source-Aware Context Retrieval for Multi-Hop Queries: LLM-Powered Keyword Filtering and Cross-Source Ranking
authors: Yuta Tsuchiya, Masayoshi Mase, Hiroya Matsuba
abstract: We propose a novel Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) approach that addresses complex queries requiring evidence from multiple documents with similar content but different specifications. Industrial equipment in energy, rail transport, and manufacturing often remains in service for over 20 years. This results in an extensive collection of maintenance manuals and troubleshooting guides covering different product generations and numerous customer-specific configurations. Although these documents may appear similar, they often have critical differences in specifications. Therefore, referencing the wrong manual can lead to serious misunderstandings and critical operational errors for maintenance engineers. We propose a lightweight, agentic RAG approach that consists of three components: 1. LLM-Based Keyword Extraction: LLM is used to extract salient keywords from the user's query, separating them into “topical” keywords and “source” keywords, avoiding generating hallucinated content; 2. Document Routing via Keyword Filtering: the extracted “source” keywords are used to filter the document corpus, effectively narrowing the search space and ensuring evidence is gathered from appropriate sources; 3. Keyword-Weighted Hybrid Retrieval with Source-wise Ranking: in addition to dense vector similarity and sparse lexical relevance, we incorporate a score based on the extracted “topical” keywords to better capture query intent, and create a ranking for each source to ensure diverse and relevant context for the generator LLM. The method was evaluated on the MultiHop-RAG benchmark, a dataset designed for complex queries requiring evidence from multiple sources. Compared to standard hybrid search and metadata filtering, our method improved Recall@4 by 29.5% and achieved better generation performance (F1 score: 0.7523 vs. 0.7156). The method is currently in production use as a maintenance inquiry agent for troubleshooting support at a service center.

title: DOS-Diff: Degradation-aware One Step Diffusion Model for Underwater Image Enhancement
authors: Haojie Chen, Zhuo Wang, Hongde Qin, Xiaoyang Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Diffusion models have achieved remarkable success in underwater image enhancement (UIE). However, most existing diffusion-based UIE methods generate clear images from random noise through numerous denoising steps, leading to significant computational inefficiency. While terrestrial enhancement methods leverage the rich prior knowledge of pre-trained Text-to-Image Stable Diffusion models to reduce sampling steps, they often yield suboptimal results in underwater environments due to distinct degradation factors such as color casts and blurriness. To address these challenges, we propose DOS-Diff, a Degradation-aware One Step Diffusion model specifically designed for UIE. First, we introduce a Dual-Supervised Degradation Representation Embedder (D2RE), which extracts unique underwater degradation representations through dual explicit and implicit supervision. Building upon the prior knowledge of a distilled Stable Diffusion model, these representations serve as adaptive guidance to replace text prompts for one step enhancement. To effectively bridge the distilled model with underwater scenarios, we further propose a Degradation-guided Low-Rank Adaptation (DLoRA) module. By utilizing degradation representations, DLoRA dynamically adjusts model weights to achieve adaptive fine-tuning for heterogeneous scenarios, enabling high-quality enhancement in a single step. Furthermore, to recover detail loss during the one step process, we design a Detail Feature Enhancement Network (DFEN) that restores high-frequency textures using multi-scale dilated convolutions and global attention modeling. Extensive experiments on public datasets demonstrate that DOS-Diff achieves state-of-the-art performance while significantly improving execution efficiency compared to multi-step diffusion-based UIE algorithms.

title: Faithful and Realistic Image Compression At Extreme-Low Bitrate Via Pretrained Discrete Priors_supp1-3689377.pdf
authors: Nam Ik Cho
abstract: Image compression is fundamentally governed by the distortion-perception trade-off, where achieving high perceptual realism often necessitates a sacrifice in pixel-wise fidelity. This challenge is significantly magnified at extreme low bitrates due to severe information constraints, creating a critical bottleneck for data representation. To address this representation bottleneck, we propose a framework that leverages pretrained discrete priors derived from large-scale vision encoders. Rather than training from scratch, we employ clustering-based initialization to map high-capacity discrete priors into a bitrate-constrained space. This ensures that reconstructed images achieve enhanced content fidelity while preserving superior perceptual realism. Furthermore, to overcome the practical bitrate overhead where arithmetic coding occasionally exceeds the theoretical uniform bound in discrete latent spaces, we introduce a dynamic entropy coding scheme. By deterministically switching between arithmetic and uniform coding on a per-image basis, our method ensures near-optimal compression efficiency. Extensive experiments confirm that our method improves content preservation while maintaining high-fidelity perceptual quality.

title: Medical Knowledge-Driven Contrastive Learning for Similar Patient Retrieval
authors: Fanqing Meng, Chong Feng, Ge Shi, Xia Liu, Bo Wang, Kaiyuan Zhang, Yan Zhuang
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Similar patient retrieval is a fundamental task in medical informatics, aiming to identify patients with similar clinical characteristics to assist in diagnosis and treatment plan recommendation. While traditional methods relying on lexical features or medical ontologies often fail to capture implicit semantic relationships, recent advancements in dense retrieval methods powered by deep learning have shown promise yet face challenges in adapting to specific tasks such as similar patient retrieval. To address these limitations, we propose a medical knowledge-driven contrastive learning approach to enhance the representation capacity of general-purpose embedding models for medical text. Specifically, our approach introduces a novel negative sampling strategy leveraging International Classification of Diseases (ICD) codes to identify hard negatives. However, due to data imbalance issues, this method struggles to adequately mine negative examples. To overcome this limitation, we develop an external knowledge-based negative sampling method that incorporates both statistical and ambiguous knowledge, thereby enhancing the model's ability to differentiate between fine-grained medical conditions and complex clinical scenarios. We then integrate these methods into a contrastive learning framework to train more robust patient representations. Extensive experiments on real-world medical datasets show that our proposed method achieves significant improvements over existing state-of-the-art baseline models.

title: A Reconfigurable Four-Level/Six-Level Inverter-Fed Variable Pole–Phase Induction Motor Drive With Fault Tolerant Capability
authors: Monika Jain, B. Prathap Reddy
abstract: This article presents a fault-tolerant multilevel inverter configuration for a nine-phase variable pole-phase induction motor (VPIM) drive, mainly targeted for electric vehicle applications where efficiency, low-torque ripple, and reliable operation are essential. The traditional VPIM drives suffer lesser efficiency, lower fault-handling capability, complex inverter circuits, high-torque ripple, and poor DC bus utilization. To overcome these issues, a new inverter structure is developed by integrating three-leg and four-leg inverter modules, where each leg consists of two switches. This configuration can generate four levels in 4pole-9phase (4po-9ph) mode and six levels in 12pole-3phase (12po-3ph) mode. This improvement in voltage levels significantly reduces voltage total harmonic distortion (THD) and current ripple, resulting in better torque ripple profile and system efficiency. The fourth leg of an inverter plays an important role in enabling the changeover of pole phase modes and suppressing circulating currents, particularly when space-vector PWM (SVPWM) is used. A detailed mathematical analysis of phase voltage is presented to explain the reduction in current and torque ripple. Further, the performance of the VPIM drive is evaluated under different torque conditions using ANSYS Maxwell simulations. A comparative analysis of VPIM drive performance is conducted for different voltage level configurations in terms of torque ripple, DC bus voltage utilization, voltage THD, efficiency, power factor, losses, etc. The experimental validation on a 5-hp prototype confirms the effectiveness of the proposed inverter under both normal and fault conditions.

title: User Interface Help Development Systems: A Survey
authors: Pierre A. Akiki, Paul A. Akiki
abstract: User interface help (UIH) is crucial in assisting end-users in navigating, understanding, and effectively using software features. User interface help development systems (UIHDSs) streamline the process of developing UIH. This review paper surveys the work on UIHDSs published between 1990 and 2025 and classifies it according to the underlying UIH development approaches. Seven approaches were identified: model-driven engineering, artificial intelligence, crowdsourcing, frameworks and toolkits, usage demonstration and logging, pixel analysis, and static help authoring. Furthermore, criteria were established and used to assess the strengths and shortcomings of UIHDSs and their approaches. These criteria were classified under the following groups: intelligence, presentation, integration, creation, and feedback. The overall assessment result shows that certain criteria received less attention than others. The results identified several areas for improvement to guide future work. In summary, possible improvements to UIHDSs include combining multiple approaches, offering more comprehensive capabilities, and enhancing the intelligence and feedback features.

title: Obtaining a Reverberant Tank’s Transfer Function and Its Application to the Calibration of Acoustic Transducers and Recorders
authors: Pedro Mendes Diniz, Caio César Marques Pereira de Alcântara, Stilson Veras Cardoso, Rafael Bruno de Jesus Trindade, Rodrigo Soares de Freitas, Fernando Luiz de Magalhães, Orlando de Jesus Ribeiro Afonso
abstract: This article describes a method for the experimental determination of the reverberant field transfer function of an acoustic test tank in the frequency domain. Once obtained, the transfer function enables the calibration of transducers and marine recorders in a reverberant field. The method relies on a room acoustics framework for mathematically separating the direct and reverberant fields in a point inside the tank, but employs a transducer-centric model that dispenses with extensive characterization of the tank itself. A calibration experiment in the 1–24 kHz band of an omnidirectional SX147 transducer is conducted in the reverberant field, resulting in mean absolute errors of 0.78 and 1.00 dB, respectively for the receive and transmit voltage responses, relative to the free-field calibration. The expanded uncertainty (coverage factor of 2) in the calibration arising from the spatial averaging of the transfer function ranges from 4.63% to 8.57% . The model is extended to include a transfer function that models the effect of an acoustic recorder’s body on the field near the hydrophone, presenting a feasible method for obtaining the sensitivity of the recorder. An experiment shows that the free-field sensitivity of the standalone hydrophone can be recovered after compensating the measurements for the tank’s and recorder’s transfer functions with absolute errors smaller than 0.92 dB. The expanded uncertainty due to spatial averaging of 20 measurements in determining the recorder’s transfer function ranges from 6.31% to 13.23% .

title: Decoupling of Closely Spaced E-Plane Patch Antennas Using Compact Shorted Stepped-Impedance Strips
authors: Shuangqing Xiong, Jinling Zhang, Xiongzhi Zhu, Zhanqi Zheng
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This letter proposes a shorted stepped-impedance strip (SSIS) decoupling approach for closely spaced E-plane patch antennas. By introducing a pair of SSISs between the two antennas, band-stop decoupling is achieved through the combined effect of the inductive/capacitive characteristics of the SSISs and their capacitive coupling with the patches. An equivalent-circuit model of the antenna is established to reveal the analytical relationship between the decoupling null and the circuit parameters, and to clarify the correspondence of these parameters to the SSIS placement and geometrical dimensions. The proposed antenna has a center-to-center spacing of only 0.295λ0, and the effective length of each SSIS is about λ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">e</sub>/6. Measured results show that the antenna achieves a maximum isolation of 34 dB and a 20-dB isolation bandwidth of 8.3%.

title: Effects of Spatiotemporal Parameters on Forearm Vibrotactile Stimulus Identification
authors: Dong-Geun Kim, Geunho Lee, Suheon Nam, Chaeyong Park, Seungmoon Choi
abstract: Touch provides a direct and private channel for transmitting information through the skin, offering a foundation for wearable tactile communication. This study examines how spatial and temporal parameters jointly determine the perceptual identifiability and information transfer of vibrotactile stimuli on the forearm. In six experiments, we characterize the effects of actuator density, vibration duration, inter-stimulus interval, and sequence length using multi-actuator arrays. Results showed that localization remained reliable up to four tactors per forearm band but deteriorated with higher spatial density. Shorter vibration durations around 0.5 s preserved accuracy while improving temporal efficiency, and an inter-stimulus interval around 0.5 s effectively reduced sequential interference. Reducing spatial density also mitigated temporal overlap, highlighting an interaction between spatial compactness and temporal clarity. These findings delineate how spatial layout and temporal timing influence identifiability and information transfer on the forearm, and inform the design of compact, efficient wearable tactile displays.

title: SOLAR: Switchable Output Layer for Accuracy and Robustness in Once-for-All Training
authors: Shaharyar Ahmed Khan Tareen, Lei Fan, Xiaojing Yuan, Qin Lin, Bin Hu
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Once-for-All (OFA) training enables a single super-net to generate multiple sub-nets tailored to diverse deployment scenarios, supporting flexible trade-offs among accuracy, robustness, and model-size without retraining. However, as the number of supported sub-nets increases, excessive parameter sharing in the backbone limits representational capacity, leading to degraded calibration and reduced overall performance. To address this, we propose SOLAR (Switchable Output Layer for Accuracy and Robustness in Once-for-All Training), a simple yet effective technique that assigns each sub-net a separate classification head. By decoupling the logit learning process across sub-nets, the Switchable Output Layer (SOL) reduces representational interference and improves optimization, without altering the shared backbone. We evaluate SOLAR on five datasets (SVHN, CIFAR-10, STL-10, CIFAR-100, and TinyImageNet) using four super-net backbones (ResNet-34, WideResNet-16-8, WideResNet-40-2, and MobileNetV2) for two OFA training frameworks (OATS and SNNs). Experiments show that SOLAR outperforms the baseline methods: compared to OATS, it improves accuracy of sub-nets up to 1.26%, 4.71%, 1.67%, and 1.76%, and robustness up to 9.01%, 7.71%, 2.72%, and 1.26% on SVHN, CIFAR-10, STL-10, and CIFAR-100, respectively. Compared to SNNs, it improves TinyImageNet accuracy by up to 2.93%, 2.34%, and 1.35% using ResNet-34, WideResNet-16-8, and MobileNetV2 backbones (with 8 sub-nets), respectively. The code of SOLAR is publicly available at: https://github.com/NAIL-UH/SOLAR and its website can be accessed at https://saktx.github.io/solar.github.io/.

title: SPAR-Det: Segmentation-guided and Prior-Aided Routing for Small Object Detection
authors: Seungchan Kwon, Gyuil Lim, Youngjoon Han
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Small Object Detection (SOD) is essential for real-world applications, including satellite image analysis and drone-based surveillance, where target objects typically exhibit limited spatial extent, high density, and visual similarity to complex backgrounds. These factors substantially hinder conventional object detection methods, resulting in weak feature representations and poor detection accuracy. To overcome these challenges, we introduce Segmentation-guided and Prior-Aided Routing for Small Object Detection (SPAR-Det), a unified framework integrating segmentation-guided attention, geometric prior supervision, and adaptive feature routing. At its core, SPAR-Det employs a Cross-Attention Heterogeneous Feature Fusion (CAHF) module that leverages pretrained segmentation backbones to enhance foreground object features while effectively suppressing background. Additionally, we propose a Geometric Prior Supervision Loss that combines Gaussian bounding box maps with segmentation feature maps, providing crucial geometric context and semantic cues to address the limited self-representation capability of small objects. Furthermore, our framework includes a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) detection head, dynamically allocating specialized classifiers according to varying scene characteristics, thereby significantly improving generalization across diverse environments. Extensive evaluations conducted on three benchmark datasets, AI-TOD, Visdrone and LEVIR-Ship, demonstrate that SPAR-Det achieves state-of-the-art performance, verifying its robustness and applicability for challenging small object detection scenarios. Code is available at https://github.com/kwonseungchan/SPAR-Det.git

title: Rug Pulls in Blockchain-Based NFT Markets: Detection Techniques and Risk Mitigation Strategies
authors: Sridevi S, Riyazulla Rahman J, Jobin Thomas, Komalavalli C, Swati Sharma, Gnanaprakash V
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The rapidly evolving NFT (Non-Fungible Tokens) ecosystem has brought about a wealth of opportunities for investors and creators alike. However, the allure of this burgeoning market has also attracted a host of malicious actors, who have exploited vulnerabilities to perpetrate rug pull incidents, a form of exit scam where project developers abruptly abandon their project, absconding with investors' funds. To safeguard oneself against these pernicious schemes, it is crucial to understand the mechanisms underlying rug pulls, as well as the strategies for detecting and avoiding them. In this paper, we discuss the anatomy of an NFT rug-pull and a detailed investigation and study on how these rug-pulls are executed. In this study, we have made an attempt to investigate the common signs along with the cautionary steps to identify and avoid them. Finally, we have also designed and proposed an effective mitigation strategy for a rug pull with deterministic mathematical modelling.

title: Advanced Mobility Management In 5G Networks using a Multi-Layered Convolutional LSTM for Proactive Handover Optimization
authors: Leela Priya Inturu, P M Benson Mansingh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Effective mobility management in 5G networks is essential for ensuring uninterrupted connectivity and efficient cell transitions, particularly as user mobility, device density, and service diversity increase. Traditional mobility management solutions face limitations in adapting to the complex, dynamic mobility patterns and scalability demands of 5G environments. This study introduces a multi-layered Convolutional LSTM model to enhance mobility management by accurately predicting future user locations and mobility trends. By leveraging Convolutional LSTMs, which combine convolutional layers with LSTM units, the model captures spatial and temporal dependencies from user mobility data, enabling predictive handover decisions and optimized resource allocation. Unlike conventional threshold-based or LSTM-only approaches, in this paper, ConvLSTM model combines both spatial dependencies (e.g., cell-to-cell correlation, geographical trajectories) and temporal dependencies (e.g., movement sequences over time). The process begins with collecting mobility datasets containing location coordinates, signal strength values, and timestamps, followed by preprocessing through normalization and sequence construction. The processed data is fed into the ConvLSTM network, where convolutional layers extract spatial mobility patterns while stacked LSTM layers learn temporal transitions. The trained model predicts the next probable user location (cell ID or coordinates) and mobility trend. These predictions are then used to enable proactive handover management by reserving resources in the predicted target cell, minimizing latency, and avoiding ping-pong effects. Finally, the model output is combined into the 5G mobility management framework to provide real-time handover decisions and resource allocation, ensuring seamless connectivity and enhanced user experience.

title: Semi-Supervised Hierarchical Open-Set Classification
authors: Erik Wallin, Fredrik Kahl, Lars Hammarstrand
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Hierarchical open-set classification handles previously unseen classes by assigning them to the most appropriate high-level category in a class taxonomy. We extend this paradigm to the semi-supervised setting, enabling the use of large-scale, uncurated datasets containing a mixture of known and unknown classes to improve the hierarchical open-set performance. To this end, we propose a teacher-student framework based on pseudo-labeling. Two key components are introduced: 1) subtree pseudo-labels, which provide reliable supervision in the presence of unknown data, and 2) age-gating, a mechanism that mitigates overconfidence in pseudo-labels. Experiments show that our framework outperforms self-supervised pretraining followed by supervised adaptation, and even matches the fully supervised counterpart when using only 20 labeled samples per class on the iNaturalist19 benchmark. Our code is available at https://github.com/walline/semihoc.

title: Healthcare AI: An Integrated AI Platform for Proactive Patient Care and Medical Data Management
authors: Akshat Bhardwaj, Mohd Murtuza Ali, Mohd Safi Uddin Tayyab, Mohd Waji Uddin Taher, Prabhu Kumar Yadala
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Fragmented patient data across disparate healthcare systems continues to impede timely clinical decision-making and equitable access to care. This paper presents HealthCare.AI, an integrated artificial intelligence platform that transforms healthcare delivery from reactive episodic management to proactive, patient-centred monitoring. The system encompasses secure medical report management, a multi-domain HeartGuard AI risk engine covering cardiovascular disease, diabetes, stroke, and chronic kidney disease, and a Health Guardian AI chatbot that leverages natural language processing to synthesise patient context and apply validated clinical risk algorithms. Evaluated against a 1,500-patient synthetic cohort grounded in NHANES 2017–2020 prevalence data, the Health Guardian AI chatbot achieved near-perfect scores across all four clinical quality dimensions (Safety = 5.00/5, Clarity = 5.00/5, Completeness = 4.96/5, Medical Accuracy = 4.85/5), a zero safety flag rate and zero hallucination flag rate across 200 evaluated responses, a 97.5% adversarial safety compliance rate across four high-risk query categories, and consistent responses above the 0.70 cosine threshold across all ten clinical topics. The synthetic cohort accurately reproduced national chronic disease prevalence rates, validating the evaluation framework. This paper describes the system architecture, implementation, and empirical evaluation, demonstrating the platform’s potential to support early disease detection, reduce preventable complications, and advance clinician–patient collaboration through data-driven health monitoring.

title: Can Image Splicing and Copy-Move Forgery Be Detected by the Same Model? Forensim: An Attention-Based State-Space Approach
authors: Soumyaroop Nandi, Prem Natarajan
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: We introduce Forensim, an attention-based state-space framework for image forgery detection that jointly local-izes both manipulated (target) and source regions. Unlike traditional approaches that rely solely on artifact cues to detect spliced or forged areas, Forensim is designed to capture duplication patterns crucial for understanding context. In scenarios like protest imagery, detecting only the forged region—e.g., a duplicated act of violence inserted into a peaceful crowd—can mislead interpretation, highlighting the need for joint source-target localization. Forensim outputs three-class masks (pristine, source, target) and supports detection of both splicing and copy-move forgeries within a unified architecture. We propose a visual state-space model that leverages normalized attention maps to identify internal similarities, paired with a region-based block-attention module to distinguish manipulated regions. This design enables end-to-end training and precise localization. Forensim achieves state-of-the-art performance on standard benchmarks. We also release CMFD Anything, a new dataset addressing limitations of existing copy-move forgery datasets. Project page and code.

title: Low-Rank Expert Merging for Multi-Source Domain Adaptation in Person Re-Identification
authors: Taha Mustapha Nehdi, Nairouz Mrabah, Atif Belal, Marco Pedersoli, Eric Granger
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Adapting person re-identification (reID) models to new target environments remains a challenging problem that is typically addressed using unsupervised domain adaptation (UDA) methods. Recent works show that when labeled data originates from several distinct sources (e.g., datasets and cameras), considering each source separately and applying multi-source domain adaptation (MSDA) typically yields higher accuracy and robustness compared to blending the sources and performing conventional UDA. However, state-of-the-art MSDA methods learn domain-specific backbone models or require access to source domain data during adaptation, resulting in significant growth in training parameters and computational cost. In this paper, a Source-free Adaptive Gated Experts (SAGE-reID) method is introduced for person reID. Our SAGE-reID is a cost-effective, source-free MSDA method that first trains individual source-specific low-rank adapters (LoRA) through source-free UDA. Then, a lightweight gating network is introduced and trained to dynamically assign optimal merging weights for fusion of LoRA experts, enabling effective cross-domain knowledge transfer. While the number of backbone parameters remains constant across source domains, LoRA experts scale linearly but remain negligible in size (ď 2% per source), reducing both the memory consumption and risk of overfitting. Extensive experiments conducted on three challenging benchmarks – Market-1501, DukeMTMC-reID, and MSMT17 – indicate that SAGE-reID can outperform state-of-the-art methods while remaining computationally efficient. Our code is available: https://github.com/nehdiii/SAGE-reID

title: Design and Evaluation of a Cost-Effective Toolkit for Modular Haptic Harnesses in Wearable Devices
authors: Sandeep Kollannur, Katherine Robertson, Heather Culbertson
abstract: We introduce an open-source toolkit for rapidly prototyping haptic harnesses via an integrated hardware-software workflow. The toolkit merges modular, 3-D printed components, magnetic connectors, and EVA-foam backings with an intuitive parametric software interface that automatically generates fabrication and assembly files. This design removes traditional CAD barriers, allowing researchers and designers to focus on haptic experience rather than mechanical design constraints. We evaluated the system through three complementary studies validating the hardware and software pipelines. First, a hardware evaluation conducted during an inclusive design workshop (n=18) demonstrated usability scores confirming accessibility across participants with diverse design experience. Second, a software usability study with haptics researchers (n=12) identified key interface refinements that guided version 2 improvements. Finally, an end-to-end workflow validation with experienced haptic researchers (n=8) assessed the complete design-to-deployment process. Researchers made custom harnesses in about 26 minutes, with 87.5 % reporting accurate actuator placement and faster iteration than traditional CAD-based methods. Across studies, the integrated system achieved a SUS score exceeding industry usability benchmarks. By combining accessible fabrication, streamlined software, and open-source dissemination, this toolkit lowers the entry barrier for haptic hardware development. It enables rapid, repeatable, and customizable design of wearable haptic interfaces, empowering broader participation in embodied interaction research.

title: Image-Guided Semantic Pseudo-LiDAR Point Generation for 3D Object Detection
authors: Minseung Lee, Seokha Moon, Seung Joon Lee, Reza Mahjourian, Jinkyu Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: In autonomous driving scenarios, accurate perception is becoming an even more critical task for safe navigation. While LiDAR provides precise spatial data, its inherent sparsity makes it difficult to detect small or distant objects. Existing methods try to address this by generating additional points within a Region of Interest (RoI), but relying on LiDAR alone often leads to false positives and a failure to recover meaningful structures. To address these limitations, we propose Image-Guided Semantic Pseudo-LiDAR Point Generation model, called ImagePG, a novel frame-work that leverages rich RGB image features to generate dense and semantically meaningful 3D points. Our framework includes an Image-Guided RoI Points Generation (IG-RPG) module, which creates pseudo-points guided by image features, and an Image-Aware Occupancy Prediction Network (I-OPN), which provides spatial priors to guide point placement. A multi-stage refinement (MR) module further enhances point quality and detection robustness. To the best of our knowledge, ImagePG is the first method to directly leverage image features for point generation. Extensive experiments on the KITTI and Waymo datasets demonstrate that ImagePG significantly improves the detection of small and distant objects like pedestrians and cyclists, reducing false positives by nearly 50%. On the KITTI benchmark, our framework improves mAP by +1.38%p (car), +7.91%p (pedestrian), and +5.21%p (cyclist) on the test set over the baseline, achieving state-of-the-art cyclist performance on the KITTI leaderboard. The code is available at: https://github.com/MS-LIMA/ImagePG

title: Modeling and Learning Multiple Hypotheses for Monocular 3D Object Detection
authors: Hyeonjeong Park, Peixi Xiong, Pei Yu, Wei Tang
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Detecting objects in 3D space using a monocular image is inherently a highly ill-posed problem: multiple plausible 3D bounding boxes can explain the same 2D observation of an object.
