Existing approaches typically follow a single-point prediction paradigm, failing to capture this multimodal nature and often regressing to an implausible mean solution. This paper introduces MonoMH, a novel multi-hypothesis framework for monocular 3D object detection. By explicitly modeling and learning the multimodal distribution of plausible 3D object configurations, MonoMH not only significantly improves detection performance but also provides richer information to support downstream decision-making. MonoMH introduces three key innovations: (1) a novel multi-hypothesis predictor that leverages spatially diverse features across different windows within an RoI to generate a rich variety of hypotheses without increasing model complexity; (2) a new multi-hypothesis learning approach that derives diverse and relevant hypotheses from single-modal ground truth by integrating uncertainty modeling with Best-of-Many learning; and (3) a hypothesis filtering mechanism that enhances detection capability by dynamically retaining a variable number of plausible hypotheses based on each object’s uncertainty. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach. Notably, MonoMH achieves 29.12/20.88/17.93 Car AP<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3D</inf> (easy/mod./hard) on the KITTI test set, significantly outperforming previous state-of-the-art methods. The code can be found at https://github.com/HyeonjeongPark37/MonoMH.

title:
ACuRE: Accurate Continuity-Regularized SpO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Estimation Using Liquid Time-Constant Networks
authors: Shahzad Ahmad, Divya Mishra, Sania Bano, Sukalpa Chanda, Yogesh Singh Rawat
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Blood oxygen saturation (SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) is a vital measure of respiratory and circulatory health, essential for detecting hypoxemia in conditions like chronic obstructive pulmonary disease and heart failure. Current non-contact SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> estimation methods using remote photoplethysmography (rPPG) struggle with motion artifacts, illumination variability, and limited temporal modeling, hindering their practical use. We propose ACuRE, a novel framework that integrates a two-branch 3D-ResNet-18 for AC/DC signal separation, Liquid Time-Constant (LTC) networks for continuous-time dynamics, and a physics-informed partial differential equation (PDE) loss based on mass conservation. ACuRE overcomes these challenges by isolating pulsatile (AC) and baseline (DC) signals for enhanced robustness, using LTC networks to capture nonlinear physiological dynamics, and applying PDE regularization to ensure signal continuity. This achieves a significant reduction in mean absolute error compared to baselines, with strong performance under motion and illumination stress. Evaluated across multiple datasets, ACuRE demonstrates robust accuracy and generalization, offering a scalable solution for video-based health monitoring in telemedicine and low-resource settings. Code available at: https://github.com/Shahzadnit/ACURE_WACV

title: A-V Representation Learning via Audio Shift Prediction for Multimodal Deepfake Detection and Temporal Localization
authors: Ashutosh Anshul, Eng Siong Chng, Deepu Rajan
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Recent multimodal deepfake detection methods typically rely on single-stage training, which can cause the model to focus on dataset-specific multimodal cues while missing important features that are helpful to detect unseen manipulations, thereby limiting generalization. While some approaches attempt to address this using self-supervised audio-visual pretraining, they may not fully exploit cross-modal temporal information. Also, they often assume that manipulations affect the entire video, ignoring more realistic cases where only short segments are altered. To overcome these limitations, we propose a two-stage training framework that first learns audio-visual temporal alignment in real videos and then uses this information to detect and localize potential deepfakes by identifying temporal inconsistencies. We propose a self-supervised shift-prediction pretraining objective to fully understand cross-modal temporal alignment across multiple temporal shifts applied to the audio input. The pretrained features enable the model to identify manipulations across entire videos as well as accurately localize deepfake segments in partially tampered content. Moreover, the pretrained components do not require task-specific fine-tuning, improving the model’s flexibility for both classification and localization. Experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate strong within-dataset performance, robust generalization to new manipulations and datasets, and accurate temporal localization.<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>

title: FB-4D: Spatial-Temporal Coherent Dynamic 3D Content Generation with Feature Banks
authors: Jinwei Li, Huan-Ang Gao, Wenyi Li, Haohan Chi, Chenyu Liu, Chenxi Du, Yiqian Liu, Mingju Gao, Guiyu Zhang, Zongzheng Zhang, Li Yi, Yao Yao, Jingwei Zhao, Hongyang Li, Yikai Wang, Hao Zhao
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: With the rapid advancements in diffusion models and 3D generation techniques, dynamic 3D content generation has become a crucial research area. However, achieving high-fidelity 4D (dynamic 3D) generation with strong spatial-temporal consistency remains a challenging task. Inspired by recent findings that pretrained diffusion features capture rich correspondences, we propose FB-4D, a novel 4D generation framework that integrates a Feature Bank mechanism to enhance both spatial and temporal consistency in generated frames. In FB-4D, we store features extracted from previous frames and fuse them into the process of generating subsequent frames, ensuring consistent characteristics across both time and multiple views. To ensure a compact representation, the Feature Bank is updated by a proposed dynamic merging mechanism. Leveraging this Feature Bank, we demonstrate for the first time that generating additional reference sequences through multiple autoregressive iterations can continuously improve generation performance. Experimental results show that FB-4D significantly outperforms existing methods in terms of rendering quality, spatial-temporal consistency, and robustness. It surpasses all multi-view generation tuning-free approaches by a large margin and achieves performance on par with training-based methods. Our code and data will be publicly available to support future research.

title: Video and Language Alignment in 2D Systems for 3D Multi-object Scenes with Multi-Information Derivative-Free Control
authors: Jason Armitage, Rico Sennrich
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Cross-modal systems trained on 2D visual inputs are presented with a dimensional shift when processing 3D scenes. An in-scene camera bridges the dimensionality gap but requires learning a control module. We introduce a new method that improves multivariate mutual information estimates by regret minimisation with derivative-free optimisation. Our algorithm enables off-the-shelf cross-modal systems trained on 2D visual inputs to adapt online to object occlusions and differentiate features. The pairing of expressive measures and value-based optimisation assists control of an in-scene camera to learn directly from the noisy outputs of vision-language models. The resulting pipeline improves performance in cross-modal tasks on multi-object 3D scenes without resorting to pretraining or finetuning.

title: SD-CSFL: A Synthetic Data-Driven Conformity Scoring Framework for Robust Federated Learning
authors: Ebtisaam Alharbi, Abdulrahman Kerim, Leandro Soriano Marcolino, Qiang Ni
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables collaborative model training without sharing raw data, but remains highly vulnerable to gradient manipulation and backdoor attacks, particularly under heterogeneous client distributions. Most existing defenses either target a narrow class of attacks, rely on client data, or fail to adapt in heterogeneous settings. We propose SD-CSFL (Synthetic Data-Driven Conformity Scoring for Federated Learning), a unified and privacy-preserving defense algorithm. SD-CSFL leverages a synthetic calibration dataset, independent of client data, to compute entropy-based nonconformity scores that capture irregularities in client updates. An adaptive percentile thresholding mechanism with stratified calibration dynamically distinguishes benign from malicious updates across training rounds. We establish a conformal prediction-based guarantee showing that percentile thresholds bound false positives under arbitrary score distributions. Experiments on CIFAR-10 and Birds-525 demonstrate up to 35% higher detection of gradient manipulation and an 80% reduction in backdoor success rates, outperforming recent defenses in heterogeneous environments. Our implementations and synthetic datasets are available at https://github.com/EbtisaamCS/SD-CSFL

title: Mean-Shift Distillation for Diffusion Mode Seeking
authors: Vikas Thamizharasan, Nikitas Chatzis, Iliyan Georgiev, Matthew Fisher, Evangelos Kalogerakis, Difan Liu, Nanxuan Zhao, Michal Lukáč
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: We present mean-shift distillation, a novel diffusion distillation technique that provides a provably good proxy for the gradient of the diffusion output distribution. This is derived directly from mean-shift mode seeking on the distribution, and we show that its extrema are aligned with the modes. We further derive an efficient product distribution sampling procedure to evaluate the gradient. Our method is formulated as a drop-in replacement for score distillation sampling (SDS), requiring neither model retraining nor extensive modification of the sampling procedure. We show that it exhibits superior mode alignment as well as improved convergence in both synthetic and practical setups, yielding higher-fidelity results when applied to both text-to-image and text-to-3D applications with Stable Diffusion.

title: GeoHSAF: Geometric Hippocampus Shape Analysis Framework for Longitudinal Alzheimer’s Disease Classification
authors: Mubarak Olaoluwa, Heni Loukil, Arafet Sbei, Hassen Drira
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease (AD) is the most common form of dementia and a progressive, irreversible brain disorder that affects millions worldwide. The majority of existing research on AD classification relies on cross-sectional brain magnetic resonance imaging studies, which consider information from a single time point and fail to account for the progressive nature of AD. Longitudinal analysis, however, is crucial for capturing AD evolution and enabling more accurate diagnosis. To address this gap, we propose GeoHSAF, a novel hippocampus-based geometric learning framework for longitudinal AD classification. To overcome the challenge of missing or inconsistent hippocampal shapes across subjects and time points, our framework includes an interpolation module that predicts intermediate shapes, ensuring temporal continuity. We evaluate the effectiveness of GeoHSAF on three public longitudinal AD datasets: ADNI, OASIS, and AIBL, and benchmark its performance against existing approaches. GeoHSAF achieves new state-of-the-art results on binary classification tasks (AD vs. NC), while also demonstrating strong performance on more challenging triple-class classification tasks (AD vs. NC vs. MCI). Our work is fully reproducible, and all code is available at: https://github.com/ayodejimb/GeoHSAF

title: EndoPBR: Photorealistic Synthetic Data for Surgical 3D Vision via Physically-based Rendering
authors: John J. Han, Jie Ying Wu
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Synthetic data has played a pivotal role in developing large-scale 3D vision models due to its high-quality annotations and ease of curation. In domains where labeled data collection is difficult, such as endoscopy, synthetic data holds promise as a means to generate the large-scale annotated datasets required to train modern neural networks. In this work, we address a core question for data-scarce applications in 3D vision: how can we generate synthetic labeled data, and how useful would the data be for training downstream vision models? First, we introduce a novel data generation module that takes images with known geometry and camera poses as input and estimates the material and lighting conditions of the scene. To stabilize training, we leverage domain-specific properties like non-stationary lighting and anatomical material priors. We model the material properties as a bidirectional reflectance distribution function, parameterized by a neural network. Via the rendering equation, we can generate photorealistic images at arbitrary camera poses. We demonstrate that this method produces competitive novel view synthesis results compared to previous work while being more lightweight, flexible, and efficient. Second, we use our synthetic data to train models on various downstream 3D vision tasks and find that models trained solely on our synthetic data generally outperform those trained on real data across various metrics and tasks. Our experiments show that synthetic data is a promising avenue towards robust 3D vision in surgical scenes.<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>

title: SAVE: Sparse Autoencoder-Driven Visual Information Enhancement for Mitigating Object Hallucination
authors: Sangha Park, Seungryong Yoo, Jisoo Mok, Sungroh Yoon
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Although Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have advanced substantially, they remain vulnerable to object hallucination caused by language priors and visual information loss. To address this, we propose SAVE (Sparse Autoencoder-Driven Visual Information Enhancement), a framework that mitigates hallucination by steering the model along Sparse Autoencoder (SAE) latent features. A binary object-presence question-answering probe identifies the SAE features most indicative of the model’s visual information processing, referred to as visual understanding features. Steering the model along these identified features reinforces grounded visual understanding and effectively reduces hallucination. With its simple design, SAVE outperforms state-of-the-art training-free methods on standard benchmarks, achieving a 10%p improvement in CHAIR<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</inf> and consistent gains on POPE and MMHal-Bench. Extensive evaluations across multiple models and layers confirm the robustness and generalizability of our approach. Further analysis reveals that steering along visual understanding features suppresses the generation of uncertain object tokens and increases attention to image tokens, mitigating hallucination. Code is released at https://github.com/wiarae/SAVE.

title: IMPACT: Interpretable Most Important Person Analysis and Classification using Transformer-based Models
authors: Akshat Rampuria, Kamakshya Prasad Nayak, Kamalakar Vijay Thakare, Tushar Joshi, Aditya Dhananjay Singh, Haesol Park, Heeseung Choi, Hyungjoo Jung, Debi Prosad Dogra, Ig-Jae Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Identifying the Most Important Person (MIP) in complex social and sports events remains a challenging problem due to the dynamic nature of group interactions, subtle visual cues, and context-dependent semantics. Traditional methods often struggle to accurately capture the interplay between individuals and the overarching activity, especially in unstructured real-world environments. In addition, the lack of strong supervision and the need for a deeper contextual understanding further complicate the task. In this work, we propose IMPACT, a novel multi-modal framework that leverages recent advances in vision language models to bridge the gap between visual perception and semantic reasoning. Our approach integrates structured scene understanding, natural language generation, and cross-modal learning to jointly model activity recognition and MIP localization. The method integrates language, vision, and spatial reasoning to improve scene interpretability as well as accuracy in group activity recognition tasks. By incorporating language-based representations, the proposed method enables interpretable and robust performance in sports-centric group activity scenarios. Comprehensive experiments on C-Sports and NCAA datasets demonstrate that the framework significantly enhances the localization of key individuals as well as the accuracy of activity prediction, laying the groundwork for a holistic scene understanding in human-centric video and image analysis. Our proposed method achieves an accuracy of 81.6% when compared with human annotator markings and an increase in mAP scores by ∼ 5% for MIP identification.

title: Enabling Plasmonic Refractive Index Nanosensing Assisted with AI
authors: Anirban Chaudhuri, Subhasri Chatterjee, Sauvik Roy, Anish Datta, Nirmalya Ghosh, Soma Bandyopadhyay, Tapas Chakravarty, Arpan Pal
abstract: Plasmonic refractive index nanosensing exploits the sensitivity of resonance modes to local dielectric changes, enabling label-free detection at the nanoscale. In this work, we present a cross-wire metamaterial–based plasmonic sensor simulated using finite-difference time-domain (FDTD) method. The device was evaluated across a refractive index range of 1–1.4, achieving a sensitivity of 249 nm/RIU. Structural modulation from symmetric to asymmetric cross configurations transformed the resonance response from Lorentzian to Fano-like, highlighting the role of asymmetry in mode hybridization and sensing resolution. Furthermore, artificial intelligence (AI) intervention successfully replicated the refractive index sensing performance and captured the influence of asymmetry on resonance behavior. These findings demonstrate the potential of combining metamaterial design with AI-driven analysis to advance next-generation intelligent plasmonic sensors.

title: Non-Invasive Screening of Prediabetes using VOC Nanosensors: Classification of Synthetic Breath with Machine Learning
authors: Paramita Kar Choudhury, Tanushree Banerjee, Souradeep Roy, Mohd Abdullah Siddiqui, Prasanta K. Guha, Avik Ghose, Sanjay Kimbahune, Arpan Pal
abstract: Prediabetes is a clinically elusive and often undetected metabolic condition, marking a critical transition from healthy to diabetic states. Non-invasive methods that can identify prediabetes as a statistically and metabolically distinct class remain rare. In this work, we present an AI powered breath analyzing system which is trained on synthetically generated volatile organic compound (VOC) mixtures, that uses a novel knowledge-driven generation technique, to mimic physiologically relevant VOC ranges found in human breath across healthy, prediabetic states. The sensor system generates dynamic response data from these controlled mixtures, which are used to construct machine learning models. The first classification approach is a three-class classification using 210 samples (70 per class), showing improved performance with an F1-score of 0.8 for the prediabetic class using Random Forest and XGBoost. The second approach employs a hierarchical classification model that first detects healthy vs non-healthy breath profiles and then distinguishes between prediabetic and diabetic sub-classes. This two-tier system yields robust identification of non-healthy individuals with 94% accuracy in Tier-1 comprising of both prediabetes and diabetes, and 74% accuracy in Tier-2 for identifying prediabetes despite controlled overlap introduced to capture transitional metabolic states. Our results establish a validated in-vitro foundation for future clinical translation. The proposed framework is now ready for field trials aimed at real-world deployment for affordable and scalable prediabetes screening.

title: A Comparative Analysis on Patch-Based Deep CNN Model for Tomato Leaf Disease Classification
authors: Dhanesh Debnath, Shyamosree Pal, Arpita Nath Boruah, Saroj Kr. Biswas
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: A leaf is a crucial part of a plant that helps the plant to prepare chlorophyll. According to the current scenario, disease classification helps in preventing large-scale yield losses and ensuring healthy crop production. Based on the various traditional deep CNN model which used to classify the diseases by scanning the entire leaf image at a time, which sometimes led to major challenges such as ignoring localized lesion patterns. It causes a major challenge that needs to be overcome. So, a patch-based deep convolutional learning framework is proposed for classifying tomato leaf diseases. Based on the proposed approach, each leaf image is divided into a smaller patches images, which allows the model to focus on fine-grained disease regions effectively and extract more useful discriminative features. In this paper, patch-based strategies were integrated and evaluated under four experimental settings, including patching and data augmentation on six pre-trained models like ResNet50, VGG16, DenseNet121, EfficientNet-V2B3, MobileNetV2, and InceptionV3. The comparative analysis has been demonstrated to show that lesion detection and classification performance are significantly enhanced by combining patch-based learning. The highest accuracy among the other models DenseNet121 achieved 97.07%. These help in improving the model robustness and generalization by offering a reliable approach for real-world plant disease diagnosis.

title: Deep learning based skin cancer classification: A comprehensive evaluation
authors: A. Chandra Sekhar, D. Venkatesh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Deep learning techniques, especially the Convolution neural networks have transformedthe field of skin cancer diagnosis, offering automated and highly accurate methods for lesion classification. CNNs operate by progressively extracting hierarchical features from raw pixel data, with early layers detecting basic structures like edges and lines, and deeper layers capturing complex patterns such as textures and object parts. These networks are capable of processing large volumes of dermoscopic images, with CNN-based models consistently achieving high accuracy, often surpassing human-level performance. However, challenges persist, such as the limited diversity of current datasets, which primarily focus on melanocytic lesions, and the need for improved model interpretability. The paper discusses the integration of these advanced models into clinical practice, highlighting their potential to assist dermatologists in providing quicker and more reliable diagnoses, thus improving early detection and patient outcomes. Despite the advancements, further research is needed to address data imbalance, enhance model generalization, and ensure the integration of these models into clinical workflows.

title: Enhancing PPG Signal Reliability: A Deep Learning Approach for Robust Signal Quality Assessment
authors: Gutti Nagaswetha, K Karthik Kumar Raju, P Jahnavi, B Hemalatha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Photoplethysmography (PPG) signals are very important for non-invasive physiological monitoring, however they are typically not very reliable because of motion artifacts and noise. For biological applications like estimating heart rate and oxygen saturation to be reliable, it is important to make sure that Signal Quality Assessment (SQA) is correct. This study presents a novel deep learning architecture for accurately capturing the temporal and spatial properties of PPG signals, which integrates CNNs and LSTM networks. By training the model on a hand-picked labeled dataset, we can reliably categorize signal segments as high- or low-quality. The suggested technique greatly outperforms conventional rule-based and traditional machine learning approaches in classification accuracy and resilience against motion-induced distortions, as shown by experimental findings. The results show how deep learning may change the way PPG-based health monitoring systems work by making them more reliable and resilient. This will lead to more accurate and real-time physiological evaluations.

title: Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning Approaches for Automated Detection of AI-Generated Text in Academic Content
authors: Nayanika Sinha, Sreyas Saravindu, Tanmoy Maitra, Debasis Giri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: This study looks at different machine learning methods to spot academic papers written by AI. We tested TFIDF using BERT, and a hybrid model that combines publication metadata (H-Score) and BERT. Based on 2,000 text samples, we find that deep learning models that is BERT and hybrid provide a significant improvement in accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(87 \%$</tex> and 94 % respectively), while lightweight model achieves only 78 % accuracy. The investigation finds that the linguistic patterns can differentiate AI-generated content and also can suggest a tiered AI-detection framework for educational settings. Moreover, the proposed hybrid model achieves a balance between computation and accuracy while maintaining the crucial human oversight.

title: LiteVis-Net: A VGG19-Based Deep Learning Approach for Multi-Focus Image Fusion in Shoreline Analysis
authors: Peddapullaiahgari Hariobulesu, Ch.Nagaraju, R.Madan Mohan, Valipireddy Mahitha, Kuruba Manogna, Reddipaka Kiran Kumar, Kanta Naveen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The analysis of shorelines needs an exact demarcation of land-water boundaries of coastal imagery that is usually problematic due to varying levels of focus, illumination, and structural ambiguities. The article proposes a new deep learning architecture LiteVis-Net, a VGG19-based solution with the high-precision shoreline retrieval by using multi-focus image fusion. In comparison with the traditional methods that have difficulties with the computational complexity or the ridges capacity, LiteVis-Net utilizes the semantic expressiveness of VGG19 with structural improvement methods to attain high-quality shoreline delineation in diverse imaging conditions. The described pipeline consists of the elements of grayscale conversion, normalization, dual-branch VGG19 encoding, focus probability maps generation, adaptive thresholding, edge-aware guided filtering, and pixel-wise blending. Experimental tests confirm LiteVis-Net as being better in edge preservation, sharpness of boundary, and consistency of details than the currently used fusion techniques, making it effective in the monitoring of the coastal region and the management of the shoreline.

title: Hybrid Solar-Wind Energy with Automatic Load Sharing and Priority Switching
authors: Adithiyan S, Ebinesh Joie E J, Navayuugan N, Suresh. K. P
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: In rural and remote regions, power supply has remained a big challenge; in terms of the continuity and reliability. Solar and wind energy with battery storage is a feasible and sustainable solution that can be used as hybrid renewable energy systems. This paper will develop a hybrid solar-wind system that incorporates an intelligent control strategy to make certain that the power delivery is stable even with the variable weather conditions. Automatic Load Sharing and Priority Switching are a major characteristic of the system with the available energy being allocated among loads automatically with regard to the real-time supply and demand. The system favors all the time the most economic and plentiful source and in this order that is solar, wind, and lastly the battery, which means that there is an efficient use of energy and a continuity of power during the fluctuations. It has been designed with MPPT (Maximum Power Point Tracking) of both the solar and wind units to draw maximum power out of any given moment. A fuzzy logic controller makes smart decisions in times of rapid weather changes and prevents the battery over- and undercharging as well as over and under discharging. The system behaviour is modelled, simulated, and tested with the help of MATLAB/Simulink. All in all, the system is supposed to provide a low cost, dependable and green power system to homes, small industry and agricultural use.

title: A Vision-Guided, IoT-Integrated Pill Dispensing System for Intelligent Medication Adherence
authors: Kailash Shaw, Shreyash Chilip, Kushal Chaudhari, Sahil Jadhav, Shubham Gaikwad, Shiv Wagh
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: Failure to take medications as prescribed is a major problem in the management of chronic disease, leading to avoidable complications and generally increasing costs of care worldwide. This project proposes a new Enhanced Intelligent Pill Dispenser backed with embedded hardware, computer vision, and IoT Services that recognize geometry, dispense automatically, and monitor adherence in real-time. A precise vision pipeline for pill dimensions is set up based on a pixel-to-centimetre mapping of a known reference. This allows for compartment and dispensing enabled by geometry selection. The system can be designed with modules, friendly for geriatrics, clinically extensible to improve safety, and reduce the routine workload for caregivers.

title: A Bibliometric and Network Analysis of Public Private Partnership's Risk Allocation Research
authors: Sushil Minz, Jyotiranjan Hota
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Either for success or failure in Public Private Partnership (PPPs) ventures, the most critical determinant, is Risk Allocation. It itself, generates, in bits and pieces, a significant amount of literature. This research adds on with a comprehensive bibliometric and visual analysis, mapping the intellectual structure and evolution in the field. 372 Documents were extracted from Scopus Database from Y2K till the Date of Retrieval 23.11.2025. Performance and science mapping techniques in addition to citation, source and keyword analyses is used. The result reveals a fastprogressing field, identifying leading authors, influential documents and key contributing countries. Network visualizations revealed two central research themes. Fundamental principles and Critical Success Factors (CSF) comprising in Basic Themes and Quantitative Risk Assessment (RA) and Value for Money (VfM) as Niche Themes. The analysis also tracks the domain's evolution till date. The gradual change in concepts from its origin and adapting to the circumstances. It revealed to the scientific community a systematic roadmap, by indicating the underexplored and unexplored areas, such as integration of smart contracts and climate related risks, to propose a robust agenda for the future.

title: A Machine Learning Approach for Crop Recommendation Using Soil Nutrients and Climate Features
authors: Omm Prakash Senapaty, Rajalaxmi Padhy, Sanjit Kumar Dash
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: In many countries where agriculture is a primary livelihood, farming is central to ensuring food availability and supporting economic growth. Traditionally, decisions about crop selection have been based largely on farmers' intuition and past experience. Such approaches often overlook critical parameters, including soil fertility, climatic conditions, and rainfall patterns, which strongly influence agricultural productivity. The present research presents a machine learning based crop recommendation system with the use of algorithms like Random Forest, XGBoost, and SVM (Support Vector Machine) with the goal to enhance this procedure. These models aid in the study of huge agricultural data sets and recommend the most advantageous crops for specific conditions. These mathematical models significantly lower incorrect classification rates and boost prediction efficiency when paired with successful pre processing and feature extraction methods. With a small amount of variations, the algorithm was able to accurately forecast every class in relation to its actual group distributions. With an accuracy of 99.32%, Random Forest outperformed each of the other two, followed by XGBoost at 98.60% and SVM at 96.82%. Having better decisions about what to grow is made easier by this research, This leads to more environmentally friendly farming, better utilization of resources, and greater crop yields. It displays how technology can improve agriculture's abilities, output, and capacity for future challenges.

title: Intelligent Monitoring System of Staircase Using Computer Vision
authors: Priyanka Vyas, Rutuja Gopale, Hrishikesh Bawale, Anjali Yadav, Ashutosh Ubhe
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Human safety is the biggest concern of a safe environment for young adults, middle-aged adults, and older people. An intelligent monitoring system that understands motion patterns and distraction behaviors, such as mobile phone usage, is required in today's environment this paper presents a ComputerVision based approach, which includes two things at a time: human motion detection and mobile phone usage recognition using the YOLOv8 architecture. The proposed system is real-time based on the detection capabilities with the simultaneous identification of human presence, posture, and handheld phone activity. A comparative evaluation with the existing detection frameworks, YOLOv8 reveals better results in 92.1 dependable solution for smart surveillance and public safety applications, is further equipped with. The proposed model, by concentrating on staircases, which are a significant cause of distracted movement accidents, helps raise people's awareness of the situation and prevents them from falling.

title: Energy-Efficient Task Offloading in Multi-Tenant Edge Clouds
authors: Srinivasu Yalamati
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The demand for processing at the network edge has been accelerated by the rapid growth of IoT (Internet of Things) devices, latency-sensitive applications, and mobile services. In multi-tenant settings, where multiple applications and service providers share limited edge resources, efficient utilization of edge infrastructure is crucial for realizing the potential benefits of edge computing, even though it offers lower latency provisioning of tasks compared with centralized cloud infrastructures. To cope with three contradictory objectives minimizing energy consumption, meeting service delay constraints, and ensuring fairness among tenants this work addresses the problem of energyaware task offloading in multitenant edge clouds. Local, edge, or cloud execution options are modeled using binary decision variables, leading to a multi objective optimization formulation of the offloading problem. Gini-based constraints are incorporated to prevent degradation in fairness and energy-delay trade-offs across tenants. The proposed framework is evaluated and compared with baseline approaches such as naive edge offloading and local-only execution using both Google Cluster Workload Traces and synthetic workloads. Results indicate that the framework prevents SLA violations for tenant-specific demands, significantly reduces overall energy consumption by ensuring more tasks are completed within deadlines, and improves fairness across tenants. The analysis further highlights that fairness-aware adaptive strategies achieve balanced gains in energy efficiency, whereas naive offloading often becomes counterproductive due to transmission overheads.

title: A Smart Piezoelectric Mat for Gesture-Based Communication in Hearing and Speech Disabilities
authors: Remya Nair, Zainab Abualhassan, Dania Husni, Fatma Abualhassan, Nader Shehata
abstract: Individuals with combined hearing and speech disabilities struggle with basic communication. This study presents an assistive framework using a nanofiber-based piezoelectric sheet to capture finger-drawn gestures and transform them into communication commands. Voltage responses were analyzed, resulting in images of three gestures—tick, question, and cross—classified by a convolutional neural network (CNN). A dataset of 304 labeled samples was used for training, validation, and testing, with performance evaluated through various metrics including accuracy, F1-score,precision, recall, specificity, and ROC–AUC, supplemented by learning curves and confusion matrices. The findings indicate high accuracy in distinguishing gestures, proposing piezoelectric sensing as a cost-effective communication solution. Nonetheless, the limited dataset and gesture classes present challenges for generalization, marking this work as a preliminary step towards a more extensive system with future expansions planned.

title: Sentimax - A Machine and Deep Learning Framework for Emotion Recognition in Digital Communication
authors: Nicholas Chung, Mira Kim
publish: 2025 Conference on AI, Science, Engineering, and Technology (AIxSET)
abstract: In the current era of rapidly advancing technology, effective communication remains a cornerstone of progress. However, the rise of text-based communication introduces ambiguity due to the absence of emotional and contextual cues typically conveyed through vocal tone, facial expressions, and body language. This limitation often results in misinterpretations with varying degrees of consequence. To address this challenge, this work presents an enhanced version of Sentimax, an AI-driven sentiment analysis system designed to infer emotional intent from text. The upgraded Sentimax architecture combines machine learning and deep learning techniques, preserving an 80% accuracy rate while improving robustness and computational efficiency. A key feature of the system is its adaptive design, which supports seamless retraining to accommodate evolving linguistic patterns. Sentimax aims to improve emotional interpretability and clarity in digital communication across both personal and professional domains.

title: CBF-Net: A Convolutional Branch Attention Fusion Network for Fine-Grained Tea Bud Maturity Assessment
authors: Rajat Amat, Ayush Kumar, Mourina Ghosh
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: To increase tea production and improve harvest quality tea bud maturity assessment is important. In this work, we propose a convolution branch attention fusion network model to classify different stages of tea bud. This model extracts feature from both the branches of convolution layes and transformers layer which enhance color and texture. Proposed CBF-Net model works better than the conventional deep designs by achieving 93.0% accuracy, 92.11% sensitivity, 97.36% specificity, 92.61% precision, and a 92.55% F1-score. It indicates that it can be implemented in real world scenerio for tea bud maturity assessment.

title: A GAN-Driven and XAI-Enhanced Framework for Reconstructive Inference and Recognition of Partially Occluded Facial Imagery
authors: Rahul Saravanan, Keerthana R, Soumyalatha Naveen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: When facial recognition is applied in public places like hospitals or security checks, it faces major challenges, mostly due to lightings and people’s accessories. People often wear accessories like masks, sunglasses, or even hats, and these accessories hide parts of their faces. This causes previously reliable algorithms to struggle to get accurate performance. Traditional systems have difficulty coping with missing or distorted facial features. This work directly addresses that problem by introducing a unified framework that combines the generative capabilities of Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) with the interpretability offered by Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI). Core of this system is a U-Net-based generator, trained against a convolutional discriminator, which learns to realistically and semantically reconstruct the occluded regions of a face. After reconstructing these occluded images, the system forwards them to a K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) recognition module that uses rich facial embeddings. This additional step gives the system flexibility to recognize individuals even when a substantial portion of their face is covered. To provide transparency, the model is combined with XAI techniques such as saliency maps. These maps display and highlight the regions of the input image that are most significantly used for pixel-level reconstruction. This helps in providing insight into the model’s reasoning. For assessment, the model relies on quantitative metrics: PSNR, SSIM, MAE, and MSE. The U-Net generator achieves a mean PSNR of 28.15 dB and an SSIM of 0.91. Compared to FSG-GAN [7], this approach increases PSNR by about 33.3% (28.15 vs 21.11 dB) and SSIM by 15.2% (0.91 vs 0.79). It also surpasses SwapInpaint [5], improving PSNR by 13% while maintaining strong structural similarity.

title: Hybrid Attention-Driven and Explainable AI for Multi-Label Retinal Disease Diagnosis
authors: Anusha P K, Nayanaa Ajith, Loveline Zeema J, Jayapriya J
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The retinal fundus imaging is an important factor in early diagnosis of ophthalmic disease where early diagnosis is vital in avoiding the loss of vision inherent in common and rare diseases. Despite the high potential of deep learning in this area, the majority of existing models are not multi-disease predictors, do not predict rare diseases because of class imbalance, and are not interpretable, which decreases clinical acceptance and use. To deal with these issues, this study suggests a two-level, explainable, multi-label diagnostic system over the Retinal Fundus Multi-Disease (RFMiD) data. In Tier One, disease detection is conducted at the high-level with methods of class imbalance mitigation to maximize the recognition of the rare diseases. In Tier Two, fine classifications carried out to identify several ocular pathologies that take place simultaneously in a single image. The framework uses a hybrid architecture, which involves EfficientNetB0, attention mechanism to acquire local features and global dependencies. Explainable techniques like Grad CAM++ and Integrated Gradients produce valuable clinically interpretable visualizations of model prediction. Macro and micro ROC-AUC, per class accuracy and F1-scores are used to evaluate performance with special attention paid to rare disease detection. This will address the problems of multi-label classification, imbalance of classes, and interpretability that a powerful, clinically certain AI system may be delivered that are capable of improving real-life retinal screening and augmenting AI-based healthcare clinician confidence.

title: Adaptive UAV telemetry using Fuzzy Kalman adaptive modulation and Hybrid error correction
authors: Sowdagar Fowzia Sultana, P.Sai Tejeswara Achari, T.Lakshmi Narashima, J.Dinesh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: UAVs and IoT devices suffer from wirelesslinks that are highly unreliable due to weather, mobility,and environmental changes. A weather-aware adaptiveframework is proposed in this paper, which integrateshybrid LoS/NLoS channel modeling with the real-timeswitching among AWGN, Rayleigh, and Nakagami-m fad-ing distributions. A Fuzzy-Kalman predictor uses onboardsensors to anticipate the modulation and coding changes, while a Hybrid Forward Error Correction (HFEC) schemeis used to infect the communication channel with bothrandom and burst errors. In this way, they quite effectivelydecrease bit errors, latency, and packet loss. Comparedto conventional fixed-mode systems, the simulation resultsdemonstrate considerably better reliability and efficiency, thus the proposed solution can be considered as beinghighly appropriate for the telemetric systems of UAVs thatneed to be robust as well as the IoT applications in thefuture.

title: Identification of Gait Phases from Wireless Surface Electromyography features with a Hybrid CNN-LSTM Architecture
authors: M V Mallikarjuna Reddy, P S Pandian, Kumaravelu S, Karthick P A
abstract: Recent advancements in technology have facilitated the incorporation of surface electromyography (sEMG) into control strategies for lower limb prostheses. However, decoding the motion intent from sEMG is challenging due to the inherent stochastic characteristics of the signals. In this study, we proposed a novel approach for multiclass gait phase classification using a hybrid convolutional neural network - long short-term memory (CNN-LSTM) architecture, based on time-domain measures of sEMG from the hamstrings and quadriceps. Surface EMG and inertial measurement unit (IMU) data are simultaneously recorded from 20 healthy participants walking on a treadmill at a speed of 2.5 kilometres per hour (km/h). The sEMG from four muscles namely rectus femoris (RF), vastus lateralis (VL), biceps femoris (BF), and semitendinosus (SEM) are considered for analysis. To train the hybrid architecture, three time-domain descriptors namely Wilson amplitude, root mean square, and zero-crossing are utilized. The results demonstrate that the proposed framework effectively distinguishes the four gait phases, achieving a maximum overall accuracy of 94.00%, with an average precision of 94.11%, an average sensitivity of 93.98% and an average specificity of 98.00%. These findings indicate that the proposed approach effectively decodes the motion intent of lower limb muscles, which could result in more accurate movement control in lower limb prostheses.

title: A Disposable Sensor for the Fast Estimation of Urine Urea using Single Frequency Impedimetry
authors: Saransh Chourey, Asmita Bose, Avishek Adhikary
abstract: This paper proposes a disposable strip type biosensor for urine urea assay which is integrable to electronic circuitry designed for the Point-of-Care (PoC) application. The sensor is cheap, environment friendly, compact and has a quick response time of four minute. The sensor works on the urease enzyme assisted hydrolysis of urea that is measured through impedimetry. The sensor base is made from paper and copper strips are used as electrodes. The work includes elemental characterization of the sensor through Scanning Electron Microscopy and Energy Dispersive Spectroscopy which substantiates uniform presence of the urease enzyme on the sensor surface. Besides, the article also includes the investigation on optimal sensing features, frequency and sensor response time. The sensing equation follows a hyperbolic equation with goodness of fit valued 0.966. Performance indices of the sensor reads full scale accuracy as 3.4%, response time of 4 minute and the range 0.5 - 3.0 g/dL.

title: AI Powered Smart Farming Ecosystem
authors: Krishnav Mahanta, Shaik Shahid Hussain, G Abinaya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: A platform was created, which utilizes AI to assist small farmers in expanding their growth during farming. It operates on real-time data of the IoT devices, has machine learning models to anticipate crop diseases and weather, and even financial planning. The primary need here is to provide farmers with good data-driven advice to make good decisions and manage their resources. This is based on a Random Forest machine learning model. It takes into account several elements such as soil, weather among other patterns of the environment to suggest the most suitable crops at a particular time of the year. Transfer learning models were also trained on Google Vertex AI Studio with a large body of categorized images of farmland and surroundings data. In this manner, the proposed platform is able to be aware of the initial signs of trouble which is quite impressive. Weather forecasting has also been implemented with the help of Weathercast API. It implies that farmers will receive alerts that may actually make them make the most of the irrigation and protect their crops. Coming to the financial aspect of this, the platform analyzes previous yield and market data to provide ROI estimates on alternative crop selections. This has provided the farmers with a better idea of their possible profit levels. There has been an excellent response due to either better disease control, smarter crop choice and better financial planning. This is all translated into the presence of high yields and higher profits to the small farmers. Anyway, it is not one smooth sailing. Acquiring quality data of sensors in those large remote locations may be challenging and unreliable connections may just inconvenience the whole process. Nevertheless, in general, this platform is an enormous support to the smallholder agriculture.

title: SpecGen: Neural Spectral BRDF Generation via Spectral-Spatial Tri-plane Aggregation
authors: Zhenyu Jin, Wenjie Li, Zhanyu Ma, Heng Guo
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Synthesizing spectral images across different wavelengths is essential for photorealistic rendering. Unlike conventional spectral uplifting methods that convert RGB images into spectral ones, we introduce SpecGen, a method that generates spectral bidirectional reflectance distribution functions (BRDFs) from a single RGB image of a sphere. This enables spectral image rendering under arbitrary illuminations and shapes covered by the corresponding material. A key challenge in spectral BRDF generation is the scarcity of measured spectral BRDF data. To address this, we propose the Spectral-Spatial Tri-plane Aggregation (SSTA) network, which models reflectance responses across wavelengths and incident-outgoing directions, allowing the training strategy to leverage abundant RGB BRDF data to enhance spectral BRDF generation. Experiments show that our method accurately reconstructs spectral BRDFs from limited spectral data and surpasses existing methods in hyperspectral image reconstruction, achieving an improvement of 8dB in PSNR. Codes link: https://github.com/sosjzy/SpecGen.

title: Quantitative Evaluation for Microcontroller based LPG Gas Control for Sustainable Safety Monitoring Using Google Cloud and Raspberry PI
authors: M Gopi, K Umamaheswari, G. Saravanan, R Giriraj, S Gokul, R Premkumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The compact chip referred to as microcontroller (MC) consists of a processor, memory, and I/O peripherals that are designed for control tasks. Due to the embedded nature of their operations, MCs process data from sensors and execute real time instructions on multiple pieces of hardware at one time. The Quantum-Inspired Machine Learning Smart Safety Model (QMLSSM) is one of the research projects that has a goal of providing a rapid response capability to detect propane leaks by utilizing thermal imagery, sensor networks, and cloud-based analytics. The QMLSSM model was created using a dataset composed of 4,860 infrared images; each image tabled a different concentration of propane. Prior to including these images into the QMLSSM dataset, each image was subjected to multiple steps of data alignment, pre-processing, and statistical noise removal. All the images were then cropped to $256 \times 256$ pixels in order to yield 3,574 free and clean samples for use in training, validating, and testing; $\mathbf{6 0 \%}-\mathbf{2 0 \%}-\mathbf{2 0 \%}$ for each. The metrics for performance analysis are QMLSSM $^{\wedge} \mathbf{2}$, QMLSSM Loss, QMLSSM MAE, and QMLSSM Gas Consumption.

title: Hybrid CMOS–Superconducting Quantum Co-Processor for Ultra-Fast Post-Quantum Cryptographic Acceleration
authors: B.S Priyanka Kumari, B.Venkataramanaiah
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Emerging post-quantum cryptographic (PQC) algorithms demand execution speeds beyond the capabilities of conventional CMOS accelerators. This work introduces a hybrid CMOS–superconducting quantum co-processor architecture that synergizes room-temperature CMOS logic with a cryogenic superconducting quantum accelerator to achieve ultra-fast PQC operations. A segmented pipeline allocates key generation and parameter management to CMOS units, while time-critical modular arithmetic and lattice-based transformations execute on a superconducting quantum processing core. Co-design optimizes interconnect latency, cryogenic interface protocols, and error-mitigation routines. Prototype evaluations demonstrate a 10× throughput improvement for key encapsulation mechanisms and a 20× reduction in energy per operation compared to state-of-the-art CMOS-only PQC accelerators. The hybrid platform supports flexible algorithm updates and mitigates decoherence through dynamic qubit refresh and adaptive error correction. Results indicate feasibility for real-time secure communications in next-generation networks.

title: Re-Imagining Supervised Learning with LLMs: A Technical Evaluation across Frontier, Open, and QLoRA-Fine-Tuned Models
authors: Saikiran Gogineni, Chinnam Yuvaraju, G Suryanarayana, Kanaka Durga Returi, Vaka Murali Mohan, M Nagabhushana Rao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown impressive generalization across many natural language processing tasks. However, the degree to which they can imitate or exceed classical supervised methods for numerical prediction tasks is less established. This paper presents a systematic metric-based evaluation of frontier, open-source and QLoRA fine-tuned LLMS. The aim is to predict price of the products from text-based descriptions from the Amazon Reviews 2023 dataset, and benchmark against traditional supervised regressors. We develop a rigorous experimental pipeline that incorporates data curation, embedding construction, quantization, and ensemble learning. We compare performance across architectures and number of parameters represented. We assess performance using RMSE, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and provide resource-cost metrics in the form of inference latency, memory footprint, and quantization-induced degradation. Our results demonstrate that fine-tuned quantized LLMs outperform traditional regressors while offering substantial reductions in computational cost. This study highlights the potential for hybrid architectures in the future in the context of supervised learning.

title: Electrical Reliability Analysis of TSVs with Structural Defects: Spherical Voids vs. Pinhole Imperfections
authors: Deepali Gupta, Chanchal Ijardar, Baljit Kaur, Manoj Kumar Majumder, Dhandapani Vaithiyanathan
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: In recent years, Through-Silicon Via (TSV) based three-dimensional integrated circuits (3D ICs) have evolved as a promising solution to meet the growing demand for electronic device requiring higher bandwidth, reduced power consumption, and compact form factors. Despite their advantages, the intricate manufacturing processes and stress-induced effects make TSVs highly susceptible to defects such as voids, misalignment, cracks, and electrical failures factors that critically affect reliability and yield of 3D ICs. This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of TSVs under two common defect scenarios: spherical voids and pinhole defects. A Carbon Nanotube FET (CNTFET) based Coupled Driver-Via-Load (DVL) setup is employed at the 7 nm technology node to evaluate performance. A distributed electrical model is developed to capture the defect conditions, and key performance metrics propagation delay, crosstalk delay, power dissipation, and peak noise are examined. The study reveals that pinhole defects have a more severe impact on TSV performance, with propagation delay, power dissipation, and peak noise worsening by $6.57 \%, 7.08 \%$, and $6.84 \%$, respectively, compared to spherical void defects. These findings underscore the critical influence of defect type on TSV signal integrity and reliability, offering insights for designing robust 3D ICs.

title: A Socratic AI Tutoring System for Reflective and Adaptive Learning Using Offline LLMs and Machine Learning Models
authors: Nithish Kumar B, Tanmay Nadig N
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Traditional digital learning systems often do not adequately foster higher-order thinking, adaptability, and personalized feedback. These platforms usually evaluate learning via fixed-response assessments and static grading, which provide limited insight into a learner's reasoning, confidence, or engagement patterns. Furthermore, their dependence on cloud-based infrastructures gives rise to concerns regarding privacy, scalability, and offline access. In this study, we put forward a hybrid intelligent tutoring framework that fuses Socratic questioning through offline-capable large language models (LLMs) with adaptive machine learning methods for assessing performance. The system takes on the role of a reflective tutor by producing tailored, open-ended prompts and simultaneously examining learners' answers for sentiment, depth of reasoning, and behavioural metrics like response time. The system uses models such as logistic regression, recurrent neural networks (RNNs), and gradient-based optimizers to dynamically interpret student performance and adjust learning pathways in real time. Our method combines dialog-driven reasoning with predictive analytics to offer a new, scalable, and privacy-conscious alternative to traditional learning platforms, with the goal of promoting understanding and learner autonomy.

title: Explainable AI for Multi-Label NCD Risk Prediction: A Comprehensive Review of Multi-Modal Approaches
authors: Robin Kumar, Raman Chadha, Rahul Bhandari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: More than 74% of the world population dies of non-communicable diseases (NCDs) and the majority of the victims are low- and middle-income nations. This is because the traditional methods of the one-size fits all approach of chronic illness management are normally doomed because of the aspects of personal risk factors. Personalized medicine - the idea of prevention and treatment specific to an individual, according to personal genetics, environment, and lifestyle is becoming a necessary paradigm of NCD care. Predictive, proactive healthcare applications through artificial intelligence (AI) can combine different sources of data, including clinical records and genomics, wearables, and sensors. Multi-label deep learning was used in this study on the WHO NCD dataset and heart disease patient data and explainable AI (DeepSHAP) was applied to simultaneously predict the risks. Findings indicated an accuracy of 93.5, increased interpretability, usability and scalability. These results demonstrate the ability of ethical AI-based systems to lessen the burden of NCDs and enhance patient outcomes, the further work on the estimation of recurrence risk and deploying clinical systems on the cloud.

title: Self-Supervised Compression and Artifact Correction for Streaming Underwater Imaging Sonar
authors: Rongsheng Qian, Chi Xu, Xiaoqiang Ma, Hao Fang, Yili Jin, William I. Atlas, Jiangchuan Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Real-time imaging sonar is crucial for underwater monitoring where optical sensing fails, but its use is limited by low uplink bandwidth and severe sonar-specific artifacts (speckle, motion blur, reverberation, acoustic shadows) affecting up to 98% of frames. We present SCOPE, a self-supervised framework that jointly performs compression and artifact correction without clean–noise pairs or synthetic assumptions. SCOPE combines (i) Adaptive Codebook Compression (ACC), which learns frequency-encoded latent representations tailored to imaging sonar, with (ii) Frequency-Aware Multiscale Segmentation (FAMS), which decomposes frames into low-frequency structure and sparse high-frequency dynamics while suppressing rapidly fluctuating artifacts. A hedging training strategy further guides frequency-aware learning using low-pass proxy pairs generated without labels. Evaluated on months of in-situ ARIS sonar data, SCOPE achieves a structural similarity index (SSIM) of 0.77, representing a 40% improvement over prior self-supervised denoising baselines, at bitrates down to ≤ 0.0118 bpp. It reduces uplink bandwidth by more than 80% while improving downstream detection. The system runs in real time, with 3.1 ms encoding on an embedded GPU and 97 ms full multi-layer decoding on the server end. SCOPE has been deployed for months in three Pacific Northwest rivers to support real-time salmon enumeration and environmental monitoring in the wild. Results demonstrate that learning frequency-structured latents enables practical, low-bitrate sonar streaming with preserved signal details under real-world deployment conditions.

title: Region-Specific Crop Recommendation System for Myanmar Using a Deep Learning Hybrid Model
authors: Theint Theint, Thin Lai Lai Thein
abstract: Agricultural productivity in Myanmar is significantly influenced by climatic variability, soil diversity, and inconsistent crop selection practices. The traditional rule based and expert driven approaches lack adaptability to dynamic environmental patterns. The proposed framework adopts a hybrid deep learning architecture in which Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) are utilized for spatial feature extraction, Bidirectional Long Short-term Memory (BiLSTM) networks are employed to capture temporal dependencies, and an attention mechanism is integrated to adaptively assign importance to the extracted features. A multi-source data set combining Sentineln2 NDVI, NASA POWER climate variables, and regional soil attributes from the department of Agriculture was used for model development. Experimental results demonstrate substantial improvements over baseline CNN-LSTM models. The proposed system emphasizes methodological rationale, contribution clarity, and improved technical coherence toward data-driven crop recommendation for sustainable agriculture in Myanmar. The system provides crop suitability recommendations to support farmer decision-making.

title: Smart Pollination System
authors: Amrutha Y N, Priyanka S, Chandana T S, Honnu Shree L
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: This paper describes a Smart Electrostatic Robotic Pollination system designed for vertical farming environments where natural pollinators are not present. The system uses a Pi Camera and environmental sensors, including a DHT22 for temperature and humidity and an LDR sensor for light intensity, to capture real-time data about plants and their surroundings. A Raspberry Pi acts as the main processing unit, running a machine learning model based on convolutional neural networks (CNN) to detect the presence of tomato flowers and analyze their flowering stages for the best pollination timing. The decision engine uses both sensor and image data to automatically determine when the flowers are ready to be pollinated. In suitable conditions, the actuation module, which includes a servo motor and an electrostatic robotic arm, delivers pollen to flowers with great accuracy. All operational data, such as tier status, flower stage, environmental readings, and pollen charge level, are sent to an IOT cloud and displayed on a web dashboard for remote monitoring and manual control. This system provides an efficient, sustainable, and scalable way to automate pollination in indoor farms, increasing fruit yield and resource use while lessening the need for manual labor.

title: VLMDiff: Leveraging Vision-Language Models for Multi-Class Anomaly Detection with Diffusion
authors: Samet Hicsonmez, Abd El Rahman Shabayek, Djamila Aouada
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Detecting visual anomalies in diverse, multi-class real-world images is a significant challenge. We introduce VLMDiff, a novel unsupervised multi-class visual anomaly detection framework. It integrates a Latent Diffusion Model (LDM) with a Vision-Language Model (VLM) for enhanced anomaly localization and detection. Specifically, a pretrained VLM with a simple prompt extracts detailed image descriptions, serving as additional conditioning for LDM training. Current diffusion-based methods rely on synthetic noise generation, limiting their generalization and requiring per-class model training, which hinders scalability. VLMDiff, however, leverages VLMs to obtain normal captions without manual annotations or additional training. These descriptions condition the diffusion model, learning a robust normal image feature representation for multiclass anomaly detection. Our method achieves competitive performance, improving the pixel-level Per-Region-Overlap (PRO) metric by up to 25 points on the Real-IAD dataset and 8 points on the COCO-AD dataset, outperforming state-of-the-art diffusion-based approaches. Code is available at https://github.com/giddyyupp/VLMDiff.

title: SCAdapter: Content-Style Disentanglement for Diffusion Style Transfer
authors: Luan Thanh Trinh
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Diffusion models have emerged as the leading approach for style transfer, yet they struggle with photo-realistic transfers, often producing painting-like results or missing detailed stylistic elements. Current methods inadequately address unwanted influence from original content styles and style reference content features. We introduce SCAdapter, a novel technique leveraging CLIP image space to effectively separate and integrate content and style features. Our key innovation systematically extracts pure content from content images and style elements from style references, ensuring authentic transfers. This approach is enhanced through three components: Controllable Style Adaptive Instance Normalization (CSAdaIN) for precise multi-style blending, KVS Injection for targeted style integration, and a style transfer consistency objective maintaining process coherence. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate SCAdapter significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both conventional and diffusion-based baselines. By eliminating DDIM inversion and inference-stage optimization, our method achieves at least 2× faster inference than other diffusion-based approaches, making it both more effective and efficient for practical applications.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning and IoT-Enabled Framework for Real-Time Monitoring and Management of Coastal Marine Litter
authors: D. Akhilandeswari, V. Esther Jyothi, Krishnaveni Musku
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Marine litter is fast becoming a problem that inflicts much damage on sea life, hurts tourism, and affects communities along coastlines. Many prevailing systems are based on drone usage or image models used to identify and mark litter on beaches, provided their intervention takes place after debris has already been washed up. They offer little help for real-time action or early warnings. This work presents a system for litter detection through drones in collaboration with water sensors, which then traces and identifies. The proposed system will integrate deep learning methods: VGG19 and transformer models to recognize litter and map its spread, while an LSTM component leverages historical maps, weather data, and ocean data for prediction of where the litter is likely to drift next. It also includes a dashboard that contributes to supporting the planning of cleanups and advises coastal officials. The experiments carried out on three coastlines were very promising, with a 91.2% classification rate in distinguishing between litter and non-litter. It has also provided an 87% classification rate on day-long forecasts, thus highly successful in comparison to prior models.

title: STARS: Self-supervised Tuning for 3D Action Recognition in Skeleton Sequences
authors: Soroush Mehraban, Mohammad Javad Rajabi, Andrea Iaboni, Babak Taati
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Self-supervised pretraining methods with masked prediction demonstrate remarkable within-dataset performance in skeleton-based action recognition. However, we show that, unlike contrastive learning approaches, they do not produce well-separated clusters. Additionally, these methods struggle with generalization in few-shot settings. To address these issues, we propose Self-supervised Tuning for 3D Action Recognition in Skeleton sequences (STARS). Specifically, STARS first uses a masked prediction stage using an encoder-decoder architecture. It then employs nearest-neighbor contrastive learning to partially tune the weights of the encoder, enhancing the formation of semantic clusters for different actions. By tuning the encoder for a few epochs, and without using hand-crafted data augmentations, STARS achieves state-of-the-art self-supervised results in various benchmarks, including NTU-60, NTU-120, and PKU-MMD. In addition, STARS exhibits significantly better results than masked prediction models in few-shot settings, where the model has not seen the actions throughout pretraining.

title: On Representation Error in Quantum Option Pricing: A Diagnostic Study of the Standard Qiskit Implementation
authors: K Soveet Kumar Prusty, Kartikeyan Premkumar, Shweta S Aladakatti, Madhura K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Quantum Amplitude Estimation (QAE) is an attractive financial derivative pricing algorithm with the potential for a quadratic speedup over classical Monte Carlo approaches. Yet, quantum advantage will only be found if not just fault-tolerant hardware but also an accurate representation of the financial problem on a quantum circuit is achieved. This paper offers a reproducible, diagnostic examination of the default Qiskit Finance application to European option pricing. We present a critical diagnostic marker: the classical discretized price, which is the classically computed expected payoff on the quantum model's actual grid. Our convergence analysis of low-qubit models (4, 5, and 6 uncertainty qubits) shows that for this particular configuration, representation error, which arises due to imprecise trigonometric encoding of the option payoff function, is the prevalent source of error, much larger compared to the discretization error. In a 6-qubit model, the representation error was 0.17699 USD, which is an order of magnitude larger than the discretization error of -0.01200 USD. We illustrate how a different Piecewise Linear (PWL) encoding can successfully remove this representation error, but at the expense of more circuit resources. Our results, for the vanilla, low-qubit Qiskit Finance circuits we used, indicate that the creation of high-fidelity, resource-conscious problem encodings is a pressing and essential problem on the road to applicable quantum advantage in finance.

title: Surface Flood Diffusion and Inundation Analysis in Multitemporal Sentinel-1 Dataset Using Improved K-Means Clustered Segmentation
authors: A. Lakshmi Parvathi, Omar Mustafa Ahmad Al-Omari, R. Sai Lavanya, Bharathi Panduri, Maranan Ramya, Ata. Kishore Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Surface Flood Diffusion (SFD) involves floodwater spreading on land, influenced by terrain steepness, surface texture, and water volume. The research introduces a Surface Flood Inference and K-Means Chaotic Mapping (SFIKCM) model that aims at enhancing the detection and mapping of floods through the use of multisource satellite imagery and hydrological-based methods. First, the Sentinel-1 SAR data is pre-processed, which involves radiometric calibration and interpolation of the missing values. A hydrological water-balance algorithm models the spread of floodwater according to the elevation of the area, thus the identification of fully and partially flooded areas is made possible by using specific thresholding techniques. Besides that, multispectral and panchromatic images are merged and normalized through methods that alleviate atmospheric differences. the images are clustered by means of an improved k-means technique which leads to the generation of detailed flood extent and depth maps, and the enhanced discrimination of land cover types. The simulation parameter is precision, specificity, accuracy, sensitivity, and FNR calculations.

title: Lie Detector A Behavioral Based Deception Detection System
authors: M. Sridevi, Akshitha Panchadi, S. Shirisha, Sriya Birudavolu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Simply observing a person cannot tell if he or she is lying, and a polygraph type method is least reliable and intrusive. With just one single video, this proposed system evaluates deceit which in turn gets the three modalities: video, audio, and text extracted. The video is informative, showing the face and the body; the audio is the tone of the voice plus the words; what the speech is, is also included. Deep learning models are used to make decisions on these elements. The results give the degree of truthfulness and deception along with the explanation. The apparatus is to supply openness and be without interference in a person's life.

title: Advancing Tuberculosis Diagnosis and Treatment Planning with Robust AI Systems
authors: B.Naveen Kumar, Sunil Kumar Naradasu, K.Amrutha Reddy, A.Asma A. Asma, B. Aakash Reddy, V. Chandra Vamsi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Tuberculosis (TB) remains a major global health challenge, necessitating rapid and precise detection for effective control. Traditional diagnostic methods— such as sputum microscopy, culture tests, and manual X-ray interpretation—are often slow, resource-intensive, and errorprone, limiting large-scale use. This study introduces a deep learning–based system employing chest X-ray images for automated TB detection. Using preprocessing techniques like resizing, normalization, and augmentation, a fine-tuned ResNet-18 CNN achieved exceptional accuracy (99.50%), precision (1.0), recall (99.39%), and F1-score (99.69%), demonstrating its reliability and scalability in aiding clinical diagnosis.

title: AI Driven Adaptive Storytelling for Language Learning Advancing Personalization Engagement and Linguistic Development
authors: Angalakuduru Aravind, Shagufta Parween, Purnachandra Rao Alapati, Kalpanapriyadharshini Thiyagarajan, B. Pavan Kumar, P.Nagaraj
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Storytelling (SLA) is an old pedagogical concept that has been used in Second Language Acquisitionn(SLA) in promoting language development, interaction, and creativity. The conventional methods, however, tend to be impersonal and utilize the generalized methods that are not relevant to the needs of the individual learners. This research proposes an AI-based adaptive narrative framework that measures user feedback and changes vocabulary, story strength and plot on-the-fly. The system offers real-time and custom narrative by incorporating large language models (LLM) natural language understanding NLU driven purpose recognition, sentiment analysis and grammar-check software. We consider the system as a quasi-experimental Pre-test posttest study in which we use 100 respondents to compare AI-generated storytelling and human-generated storytelling in a variety of factors: writing quality, the accuracy of grammar, the clarity of the narrative, and interest. Findings showed statistically significant increases in the experimental group: grammar accuracy (p < 0.001, Cohen d = 1.24), vocabulary expansion (p < 0.001, Cohen d = 1.18), narrative coherence (p < 0.001, Cohen d = 1.32) and learner engagement (p < 0.001, Cohen d = 1.45).The intent recognition component obtained 98.6% accuracy. The result make the use of AI-driven storytelling as evidence- based and scalable language learning tool.

title: Physics Inspired Approach for Enhancing Underwater Images using Virtual Polarizer
authors: Alok Rajput, Vidhi Khakholia, Rakib Rizuwan Riyad, Shayan Roy, Biswarup Ganguly
abstract: Underwater image enhancement is a challenge due to the absorption and scattering of light, which results in severe color distortion and hazy images that reduce contrast. In recent years, researchers have explored a variety of image-processing approaches such as illumination compensation and pyramid-based blending, CLAHE-based intensification filters, color balance combined with edge sharpening, and improved white balance algorithms to restore visibility in underwater environments. Building on these works, in this study we develop a novel variant of the polarization technique, clubbed with existing models like CLAHE, white balancing, and sharpening, to achieve better image quality. The proposed method, termed the virtual polarizer dehazing algorithm, employs a physics-backed model using pure image processing to deliver robust and visually consistent results.
