Under the specified approach, high-pass subimages are fused with LiqDRYNN and low-pass subimages are fused with the pixel-force operator which preserves regional energy and structural regularity. A broad evaluation of different datasets, including the Alzheimer disease, glioma and encephalitis, demonstrated that LiqDRY-Net was betterthan standard fusion techniques both qualitatively and. quantitative visualization. The results confirmed that LiqDRY-Net created diagnostically good fused images, which enhanced clinical decision-making.LiqDRY-Net, which is a new method of image fusion of multimodal fusion, is a combination of the. strengths of Liquid Rybak Neural Networks (LiqDRYNN) using nonsubsampled shearlet transform (NSST) to improve medical imaging services. This new model uses the flexibility of liquid neural dynamics and the biological similarity of the Rybak Neural networks to efficiently combine complementary information of MRI, PET, and SPECT modalities. LiqDRYNN is applied to high-pass subimages to optimize the fusionprocess of the subimages, but low-pass subimages are not optimized. The fusion of subimages is done with a pixel-force operator that maintains the local energy and integrity.LiqDRY-Net was properly tested on the basis of various datasets that involve Alzheimer cases of disease, glioma and encephalitis. The findings showed that it is better than the traditional fusion procedures. both qualitative and quantitative tests. LiqDRY-Net has created diagnostically sound fused images. demonstrated a strong potential to enhance the clinical decision-making processes. This is a development in the field of medical imaging technology may result in improved diagnosis and planning of treatment in different neurological conditions. conditions, which eventually improve patient care and outcome.

title: Federated Learning for Smart Grid Energy Forecasting: A Privacy-Preserving Approach
authors: Vegi Fernando A, Mithaguru, M. Lakshmanan, Mude Nagarjuna Naik, Sriramkumar R, Sugandha Saxena
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: The increasing deployment of smart meters and IoT-enabled sensors in modern power grids has enabled fine-grained monitoring of energy consumption and renewable generation. However, centralized training of machine learning models on this data raises privacy, bandwidth, and governance challenges. In this study, we propose a federated learning framework for short-term load and renewable generation forecasting in smart grids. Using datasets from UMass Smart*, NREL Smart-DS, and Kaggle Smart Grid monitoring data, we train LSTM and GRU models under federated settings and evaluate their performance against centralized and local baselines. We further incorporate privacy-preserving techniques such as Differential Privacy (DP) and Secure Multiparty Computation (SMPC) to ensure data confidentiality. The results show that federated models have similar accuracy to centralized training (2–3% MAE difference), lower privacy risks, and lower communication overhead, indicating the promising capability of federated AI for collaborative, secure, and scalable smart grid forecasting.

title: Evaluating the Capability of Video Question Generation for Expert Knowledge Elicitation
authors: Huaying Zhang, Atsushi Hashimoto, Tosho Hirasawa
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Skilled human interviewers can extract valuable information from experts. This raises a fundamental question: what makes some questions more effective than others? To address this, a quantitative evaluation of question-generation models is essential. Video question generation (VQG) is a topic for video question answering (VideoQA), where questions are generated for given answers. Their evaluation typically focuses on the ability to answer questions, rather than the quality of generated questions. In contrast, we focus on the question quality in eliciting unseen knowledge from human experts. For a continuous improvement of VQG models, we propose a protocol that evaluates the ability by simulating question-answering communication with experts using a question-to-answer retrieval. We obtain the retriever by constructing a novel dataset, EgoExoAsk, which comprises 27,666 QA pairs generated from Ego-Exo4D’s expert commentary annotation. The EgoExoAsk training set is used to obtain the retriever, and the benchmark is constructed on the validation set with Ego-Exo4D video segments. Experimental results demonstrate our metric reasonably aligns with question generation settings: models accessing richer context are evaluated better, supporting that our protocol works as intended. The EgoExoAsk dataset is available in our project page.

title: Web Attack Detection Using Machine Learning: Insights from the CICIDS2017 Dataset
authors: Abijith Arun, Agnel Joji, Abel George, Albin Joy
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: As web-based attacks continue to evolve and change over time, the traditional signature-based intrusion detection systems fail to detect more sophisticated attack patterns and cannot detect the more novel attacks. This paper replicates the studies on this dataset and expanding upon the past work, this study tests the nine Machine learning models like Random Forest, Decision Trees, and others. This experiment reveals that, Random Forest is the most successful scoring 88.17 weighted accuracy. During this some obstacles were observed like extreme class imbalance. The results of this study includes the practicality of Machine learning models as well as to identify the gaps. The goal of this was not only to find the best performing model but also to find how these models behave under highly imbalanced traffic distributions. The analysis shows even though the overall accuracy is acceptable some minority attack classes still remain hard to detect reliably. This underlines that just simply training a model on the dataset is not enough and the importance of handling imbalance and rare attacks is necessary.

title: MomentMix Augmentation with Length-Aware DETR for Temporally Robust Moment Retrieval
authors: Seojeong Park, Jiho Choi, Kyungjune Baek, Hyunjung Shim
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Video Moment Retrieval (MR) aims to localize moments within a video based on a given natural language query. Given the prevalent use of platforms like YouTube for information retrieval, the demand for MR techniques is significantly growing. Recent DETR-based models have made notable advances in performance but still struggle with accurately localizing short moments. Through data analysis, we identified limited feature diversity in short moments, which motivated the development of MomentMix. MomentMix generates new short-moment samples by employing two augmentation strategies: ForegroundMix and BackgroundMix, each enhancing the ability to understand the query-relevant and irrelevant frames, respectively. Additionally, our analysis of prediction bias revealed that short moments particularly struggle with accurately predicting their center positions and length of moments. To address this, we propose a Length-Aware Decoder, which conditions length through a novel bipartite matching process. Our extensive studies demonstrate the efficacy of our length-aware approach, especially in localizing short moments, leading to improved overall performance. Our method surpasses state-of-the-art DETR-based methods on benchmark datasets, achieving the highest R1 and mAP on QVHighlights and the highest R1@0.7 on TACoS and Charades-STA (such as a 9.62% gain in R1@0.7 and an 16.9% gain in mAP average for QVHighlights). The code is available at https://github.com/sjpark5800/LA-DETR.

title: Reciprocal Teaching: Dynamic Multi-Model Teacher-Student Learning for Multiple Noisy Annotations
authors: Wenjie Ai, Cuong C. Nguyen, Adrian Hilton, Gustavo Carneiro
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: As datasets grow, expert-based annotation becomes impractical, making crowdsourcing a scalable alternative. In crowdsourcing, samples are typically annotated by multiple workers and aggregated via majority voting, which ignores annotator-specific biases and introduces noisy labels that impair downstream models. Traditional multi-rater methods attempt to model annotator biases but often overfit with many classes or few, noisy annotators. Learning with Noisy Labels (LNL) methods offer more robust strategies for handling noisy labels, but their assumption of a single noisy label per sample makes extending them to multi-annotator settings non-trivial. To bridge this gap, we propose the Reciprocal Teacher-student Learning from Multi-rater Noisy Annotation (RETINA), which trains annotator-specific models and employs a dynamic teacher–student process to separate clean from noisy samples. Progress in multi-rater learning has also been limited by benchmarks with few classes, fixed noise rates, and no control over annotators. To address this, we introduce the Synthetic MRL (SynMRL) benchmark that contains many classes and controllable noise and annotator settings for systematic evaluation. Experiments on synthetic and real-world data show that RETINA outperforms existing multi-rater methods, particularly in high-noise, low-annotator, many-class settings.

title: AetherHire: An AI-Powered Autonomous Ecosystem for Candidate Screening, Engagement, and Hiring
authors: Karthikeyan P, Madhu Sankar S, Mr.Alline Geo A V
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Recruitment is one of the most vital but tedious human resource tasks, as it cannot be efficient due to manual screening of the resumes, lack of uniform communication with the applicants and poor organization of the interview processes. In this paper, I will discuss the case of Aether-Hire, an independent agent-based recruitment engine that uses artificial intelligence to automate the hiring processes in their entirety. The system can automate its activities such as job description generation, semantic candidate matching, interview scheduling, candidate engagement and offer generation using a multi-agent ecosystem. The system combines the multi-channel communication, calendar synchronization, and e-signature to boost the speed, accuracy and cost-effectiveness of the recruitment. Market research shows that AI recruitment technologies are expanding at a very fast rate which helps in commercial feasibility. Performance appraisal shows that there are drastic changes in the amount of manual efforts saved and enhanced quality and interaction with the candidate. Such issues like AI bias and the scalability of its implementation are discussed as well as improvement in the future. This study presents an innovative way of recruiting with smart automation.

title: A Model Fusion Approach for Enhancing Credit Approval Decision Making
authors: Yuanhong Wu, Jingyan Xu, Wei Ye, Christina Schweikert, D. Frank Hsu
abstract: Credit default poses significant challenges to financial institutions and consumers, resulting in substantial financial losses and diminished trust. As such, credit default risk management has been a critical topic in the financial industry. In this paper, we present Combinatorial Fusion Analysis (CFA), a model fusion framework, that combines multiple machine learning algorithms to detect and predict credit card approval with high accuracy. We present the design methodology and implementation using five pre-trained models. The CFA results show an accuracy of 89.13% which is better than conventional machine learning and ensemble methods.

title: Ordinal-Aware Multimodal Engagement Recognition for Collaborative Learning
authors: Nha Tran, Dat Ly, Phi Ta, Hung Nguyen, Hien D. Nguyen
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Assessing student engagement is critical for collaborative learning but remains a challenging task. Existing approaches often rely on controlled laboratory or online settings, which fail to capture the complexity of real-world classrooms. Furthermore, current datasets are scarce and rarely provide both individual-and group-level annotations, limiting the development of robust and generalizable models. To address these gaps, we propose CORE-Net, a multimodal architecture that integrates context modeling to capture group-level dynamics and ordinal supervision to account for the ordinal nature of engagement levels. We also present COLER, a large-scale dataset collected in authentic classroom environments with rich annotations at multiple levels. Experiments demonstrate that CORE-Net achieves 89.63% accuracy and 94.80 QWK, significantly outperforming strong baselines such as BlockGCN and MoViNet. Ablation studies further highlight the critical role of both context modeling and ordinal supervision. Our work establishes a robust and scalable foundation for automated engagement assessment, supporting timely feedback and enhancing the effectiveness of collaborative learning.

title: AGC-WGAN: Adaptive Granularity Conditional Wasserstein GAN for Time Series Forecasting Using Multivariate Data
authors: Sambit Pattanaik, Debasish Das, Diptendu Sinha Roy, Rabindra Kumar Barik
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: The proposed work presents a comprehensive framework for multi-horizon time-series forecasting and synthetic data generation using an adaptive Granularity Module. We utilise the Earth-Mover's Distance (Wasserstein-1 metric), which efficiently mitigates mode collapse by ensuring smoother critic gradients. To preserve Lipschitz continuity without limiting network weights, a Gradient Penalty regularisation scheme is additionally implemented, resulting in stable critic updates. A lightweight gated recurrent neural architecture, Fast-GRNN, with a Three-Layer Cascading architecture, is also suggested for temporal representation learning and long-term dependencies. Strong alignment between synthetic and real data distributions is demonstrated by experimental evaluations, which achieve competitive metrics, including the KS Statistic (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.28-0.35$</tex>), JS Divergence (0.32-0.39), Wasserstein Distance (0.36-0.56), Pearson Correlation (up to 0.18), and MMD (0.07-0.20). Additionally, with MSE values ranging from 0.0535 to 0.1833 and MAE values ranging from 0.1995 to 0.3472 across Users, Packets, and Bytes, our forecasting model achieves low out-of-sample errors on the test set.

title: Multi-View Learning with Contrastive Representation for Creditworthiness Assessment
authors: Krishna Chaitanya Yarlagadda
abstract: In financial services, assessing a borrower's creditworthiness enables lenders to make reliable credit decisions. Though the complexity of an individual's financial behavior is often not fully captured by traditional methods, which characteristically rely on a single-dimensional view, such as credit history and demographic information. These methods suffer from missing information, a minimum combination of diverse data sources, and inadequate differentiation between contributing factors. To improve credit rating precision, this work proposes the combination of contrastive representation with a multidimensional learning framework. Through combining different types of financial behavioral data, the novel method creates a unified representation that captures correlations between different views. Through improving the distinction between individuals belonging to various categories, contrastive learning allows the extraction of more meaningful patterns from the wide range of data sources. This combination provides a more complete understanding of credit risk and improves the system's ability to cope with noisy data. For the experiments, actual financial data sets were used, and the proposed method was compared with conventional one-way methods. According to the results, the multi-view contrastive representation performs significantly better than the baselines, showing an improved identification of high-risk users and a higher accuracy of predictions. Apart from offering accurate predictions in actual financial scenarios, the work also demonstrates its strength in dealing with missing data. This study demonstrates the probable to integrate contrast and multi-view learning into financial risk assessment, offers a scalable way to reduce financial risks, improve lender decisionmaking, and provide more fair credit ratings for applicants.

title: AI-Driven Semantic Question Management System for Interactive Conferences
authors: Sahana Sharma M, Shiva Prasad, Shaik Moinuddin, Ankita S Patil, Sameer Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The current work focuses on audience interaction system used by large scale live events, to handle semantically similar questions in a scalable and context-aware manner. Most of the available question-and-answer platforms at present are based on keyword matching, followed by manual moderation or popularity-based ranking, leading to repeated questions, misinterpretation of audience intent, and increasing the cognitive load for event organizers. The proposed work ASKQUESTIONS relies on semantic question management for real time audience engagement in online and hybrid conferences. It integrates sentence-level semantic embeddings through a FAISS-based vector similarity search framework for efficient and low-latency retrieval of relevant audience questions. Employing hierarchical semantic clustering mechanisms, this groups questions that express the same intent in different phrasing into smaller clusters, followed by representative question selection and ranking to reduce redundancy while maintaining the diversity of audience concerns. In this way, the overall system is implemented as an event-driven backend pipeline, optimized for real-time operation. Experimental results indicate that the proposed approach achieves an overall retrieval accuracy of 95% and a macro-averaged F1-score of 0.95, while maintaining sub-second average response times ( ≈ 0.10 s). Among the embedding models evaluated, MiniLM-L6-v2 provided the most effective trade-off between semantic accuracy and computational efficiency.

title: Improved Lymph Node Metastasis Classification Through Data Augmentation Using Generative Modeling and Filtering
authors: Shunki Hattori, Yuji Iwahori, Kenji Funahashi, Akira Ouchi, Yasuhiro Shimizu
publish: 2025 Conference on AI, Science, Engineering, and Technology (AIxSET)
abstract: When a patient is diagnosed with rectal cancer, staging is performed to assess whether the cancer has metastasized to other parts of the body using diagnostic imaging such as CT scans. Recently, there has been growing interest in applying machine learning for diagnostic support. In our previous studies, we proposed methods to augment limited datasets using generative models; however, concerns were raised that these models might generate unintended images. In this study, we aim to improve the accuracy of lymph node classification. We introduce Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (DDPM) in addition to the Denoising Diffusion Implicit Models (DDIM) employed in prior work. Furthermore, we propose two filtering methods to eliminate unintended images. Experimental results show that data augmentation using DDPM is more effective than DDIM, and applying the proposed filtering methods further improves accuracy for the same number of augmented images.

title: POSBERT: Particle-Optimized Sentiment BERT for polarity detection on Twitter data with explainable AI
authors: A. Emily Jenifer, Thaventhiran Chandrasekar, Praveen Kumar Karunanithi, B.Bharathi Kannan, M Prasannakumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Sentiment analysis has become vital for analyzing social media trends, consumer feedback, and public opinion in recent years. This study investigates using Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) for sentiment analysis. Bidirectional context comprehension is made possible by BERT’s novel design, which is a significant advancement over conventional left-toright or right-to-left text processing approaches. We gain better results in sentiment classification tasks by using pretrained BERT models and optimizing them with Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO): POSBERT on the Twitter sentiment dataset with positive, negative and neutral labels. Our findings show that BERT performs better than baseline models with 92% accuracy in classifying text into sentiments. Furthermore, the benefits of utilizing explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) frameworks like Local Interpretable Modelagnostic Explanations (LIME) and SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) for exploring and enabling more precise and sophisticated sentiment identification are discussed.

title: Accelerating Machine Learning: Hardware Implementations of Sigmoid and Tanh Activation Functions
authors: Manish I. Patel, Aditya Vartak, Tanishka Vaishnav
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: In today’s world, the demand for Machine Learning algorithms, mainly neural networks, and its software implementation has increased. Considering this, the need for developing a cost effective and resource efficient hardware architecture has also increased. Neural networks incorporate the use of activation functions. Activation functions induce non-linearity which enables users to formulate real-world scenarios in artificial neural networks. The software implementation of activation function running on a general-purpose processor is common but slower; however, for faster processing its hardware implementation is challenging. In this work, the hardware implementation of the sigmoid activation function and the tanh activation function is realized using piecewise linear approximation method and regression tree classifier methods. Our implementations started with the Verilog design. Following that, the RTL to GDSII flow was performed using Cadence tools. For our implementations, the hardware resources that are being used and the error between the expected and calculated values has been presented. Following this, reports for timing, area and power have been generated and analyzed. The highest frequency 333.33 MHz is achieved for tanh activation function, while the minimum power of 0.5733 mW and area of $2554.675 \mu \mathrm{~m} 2$ is observed for the sigmoid activation function. The implementation was performed in 180 nm technology and the results were compared for the same.

title: Innovative Deep AI Financial Forecasting Algorithms: Improving Market Predictions Through Economic Indicators and Temporal Learning
authors: Venkateswaran Petchiappan, Kailasam Muthukumarasamy, Vaishnavi, A. Kanimozhi, E. Sivajothi, S. Jayanthi
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: Macroeconomic considerations, which include many economic issues, affect financial markets in many ways. The changing global economy, investor temperament, and potential geopolitical crises contribute to this. Standard techniques may struggle to define financial time series for forecasting. This is feasible. This is because these time series have nonlinear interactions and long-distance links. This work examines deep learning algorithms that combine economic variables with complex temporal learning methods to improve financial forecasting. This research aims to improve accuracy. This research is being conducted with the intention of achieving the intended result, which is to increase the accuracy of financial forecasts. The research is centered on the development and testing of models that are able to recognize patterns in market data at several scales. Recurrent neural networks, attention-based architectures, and temporal convolutional structures are all utilized in conjunction with one another in order to realise this objective. In order to obtain a better understanding of market behavior inside the framework of the market, these models make use of auxiliary inputs such as major economic indicators, rates of unemployment and inflation, consumer confidence indices, and patterns of commodities pricing. Economic reasoning and temporal deep learning are the best methods for decreasing noise sensitivity, improving predictive stability, and generating better market projections. This approach has been created and implemented. This research examines feature engineering methods that leverage lag structures, rolling windows, and multivariate indicator normalization to improve deep model data representation. This method enhances data representation. Large-scale economic data, sector-based performance metrics, and pricing histories enabled a full experimental setup.

title: A Lightweight Few-Shot Framework for Rare Disease Prediction
authors: Nandana Rajesh Menon, Rohini V, Meenakshi Malviya Rajoriya
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Hundreds of millions of people around the world are affected by rare illnesses, but they are hard to predict because of insufficient data. This paper presents a few-shot learning (FSL) system using structured clinical information, designed to be understandable and accessible. It is built by merging Prototypical Networks with episodic training and t-SNE-based visualization of the embedding space to produce interpretable predictions. A controlled synthetic experiment was conducted to simulate the problem of class imbalance between common and rare diseases, and to demonstrate proof of concept. The model achieved an average accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$61.1 \% (\pm 6.7\%)$</tex> and a macro F1-score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$60.5\% (\pm 6.7 \%)$</tex>, outperforming a logistic regression baseline <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(58.7 \% \pm 6.3 \%$</tex> accuracy). This indicates that metric-based meta-learning can generalize to previously unseen diseases with only a few examples, while providing interpretable explanations for its predictions.

title: AI-Powered Framework for Strengthening Climate Resilience in Urban Infrastructure Systems
authors: Franciskus Antonius Alijoyo, Narinder Kumar Bhasin, Bobonazarov Abdurasul, Shamim Ahmad Khan, S. Suma Christal Mary, R Saravanakumar
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: AI as a resilience enabler based on predictive modeling and adaptive decision-making is what this research is going to propose its framework in order to enhance urban infrastructure system’s climate risk resilience. Unlike other climate danger models governed by legacy sources of data that remain running in silos, this process combines various supply of information, such as satellite image, IoT sensor networks, and socio financial indicators, into a single and context-aware gadget. Essentially, the model incorporates a hybrid ensemble of ML and DL models, including convolutional neural networks, spatiotemporal models, for the prediction of urban-specific climate hazards like the flood, heat waves, and infrastructure stress. One of the special characteristics of this framework is that this feedback loop is dynamic meaning that it keep learning and continually improve the model in response to new environmental and infrastructural conditions it is occurring in real time. The suggested model especially stands out because it is scalable, flexible and applicative in the real world, presenting an actual progress advancement in urban climate imperviousness systems and judicious city planning.

title: A Comparative Study of Variable-Length Integer Coding Techniques for Lossless Data Compression
authors: Zaw Win Myat
abstract: Data compression is essential for efficient storage and transmission. This paper presents a comparative study of variable-length integer coding techniques, including EvenRodeh, Levenshtein, Golomb, Rice, Fibonacci, Goldbach G0, Goldbach G1, Elias Omega, Elias Gamma, Elias Delta, punctured Elias, and Ternary Comma codes. Codewords were generated using standard encoding rules, and code lengths, defined as the number of bits required to represent integers from 1 to 1024, were measured and compared. The resulting code-length growth patterns are analyzed to examine the effects of recursion, prefix structure, and parameter dependence on compression efficiency. The results show that recursive schemes such as Fibonacci and Elias Omega achieve more compact growth over wide ranges, while parameterized methods such as Golomb and Rice perform best when appropriately tuned. These findings provide practical guidance for selecting integer coding techniques in lossless data compression applications.

title: You May Speak Freely: Improving the Fine-Grained Visual Recognition Capabilities of Multimodal Large Language Models with Answer Extraction
authors: Logan Lawrence, Oindrila Saha, Megan Wei, Chen Sun, Subhransu Maji, Grant Van Horn
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Despite the renewed interest in zero-shot visual classification due to the rise of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), the problem of evaluating free-form responses of auto-regressive models remains a persistent challenge. Most existing works focus on language-only tasks or don’t consider Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) beyond 5-way options, both of which are critical capabilities to solve tasks in Fine-Grained Visual Classification (FGVC) where choice counts are in the hundreds to thousands and the choices are highly related. Furthermore, in this highly multi-way MCQ setting it is not clear how to extend LLM choice extraction to retrieval-based problems, where computing probabilities over the choice set is computationally costly. In this work we investigate nlg2choice, a simple two-stage method which first asks the MLLM an open-ended question for the task with minimal constraints, then uses text-only constrained decoding to predict the most likely choice. In retrieval settings, we compute the probability of the constrained response taking that choice with an early stopping method to significantly improve throughput. Our results show improvement over a suite of seven fine-grained visual datasets when evaluating in terms of classification and retrieval, and show that this performance holds over the various ways that users of LLMs can implement tasks in natural language.

title: Explainable Deep Learning Framework for EEG-Based Prediction and Visualization of Accelerated Brain Aging
authors: Rudrashetty Tarini, Prabhakar Kandukuri, Prakarsha Kodunuri, Sravani Yerra
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Recently, accelerated brain aging has been identified as one of the biomarkers of cognitive decline and neurodegenerative conditions. In this paper, we are suggesting a deep-learning architecture that can be used to predict brain age using EEG and also display the way in which the patterns of aging are formed. We then apply raw EEG recordings in training deep neural networks such as CNNs and LSTMs which are used to predict cognitive aging indicators. To explain the actions of the models, we use XAI techniques namely SHAP to identify the electrodes and time windows that contribute most to the predictions. By visual explanations, our experiments indicate that the framework does not only have high predictive accuracy, but also provides a useful neurophysiological interpretation. The explanation of EEG data does assist in identifying early age-related cognitive impairment and assists in the process of clinical decision-making by providing easy to interpret EEG results that indicate potential outcomes.

title: DARB-Splatting: Generalizing Splatting with Decaying Anisotropic Radial Basis Functions
authors: Hashiru Pramuditha, Vinasirajan Viruthshaan, Vishagar Arunan, Saeedha Nazar, Sameera Ramasinghe, Simon Lucey, Ranga Rodrigo
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Splatting-based 3D reconstruction methods have gained popularity with the advent of 3D Gaussian Splatting, efficiently synthesizing high-quality novel views. These methods commonly resort to using exponential family functions, such as the Gaussian function, as reconstruction kernels due to their anisotropic nature, ease of projection, and differentiability in rasterization. However, the field remains restricted to variations within the exponential family, leaving generalized reconstruction kernels largely underexplored, partly due to the lack of easy integrability in 3D to 2D projections. In this light, we show that a class of decaying anisotropic radial basis functions (DARBFs), which are non-negative functions of the Mahalanobis distance, supports splatting by approximating the Gaussian function's closed-form integration advantage. With this fresh perspective, we demonstrate varying performances across selected DARB reconstruction kernels, achieving comparable training convergence and memory footprints, with on-par PSNR, SSIM, and LPIPS results.

title: Dynamic Reward Shaping in Multi-Agent UAV Environments using Graph Attention Reinforcement Learning
authors: Sakthivel A, Karunanithi Praveen Kumar, S Jeevanandham, Ramya SR, Akshya. J, M Sundarrajan, Mani Deepak Choudhry
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The rapid growth of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) applications in surveillance, disaster response, agriculture, and urban mobility has created a strong need for intelligent multi-agent coordination to enable secure, energy-efficient, and scalable autonomous navigation. While traditional centralized control and early multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) methods such as MADDPG, COMA, and value-decomposition algorithms show promise, they suffer from rigid communication structures, slow convergence, poor adaptability to dynamic swarm networks, and limited safety guarantees in complex environments. These methods often rely on sparse or fixed rewards, leading to suboptimal cooperation, inefficient communication, and risky navigation. To address these challenges, this study introduces a GAT-DRL architecture with Dynamic Reward Shaping, which selectively shares information using context-based attention and adaptively reinforces collision avoidance, energy efficiency, formation control, and mission execution. Simulation results under dynamic obstacles and varying swarm densities show 25–35% faster convergence, 30–40% lower communication overhead, and ~80% fewer collisions compared to standard MARL baselines. The adaptive reward strategy also improves mission success rates by more than 15×, demonstrating a scalable and effective framework for safe, efficient cooperative UAV learning.

title: Advancing Player Identification and Tracking with Global ID Fusion (GIF)
authors: Karol Wojtulewicz, Minxing Liu, Niklas Carlsson
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Rapid player motion, occlusions, substitutions, and jersey changes pose major challenges for identity-consistent tracking in sports. Existing multi-object tracking (MOT) methods struggle in long-term and multi-perspective settings like broadcast footage, where views change frequently. To address this, we first introduce MuPNIT, the first MOT and ReID benchmark to provide identity-consistent labeling under multi-perspective dynamics and long-term appearance variations across seasons, teams, and jersey changes. Second, we propose Global ID Fusion (GIF), a novel, online framework that performs single-pass global ID association and supports zero-shot identification. Our approach achieves state-of-the-art results, improving HOTA by 25.3% and IDF1 by 79.5% over OC-SORT. Finally, to assess identity consistency, we introduce five global ID metrics that reveal tradeoffs in tracking stability. By bridging MOT and ReID, our work advances identity-aware player tracking in sports and sets a new benchmark with applications in sports analytics, surveillance, and long-term person search. Code and datasets: https://github.com/Wojak27/GIF

title: Image-Based Steganographic Encryption for Secure Communication
authors: S. Ramacharan, Anushka Mamidi, Sreeja Ganji, Sneha Bala Nandita Duba
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The project describes a secure and efficient way of transmitting messages which is based on a combination of two powerful techniques, cryptography and steganography, implemented in MATLAB. This system allows for the transmission of messages using pictures, grayscale and colored, as covers and the hidden data can be accessed only by authorized persons. AES-GCM uses a 256-bit key to encrypt the message and watermark. The main technique for encrypting the AES key is RSA. This is done by generating a set of public and private keys on registration at the site. The encrypted message is hidden in a normal image using the Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) and Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT). The decryption of the message is obtained by the inverse transformations of the above methods. At this stage decryption occurs which is under the ambit of authorized persons, the messages remain hidden in normal images so as to remain unnoticed by outside agents. As a further enhancement of the security, watermark information is included, which is the name of the sender, the type of the message, the time stamp etc., for reasons of easy identification. The encrypted message remains undetectable to outsiders since the images acquired have no apparent change as compared to the originals, even without the knowledge of a private key. The project implements a scheme which is both robust and effective for secure communication.

title: A Review of Recent Developments in SAR ADCs: Comparator Optimization and CMOS Scaling Trends
authors: K. Ravinder, P. G. Gopinath
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: ADCs [analog-to-digital converters] are essential for closing the gap between real-world analog signals and digital processing systems. The Successive Approximation Register [SAR] ADC is one of the ADC architectures that has gained improvement due to its balance of resolution, power effectiveness, and speed, making it largely suitable for biomedical, IoT, and embedded operations. This paper provides the recent development strategies aimed at optimizing SAR ADC performance in terms of power, speed, area, and resolution. Special emphasis is given to comparator infrastructures, as they represent the most critical block impacting the overall effectiveness. A review of multiple research contributions demonstrates that trade-offs are achieved through inventions in comparator design, DAC [digital-to-analog converters] and CMOS process scaling. The relative analysis suggests that adopting CMOS technologies can significantly reduce power and area while enhancing speed and integration. This study serves as a reference for the low-power, high- performance SAR ADC designs adopted for future applications.

title: Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning Approaches for AI Integrated Coding Platform
authors: Ishita Agarwal, Ajay Kumar, Pradeep Kumar, Bhaskar Manral, Shreyansh Garg
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) integration in programming education can improve learning efficiency, provide individualized feedback, and offer adaptive assessments for students of different abilities. Most current coding platforms focus on competition among students instead of offering intelligent tutoring, graphics visualization tools, and collaborative capabilities. This paper compares three supervised machine learning algorithms to predict student programming success and future AI-assisted learning in the classroom: Support Vector Machines (SVM), Random Forests (RF), and Artificial Neural Networks (ANN). Using datasets collected from the UCI Student Programming Performance repository and IBM CodeNet, we trained and evaluated each model based on the programming characteristics that are lexical, structural, and behavioral. The ANN model performed best, achieving 93.60% accuracy, 0.94 precision, 0.93 recall, and 0.93 F1-score—making it superior to both RF and SVM. Our research suggests that ANN models excel in identifying nonlinear and complex patterns of learning, making them the optimal choice for intelligent adaptive coding educational platforms. In the future, we will build an integrated AI-driven collaborative coding platform that includes personalized tutoring and real-time feedback.

title: HodgeFormer: Transformers for Learnable Operators on Triangular Meshes through Data-Driven Hodge Matrices
authors: Akis Nousias, Stavros Nousias
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Currently, prominent Transformer architectures applied to graphs and meshes for shape analysis tasks employ traditional attention layers that heavily utilize spectral features requiring costly eigenvalue decomposition-based methods. To encode the mesh structure, these methods derive positional embeddings that heavily rely on eigenvalue decomposition-based operations, e.g. on the Laplacian matrix, or on heat-kernel signatures, which are then concatenated to the input features. This paper proposes a novel approach inspired by the explicit construction of the Hodge Laplacian operator in Discrete Exterior Calculus as a product of discrete Hodge operators and exterior derivatives, i.e. $\left({L: = \star _0^{ - 1}d_0^T{ \star _1}{d_0}}\right)$. We adjust the Transformer architecture in a novel deep learning layer that utilizes the multi-head attention mechanism to approximate Hodge matrices ⋆<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf>, ⋆<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf>, and ⋆<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> and learn families of discrete operators L that act on mesh vertices, edges and faces. Our approach results in a computationally-efficient architecture that achieves comparable performance in mesh segmentation and classification tasks, through a direct learning framework, while eliminating the need for costly eigenvalue decomposition operations or complex pre-processing operations.

title: Multi-Directional SPAM Feature Extraction for Enhanced LSB Steganalysis using Random Forest Ensemble Learning
authors: Vibhor Agrawal, Priyanshi Gupta, Islabudeen Mohamed Meerasha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Image steganography poses a major problem to digital forensic experts and law enforcement, as the current modes of embedding may conceal criminal information in most media. Deep learning based steganalysis has high levels of detection accuracy, but has high levels of computational intensity, which can be problematic in resource-constrained settings. In addition, with less complicated models, one might be able to achieve similar detection accuracy. We suggest a light and CPU-efficient framework utilizing classical statistical characteristics and ensemble learning in this study. Residual extraction is carried out with a multi-directional Subtractive pixel adjacency matrix (SPAM) strategy in four directions and with Gray-Level Co-occurrence Matrices (GLCM) for texture analysis and Bit-Plane Distortion metrics. Features are joined together into a 201-dimensional vector, and they are trained on a 200 estimator Random Forest classifier. In experimental work with the BOSSBase v1.01 dataset, it is evident that the peaked detection rate of $92.2 \%$ of 0.5 bpp LSB payloads requires a batch inference time of 44.9 ms per image when using the native $512 \times 512$ resolution. The analysis of importance of features shows that horizontal and vertical residues have the largest discriminative power ($56.3 \%$ combined). This paper provides a computationally efficient alternative to deep learning by focusing on feature engineering; however, it is only applicable to LSB steganography on a single benchmark dataset.

title: Performance Evaluation of Packet Classification Methods for Scalable Network Security
authors: Sangita Wamane, Balasaheb Agarkar
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: Packet classification is an essential element of network security, which supports effective packet filtering, forwarding, and prioritization. In this paper, the performance of three packet classification algorithms is compared: Offline Partition Sort, Online Partition Sort, and Priority Tuple Partition Sort. In our experiments, construction time, classification time, average tables queried, Memory usage and Throughput are measured for varied rule sets of different sizes 1K, 2K, 4K. Based on nine actual rule sets, our findings offer observations on the scalability and performance of each approach under various classification scenarios. This work contributes to highperformance packet classification solutions for current network security needs.

title: Learning-Based Real-Time Emotion Analytics for Multi-Person Settings with Confidence Visualization
authors: Vaishnavi Janjanam, Devi Meghana Thallam, B G P Spoorthi, K. G. Suma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: This paper presents a two-stage framework for real-time, multi-person facial emotion recognition with an emphasis on computational efficiency and interpretability. In the first stage, faces are detected from video streams using a Multi-task Cascaded Convolutional Network (MTCNN), enabling accurate localization of multiple faces under diverse conditions. In the second stage, each detected face is classified into one of seven universal emotion categories using a lightweight four-layer convolutional neural network. To enhance interpretability in real-time applications, a confidence score derived from the classifier’s softmax output is visualized alongside each predicted emotion and bounding box. The model is trained and evaluated on the FER-2013 dataset, achieving a significant validation accuracy while maintaining an average processing speed of 28.5 FPS on standard hardware. These results demonstrate a practical tradeoff between recognition performance and real-time efficiency, making the proposed framework suitable for interactive and resource-constrained emotion-aware systems.

title: ML-GSQC: Machine Learning for Gas Sensor-Based Quantification and Classification
authors: Ujjwal Singh, Akanksha Dwivedi, Ajay Agarwal
abstract: The integration of gas sensors with machine learning enables an effective non-invasive approach for respiratory disease monitoring. We present ML-GSQC, an AI/ML-enabled framework built on our nanomaterial-coated MOS gas sensor system to quantify and classify key breath biomarkers (NO, NO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, NH<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>, C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf>O) and their mixtures. To overcome cross-sensitivity and environmental interference in raw sensor signals, we combined Principal Component Analysis (PCA) for unsupervised analysis with ten supervised models trained on labeled time-series data. Random Forest (RF) achieved the best performance, with 96.7% accuracy for NO detection and overall accuracies of 98.6% for individual gases, 97.5% (NO: NO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>), 96.3% (NO: NH<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>), and 89.7% for ternary mixtures. The novelty of ML-GSQC lies in transforming a single cross-sensitive MOS sensor into a robust diagnostic platform capable of resolving complex gas mixtures, classifying and quantifying concentrations, and enabling real-time decision support, with clinical validation as future work.

title: A Scalable Smart Grid Load Balancing Framework Using MQTT and Blockchain with Hybrid PBFT-PoS Consensus
authors: Siri Sanjana Pasunoori, Swetha U, Ravi Kanth Kotha, Kumar Dorthi, Priyanka P, Sathish Jelli, Anil Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: The increasing integration of distributed energy resources (DERs) into modern smart grids has created new challenges related to load balancing, real-time coordination, and secure energy transactions. Traditional centralized grid architectures are no longer sufficient to handle bidirectional energy flow, dynamic pricing, and operational requirements. The current paper proposes a scalable smart grid load balancing framework by integrating lightweight Message Queuing Telemetry Transport (MQTT) communication with a hybrid blockchain-based consensus mechanism. Practical Byzantine Fault Tolerance (PBFT) and Proof of Stake (PoS) were used to achieve consensus. MQTT provides low-latency and efficient communication among prosumer devices. And the blockchain layer ensures secure, tamper-evident, and auditable power transactions. The proposed hybrid consensus model achieves quicker transaction finality and byzantine fault tolerance within local microgrids and supports a scalable and economically secure environment through PoS. Smart contracts were utilized to automate important functions such as settlement, marginal pricing, and bid matching. The simulation outcome shows communication latency within a second, around 85% prosumer participation in demand response programs, and also a 23% increase in renewable energy utilization. The proposed framework provides a secure, transparent, and interoperable solution for next-generation decentralized smart grid systems.

title: Edge Intelligence Enabled Predictive Maintenance Framework for Smart Manufacturing IoT Environments
authors: K. Radha, A. Vanathi, S. Agnes Shifani, S. Madhubalan, Chinta Sri Divya, R Kamalakannan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The increasing complexity of smart manufacturing systems has amplified the need for real-time, accurate, and low-latency predictive maintenance mechanisms. Conventional cloud-based solutions are prone to large communication lags and lack of privacy, whereas conventional machine learning models are not flexible to data drift and rare events. Today, in the given paper, we introduce a new Edge-Twin Transformer with Gradient Drift Calibration (E2T2-GDC) architecture, which is aimed to offer intelligent fault detection and prognosis at the edge. The framework consists of quaternion-based Kalman fusion to create sensor harmonization, Wasserstein barycenter alignment to create domain adaptation, Synchrosqueezing Transform to analyze high-resolution signals, and a VIBGAN encoder to represent rare faults. Lightweight Hyperdimensional Computing (HDC) and Mutual Information-Aware Federation Learning guarantee the compatibility of the edges and guarantee the security of the fusion of features. Evaluations on ten types of faults have shown that the proposed model can be evaluated at 98.7 accuracy and 98.4% F1-score and can also be utilized in concept drift with time with less than 3.5 % degradation over 120 hours. E2T2-GDC has better generalization, reduced latency and friendliness at the edges compared to baselines, such as CNN, LSTM, and Transformer. This framework reinvents the paradigm of predictive maintenance by integrating explainable intelligence, robust, and edge deployability of next-generation Industrial IoT (IIoT) settings.

title: Integrated IoT and Deep Learning Architecture for Real-Time Crop Stress Detection and Irrigation Optimization
authors: S.P. Saranya, S. Abinaya, K Manoj Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) has become a crucial instrument of solving the resource optimization problems, climate resilience, and food security in poor countries due to precision agriculture. To implement real-time monitoring and decision support, this study proposes a combined architecture comprising of deep learning algorithms, multispectral drone cameras and appliance of the IoT, soil moisture sensors and nutrient sensors of the soil. A time-series predictor based on LSTM generated $\mathbf{9 2. 1 \%}$ accuracy in irrigation forecasting, and a crop-stress detection model based on CNN generated 94.8% classification accuracy. The system was introduced and evaluated over two crop cycles over a $\mathbf{1 5 0}$-hectare area of smallholder farms in rural India with significant improvements in agricultural productivity. The accuracy rate of 89.4% in identifying early diseases and pests led to a 28.6% decrease in water use, $17.5 \%$ decrease in the use of fertilizers and $14.2 \%$ decrease in the use of pesticides compared to the traditional methods. Yield performance was greatly improved with an average increase in crop production of $21.3 \%$ and reduction in post-harvest losses of $19.6 \%$. Socioeconomic effects of the system were also proved with the reduction in labor dependency $(32 \%)$, a reduction in operating costs $(18.7 \%)$, and a net farmer income increase ($24.5 \%$). The AI-based decision-support tool was also scalable by adapting to changing weather trends, with a $96.2 \%$ generalization accuracy in different types of crops and soils. These results show that AI-enabled precision agriculture is a sustainable, flexible and cost-effective way to transform agricultural practices in poorly developed regions that ensure long-term stability and enhance livelihoods.

title: Adaptive Non-Coherent Multi-Connectivity For mmWave/sub-THz Systems With Minimal Coordination
authors: Maddala Vijayalakshmi, M Saara Banu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Millimeter high frequency communication. wave and sub terahertz bands is extremely broad band. but is afflicted with great signal blockage and quick connection. fluctuations. Co- Multi connectivity is able to enhance reliability. herent joint transmission needs a rigorous phase matching and high coordination overhead. It is an adaptation in this work. non coherent multi connectivity method which is non-operative. phase synchronization and at the same time, good performance. A phase diversity method is applied to reduce power variations, and an intermittent blockage model is used for realistic link behavior. MATLAB simulations evaluate outage performance, rate distribution, and ergodic capacity. The results show that the proposed method improves link stability, reduces outage under blockage, and provides diversity gains with minimal coordination, making it suitable for practical future wireless systems.

title:
MAFM
                    <sup>3</sup>
                    : Modular Adaptation of Foundation Models for Multi-Modal Medical AI
authors: Mohammad Areeb Qazi, Munachiso S Nwadike, Ibrahim Almakky, Mohammad Yaqub, Numan Saeed
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Foundational models are trained on extensive datasets to capture the general trends of a domain. However, in medical imaging, the scarcity of data makes pre-training for every domain, modality, or task challenging. Instead of building separate models, we propose MAFM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> (Modular Adaptation of Foundation Models for Multi-Modal Medical AI), a framework that enables a single foundation model to expand into diverse domains, tasks, and modalities through lightweight modular components. These components serve as specialized skill sets that allow the system to flexibly activate the appropriate capability at the inference time, depending on the input type or clinical objective. Unlike conventional adaptation methods that treat each new task or modality in isolation, MAFM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> provides a unified and expandable framework for efficient multitask and multimodality adaptation. Empirically, we validate our approach by adapting a chest CT foundation model initially trained for classification into prognosis and segmentation modules. Our results show improved performance on both tasks. Furthermore, by incorporating PET scans, MAFM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> achieved an improvement in the Dice score 5% compared to the respective baselines. These findings establish that foundation models, when equipped with modular components, are not inherently constrained to their initial training scope but can evolve into multitask, multimodality systems for medical imaging. The code implementation of this work will be made available upon acceptance. The code implementation of this work can be found at Code

title: AI BASED BLENDED LEARNING FOR HIGHER EDUCATION IN SCIENCE, ENGINEERING & TECHNOLOGY
authors: Bishnu Prasad Samantaray, Sisir Ranjan Dash, Sabyasachi Dey, Retno Agus Setiawan, Rajani Agrawalla, Rashmikanta Tripathy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has emerged as a transformative force in education, offering adaptive, data-driven, and personalized learning experiences that can significantly enhance the teaching of science, engineering, and technology. This paper examines the integration of AI-based learning systems into these disciplines, highlighting their ability to tailor content delivery, provide real-time feedback, and foster self-paced learning. Leveraging technologies such as intelligent tutoring systems, natural language processing, and predictive analytics, AI enables educators to address diverse learner needs while optimizing curriculum effectiveness. The study explores current applications, potential benefits, and challenges-including ethical considerations, infrastructure requirements, and faculty preparedness. Findings suggest that AI-powered tools can bridge skill gaps, promote interdisciplinary problem-solving, and prepare students for the rapidly evolving demands of Industry 4.0. The paper concludes by outlining strategies for effective adoption and future research directions aimed at sustainable and equitable AI-enhanced education.

title: AI Powered Environmental Monitoring and Action Platform
authors: M Yuvaraj, M Rohith, C. Jeevapriya
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: Environmental degradation and climate change pose critical challenges worldwide, requiring intelligent and automated monitoring solutions. Traditional sensing and reporting methods often lack scalability, timeliness, and actionable insights for real-world interventions. In this work, we propose an AI-powered environmental monitoring and action platform that leverages advanced machine learning and deep learning models for analyzing diverse data sources, including satellite imagery, sensor streams, and geospatial information. The platform employs convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and transformer-based architectures for feature extraction, while graph neural networks (GNNs) capture spatial and relational dependencies across environmental factors such as air quality, vegetation health, water contamination, and disaster risks. Actionable insights are generated through predictive analytics and anomaly detection, enabling proactive environmental management. Experiments conducted on publicly available datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the system in identifying pollution hotspots, detecting deforestation, and monitoring natural hazards. Comparative evaluations with baseline models validate the platform’s accuracy and robustness. This study highlights the potential of AI-driven platforms to support sustainable development, disaster preparedness, and data-driven policy making.

title: Empirical Analysis of Quantum vs. Classical Kernels
authors: R Shreyas, Pruthvi Sriram Bhat, Malini M Patil
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Quantum Machine Learning (QML) is theoretically posited to solve high-dimensional problems intractable for Classical Machine Learning (CML). However, the practical boundaries of this advantage on current noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) devices and simulators remain ill-defined. This paper conducts a direct, quantitative benchmarking of classical Support Vector Machines (SVM) and Regressors against their quantum equivalents (QSVC, QSVR) across a diverse suite of datasets, ranging from standard tabular data to specialized parity problems. By isolating the kernel function—comparing Radial Basis Functions (RBF) against Entanglement-based Quantum Kernels—a distinct performance dichotomy is observed. While classical methods retain superiority in efficiency and accuracy for continuous geometric data (e.g., Iris, MNIST), quantum kernels utilizing the ZZ-feature map demonstrate a decisive advantage in logical problems governed by global correlations, such as N-bit parity tasks. It is explicitly emphasized that these results demonstrate algorithmic potential in ideal simulation; practical hardware implementation currently faces significant scaling challenges. The findings suggest that QML should not be viewed as a universal replacement for CML, but rather as a specialized accelerator for specific problem classes where data exhibits non-local entanglement.

title: IoT-LoRa Synergized Framework for Urban Sustainable Energy Solutions
authors: Mukesh Choubisa, Raja VaraPrasad, Annushree Bablani, Bheemappa Halavar, Harish Ragavender P, Maithreyan, Ritovan Dasgupta
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The accelerating rate of urbanization has exaggerated the need for sustainable, efficient, and intelligent energy solutions in contemporary cities. The primary objective of smart cities is to establish sustainable, effective, and comfortable living environments that enhance the overall well-being of residents. Given the major challenges posed by twenty-first-century urbanization-particularly concerning public welfare, mobility, sustainability, and resource management-smart cities employ futuristic technologies such as IoT (Internet of Things) and AI (Artificial Intelligence) to proactively address these issues. LoRa is a dependable communication channel in these smart cities since it is affordable, energy-efficient, and future-scalable. An IoT-based LoRa architecture for energy management is presented in this research. The proposed system integrates with RS-485 meter, LoRa and Raspberry Pi. Consumption of energy data is sending to the firebase data-base and that data is visualized on dashboard. By leveraging the system improve the admin ability to make dataoriented decision for optimizing demand and utilization of resources. System results validate that IoT-LoRa offers a reliable and scalable solution for smart city developments with energy efficiency and sustainability.

title: ORCA: Object Recognition and Comprehension for Archiving Marine Species
authors: Yuk-Kwan Wong, Haixin Liang, Zeyu Ma, Yiwei Chen, Ziqiang Zheng, Rinaldi Gotama, Pascal Sebastian, Lauren D. Sparks, Sai-Kit Yeung
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Marine visual understanding is essential for monitoring and protecting marine ecosystems, enabling automatic and scalable biological surveys. However, progress is hindered by limited training data and the lack of a systematic task formulation that aligns domain-specific marine challenges with well-defined computer vision tasks, thereby limiting effective model application. To address this gap, we present ORCA, a multi-modal benchmark for marine research comprising 14,647 images from 478 species, with 42,217 bounding box annotations and 22,321 expert-verified instance captions. The dataset provides fine-grained visual and textual annotations that capture morphology-oriented attributes across diverse marine species. To catalyze methodological advances, we evaluate 18 state-of-the-art models on three tasks: object detection (closed-set and open-vocabulary), instance captioning, and visual grounding. Results highlight key challenges, including species diversity, morphological overlap, and specialized domain demands, underscoring the difficulty of marine understanding. ORCA thus establishes a comprehensive benchmark to advance research in marine domain.

title: Fraud Detection in Online Banking using Hybrid Lepidoptera–Physarum Optimization based Gradient Forest Logit Synergy Model
authors: Sambasivarao Kommu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Fraud detection in online banking can be defined as the process of detecting and stopping non-authorized or fraudulent financial operations in digital payment systems, which has proven to be an extremely important responsibility as a result of the speed with which electronic and mobile banking operations expand. Nevertheless, currently used machine learning models, have serious issues when it comes to dealing with big and highly imbalance datasets in which the rate of fraud transactions amounts to insignificant. Such models have a tendency to be poorly interpretable, overfit, sensitive to noisy features and inefficiently tuned by hand, resulting in poor detection performance and a high misclassification error. To overcome these shortcomings, a new Gradient-Forest-Logit Synergy Model combined with Hybrid Lepidoptera-Physarum Optimization Algorithm (GFLSM-HLPOA) is proposed in this study. The proposed GFLSM is a hybrid ensemble learning model that combines linear probabilistic modeling, tree-based hierarchical learning, and gradient-optimized boosting to enable a trade-off between interpretability and non-linear adaptability. The integrated HLPOA optimally tunes the hyperparameters by adaptively exploring and exploiting the exploration-exploitation mechanism, making sure that the model is optimally configured and converges quickly. The combination helps to increase the capability of the model to describe complex patterns of transactions, reduce false positives and false negatives, and strengthen the overall classification. The PaySim Synthetic Financial Dataset experimental results show that GFLSM-HLPOA performs well, with an accuracy of 99.38, precision of 98.17, recall of 95.62, F1-score of 96.87, and AUC-ROC of 0.996 which is much better than that of the base models. These improvements are statistically validated using paired t-tests, which proves that they are very significant (p < 0.05).

title: Multi-Grained Text-Guided Image Fusion for Multi-Exposure and Multi-Focus Scenarios
authors: Mingwei Tang, Jiahao Nie, Guang Yang, Ziqing Cui, Jie Li
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Image fusion aims to synthesize a single high-quality image from a pair of inputs captured under challenging conditions, such as differing exposure levels or focal depths. A core challenge lies in effectively handling disparities in dynamic range and focus depth between the inputs. With the advent of vision–language models, recent methods incorporate textual descriptions as auxiliary guidance to enhance fusion quality. However, simply incorporating coarse-grained descriptions hampers the understanding of fine-grained details and poses challenges for precise cross-modal alignment. To address these limitations, we propose Multi-grained Text-guided Image Fusion (MTIF), a novel fusion paradigm with three key designs. First, it introduces multi-grained textual descriptions that separately capture fine details, structural cues, and semantic content, guiding image fusion through a hierarchical cross-modal modulation module. Second, it involves supervision signals at each granularity to facilitate alignment between visual and textual features and enhance the utility of auxiliary text. Third, it adopts a saliency-driven enrichment module to augment training data with dense semantic content, further strengthening the cross-modal modulation and alignment. Extensive experiments show that MTIF consistently outperforms previous methods on both multi-exposure and multi-focus image fusion tasks.

title: Forecasting Crowd Dynamics: A Hybrid Approach for Crowd Mobilization Prediction Integrating CNN, k-NN, GRU, and DBSCAN
authors: N. R. Reshma, S. Gokila
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: The Prediction of Crowd Movement is very important for improvement of transport systems, public safety and urban management. In this paper, we have proposed a hybrid prediction approach that consists of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for spatial feature extraction, Gated Recurrent Units (GRU) for temporal pattern extraction, Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise (DBSCAN) for hot spots identification and $\mathbf{k}$ - Nearest Neighbors (k-NN) for classification. The dataset used to train and test the model contained 4,996 mobile signal records from OpenCellID that consisted of location, time, and signal strength data. The experimental results demonstrate that the overall approach produced a Mean Squared Error of 0.02, Mean Absolute Error of 0.12 and $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$ Score of 0.98 which outperformed the GRU, LSTM, Random Forest, and linear regression approaches. The results demonstrate the model’s ability to capture complex spatial-temporal crowd behaviors which support applications such as real-time transport scheduling, crowd control during events, and adaptive urban planning.

title: MEDAL: multi-modal MEta-space Distillation and ALignment for Visual Compatibility Learning
authors: Dween Rabius Sanny, Vinay Kumar Verma, Prateek Sircar, Deepak Gupta
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Visual compatibility recommendation systems aim to surface compatible items (e.g. pants, shoes) that harmonise with a user-selected product (e.g., shirt). Existing methods struggle in three key aspects: they rely on global CNN representations that overlook fine-grained local cues critical for visual pairing; they force all categories into a single latent space, ignoring the fact that compatibility rules differ across product-type pairs; and they demand costly, expert-annotated outfit labels. We introduce MEDAL(Meta-space Distillation and Alignment ), a self-supervised framework that addresses all three challenges simultaneously. MEDAL (i) employs a local–global augmentation curriculum inside a teacher–student ViT to emphasise patch-level texture and pattern similarities while suppressing confounding global shape cues; (ii) partitions the joint feature manifold into learnable, pair-specific meta-spaces so that, for example, {shirt,pants} and {pants,shoes} relationships are modelled with distinct projection masks; and (iii) replaces manual labels with distantly supervised KD, harvesting pseudo-compatible sets via object detection on web images, thus scaling to millions of real-world examples. We further fuse perceptually uniform LUV colour histograms to capture global colour harmony often missed by pure vision transformers. Extensive experiments on Polyvore disjoint/non-disjoint and a 2M-image in-house dataset show state-of-the-art gains of up to +3.72/+2.7FITB and +9.58R@10 over the strongest baseline, whilst cutting annotation cost to zero. Qualitative studies confirm that MEDAL retrieves stylistically coherent outfits and correctly penalises mismatched colour palettes.

title: Harnessing Transformer-Based Deep Learning for Online Discourse Classification in Indonesia: A Comparative Study of RoBERTa and IndoBERT Models
authors: Kevin Joseph Handoyo, Kristian Binsar Pardamean Pasaribu, I Kadek Defa Danuarta, Eduard Mario Kayesa, Yulyani Arifin
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: The rapid growth in the area of digital communication in Indonesia has increased online discourse's intensity, followed by an increase in the spread of hate speech and toxic content. This research aims to develop and conduct a performance comparison between two Indonesian language-based transformer models, IndoBERT, and RoBERTa, which classify toxic and non-toxic texts by using the dataset IndoToxic2024. The research methodology includes stages of cleaning text data, tokenization, handling class imbalance through class weighting, and model training. The evaluation metrics used in this study are accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC. The experiment's results show that both models perform suitably but the IndoBERT model is superior on most metrics: Accuracy, Precision, F1-Score, and ROC-AUC, while RoBERTa has a slightly higher recall, proving stronger sensitivity in detecting toxic text. The contribution of this study in strengthening Indonesian NLP is toward the development of artificial intelligence systems for maintaining the health of digital discourse.

title: Real-Time Infant Emotion Recognition Using EfficientNet-B0 with Explainable Deep Learning
authors: Shivam Kumar Singh, Harshit Pandey, Pooja Bharti, Paras Kumar Yadav, Khushboo Tripathi, Ovais Gashroo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Infants are challenging to read for many reasons, including their facial expressions that contain very subtle clues; there is usually not a large amount of labeled data available, and infants cannot verbally complain or tell us what their current emotional state is. However, it is important for healthcare professionals and caregivers to understand when an infant is in a state of discomfort or experiencing pain. In this paper, we present a new automated framework, utilizing deep learning, to provide an accurate classification of infant facial images based on their current emotional state. The framework uses an EfficientNet-B0 architecture to classify images into one of five categories of affective state: Neutral, Happy, Crying, Discomfort, and Pain. In order to increase the generalization of the trained model across various sources of images and overcome the shortage of data available, we have employed a transfer learning approach along with a data augmentation strategy, using datasets collected from multiple public sources. Additionally, to increase the transparency and trustworthiness of the model’s classification process in a healthcare setting, we have added the use of a type of Explainable AI algorithm, Grad-CAM, which outlines the face regions of interest that contributed to a prediction by the model. Testing of the model showed an overall test accuracy of about 78% while using efficient computational methods that will support real-time use. The lightweight and Explainable AI framework presented here provides a real solution for the automatic recognition of infant emotions and will assist parents and healthcare providers alike in monitoring and caring for infant health and development.

title: Training-free Multi-view 4D Human Motion Reconstruction Virtual Reality System
authors: Yijie Li, Ce Zheng, Yijie He, Joel Julin, Ryosuke Ichikari, Satoki Ogiso, Satoshi Nakae, Akihiro Sato, Takeshi Kurata, László A. Jeni
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Human mesh recovery offers substantial potential for detailed behavior analysis and understanding of complex human-environment interactions. In this paper, we propose a novel 4D Human Motion Reconstruction Virtual Reality System that integrates advanced 4D multi-view human mesh recovery and high-quality 3D environment reconstruction using 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS). Our system seamlessly combines detailed 4D human behavior capture with accurate 3D environment reconstruction, significantly extending traditional visual monitoring approaches. Visualization through an interactive Virtual Reality (VR) platform enables dynamic interaction representation using accurately reconstructed virtual environments and computer-generated (CG) avatars. Experimental results from realistic scenarios validate the effectiveness of our framework in providing immersive experiences and precise human-environment modeling, demonstrating a significant advancement in a practical human-centered representation approach. Our approach consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods, achieving reductions in mesh errors of 24% in PVE and 32% in MPJPE on the CHI3D dataset, and 17% in MPJPE and 64% in translation error on the Hi4D dataset compared to other multi-view methods. The project is available at: https://att100.github.io/MV4DHMR.

title: CrypTon: A Hybrid Quantum-Classical Framework Integrating BB84 Quantum Key Distribution with AES for Secure Communication
authors: Vishesh Goyal, Raguru Jaya Krishna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: The emergence of quantum computing poses a significant threat to classical cryptographic systems, particularly those relying on mathematical hardness assumptions for key exchange. While symmetric algorithms such as the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) remain efficient and widely deployed, their long-term security ultimately depends on the secrecy of keys.
