title: Pretraining Helps When Capacity Allows: Evidence from Ultra-Small ConvNets
authors: Srikanth Muralidharan, Heitor R. Medeiros, Masih Aminbeidokhti, Eric Granger, Marco Pedersoli
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Robust visual recognition on embedded platforms requires models that both generalize out-of-distribution (OOD) and fit into tiny compute/memory budgets. While pre-training is a standard route to robustness for mid/large backbones, its value in the ultra-small regime remains unclear. We present a capacity-aware study of pre-training for two efficient ConvNet families (EfficientNet and MobileNetV3) scaled from "small" to "ultra-small" via a simple, reproducible recipe. We compare three initializations — ImageNet→COCO pretraining, ImageNet classification pretraining, and training from scratch—on two axes of distribution shift: (i) cross-dataset RGB→RGB transfer between LLVIP and FLIR (ii) cross-modality detection where models are fine-tuned on RGB and evaluated on infrared (IR). A complementary classification study on DomainNet probes whether the trends extend beyond detection. Across settings, we find that pretraining’s benefit is conditional on both backbone capacity and shift difficulty. Task-aligned Imagenet→COCO pretraining is the most reliable starting point at moderate sizes and for the easier transfer direction. In the low-capacity regimes, differences are typically within run-to-run variation, and training from scratch can match or surpass pre-training. Classification mirrors this capacity gating. Our results test the premise "pretraining always helps" and instead quantify when task-aligned pretraining pays off for ultra-small backbones and when it likely does not <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>.

title: High Efficiency Smart GaN Multi Output Step Down Converter for EV Applications
authors: Gokulnaath M, Kiruthika S, Kabilasri S, Sasirekha P
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The paper is a high efficiency, intelligent Gallium Nitrate (GaN)-based multi-output step-down converter that has been designed to fit the latest electric vehicle (EV) systems. The given design is interlocked with a synchronous topology of the buck with use of GaN transistors, which allows high frequency switching, low conductive losses, and small scale implementation which is applicable in the automotive setting. The converter is successful when reducing a high voltage DC bus (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$400-800 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex>) to several regulated outputs (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$48 ~\mathrm{V}, 12 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex>, and 5 V) to energize auxiliary EV sub systems such as control units, infotainment, sensors, and lighting. A mix of digital control strategy of phase interleaving, adaptive dead-time adjustment and dynamic load sharing maximizes transient response and results in over 96 % efficiency. The CAN communication provides real-time monitoring and enables intelligent fault detection, diagnostics, and control of energy, which improves the reliability of the system. Simulation and experimental outcomes show that the operation is stable, highly efficient in varying load conditions and lower thermal and electromagnetic stress. The suggested smart GaN converter has low power loss and EMI, which leads to lightweight EV design and a long driving range. This paper highlights why GaN-based multi-output converters would be an effective highly robust energy-efficient solution in next-generation EV power architectures.

title: Analysis of Atmospheric Attenuation Effects on mmWave Signal Propagation in Future 6G Wireless Communication Networks
authors: Amaleswari Rajulapati, Vijayakumar Sivasundar, Anusha Peyyala, Movva Naga Swapna Sri, Bhiksha Gugulothu, A.P. Pushpalatha
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: The objective of this study was to assess the effects of the environment on mm wave communications in reference to a 6 G wireless network. As there was no available measured data, the researchers used simulations created using synthetic data to determine how different environmental conditions affect the mm wave communications. The results showed that frequency bands above 20 GHz are significantly attenuated, especially at 22, 60 and 120 GHz due to oxygen and water vapor absorption. Also, rain, fog, and humidity were shown to weaken the signal as well as increase the effect of distance on signal strength loss. The random variations produced by changing weather conditions were simulated using Monte Carlo simulations. A hybrid RABA-AMCS simulation model was developed and tested and found to improve error rates relative to the Free Space and ITU-R models. The results also indicated that the atmosphere is likely to be a significant factor in signal loss for a reliable 6 G communication system and thus it should be considered during design development.

title: Automated ECG Arrhythmia Classification Using Logistic Regression and Random Forest
authors: S Senthilvadivu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Electrocardiogram (ECG) plays an important role in the early detection and diagnosis of cardiac arrhythmias, which is one of the leading causes of cardiovascular morbidity and mortality globally. The process of manual interpretation of ECG signals is time-consuming and subjective, particularly in long term or large-volume recordings. The paper will rely on a machine learning-based automated ECG beat classification system to overcome these problems by utilizing classical algorithms of machine learning over the MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Dataset. The researchers compare a linear model (Logistic Regression) and an ensemble-based model (Random Forest) for classifying multiclass ECG beats in an experimental reproducible environment. The segments of a heartbeat are each modeled as a 187 -sample number vector with five categories of heartbeats. Training and testing of 87,554 and 21,892 samples were done, respectively. To enhance scale-sensitive models, feature standardization is used to enhance numerical stability. Accuracy, precision, recall, and F1score (weighted) are taken to learn performance in consideration of class imbalance. According to experimental findings, the performance of Random Forest is much more effective than that of Logistic Regression in all metrics because it reaches $97.47 \%$ accuracy and $97.30 \%$ weighted F1- score, whereas the accuracy of Logistic Regression is $\mathbf{9 1. 4 8} \boldsymbol{\%}$. The analysis based on the classes shows that the random forest recall is higher in the minority classes of arrhythmias and shows strong performance for nonlinear behavior and skewed class distribution. Such results confirm that ensemble-based classical machine learning models are effective and computationally efficient for ECG classification tasks. The code is available on the GitHub repository: https://github.com/mldlwork2026/ECG-LR-vs-RF

title: BoxSplitGen: A Generative Model for 3D Part Bounding Boxes in Varying Granularity
authors: Juil Koo, Wei-Tung Lin, Chanho Park, Chanhyeok Park, Minhyuk Sung
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Human creativity follows a perceptual process, moving from abstract ideas to finer details during creation. While 3D generative models have advanced dramatically, models specifically designed to assist human imagination in 3D creation–particularly for detailing abstractions from coarse to fine–have not been explored. We propose a framework that enables intuitive and interactive 3D shape generation by iteratively splitting bounding boxes to refine the set of bounding boxes. The main technical components of our framework are two generative models: the box-splitting generative model and the box-to-shape generative model. The first model, named BoxSplitGen, generates a collection of 3D part bounding boxes with varying granularity by iteratively splitting coarse bounding boxes. It utilizes part bounding boxes created through agglomerative merging and learns the reverse of the merging process—the splitting sequences. The model consists of two main components: the first learns the categorical distribution of the box to be split, and the second learns the distribution of the two new boxes, given the set of boxes and the indication of which box to split. The second model, the box-to-shape generative model, is trained by leveraging the 3D shape priors learned by an existing 3D diffusion model while adapting the model to incorporate bounding box conditioning. In our experiments, we demonstrate that the box-splitting generative model outperforms token prediction models and the inpainting approach with an unconditional diffusion model. Also, we show that our box-to-shape model, based on a state-of-the-art 3D diffusion model, provides superior results compared to a previous model.

title: One-Cycle Structured Pruning via Stability-Driven Subnetwork Search
authors: Deepak Ghimire, Dayoung Kil, Seonghwan Jeong, Jaesik Park, Seong-Heum Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Existing structured pruning typically involves multi-stage training procedures that often demand heavy computation. Pruning at initialization, which aims to address this limitation, reduces training costs but struggles with performance. To address these challenges, we propose an efficient one-cycle structured pruning framework that integrates pre-training, pruning, and fine-tuning into a single training cycle without compromising model performance, referred to as the ‘one-cycle approach’. The core idea is to search for the optimal sub-network during the early stages of network training, guided by norm-based group saliency criteria and structured sparsity regularization. We introduce a novel pruning indicator that identifies the stable pruning epoch by measuring the similarity between evolving pruning sub-networks across consecutive training epochs. Additionally, group sparsity regularization helps accelerate the pruning process, thereby speeding up overall training. Extensive experiments on the CIFAR-10/100 and ImageNet datasets using VGGNet, ResNet, and MobileNet architectures demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art accuracy while being one of the most efficient pruning frameworks in terms of training time. Our code is available at https://github.com/ghimiredhikura/OCSPruner.

title: EcoBot: An Intelligent Waste Classification and Recycling Assistant
authors: M. Jyothi, Raga Divya Sri Mulugu, Umarani Darapu, Akshitha Reddy Moddu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The problem of improper waste management is an increasing environmental concern that is commonly made worse by low awareness of proper waste sorting and recycling practices for everyday items. This paper explores an intelligent waste management assistant that utilizes artificial intelligence to augment automated waste sorting, providing users with real-time recycling information. The assistant uses the user’s camera to take an image, or an image from the user's upload, and uses deep learning powered image recognition to identify wastes (plastic, glass, paper, organs). To further increase usability of waste sorting, and help manage eco behaviors, the assistant has a chatbot integrated to communicate with users and offer personalized advice on recycling, reusing, or disposing of the item. The chatbot makes recommendations in response to questions relating to the process, and finds local recycling options. By integrating visual classification and conversational engagement to facilitate waste management and sorting, the waste management assistant endeavors to create sustainable environments within the home, school, or public space, while increasing the waste sorting experience by making it simple.

title: HistoMILKD: A Multiple Instance Learning based Multi-Teacher Knowledge Distillation Framework for Whole Slide Image Classification
authors: Mayur Mallya, Ali Khajegili Mirabadi, Hossein Farahani, Ali Bashashati
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Foundation models (FM) in digital pathology have revolutionized the field of whole slide image (WSI) analysis, with models such as UNI, Virchow, Prov-GigaPath, and many more outperforming the previously established benchmarks set by the ImageNet-based backbones. However, despite several benchmarking studies, there has been no clear consensus on the choice of a single FM that is best suited for a variety of histopathology datasets and/or tasks. With more than 25 pathology FMs in the literature so far, the challenge of model selection is a growing concern. Although an ensemble of FMs can circumvent this issue, given the bulky nature of individual FMs, the inference time and computational cost drastically increase with the addition of each FM into the ensemble. To this end, we propose HistoMILKD, the first multi-teacher knowledge distillation (MKD) framework for WSI classification. To handle the gigapixel resolution of WSIs, we use multiple instance learning (MIL), making this also the first work to integrate MIL and MKD frameworks into a single model. Our approach leverages the complementary representations of different FMs to distill collective task-specific knowledge into a single trainable MIL adapter on top of the student FM, which is utilized during inference. Evaluated on five public datasets, the proposed approach significantly (p < 0.05) outperforms the individual FMs, their ensemble, and previous MKD approaches in WSI classification. Codes are available at https://github.com/AIMLab-UBC/HistoMILKD.

title: Hybrid Model: Predicting Heart issues by combining SVM and ANN/CNN Approaches
authors: Erasappa Murali, E.Murali, Dasari Lahari, Makena Akshaya Nikitha, Mareppagari Manoj, Battala Vamsi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Heart problems are common now a days, it creates a challenging task over globe to doctors, millions of peoples are dead with this problem, need to identify this kind of health complications at early stages is more important, and also need to monitor patients conditions continuously may predict from health emergencies. HoT devices are preferred to use in the field of healthcare to monitor health conditions by taking many features values time to time and that provide an opportunity to doctor to know patients health conditions timely. Existing similar approaches has limitations like noisy points are not identified properly, working with slow drift, suitable for static data analysis, cannot adopt sudden changes to cluster, and shows less performance. In this research we proposed hybrid prediction approach by combining SVM (Support Vector Machine) with ANN or CNN (Artificial Neural Networks or Convolution Neural Network) to predict heart issues with help of ECG values that can be captured with help of IIoT devices from patients body. To calculate performance four types of datasets are taken into considerations, that are MIT - BIH dataset, PTB dataset, PAF dataset, and IIoT generated ECG dataset. First datasets are preprocessed to identify features (nonlinear, spectral, morphological, and statistical). Experiments are conducted, results are compared with other similar kinds of approaches, from results we observed that proposed hybrid prediction model exhibits 95.4 percent accuracy, 97.12 percent recall value, shows 0.98 value of AUC, and our model exhibits high performance then other models.

title: AdaptViG: Adaptive Vision GNN with Exponential Decay Gating
authors: Mustafa Munir, Md Mostafijur Rahman, Radu Marculescu
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Vision Graph Neural Networks (ViGs) offer a new direction for advancements in vision architectures. While powerful, ViGs often face substantial computational challenges stemming from their graph construction phase, which can hinder their efficiency. To address this issue we propose AdaptViG, an efficient and powerful hybrid Vision GNN that introduces a novel graph construction mechanism called Adaptive Graph Convolution. This mechanism builds upon a highly efficient static axial scaffold and a dynamic, content-aware gating strategy called Exponential Decay Gating. This gating mechanism selectively weighs long-range connections based on feature similarity. Furthermore, AdaptViG employs a hybrid strategy, utilizing our efficient gating mechanism in the early stages and a full Global Attention block in the final stage for maximum feature aggregation. Our method achieves a new state-of-the-art trade-off between accuracy and efficiency among Vision GNNs. For instance, our AdaptViG-M achieves 82.6% top-1 accuracy, outperforming ViG-B by 0.3% while using 80% fewer parameters and 84% fewer GMACs. On downstream tasks, AdaptViG-M obtains 45.8 mIoU, 44.8 APbox, and 41.1 APmask, surpassing the much larger EfficientFormer-L7 by 0.7 mIoU, 2.2 APbox, and 2.1 APmask, respectively, with 78% fewer parameters. Code: https://github.com/mmunir127/AdaptViG.

title: LiDAR-DHMT: LiDAR-Adaptive Dual Hierarchical Mask Transformer for Robust Freespace Detection and Semantic Segmentation
authors: Siyu Chen, Ting Han, Changshe Zhang, Xin Luo, Huan Chen, Meiliu Wu, Guorong Cai, Jinhe Su
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Inaccurate freespace detection remains a significant challenge to the safety of autonomous driving. However, we observe that current multisource fusion approaches rely on converting LiDAR point clouds into depth maps, often lose crucial 3D geometric cues. This compromises the spatial consistency of predictions, especially in complex urban scenes. To address this limitation, we propose LiDAR-DHMT (LiDAR-Adaptive Dual-branch Hierarchical Mask Transformer), a novel framework designed for spatial-consistent freespace detection and semantic segmentation. Our key innovation lies in the introduction of a 3D Relative Position Bias module, which effectively captures LiDAR’s inherent spatial priors. This is coupled with a Dynamic Bias Attention mechanism that adaptively incorporates the 3D positional cues into the Transformer’s attention computation, enhancing spatial coherence. Additionally, we employ a Mask Interaction module and a global-local fusion strategy to jointly model contextual semantics and fine-grained structural details. Extensive experiments conducted on the KITTI Road, KITTI-360, Cityscapes datasets demonstrate that LiDAR-DHMT consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods, achieving a competitive 97.59% F1 score in freespace detection and 69.45% and 84.4% mIoU in semantic segmentation. Our findings suggest that LiDAR-DHMT offers a practical solution for deploying robust freespace perception in complex urban driving environments.

title: MorphXAI: An Explainable Framework for Morphological Analysis of Parasites in Blood Smear Images
authors: Aqsa Yousaf, Sint Sint Win, Megan Coffee, Habeeb Olufowobi
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Parasitic infections remain a pressing global health challenge, particularly in low-resource settings where diagnosis still depends on labor-intensive manual inspection of blood smears and the availability of expert domain knowledge. While deep learning models have shown strong performance in automating parasite detection, their clinical usefulness is constrained by limited interpretability. Existing explainability methods are largely restricted to visual heatmaps or attention maps, which highlight regions of interest but fail to capture the morphological traits that clinicians rely on for diagnosis. In this work, we present MorphXAI, an explainable framework that unifies parasite detection with fine-grained morphological analysis. MorphXAI integrates morphological supervision directly into the prediction pipeline, enabling the model to localize parasites while simultaneously characterizing clinically relevant attributes such as shape, curvature, visible dot count, flagellum presence, and developmental stage. To support this task, we curate a clinician-annotated dataset of three parasite species (Leishmania, Trypanosoma brucei, and Trypanosoma cruzi) with detailed morphological labels, establishing a new benchmark for interpretable parasite analysis. Experimental results show that MorphXAI not only improves detection performance over the baseline but also provides structured, biologically meaningful explanations.

title: Adversarial Pseudo-replay for Exemplar-free Class-incremental Learning
authors: Hiroto Honda
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Exemplar-free class-incremental learning (EFCIL) aims to retain old knowledge acquired in the previous task while learning new classes, without storing the previous images due to storage constraints or privacy concerns. In EFCIL, the plasticity-stability dilemma, learning new tasks versus catastrophic forgetting, is a significant challenge, primarily due to the unavailability of images from earlier tasks. In this paper, we introduce adversarial pseudo-replay (APR), a method that perturbs the images of the new task with adversarial attack, to synthesize the pseudo-replay images online without storing any replay samples. During the new task training, the adversarial attack is conducted on the new task images with augmented old class mean prototypes as targets, and the resulting images are used for knowledge distillation to prevent semantic drift. Moreover, we calibrate the covariance matrices to compensate for the semantic drift after each task, by learning a transfer matrix on the pseudo-replay samples. Our method reconciles stability and plasticity, achieving state-of-the-art on challenging cold-start settings of the standard EFCIL benchmarks. Code is available at https://github.com/hirotomusiker/APR-EFCIL.

title: SilentVoice: A Deep Learning System for Recognizing Silent Speech Using EMG Muscle Signals
authors: Nandhini Venkatesan, J Nirmalraj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: This paper introduces SilentVoice, a deep learning-based system for silent speech recognition using surface electromyography (sEMG) signals. Many people in the world are unable to speak due to medical conditions such as laryngectomy, or because they work in extremely noisy environments. For such individuals, a system that can understand spoken intent without sound is very important. The SilentVoice system uses eight electrode sensors placed on the face and throat muscles to record electrical signals when a person silently mouths words. These signals are processed using a hybrid CNN-LSTM architecture with an additive attention mechanism. The convolutional layers generate the spatial information of the incoming signals; the LSTM layers reveal the temporal variations of the incoming signals, and the attention mechanism helps the model to focus more on the most salient temporal parts. The system is trained and evaluated using the publicly available Gaddy-Klein EMG dataset, which contains 2,088 real recordings from eight electrode channels sampled at 800 ~ H z. On a 20-word classification task, the proposed model achieves a peak validation accuracy of 62.5%. This result is significantly better than the four baseline systems tested Support Vector Machine (SVM) at 1 2. 3%, Random Forest at 15.2%, CNN-only at 20.6%, and LSTM-only at 21.6%. An ablation study was also conducted, which showed that removing either the bidirectional LSTM or the attention mechanism causes accuracy to drop to approximately 22%. The results of this study demonstrate that CNN-LSTM with attention is an effective and promising approach for EMG-based silent speech recognition.

title: Deep Learning-Based Intrusion Detection System
authors: Thanneru Venkata Ramya, Kanuganti Vinay Kumar, Mulugu Nandini Reddy, CVP Supradeepthi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Traditional intrusion detection systems (IDS) find it difficult to keep up with sophisticated attacks due to the rapid rise in cyber threats. The goal of this project is to create a sophisticated network intrusion detection system (NIDS) that can efficiently identify and categorise network intrusions using deep learning techniques. Using deep learning models, such as machine learning models, the system analyses real-time network traffic and determines if it is harmful or not. One-hot encoding and min-max scaling are two feature engineering techniques that have been used to prepare features in order to improve the dataset. A flask-based web application that continuously tracks network traffic and notifies administrators of possible threats uses the trained model. By identifying patterns from prior intrusions, this approach, in contrast to conventional signature-based ID, can identify zero-day attacks. comparable to the strategy put out by Ashiku and Dagli [1]. Our goal is to detect intrusions with high accuracy by evaluating various deep learning architectures. The suggested approach improves network security and assists businesses in successfully preventing illegal access and data breaches.

title: A Review of Integrated Systems for Student Performance Prediction and Secure Attendance Verification
authors: Manas Ohal, Vaibhav Kalvankar, Vivek Kakade, Vivek Bhosale, Veena Kadam, Sandeep Kadam
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The digitization of higher education has generated vast datasets, creating a dual opportunity to enhance academic outcomes and optimize administrative integrity. This paper conducts a structured survey of two critical, yet frequently siloed, research domains: (1) machine learning for student performance prediction and (2) secure, automated attendance systems. Our review traces the evolution of predictive models from traditional classifiers to high-accuracy ensemble methods like Random Forest and XGBoost, noting the parallel rise of eXplainable AI (XAI) to ensure stakeholder trust. Concurrently, we analyze the technological progression of attendance systems from manual logs to modern, fraud-resistant solutions, such as QR codes combined with geofencing, designed to combat proxy attendance. We identify a significant research gap: the lack of integration between these two fields. While performance models rely on attendance data, they seldom account for its integrity. We conclude by proposing a conceptual framework for a unified system that feeds high-integrity, verified attendance data directly into an XAI-driven predictive engine. This holistic approach would provide educational institutions with a single, reliable tool to support at-risk students and improve overall success.

title: Robust Vision–Language Reasoning: Spatially Structured and Uncertainty-Aware Summarization Models
authors: P Deepthi, Sriramsuthir
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The latest developments in the area of Vision-Language (VL) models have shifted to hierarchical summarization, and one of the methods to introduce semantic depth is to rely on scene graphs and uncertainty-conscious segmentation. Nevertheless, the trade-off between spatial complexity (i.e. hierarchical scene graph generation) and computational robustness (i.e. probabilistic attention mechanisms) is a bottleneck that is fundamental to the implementation in the real world in presence of noisy or adversarial environments. The survey gives a universal taxonomy of currently existing VL frameworks, differentiating between spatially organized summarization (e.g., BLIP, GPT-4V) or uncertainty-aware segmentation (e.g., Bayesian Segment Anything Model). By critically comparing MS-COCO, CUHKPEDES and NoisyCOCO with CIDEr, BLEU-4, METEOR, ROUGE-L and Uncertainty Calibration Error (UCE), we find that scene graph-based techniques are better in structured scene knowledge, but fail at occlusion and mobile objects, as compared to uncertainty-based models which have higher latency. We find that the main open issues within real-time tracking, crossmodal alignment, adversarial resilience, and suggest a roadmap to implement uncertainty quantification (UQ) into hierarchical summarization pipelines and increase noise leniency, as well as scalability to autonomous driving, healthcare, and augmented reality applications. The study is a strategic guide that can be used by the researcher when trying to find the balance between spatial awareness and computational reliability in the next generation VL systems.

title: Diagnose Like A REAL Pathologist: An Uncertainty-Focused Approach for Trustworthy Multi-Resolution Multiple Instance Learning
authors: Sungrae Hong, Sol Lee, Jisu Shin, Jiwon Jeong, Mun Yong Yi
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: With the increasing demand for histopathological specimen examination and diagnostic reporting, Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) has received heightened research focus as a viable solution for AI-centric diagnostic aid. Recently, to improve its performance and make it work more like a pathologist, several MIL approaches based on the use of multiple-resolution images have been proposed, delivering often higher performance than those that use single-resolution images. Despite impressive recent developments of multiple-resolution MIL, previous approaches only focus on improving performance, thereby lacking research on well-calibrated MIL that clinical experts can rely on for trustworthy diagnostic results. In this study, we propose Uncertainty-Focused Calibrated MIL (UFC-MIL), which more closely mimics the pathologists’ examination behaviors while providing calibrated diagnostic predictions, using multiple images with different resolutions. UFC-MIL includes a novel patch-wise loss that learns the latent patterns of instances and expresses their uncertainty for classification. Also, the attention-based architecture with a neighbor patch aggregation module collects features for the classifier. In addition, aggregated predictions are calibrated through patch-level uncertainty without requiring multiple iterative inferences, which is a key practical advantage. Against challenging public datasets, UFC-MIL shows superior performance in model calibration while achieving classification accuracy comparable to that of state-of-the-art methods.

title: Insider Threat Detection with Real-Time User Behavior Analytics
authors: Guna Santhoshi, G.LaxmiPrasannna G. LaxmiPrasannna, Ragam Sreeshvini, Bandi Lakshya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Insider attacks are a serious threat to organizational security and frequently sidestep conventional defense measures like firewalls and antivirus software. They arise due to authorized individuals such as employees, contractors, and business partners who deliberately or involuntarily abuse their access permissions to steal sensitive information. This paper introduces the design and development of a real-time User Behavior Analytics (UBA) solution to detect insider threats in an efficient and proactive manner. This solution continuously tracks user activities in several vectors such as login behaviors, file access, e-mail interactions, and peripheral access in real-time and recognizes anomalous activities. By exploiting machine learning algorithms like Isolation Forest and One-Class SVM and a contextual risk-scoring engine, the solution dynamically assesses deviation from normal behavioral profiles or role-based conduct. Based on the severity, occurrence, and context of anomalies, a risk score is given toleach user and early detection of credential theft, data exfiltusion, and privilege abuse is facilitated with the proposed UBA architecture. With real-time tracking, anomaly identification, and risk assessment, this solution presents an end-to-end solution to bolster organizational security, strengthen threat response, and prevent valuable information assets from insider attacks.

title: A Comparative Study on a Wearable Non-Invasive Hydration Monitoring System based on Multifrequency Bioimpedance Analysis with Commercial and Predictive Models
authors: Srividhyasakthi V, Govind U, Amaresh S, Swaminathan Sundararaman, Hardik J. Pandya
abstract: The wearable, non-invasive hydration monitor based on Multi Frequency Bio Impedance Analysis (MFBIA) to measure Intra Cellular Water (ICW), Extra Cellular Water (ECW), and Total Body Water (TBW) in real time is presented in this paper. The Hydra Sense device delivers a low-amperage, multifrequency current according to the IEC60601-1 standard via a tetrapolar wrist electrode configuration and measures impedance over discrete frequencies from 1 Hz to 200 kHz. The technology provides continuous monitoring and early warning. The hydration indices of the Impedance Analyzer PCB were compared with the Samsung Galaxy Watch 4 and Watson prediction formula. Statistical analysis indicated high correlations (r = 0.94) between the TBW results of Hydra Sense, Samsung Galaxy Watch 4, and the Watson prediction formula. The both hands measurement setup of the Hydra Sense had the lowest mean absolute difference (MAD%) of 5.26% compared to the Watson prediction formula. In addition, error analysis on alterations induced by the use of moisturizer proved that the device was more robust in measuring hydration at greater depths (cellular level) than the shallow skin hydration, with error percentages of impedance analyzer PCB much lower compared to Samsung Galaxy Watch 4 readings. The initial findings verify the accuracy of the device, increased sensitivity to hydration alterations, and consistency across changing conditions, showing its applicability to continuous field and clinical monitoring.

title: Remote Sensing Based pH Estimation in Eastern Uttar Pradesh, Using Sentinel-2 and Machine Learning
authors: Ashwin Chitravanshi, Pramod Soni
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The rivers in eastern Uttar Pradesh are considered an important freshwater resource for various activities including domestic, agricultural, and environmental needs. However, river water quality monitoring indeed a complex and vast task, which depends on the field observations. The field observations are mostly spatially and temporally sparse hence, in this study, Sentinel-2 satellite data were utilized along with machine learning methods to predict pH in the river water of Varanasi and nearby areas in the Indo-Gangetic Plain. For this purpose, multispectral reflectance values and some commonly used water indices were extracted with the help of the Google Earth Engine platform for the specific sites having pH observations. Thereafter, reductive models were developed using Random Forest and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost). The performance of both models was evaluated using independent validation data. The results showed that the XGBoost performed comparatively better, with a testing R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> value of 0.41, while the Random Forest model achieved a slightly lower R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> value of 0.32. Albeit the agreement between predicted and observed values is moderately accurate, the models were able to reflect spatial differences in river water pH. The results conclude that satellite-based methods, combined with machine learning, can serve as a useful proxy tool for assessing river water quality, particularly in areas with low field observations. This method has the potential to support large-scale and continuous assessment of water quality conditions in the Ganga Basin.

title: An Intelligent Wearable Health Band with Supervised Machine Learning-Driven Alerts
authors: S Gowtham, S Gabriel Isaac, M. Sudhagar, M. Benisha, S. Revathy, P. Gobi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The proposal of a Smart Health Band with AI Alerts will help to ensure the availability of $\mathbf{2 4}$-hour, real-time health tracking and anticipating of possible medical threats. This system combines the wearable sensor with artificial intelligence algorithms to monitor the key vital parameters, including heart rate, body temperature, body oxygen level, physical activity, and sleep patterns. The data obtained is processed with the help of machine learning models that recognize the abnormal trends, and forecasts health problems before these get out of the control. In case of irregularities, the smart band will send immediate AIbased alerts to alert a user, a caregiver, or a healthcare specialist to intervene in time. The given solution will improve preventative health care, as it will shorten the response time, improve individual knowledge of health conditions, and assist with remote monitoring of patients. It is small in size, consumes little power and has the ability to be connected wirelessly, thus makes it usable on a daily basis. Altogether, the Smart Health Band with AI Alerts represents an effective, economical method of the personal healthcare management and proactive monitoring of wellness levels.

title: Context-Aware Particle Filtering Framework for Belief-Driven Human-Robot Navigation
authors: Nimantha Adikaram, Isuru Munasinghe, Charitha Dombawala, Diluka Moratuwage, Asanka Perera
publish: 2026 International Conference on Image Processing and Robotics (ICIPRoB)
abstract: Human-robot interaction (HRI) navigation in crowded indoor spaces must prioritize safety and social comfort. Particle Filters (PFs) are widely used to track dynamic agents because they can represent non-Gaussian, multimodal human motion. However, existing literature often employs fixed-uncertainty tracking and standard resampling, which can lead to overconfidence, particle impoverishment, and poor recovery during missed detections and occlusions. We introduce a belief-aware HRI navigation pipeline that explicitly maintains and propagates realistic uncertainty about human states. We propose a context-aware particle filter (CA-PF) that adapts motion uncertainty online based on interaction distance, robot motion, visibility, and observation reliability, together with a context-aware resampling strategy that conditions resampling on effective sample size, occlusion status, and risk level to preserve hypothesis diversity in ambiguous situations. Experimental results in crowded navigation scenarios demonstrate improved robustness under occlusion, better hypothesis retention, a 12.7% reduction in resampling operations, and a 5.1% improvement in motion smoothness (RMS acceleration).

title: Identification and Categorization of Gemstones Through Deep Learning Approach
authors: Anuradha Shantanu Kanade, Devesh Kumar Srivastava, Pradeep Kumar Tiwari, Shantanu Pandurang Kanade
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: In this research work, machine learning and convolutional neural network (CNN) techniques are employed for object recognition and classification of gemstones. By using CNN, we can identify patterns in a gemstone to determine its proper classification. The images were cropped using bounding boxes to only retain the necessary areas. Furthermore, CANNY and VGG16 have been used for image recognition. Canny is used to find the coordinates in the image of the gemstone and create a virtual boundary around the image. VGG-16 is used for image classification and recognition. A dataset [2] from Kaggle was used for the completion of this study. Because ADAM method is so effective to handle complex problems with huge amount of data and many parameters, so it is chosen for Optimization. Since it is easy to optimize models that are close to linear, RELU (Rectified Linear Unit) function is chosen as the activation function for CNN's input layers. Canny and Hough line method are used for edge and border detection of the gems. SOFTMAX is utilized for the output layer. Its functions similarly to the sigmoid function and divides each output into equal halves such that the sum of all the outputs produces one. While processing this model, an accuracy of 87% is attained.

title: Fake News Detection for Indian News Dataset: Model Based on Additional Bias Using Sentiments
authors: Aishwarya C, Venkatesan M, Prabhavathy P, Anshul Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: A "subtle" type of fake news has come about in response to the spread of advanced false information on Indian internet news sites. This type of false news closely copies the tone, subject matter, and choice of keywords used in real journalism. This makes traditional ways of finding things useless. This study examines whether models necessitate supplementary contextual information regarding words or if superficial indicators, such as sentiment and word frequency, are adequate for differentiating between authentic and counterfeit material. We used the "Indian Fake News Dataset" from Kaggle to get a good mix of real and fake articles. After that, we make a comparative pipeline using a RandomForest classifier. We use TF-IDF to find lexical features and VADER sentiment scores to find emotional features. This is comparable to prior research employing emotional bias and linguistic manipulation. We also create a contextual model by using a multilingual SentenceTransformer to turn the title and body of each article into dense semantic embeddings at the same time. Both models are trained and tested using the same methods, which are Random Over-Sampling and standard classification criteria. The hybrid model that combines TF-IDF and VADER only gets 63% accuracy. The SentenceTransformer-based model, on the other hand, gets 73% accuracy and is much better at finding fake news. This ten-point gain shows that the main "fingerprint" of false information is in the context of the material, not just the signals of phrases or feelings.

title: Feature Selection and Dimensionality Reduction for Software Defect Prediction Using Hybrid Ensemble Learning
authors: Damanjit Singh, Ramandeep Kaur, Inderpal Singh, Anurag Sharma
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: In the digital era, The Software Defect Prediction (SDP) is being very vital tool that enhances the reliability of software and decreases the expenses of software maintenance through predicting defect-prone modules at very early stages of development. In this paper, the researcher proposed a streamlined hybrid model using ensemble technique that combines feature selection, dimensionality reduction, and various classification methods to create one pipeline. Information Gain (IG) and Principal Component Analysis (PCA) are utilized to select the most pertinent software metrics and to reduce dimension respectively. To reduce the imbalance between the classes and improve the generalization, data preprocessing methods such as normalization and the resampling method by using SMOTE are used. The proposed model is a combination of the five heterogeneous classifiers that consist of the RF, SVM, naive bayes, decision tree, and KNN with an average voting mechanism. As it is experimentally validated on NASA KC1 dataset, the hybrid ensemble performs even better than the individual classifiers with the accuracy of 96% with improved precision, F1-score, and recall. The findings indicate the usefulness of feature optimization and ensemble learning in terms of improving the accuracy, strength the interpretability. This is a method of scalable and dependable solution to software quality assurance systems in real-life situations.

title: AortaDiff: A Unified Multitask Diffusion Framework for Contrast-Free AAA Imaging
authors: Yuxuan Ou, Ning Bi, Jiazheng Pan, Jiancheng Yang, Boliang Yu, Usama Zidan, Regent Lee, Vicente Grau
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: While contrast-enhanced CT (CECT) is standard for assessing abdominal aortic aneurysms (AAA) , the required iodinated contrast agents pose significant risks, including patient allergies and environmental harm. To reduce contrast agent use, methods have focused on generating synthetic CECT from non-contrast CT (NCCT) scans. However, most adopt a multi-stage pipeline that first generates images and then performs segmentation, which leads to error accumulation and fails to leverage shared information. To address this, we propose a unified framework that generates synthetic CECT images from NCCT scans while simultaneously segmenting the aortic lumen and thrombus. Our approach integrates conditional diffusion models (CDM) with multi-task learning, enabling end-to-end joint optimization of image synthesis and anatomical segmentation. Unlike previous multitask diffusion models, our approach requires no initial predictions (e.g., a coarse segmentation mask), shares both encoder and decoder parameters across tasks, and employs a semi-supervised training strategy to learn from scans with missing segmentation labels, a common constraint in clinical data. Evaluated on a cohort of 264 patients, our method consistently outperformed state-of-the-art single-task and multi-stage models. For image synthesis, it achieved a PSNR of 25.61 dB, compared to 23.80 dB from a single-task CDM. For segmentation, it improved the lumen Dice score to 0.89 from 0.87 and the challenging thrombus Dice score to 0.53 from 0.48 (nnU-Net). These segmentation enhancements led to more accurate clinical measurements, reducing the lumen diameter MAE to 4.19 mm from 5.78 mm and the thrombus area error to 33.85% from 41.45%. Code is at https://github.com/yuxuanou623/AortaDiff.

title: Bitcoin Price Detection Forecasting Using Time-Aware Machine Learning Models
authors: K Senthil
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The crypto markets are volatile, non-linear, and change regimes rapidly; therefore, predicting any movement in price is a very challenging task for the conventional financial model. Bitcoin (BTC) is also among the digital assets that have attracted a lot of attention from researchers and investors because of its market dominance and its sensitivity to speculative actions. This paper suggests a better machine learning-powered predictor of the short-term price direction of the Bitcoin price movement by combining efficient feature engineering, time-aware data division, and sophisticated machine learning classification models. Historical price data of Bitcoin are preprocessed to yield useful statistical indicators and technical indicators, such as logarithmic returns, moving averages, volatility, and momentum-based attributes. It uses a time-conscious holdout approach to maintain time dependencies, which, in out-of-sample evaluation, is realistic. In order to reduce the problem of class imbalance between uphill and downhill price changes, the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) is used only on the training set. Two strong machine learning models are trained and assessed using extensive performance measures of Support Vector Machine (SVM) with radial basis function kernel and Gradient Boosting classifier. The results of the experiments prove that the suggested framework shows better predictive performance than baseline models, and both SVM and Gradient Boosting outperform the baseline models in terms of accuracy. The results verify that the construction of features and time-series-consistent validation are more significant than model complexity. The code is available on the GitHub repository: https://github.com/aimlprojects2025/btcSVMvsGB

title: Short-Term Electricity Load Forecasting Using Single-Input Transfer Function Models
authors: Wanyuan Liu, Yanyu Qi, Junlin Liang, Jia Na, Guangjie Liu, Runying Zhao
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Renewable Energy and Electrical Technology (ICREET)
abstract: Electric power remains a fundamental driver of industrialization and technological development, with demand continuing to rise worldwide. Managing the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity presents significant challenges due to high costs and system complexity, making accurate consumption forecasting essential for effective grid operation. Traditional statistical methods such as ARIMA, as well as hybrid models like ARIMA combined with artificial neural networks, have been widely adopted. However, these approaches often struggle to capture the nonlinear and dynamic characteristics of electricity demand, particularly in multivariate settings. To address this limitation, this study evaluates the forecasting performance of transfer function models with different input structures. Specifically, the research compares single-input and multi-input transfer function approaches using data from two South China Grid subsystems, Local load point and Regional load subsystem. The findings indicate that the single-input transfer function delivers more stable and accurate results, achieving an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> of 0.90 in both training and testing phases. In contrast, the multiinput transfer function demonstrates weaker generalization ability, with its <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> dropping from 0.76 in training to only 0.69 in testing. These results suggest that, under the examined conditions, single-input transfer function models are better suited for shortterm electricity consumption forecasting. Nonetheless, the moderate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> values also highlight the strong nonlinear nature of the consumption data, indicating the need for more advanced modeling frameworks in future work.

title: A Simulation-based Analysis of SMRF in Quantum Circuits
authors: Deebaroti Dey, Indradeep Chowdhury, Shakil Anowar Mallik, Mousum Handique, Megha Arakeri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Quantum computing has been identified as a rapidly developing area that has the potential to outperform classical computing systems for many computational problems. However, the reliability of quantum circuits is still a major challenge due to imperfections in their hardware systems and control errors. This paper examines the effect caused by Single Missing Rotation Faults (SMRF) in the quantum circuits. The method used in this paper works by the elimination of selected rotation gates to generate faulty circuits using statevector simulation to analyze the effect by various metrics such as state fidelity, process fidelity, phase and probability deviation. The experimental results are demonstrated that observable phase and amplitude deviations can be realized by even a single missing rotation fault in the quantum circuit, which are not observed by probability distribution alone. The SMRF analysis carried out provides knowledge about error prone areas in the quantum circuit, thus establishing a foundation for a comprehensive reliability evaluation in NISQ-era hardware.

title: SGALL: SinGAN and YOLOv8 generated Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia dataset for enhanced Detection and Classification
authors: Rajashree Nayak, Amrita Bagchi, Bunil Kumar Balabantaray
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Early detection of Acute Lymphoblastic Leukaemia (ALL) using deep learning-enabled CAD models (DL-CAD) outperforms the conventional models (slow and prone to interpersonal observability). Development of a generalizable clinical utility DL-CAD model firmly depends on the size and variability of the dataset used for the training and validation process. GANs solve the data scarcity problem by synthetically generating diversified realistic samples. However, affirming the realism of generated samples is essential. Their use without appropriate validation introduces noise in the training data and potentially misguides the entire learning process. Moreover, training of GANs is computationally intensive and requires significant processing power and time. To address these bottlenecks, we propose a DL-CAD model that enables (i) generation of a diversified ALL dataset, namely SGALL, by leveraging the power of the lightweight generative model SinGAN and the YOLOv8 algorithm and (ii) classification of ALL samples using different pre-trained models. SinGAN, is trained on a single blood smear image through multi-scale pyramidal structure and has the capability to add variability in the generated dataset. YOLOv8 algorithm annotates the generated dataset to validate authentic samples and discards the ambiguous samples. Through extensive experimentation, performance analysis of different pre-trained classification models is verified on ALL samples generated through conventional data augmentation techniques, only SinGAN technique, and Annotated-SinGAN techniques (SGALL dataset). The DenseNet169 model trained on the SGALL dataset achieved 99.98% accuracy and 100% precision in diagnosing ALL samples.

title: Enhance Customer Experience in E-Commerce Marketing Through AI-Based Predictive Modelling
authors: Tanmay Kumar Shah
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The combination of AI and commerce creates an innovative revolution which transforms both marketing practices and consumer interactions. The study presents a Bi-LSTM Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory model which uses AI technology to achieve precise Amazon review tone detection. The proposed research uses a bi-LSTM sentiment analysis system to classify Amazon product review data. The proposed model performance is compared to traditional machine learning models which include support vector machine (SVM) and Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) and logistic regression (LR) and random forest (RF) methods. Bi-LSTM model performs better than the three baseline models. The system obtained an accuracy of 95.34, a precision of 94.42, a recall of 95.45, an F1 score of 94.76, and a ROC-AUC score of 0.9773. The findings indicate that deep learning models are able to reproduce short-term and long-term trends of customer language to improve their capacity to detect customer sentiment. These insights can be used by e-commerce enterprises to enhance their personalized recommendations and customer communication approaches and their marketing strategies. The research offers a great basis on which to make online business decisions since it explores the application of complex neural networks in carrying out massive sentiment analyses.

title: Phishing Detection Using a TabNet and Graph Neural Network Hybrid Approach
authors: K. Ananthajothi, K. Lokesh
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: Phishing attacks continue to be one of the most prevalent cyber threats, exploiting human trust and online vulnerabilities to steal sensitive information. Detecting these attacks requires analyzing both structured features such as URL patterns, SSL certificate details, and domain attributes, as well as the complex relationships among websites and links. This study presents a hybrid deep learning framework that integrates the strengths of TabNet and Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to effectively identify phishing websites. The TabNet component focuses on extracting and interpreting tabular features, leveraging attention-based feature selection for improved transparency, while the GNN component models the interconnected structure of domains and hyperlinks to capture relational dependencies. The proposed hybrid architecture enables the system to learn from both feature-level and graphlevel representations, providing a comprehensive understanding of phishing behavior. Using the Phishing Websites Dataset along with graph-based domain relationships, the hybrid model achieved an accuracy of 97.3%. Additionally, the model offers interpretability, where TabNet highlights the most influential features contributing to predictions and GNN reveals how suspicious domains are interconnected. This research demonstrates that the integration of tabular and graph-based learning provides a reliable, scalable, and explainable defense mechanism for phishing detection, enhancing cybersecurity resilience in real-world web environments.

title: A Lightweight Hybrid DVFS Governor with EMA Smoothing and Asymmetric Hysteresis: FPGA Based Evaluation and Node-Scaled Analysis
authors: Chirag Tripathi, R.K. Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Dynamic Voltage and Frequency Scaling (DVFS) continues to be a core method of performance and power adaptation of digital systems. However, the conventional DVFS governors which only rely on instantaneous workload measurements often have mode-chattering, unstable transitions, and cannot handle bursty loads. This paper presents a lightweight hybrid DVFS governor that applies an Exponential Moving Average (EMA) smoothing filter with an asymmetric hysteresis mechanism to achieve improved transition stability while maintaining hardware simplicity.The hybrid governor is evaluated using an FPGA-targeted Vivado flow for a Xilinx Artix-7 device and compared with a baseline threshold-based controller with the same workload stimuli. Experiments indicate that the hybrid governor decreases mode switches by 28%, which results in a more stable and smoother DVFS operation. Due to the static-power dominated nature of 28 nm FPGAs, it causes the total power to be almost constant (0.093-0.094 W), but dynamic power marginally grows with the additional arithmetic units. The technology-scaled power model to test its extrapolation to larger models than FPGA is built over 180 nm, 90 nm and 65 nm CMOS nodes, and demonstrates that the hybrid governor has definite dynamic power advantages in older node products or larger SoCs where transition cost is dominant. The proposed approach therefore provides a low-overhead DVFS solution for embedded processors, microcontrollers, and FPGA-based compute platforms.

title: VeryNews: A Multi-Agent System for Automated News Authenticity Verification
authors: Guang Yang, Peter Trinh
abstract: Misinformation and disinformation in online news pose significant risks to public trust and societal stability. We present VeryNews, a multi-agent system for automated news authenticity verification. VeryNews decomposes news content into structured 5W1H elements, orchestrates collaborative factchecking and evidence aggregation using trusted sources, and generates transparent, auditable reports. Leveraging advanced large language models (LLMs) and real-time web search, VeryNews delivers both a JSON verdict and a comprehensive Markdown report, supporting explainable and scalable factchecking. We demonstrate the system's architecture, workflow, and evaluation on real-world news claims, highlighting its effectiveness and extensibility for trustworthy AI applications.

title: CLIP-IT: CLIP-based Pairing of Histology Images with Privileged Textual Information
authors: Banafsheh Karimian, Giulia Avanzato, Soufiane Belharbi, Alexis Guichemerre, Luke McCaffrey, Mohammadhadi Shateri, Eric Granger
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Multimodal learning has shown promise in medical imaging, combining complementary modalities like images and text. Vision-language models (VLMs) capture rich diagnostic cues but often require large paired datasets and promptor text-based inference. Their practicality is therefore limited due to annotation cost, privacy, and compute demands. Unpaired external text, like pathology reports, can still provide complementary diagnostic cues if semantically relevant content is retrievable per image. To address this, we introduce CLIP-IT, a novel framework that relies on rich unpaired text reports. Specifically, CLIP-IT uses a CLIP model pre-trained on histology image–text pairs from a separate dataset to retrieve the most relevant unpaired textual report for each image in the downstream unimodal dataset. These reports, sourced from the same disease domain and tissue type, form pseudo-pairs that reflect shared clinical semantics rather than exact alignment. Knowledge from these texts is distilled into the vision model during training, while LoRA-based adaptation mitigates the semantic gap between unaligned modalities. At inference, only the vision model is used, maintaining low overhead while still benefiting from multimodal training without requiring paired data in the downstream dataset. Experiments<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup> show that CLIP-IT consistently improves classification accuracy over both unimodal and multimodal CLIP-based baselines in most cases, without requiring paired annotations per dataset or incurring additional inference-time complexity.

title: Hybrid Machine Learning and Queuing Theory Approach for Real-Time Hospital Resource Allocation
authors: K. Sasikala, S. Karpagalakshmi, M. Rajeswari, C Abinaya, E Janani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Healthcare resources are dispersed in accordance with established norms. However, patients enter the system in an unpredictable manner. It causes healthcare inefficiencies, which must be addressed. As a result, solutions for simply satisfying demand, such as resource use patterns, must be developed. Traditional hospital queuing and forecasting systems have been ineffectual. The results are disappointing in two ways: there are either too few or too many patients. Both waste hospital resources. To address these concerns, a hybrid machine learning-queueing theory framework was developed. The proposed system forecasts workloads and employs hybrid queue modeling and real-time optimization to change elastic queue sizes as needed. It uses a KSVR prediction model and an RL dispatcher to make real-time bed and personnel allocation decisions. Forecast outputs are updated periodically throughout the process to adjust and balance queue parameters. The study results, which preserve privacy, show a $31.8 \%$ decrease in patient wait time and $21.9 \%$ decrease in unoccupied beds. The forecast error (MAPE) is about $1.2 \%$ lower than the same organization’s queuing and single ML model. The proposed method is both cost-effective and scalable, relying on neural networks and machine learning for real-time, adaptive hospital resource distribution.

title: Model Context Protocol Driven Reinforcement Learning Framework for Next Generation LLM Training
authors: Devendra Babu Tati, Bhanu Prakash Reddy Nareddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Most Large Language Models (LLMs) perform particularly well on tasks, but often they are highly unstable, fail to follow their policies, and are unpredictable when using adversarial, ambiguous, or risky prompts. To overcome these limitations, this paper introduces a Protocol-Driven Reinforcement Learning (PD-RL) framework which incorporates protocol reasoning into the learning and optimization pipeline. The design is based on validator-nonrewarded reward signals, multi-step execution of the reinforcement process, the prioritized replay of non-compliant outputs, and an autonomous repair process to ensure behavior adhering to rules is stable without a reduction in utility. It uses a common evaluation environment to compare PD-RL with two baseline models in terms of several dimensions, such as compliance, governance drift, utility, rare-case reliability, computation overhead, and recovery speed. The findings reveal that PD-RL allows obtaining over 90 percent compliance, greatly reducing governance violations, and quickly recovering safe behavior within minutes. These results prove that the combination of protocol constraints with reinforcement learning produces not only the performant but effectively aligned and reliable LLMs. Therefore, PD-RL provides a capable and scalable channel to build reliable LLMs that can be applied in enterprises and safety issues.

title: AI-Enabled Hybrid Decision Engine for Real-Time Financial Risk Forecasting in Enterprise Capital Management
authors: N Srinu, Thaer Faisal Abdelrahim Qushtom, K Latha, Preeti Khurana, S. Famitha, D Siri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The paper presents an AI-based hybrid decision engine to forecast financial risks in real-time in enterprise capital management, a new and rare combination of advanced and rare modeling algorithms: Multiresolution Dynamic Time Warping, Hierarchical Quantum Recurrent Reservoirs (HQRR), and Multi-Head Hypergraph Attention Networks (MH-HGAT). It goes beyond the short-term dependence that conventional models have in dealing with long-term dependencies, nonlinear market behavior, and non-frequency financial disruption by introducing multi-scale financial information into a tensorized representation and subjecting it to quantum-enhanced reservoirs and hypergraph-based attention layers. Conformal predictors that are risk-sensitive and GAN-based stress simulators contribute even more to the robustness of the model in the face of uncertainty and volatility. Results on experimental data of various datasets and market regimes show better performance, where the proposed model has a forecast accuracy of 94.3% at 1-day horizons and has 74.4% in 150 days, much better than LSTM, ARIMA and XGBoost baselines. The system also reduces the deviation of Value at Risk (VaR), better calibration of the confidence interval and quicker concept drift identification. The risk identification by classes presents an improved F1-score in both high-risk and rare classes. The framework helps in producing real-time interpretable and adaptive financial decisions which makes it a scalable answer to risk governance in dynamic enterprises.

title: Salience-SGG: Enhancing Unbiased Scene Graph Generation with Iterative Salience Estimation
authors: Runfeng Qu, Ole Hall, Pia K Bideau, Julie Ouerfelli-Ethier, Martin Rolfs, Klaus Obermayer, Olaf Hellwich
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Scene Graph Generation (SGG) suffers from a long-tailed distribution, where a few predicate classes dominate while many others are underrepresented, leading to biased models that underperform on rare relations. Unbiased-SGG methods address this by implementing debiasing strategies, but often at the cost of spatial understanding—resulting in over-reliance on semantic priors. We introduce Salience-SGG, a novel framework featuring an Iterative Salience Decoder (ISD) that emphasizes triplets with salient spatial structures. To support this, we propose semantic-agnostic salience labels guiding ISD. Evaluations on Visual Genome, Open Images V6, and GQA-200 show that Salience-SGG achieves state-of-the-art performance and improves existing Unbiased-SGG methods in their spatial understanding as demonstrated by the Pairwise Localization Average Precision. Code is available at: https://github.com/runfeng-q/Salience-SGG.

title: Medtrack: An Integrated mhealth Platform for Emergency Blood and Antivenom Resource Discovery with Medication Management
authors: Mamta B Savadatti, Rose Mary Chacko, Ganesh Ramachandra Hegde, Diganth H S, Dhanush M, Hanumantharaya, Ajay Sudhir Bale
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: MedTrack is a comprehensive mobile health platform which will be very useful in case of emergency healthcare, whereby the users will be able to quickly locate the nearest hospitals when there is need of such blood Types or antivenoms get directions (Google Maps) and then contact the hospitals directly. The system will be allowed to have unregistered emergency lookup (no user registration required) and advanced features for the registered users, like appointment booking. The hospital executives will be able to register, verify, and then also manage their inventory and t o m a k i n g appointments using a very secure admin portal with JWT-based authentication. We built MedTrack using React.js (front end), Java/Spring Boot (back end), and MySQL. The performance testing showed a median response time of 11 ms and p95 of 104 ms when the load was 100 virtual users. During the pilot usability study (N=15), the System Usability Scale (SUS) score was 84.3 and the task completion rate was 92%. The MedTrack’s architecture consists of authenticated hospital inventory updates, TTL or Time To Live based inventory validation, and optional HL7 FHIR compatible endpoints for hooking up with hospital systems.

title: Enhanced Brain Tumor Segmentation in MRI Using EfficientNetB7 and U-Net Architecture
authors: J.Benita, Nelakurthi Raj Kumar, S. Maheswara Reddy, S.Ruthrasankar, Bathina Ganga Darahaas Reddy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Brain tumors can be detected and segmented to diagnose and treat them properly. MRI images are a wonderful representation of brain being able to see in detail however the manual system of highlighting the location of the tumors can be very time consuming and can encompass human error. Our paper provides a deep-learning-based encoder-decoder architecture to implement the concept of automatic and precise tumor segmentation in magnetic-resonance-imaging; a deep-learning-based EfficientNetB7 and U-Net hybrid that aims to combine the two to form a unified algorithm. EfficientNetB7 is computationally efficient encoder; the advantage of EfficientNetB7 as compared to other encoders is that rich and significant features of an MRI can be extracted. U-Net is a deep-learning architecture that is used in medical images processing to redesign the mask of the segmentation in the right way, as its decoder path and skip connectivity during architecture. We also implement the principal preprocessing steps of skull stripping, normalization, and scaling, and implement data augmenting steps to rotation and flipping in order to implement robustness and reduce the model overfitting. Transfer learning is applied to enhance performance training of a model that has already been trained on publicly available brain tumor data (BraTS). We discovered that our model was rich in Dice similarity coefficient (DSC) of 0.92 which was big in capacity than the preceding segmentation models, especially in distinguishing smaller and egregious tumor fragments. In general, as stated, the model produced clean and consistent boundaries and with fewer false positives. All in all, the results suggest that the given method might be applied to the real-world by clinical specialists to guarantee the correct and timely diagnosis and, ultimately, a better patient outcome.

title: Smart Contract Optimization in Business Workflows Using Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Kathari Santosh, Neha Jain, Annapurna Mishra, Sajiv G, G. Sreeram, I Infant Raj
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Smart contracts are self-executing digital agreements deployed on blockchain platforms that automate business processes with transparency and security. While they eliminate the need for intermediaries, their major limitation lies in their static logic, which lacks adaptability to dynamic conditions such as supply chain disruptions, market fluctuations, or contract breaches. This rigidity often leads to inefficiencies, delays, and financial losses in real-world applications. To address this challenge, we propose a hybrid framework called SmartGPO, which integrates Graph Neural Networks with Proximal Policy Optimization. The research aims to enhance the adaptability and intelligence of smart contracts by combining structural awareness and decision-making capabilities. The proposed system models the contract environment as a graph, where nodes represent entities and edges denote their interactions. GNNs generate relational embeddings, which are then used by the PPO agent to learn optimal contract execution policies through reward-driven training. SmartGPO achieves superior performance in dynamic contract workflows, with an execution success rate of 98.4 % and a decision-making accuracy of 98.1 %, outperforming traditional and standalone models. The framework also demonstrates improved gas cost reduction and faster processing time. Future enhancements include integrating multi-agent learning, real-time oracle connectivity, and legal compliance layers to further improve security, scalability, and trust. This research marks a step forward in developing intelligent, adaptive smart contracts for real-world blockchain applications.

title: Smart Robotic Arms Trained with RL for Assembly-Line Automation
authors: Balamurugan. M, Ahmad Abdelhafiz Ali Samhan, Geetha R, V. Ramabhai, N. Srihitha Gunapriya, S. Kaliappan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Assembly-line automation requires robots that can react dynamically to a range of parts and extremely precise fits, but legacy systems that rely on fixed paths and teach-pendant programming are inflexible the reconfiguration force uncertainty, downtime is high during set-up for new products. In one example, a smart assembly framework leverages deep reinforcement learning to guide dual-arm collaboration through visual pose estimation and force–torque sensing as well as curriculum driven feedback-based training on tasks that span insertion, alignment, and handover. The results based on three product variants demonstrate success rate improvement from 88% up to 97%, cycle-time reduction of 21 %, and performance drop of only 6 % for unseen geometries. Stimulation and analysis results show better stability than in worst-case conditions and lower peak contact forces, leading to a more reliable quality and less mechanical faults. The results indicate the potential for scalable adaptation with minimal retraining, which enables agile manufacturing and industrial smart-factory schemes.

title: AutoSew: A Geometric Approach to Stitching Prediction with Graph Neural Networks
authors: Pablo Ríos-Navarro, Elena Garces, Jorge Lopez-Moreno
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Automating garment assembly from sewing patterns remains a significant challenge due to the lack of standardized annotation protocols and the frequent absence of semantic cues. Existing methods often rely on panel labels or handcrafted heuristics, which limit their applicability to real-world, non-conforming patterns. We present AutoSew, a fully automatic, geometry-based approach for predicting stitch correspondences directly from 2D pattern contours. AutoSew formulates the problem as a graph matching task, leveraging a Graph Neural Network to capture local and global geometric context, and employing a differentiable optimal transport solver to infer stitching relationships—including multi-edge connections. To support this task, we update the GarmentCodeData dataset modifying over 18k patterns with realistic multi-edge annotations, reflecting industrial assembly scenarios. AutoSew achieves 96% F1-score and successfully assembles 73.3% of test garments without error, outperforming existing methods while relying solely on geometric input. Our results demonstrate that geometry alone can robustly guide stitching prediction, enabling scalable garment assembly without manual input. Our dataset and code are available online.<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>

title: Explainable Quantum-Enhanced Federated Learning Framework for Real-Time Cardiovascular Risk Assessment
authors: Sriramkumar R, Nagarjuna Naik Mude, Rakshita R, Mithaguru, A.Vegi Fernando, Joshuva Arockia Dhanraj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: According to the World Health Organization, cardiovascular diseases are responsible for several million deaths worldwide each year, thus highlighting a prime necessity for developing methods of diagnosis that are accurate and rapid. Although recent advancements in quantum-enhanced machine learning have demonstrated enhanced prediction performance, current research is still limited by the reliance on static datasets, the lack of interpretability, and the lack of privacy-preserving mechanisms for data sharing across institutions. In this paper, we introduce an Explainable Quantum-Enhanced Federated Learning Framework for Real-Time Cardiovascular Risk Assessment that integrates the scalability of federated learning with the computational benefits of quantum models to allow multiple healthcare centers and remote devices to train models collaboratively without sharing raw patient data. Quantum-inspired classifiers such as variational circuits and Quantum SVM are integrated into the federated pipeline to accelerate convergence and improve robustness. An explanation layer was implemented to enhance clinical trust by applying changed feature attribution methods that help the physicians comprehend how much the important factors like cholesterol, blood pressure, and ECG patterns contribute to the risk predictions. In addition, the system is meant for constant watch by the mixing of electronic health records and wearable sensor streams, thus making it possible to switch from one-time prediction to dynamically and personally tailored risk assessment. The proposed technique has been validated through experiments on standard heart disease datasets where it is also said to have done the opposite: it has improved predictive accuracy, reduced communication overhead compared to traditional federated systems, and provided insights that are ready for supporting clinical decision-making in real practice.
