Consequently, this framework is a first step towards quantum-driven healthcare solutions that are safe, scalable, and explainable.

title: An IoT-Based Wearable for Real-Time Stress Detection in Classroom Environments
authors: Sharada Adepu, Mamatha Racharla, Jayashree S Patil, Pallavi Reddy Reddygari, Lalitha Parameswari Dantu Venkata, Bhageshwari Ratkal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Mental pressure is one of the growing problems of the prevailing society. long term results of pressure can cause problems together with high blood pressure, cardiovascular diseases, and reminiscence troubles. timely stress detection can help to save you pressure related health problems. The intention of the project is to design an IOT based wearable, fee effective and occasional strength clever band to detect mental strain in students and perceive the pressure inducing elements in colleges and schools. stress detection era should help people better recognize and relieve strain through increasing their cognizance of heightened stage of strain that would otherwise move undetected.WE-CARE (Revelation classroom wearables) The wristband determines the degree to which the student is under stress based on a number of factors, including body temperature and heart rate. Numerous sensors, such as electrocardiograms (ECGs), galvanic pores and skin response (GSRs), infrared (IR), and pink LEDs made of materials like GaAs, also known and aluminum gallium arsenide (AlGaAs), can be used to obtain these statistics. These LEDs produce a gentle light that can pass through the skin and detect variations in blood flow. The product is solely constructed for college students to identify the various pressure inducing elements within the study room surroundings on a every day foundation. The band is activated most effective by using an external sensor while the student enters the study room and therefore may be used simplest in the classroom premises. every wristband can have a completely unique RFID (Radio Frequency identity) that's mapped with the respective student identity. The raw records accrued via the sensors of the band is mapped to the cloud in which it is first synchronized, filtered, segmented, and many others. as soon as these preprocessing steps are completed, functions are computed, aggregating the statistics found in every sign section. finally, classification is achieved through mapping among the characteristic space and the desired labels. If the calculated stress exceeds the brink restrict, an alert could be generated. The statistics will be stored inside the cloud simultaneously. These statistics can later be used identifying the stress patterns in students and assist in the implementation of powerful techniques to reduce strain.

title: Climate Based Crop Zoning Using Artificial Intelligence
authors: R Nancy Deborah, S Alwyn Rajiv, R Kavitha, P Sahana, M Petchiammal Monisha, S Esther Jenifa
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: Climate variability and resource limitations have made traditional crop planning methods increasingly unreliable. Recent research shows that integrating data-driven decision support systems into agriculture can improve sustainability and productivity. Existing studies have used statistical modeling, machine learning, and remote sensing to predict crop suitability, but many face challenges in feature selection, model tuning, and adaptability across different soil and climate conditions. In this paper, we propose a Bayesian-optimized Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) framework for predicting crop suitability. The system uses important environmental and soil factors—rainfall, temperature, soil pH, slope, and crop type—to accurately classify suitability levels. The feature module ensures systematic preprocessing and label encoding, while Bayesian optimization automates hyperparameter tuning, which reduces the need for manual trial-and-error. Experimental results on a curated dataset show better classification performance and generalization compared to conventional methods. The proposed system not only improves predictive reliability but also provides a practical tool for farmers, agronomists, and policymakers to make informed choices about crop selection, land management, and sustainable resource use. By combining machine learning with real-world agricultural factors, this work connects academic research with practical applications in precision agriculture.

title: UGF-Net: Uncertainty-Guided Fusion for Robust RGB-NIR Traffic Semantic Segmentation in Adverse Weather
authors: Siddhartha Pittala, Subba Reddy Chavva, Govardhan Rao Kasarelli, Peyyala Sanjay Kumar
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Semantic segmentation in autonomous driving needs to be strongly invariant to different weather conditions, where single-camera approaches are prone to failure. Though fusion of RGB-NIR images has shown promise, previous methods have relied upon pre-defined fusion strategies, which are suboptimal, or upon weather supervision, which is undesireable. To address this, we present a new deep network, UGF-Net, in which a self-adaptive fusion of RGB-NIR images is performed using dynamically updated weights estimated from epistemic uncertainty of additional segmentation heads. On IDD-AW, our self-adaptive solution demonstrates 58.4% mIoU (87.2% of supervised SOTA performance) without weather annotation, which is very high in fog (69.2%) and rainy (61.8%) conditions. Gate analysis demonstrates interpretable, weather-aware preferences of sensors (Fog: 4.44:1, dominantly RGB, Rain: 3.70:1, well-balanced).

title: UnderWater SLAM with Laser-light sectioning method using ST-GAT
authors: Heyang Gao, Kazuto Ichimaru, Takafumi Iwaguchi, Hiroshi Kawasaki
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Multi-line laser ID assignment is crucial for underwater 3D reconstruction but fails when lines fragment. We reformulate this as a graph-based sequence labeling task and propose a novel two-stage hierarchical framework using Spatio-Temporal Graph Attention Networks (ST-GAT). Our method first reasons over a spatio-temporal graph of laser endpoints and intersections to handle local fragmentation, then elevates this to a global segment-level optimization with trajectory-constrained Viterbi decoding to ensure temporal consistency. This GNN-based approach eliminates the reliance on complete epipolar geometry. Experiments on real underwater datasets demonstrate superior reconstruction completeness and temporal stability, especially in challenging environments where traditional methods fail.

title: VogueVault-Intelligent Digital Closet and AI-Powered Personal Stylist
authors: Mehak Nasheen Aslam, Jayanth S, Sunil Kumar H Soddagi, Madhu Manojkumar Mughannavar, Lakshmana
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: The modern fashion industry offers consumers extensive choices, often leading to "wardrobe paralysis"-a state of decision fatigue caused by excessive options. This issue, amplified by fast fashion and limited online shopping tools, results in high product return rates (over 20%) and environmental impact. This paper presents VogueVault, an AI-powered platform that integrates an intelligent digital closet with a personalized virtual try-on (VTO) system. The proposed architecture combines advanced computer vision and natural language processing models to enhance user experience and sustainability. Key modules include U2-Net for garment segmentation, ResNet for clothing classification, SMPL for generating 3D user avatars, CLIP for natural language-based styling recommendations, and a hybrid SMPL-CP-VTON model for realistic virtual try-on. By providing a seamless and interactive interface, VogueVault aims to reduce decision fatigue, improve purchasing confidence, and encourage sustainable fashion choices, thereby addressing both user and environmental challenges in the contemporary fashion ecosystem.

title: AgencyTree: Automated Multimedia Workflow Generation with Robust Planning via an Abstract Capability Tree
authors: Wei Liu, Bowen Luo, Xiaohong Chen, Dongming Jin, Zhi Jin, Chengfei Yuan

title: On DNN Model Partitioning and Deployment for Collaborative Multi-Edge AI Computing_supp1-3682802.pdf
authors: Weibo Chu
abstract: Offloading DNN workload to an edge network for parallel and distributed computing is a promising solution to hosting computationintensive DNN services. The challenge is how to partition DNN models and to reduce the communication to realize a high-performance multiedge DNN computing. In this paper, we first propose a novel two-stage bayesian optimization based approach to DNN model partitioning, which can decouple a DNN model into components at appropriate granularity while at the same time incur less communication cost between them. The proposed approach is physical infrastructure agnostic and can achieve a decomposition ratio of 3.2× to 35.0× on popular DNN models while respecting their execution parallelism. Moreover, we consider online DNN model deployment scenarios, with the aim to maximize the rate of requests supported by the network. We formulate a mixed integer linear programming (MILP) problem for the task and establish its NP-hardness. To tackle it efficiently, we decompose the problem into two sub-problems, and propose a binary search method to the upperlevel one and a backtracking based algorithm to the lower-level one. We evaluate our mechanisms through both simulation and experiments, and results show they are effective as compared to baselines.

title: Opinion Dynamics on Higher-Order Social Networks: A Continuous-Time Perspective
authors: Zhaoyang Duan, Jiangwei Yan, Dong Xue, Fangzhou Liu, Yang Tang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: Opinion dynamics models that elucidate the evolution and formation of opinions conventionally focus on pairwise interactions within graphs, often overlooking the complex higher-order interactions that arise in real-world social networks, such as online meetings and group chats. In this article, a continuous-time dynamical system is developed to study opinion-forming processes over higher-order networks associated with undirected hypergraphs. The proposed model introduces a novel diffusion-like interaction function to characterize interactions of different orders over hypergraphs. The convergence and stability of the dynamical systems are further examined in both the presence and absence of stubborn individuals. Building on traditional opinion dynamics models and integrating weak-tie theory, we emphasize the critical role of higher-order interactions in shaping individual opinions, enhancing network communication efficiency, and mitigating opinion polarization. Finally, all theoretical results are extensively investigated and empirically validated through numerical experiments on both synthetic and real-world network datasets.

title: Stacked Intelligent Metasurfaces for 6G: Element-Level Modeling, Reliability Analysis, and Unit Design
authors: Yuan Cao, Wei Peng
abstract: For next-generation wireless communications pursuing high energy efficiency and high throughput, stacked intelligent metasurfaces (SIM) have attracted growing attention due to the signal processing capability in the wave domain. The SIM is composed of multiple layers of programmable transmissive metasurfaces that cooperatively manipulate the amplitude and phase of electromagnetic waves. Currently, most existing studies are conducted under the ideal phase-shift assumption, neglecting the practical modeling based on the electromagnetic response, and often overlook practical hardware non-idealities, including inter-layer misalignment, random unit failures, and manufacturing tolerances, which may significantly degrade the system reliability and overall performance. In this paper, we first establish a element-level modeling framework by integrating the physical attributes of transmissive elements with their equivalent circuit responses, ensuring an electromagnetic characterization that is more consistent with reality. This foundation provides a more practical basis for subsequent system-level algorithm designs. Building on this, various hardware errors encountered in practical deployments are categorized and comprehensively analyzed. Furthermore, we survey the current landscape of element design and explore future research directions and challenges for SIM applications. Finally, potential solutions are provided alongside an outlook on the vision of SIM-aided next-generation communication systems.
